105.title: C8-Test 4-Section 1
transcript: Hello, West Bay Hotel.Can I help you?
你好，西海湾酒店，我能帮你做什么
Oh, good morning. I'm ringing about your advertisement in the Evening Gazette.
早上好，我打电话是为了你们在《Evening Gazette》上登的广告
Is that the one for temporary staff?
招临时工的那个吗
That's right.
是的
Yes.
好的
I'm afraid the person who's dealing with that isn't in today, but I can give you the main details if you like.
恐怕负责人今天不在，但是如果你乐意，我可以为你介绍一些主要的内容，
Yes please.Could you tell me what kind of staff you're looking for?
好的，你能告诉我你们在招哪种员工吗
We're looking for waiters at the moment. There was one post for a cook, but that's already been taken.
我们现在正在招服务生有一个厨师的岗位，不过已经有人了。
Oh right. Erm, what are the hours of work?
好的工作时间是什么
There are two different shifts-there's a day shift from 7 to 2 and a late shift from 4 till 11.
有两种不同的班，早班是7点到两点，晚班是4点到11点。
And can people choose which one they want to do?
员工可以选择自己想上哪一种吗
Not normally, because everyone would choose the day shift I suppose.You alternate from one week to another.
通常不能，因为我认为每个人都想选择早班，每周轮换一次
Okay. I'm...I'm just writing all this down. What about time off?
好的，我把这些都记下来了，关于休息日呢
You get one day off and I think you can negotiate which one you want, it's more or less up to you. But it has to be the same one every week.
你有一天的休息，我认为你可以协商选哪天休。这或多或少取决于你，但是必须每周都是同一天。
Do you know what the rates of pay are?
你知道薪水是多少吗
Yes, I've got them here. You get ￡5.50 an hour, and that includes a break.
是的，我这里有，每小时5.50英镑，包括一个短暂的休息时间，
Do I have to go home to eat or...
我得回家吃饭还是……
You don't have to.You can get a meal in the hotel if you want to, and there's no charge for it so you might as well.
你不必，如果你愿意，你不妨在酒店吃一顿饭，而且是免费的。
Oh good.Yes, so let's see.
很好，是的，让我看看
I'd get...er, two hundred and twenty one, no, two hundred and thirty one pounds a week?
221磅，不是，是231英镑
You'd also get tips-our guests tend to be quite generous.
你还能收到小费，我们的客人通常很大方。
Erm, is there a uniform? What about clothes?
有制服吗，服装怎么样
Yes, I forgot to mention that.You need to wear a white shirt, just a plain one, and dark trousers.
是的，我忘了说了，你得穿白衬衫，普通的那种，深色的裤子，
You know, not green or anything like that. And we don't supply those.
不能是绿色或类似这种的颜色，我们不提供这些
That's okay, I've got trousers, I'd just have to buy a couple of shirts.
没关系，我有裤子，我只需买几件衬衫。
What about anything else? Do I need a waistcoat or anything?
其他的事情呢，我需要马夹或其他什么吗
You have to wear a jacket, but the hotel lends you that.
你得穿外套，不过酒店会借给你。
I see. Er, one last thing-I don't know what the starting date is.
我明白了，最后一件事，我不知道开始日期是几号。
Just a minute, I think it's sometime around the end of June. Yes, the 28th, in time for the summer.
稍等。我想时间是在6月底左右。，是的，在夏季的28日。
That's great. I'm available from the 10th
好极了，我从10号开始就有空了
Oh good.
很好
Well, if you can call again you need to speak to the Service Manager.
如果你再打来电话，你需要找服务经理，
Her name's Jane Urwin, that's U-R-W-l-N, and she'll probably arrange to meet you.
她叫简·厄温，拼写是U-R-W-I-N，她可能会安排与你见面。
Okay. And when's the best time to ring?
好的，什么时候打电话最合适
Could you call tomorrow?
明天可以吗
Um, she usually starts checking the rooms at midday, so before then if you can, so she'll have more time to chat.
她通常在中午开始检查房间，所以如果可以的话在此之前打来，这样她有更多的谈话时间。
I'll just give you her number because she's got a direct line.
我把她的电话给你，她有直线电话
Thanks.
谢谢
It's 832 double-0 9.
823 double-0 9?
Oh, okay. Yes, I'll do that.
好的，我会打的
And by the way, she will ask you for a reference, so you might like to be thinking about that.
顺便说一下，她会问你要推荐信，所以你可以想一想，
You know, just someone who knows you and can vouch for you.
就是有人了解你并能为你做担保
Yes, no problem. Well, thanks very much for your help.
好的，没问题，非常感谢你的帮助，
You're welcome. Bye.
不客气，再见
Bye.
再见

106.title: C8-Test 4-Section 2
transcript: Good morning and welcome again to Your City Today.
早上好，欢迎再次来到《今日城市》节目。
With me today is Graham Campbell, a councilor from the city council.
和我在一起的是市政厅的议员格雷厄姆·坎贝尔
He will be telling us about the plan to improve the fast-growing suburb of Red Hill.
他将告诉我们关于改善正在蓬勃发展的红山郊区计划
Good morning Graham and welcome to the show.
早上好，格雷厄姆，欢迎来到此次节目
Good morning, Carol.
早，卡罗尔
Now, Graham, I understand that there has been a lot of community consultation for the new plan.
格雷厄姆，据说这个新计划已经进行了许多民意咨询？
Yes, we've tried to address some of the concerns that local groups told us about.
是的。我们已经竭尽全力去应对那些本地组织向我们反映的一些问题。
People we've heard from are mainly worried about traffic in the area, and, in particular, the increasing speed of cars near schools.
人们主要担心该地区的交通问题，特别是校区附近日益增加的车速。
They feel that it's only a matter of time before there is an accident as a lot of the children walk to the school.
有很多学生步行上学，他们觉得出事只是时间问题。
So we're trying to do something about that.
所以我们试着在这方面改善
Another area of concern is the overhead power lines.
另一个令人担忧的问题是头顶的电线
These are very old and a lot of people we spoke to asked if something could be done about them.
它们非常旧，我们问到的许多人都想知道是否可以改善一下它们。
Well, I'm happy to report that the power company have agreed to move the power lines underground at a cost of $800,000.
我很高兴地告诉大家，电力公司已经同意耗资800，000美元将这些电线转移到地下。
I think that will really improve the look of the area, as well as being safer.
我认为这会改善该地区的面貌，并且变得更安全。
That's good to know, but will that mean an increase in rates for the local businesses in that area?
这是好消息，但这是否意味着该地区的企业税会增加？
Well, the power company have agreed to bear the cost of this themselves after a lot of discussion with the council.
在与委员会经过多次讨论后，电力公司同意承担这方面的花费。
This is wonderful news as the council now has some extra funds for us to put into other things like tree planting and art work.
这是一个好消息，委员会现在有更多的资金为我们建设其他的东西，如植树和艺术作品。
Now, we've also put together a map which we've sent out to all the residents in the area.
我们还整理了一份地图发放给该地区的所有居民
And on the map we've marked the proposed changes.
我们在地图上标记着所有提议改善的地方
Firstly, we'll plant mature pine trees to provide shelter and shade just to the right of the supermarket in Days Road.
首先，我们将在天路上超市的右边种植成年松树以提供荫凉。
In order to address the traffic problems, the pavements on the corner of Carberry and Thomas Street will be widened.
为了解决交通问题，我们将会加宽卡伯里与托马斯街道拐角处的人行道。
This will help to reduce the speed of vehicles entering Thomas Street.
这将帮助减少进入托马斯街道的行车速度
We think it's very important to separate the local residential streets from the main road.
我们认为将住宅区旁的街道与主干道分开也很重要
So the roadway at the entry to Thomas Street from Days Road will be painted red.
所以从天路进入托马斯街道的路面将被刷成红色
This should mark it more clearly and act as a signal for traffic to slow down.
它将被标记得更加明显，对于交通来说是一个减速信号。
One way of making sure that the pedestrians are safe is to increase signage at the intersections.
确保行人安全的方法之一是增加十字路口的标志
A " keep clear "sign will be erected at the junction of Evelyn Street and Hill Street, to enable traffic to exit at all times.
“让路”标志应该放在伊芙琳街和希尔街的交叉路口，使车流可以随时出来。
Something we're planning to do to help control the flow of traffic in the area is to install traffic lights half way down Hill Street where it crosses Days Road.
为了控制交通量我们计划去做一些事情，如在希尔街的中点，即与天路相交处安装交通灯。
Now, we haven't only thought about the cars and traffic, of course, there's also something for the children.
现在，我们不仅考虑了汽车和交通，当然，还要考虑儿童。

We're going to get school children in the area to research a local story, the life of a local sports hero perhaps, and an artist will incorporate that story into paintings on the wall of a building on the other side of Hill Street from the supermarket.
我们将动员当地学校的孩子们挖掘一个本土故事，也许是当地一位体育明星的生平。然后请一位艺术家把这个故事融入到画作中。该画将挂在希尔街超市对面的大楼墙上。
And finally, we've agreed to build a new children's playground which will be at the other end of Hill Street close to the intersection with Carberry Street.
最后，我们一致同意在希尔街邻近卡伯里街的十字路口的尽头为孩子们建一个游乐场。
Wonderful, now, what's the next stage?
好极了，下一步是什么，
"Well, the final plan...
最后的计划是……"

107.title: C8-Test 4-Section 3
transcript: Hi Jeannie.
你好，珍妮
How's it going?
最近怎么样
Oh, hello Dan.
你好，丹
Pretty well, thanks.
我很好，谢谢
Have you managed to get the money for the course yet?
你筹到上学的钱了吗
Yes, that's all sorted out now, thanks.
是的，现在都解决了，谢谢
It took long enough, though.
尽管花了很长时间
It was practically a year ago that I applied to my local council for a grant, and it took them six months to turn me down.
差不多一年前我向地方政府申请助学金，他们花了六个月的时间拒绝了我的申请
That's really slow.
太慢了
And I thought I was eligible for government funding, but it seems I was mistaken.
我想我有资格申请政府资助，但是看起来我弄错了
So then I asked the boss of the company I used to work for if they would sponsor me, and much to my surprise, he said they'd make a contribution.
所以我之后问我曾经公司的老板是否他们能资助我，令我吃惊的是他说他们可以出资。
But what about college grants and scholarships?
但是学院奖学金怎么样
There must be some you could apply for.
肯定有你能申请的项目
Yes, there are, but they're all so small that I decided to leave them until I was desperate.
是有一些，但都太少了，我决定先不申请，等急需的时候再说，
Uhuh.
嗯
And in fact I didn't need to apply.
其实我不用申请了
My parents had been saying that as I already had a job, I ought to support myself through college.
我父母曾说既然我有工作，我应该自己交大学学费。
But in the end they took pity on me, so now I've just about got enough.
但最终他们对我表示同情，所以现在我的钱差不多够了，
That's good.
太好了
So now I can put a bit of effort into meeting people--I haven't had time so far.
所以现在我可以在社交上下点功夫了，到目前为止我还没有时间，
Any suggestions?
有什么建议吗
What about joining some college clubs?
加入一些大学俱乐部怎么样
Oh right.
好的
You joined several didn't you?
你已经加入了几个，不是吗，
Yes, I'm in the drama club.
是的，我加入了戏剧社。
It's our first performance next week, so we're rehearsing frantically, and I've got behind with my work, but it's worth it.
下周是我们第一次演出，所以我们正在疯狂的排练，我的学业都耽误了，但是值得。
I'm hoping to be in the spring production, too.
我还希望参加春季演出
I've never liked acting.
我从不喜欢表演
Are you doing anything else?
你还做别的事情吗
I enjoyed singing when I was at school, so I joined a group when I came to college.
我在学校的时候还喜欢唱歌，所以我到大学后参加了一个团体。
I don't think the conductor stretches us enough, though so I'll give up after the next concert.
我认为管理者不能充分发挥我们的才能，所以我将在下一个音乐会后退出。
And I also joined the debating society.
我还加入了辩论社
It's fun, but with all the rehearsing I'm doing, something has to go, and I'm afraid that's the one.
很有意思，但现在我有许多排练，不得不舍弃个别活动，恐怕就是它了。
Do you do any sports?
你做运动吗
Yes, I'm in one of the hockey teams.
是的，我加入了其中一个曲棍球队，
I'm not very good, but I'd really miss it if I stopped.
我打得不太好，但如果不参加了我会非常想念它。
I decided to try tennis when I came to college, and I'm finding it pretty tough going.
刚上大学时我决定尝试打网球，但后来发现太难了，
I'm simply not fit enough.
我根本没法适应
Nor me.
我也是
I think I'll give that a miss!
我想我不会参加
I'm hoping it'll help me to build up my stamina, but it'll probably be a long haul.
我希望它能帮我增强耐力，但是这可能是一个长期的过程。
Good luck.
祝你好运
Thanks.
谢谢
How are you finding the course?
你觉得课程怎么样
I wish we had more seminars.
我希望我们能多一些研讨会
What?
什么
I'd have thought we had more than enough already.
我认为我们已经够多了
All those people saying clever things that I could never think of- it's quite interesting, but I wonder if I'm clever enough to be doing this course.
他们说一些我从没想过的聪明点子，很有趣，但是我想知道我是否足够聪明能应付这门课。
I find it helpful to listen to the other people.
我觉得聆听他人很有帮助
I like the way we're exploring the subject, and working towards getting insight into it.
我喜欢我们对研究题目进行探索的方式，不断努力获得更深刻的理解。
How do you get on with your tutor?
你和你的导师相处得怎么样
I don't think I'm on the same wavelength as mine, so I feel I'm not getting anything out of the tutorials.
我认为我和我的导师不合拍，所以我感觉我没有从课堂上学到任何东西，
It would be more productive to read a book instead.
还不如看书更有用
Oh, mine's very demanding.
我的导师要求很高
She gives me lots of feedback and advice, so I've got much better at writing essays.
她给我很多反馈和建议，所以我在论文写作方面有了很大提高
And she's helping me plan my revision for the end-of-year exams.
她还帮我计划年底的考试复习
Do tell me, I always struggle with revision.
快告诉我怎么弄的，我总是纠结于论文修改
Well, the first thing is to find out exactly what's required in the exams.
首先是找到考试到底要求什么
Mm.
嗯
Would it help to get hold of some past papers?
掌握一些过去的论文对此有帮助吗
Yes.
是的
They'll help to make it clear.
它们能帮你弄清思路
Right, I'll do that. Then what?
好的，我会这么做。然后是什么？
Then you can sort out your revision priorities, based on what's most likely to come up.
然后根据最可能出现的问题，你可以挑出优先复习的地方。
I put these on a card, and read them through regularly.
我把这些写在一张卡片上，时不时的读一读
Uhuh.
额
But that isn't enough in itself.
但是这还不够
You also need a timetable, to see how you can fit everything in, in the time available.
你还需要一张时间表，看看如何在有限的时间里完成一切，
Then keep it in front of you while you're studying.
然后在你学习时，把它放在你的前面。
I've done that before, but it hasn't helped me!
我以前这么做过，但是对我没帮助。
Maybe you need to do something different every day, so if you break down your revision into small tasks, and allocate them to specific days, there's more incentive to tackle them.
也许你需要每天做些不同的事情。所以如果你把复习任务分成很多小任务，并把这些小任务分配到具体的每一天，你就会有更强的动力去完成。
With big topics you're more likely to put off starting.
大的主题往往迟迟难以开始
Good idea.
好主意
And as I revise each topic I write a single paragraph about it-then later I can read it through quickly, and it helps fix things in my mind.
当我复习每个题目时，我会写上一段文字，之后我可以快速地阅读它，这帮我在脑海里解决很多事情。
That's brilliant.
太棒了
I also write answers to questions for the exam practice.
我也会写出一些考试问题的答案
It's hard to make myself do it, though!
尽管这对我来说很难
Well, I'll try.
我会试试
Thanks a lot, Jeannie.
非常感谢你，珍妮
That's a great help.
这很有帮助
No problem.
不客气
See you around.
回头见
Bye.
再见

108.title: C8-Test 4-Section 4
transcript: Good morning, everyone.
大家早上好
I've been invited to talk about my research project into Australian Aboriginal rock paintings.
我应邀来此，与大家聊聊我的研究项目，澳大利亚原住民的岩石绘画，
The Australian Aborigines have recorded both real and symbolic images of their time on rock walls for many thousands of years.
澳大利亚原住民已经持续数千年在石墙上记录他们所处时代现实的及非现实的画面
Throughout the long history of this tradition, new images have appeared and new painting styles have developed.
在这个传统的悠久历史中，新的图像和绘画风格不断更迭演化。
And these characteristics can be used to categorize the different artistic styles.
这些特点可以用来对不同种的艺术风格进行分类
Among these are what we call the Dynamic, Yam and Modern styles of painting.
我们所说的“活力”，“甘薯”和“现代”绘画风格也包含在其中。
One of the most significant characteristics of the different styles is the way that humans are depicted in the paintings.
这些不同的画风中最重要的特色之一就是画面上描绘人类的方式
The more recent paintings show people in static poses.
最近的绘画显示人们处于静止状态
But the first human images to dominate rock art paintings, over 8,000 years ago, were full of movement.
但是8000多年前的早期主流石画中的人物形象却充满了动感
These paintings showed people hunting and cooking food and so they were given the name 'Dynamic' to reflect this energy.
这些绘画展现了人们狩猎和煮饭的样子，因此它们被命名为“活力”以反映这种精力。
It's quite amazing considering they were painted in such a simple stick-like form.
能将他们画成这种简单的，线形画是很惊人的。
In the Yam period, there was a movement away from stick figures to a more naturalistic shape.
在“甘薯”石画时期，线形画开始向更加自然的形态转变。
However, they didn't go as far as the Modern style, which is known as "X-Ray" because it actually makes a feature of the internal skeleton as we" as the organs of animals and humans.
然而，它们不及“现代”风格。后者被称为“X光”，因为事实上它的特点是描绘了动物和人的骨架及器官。
The Yam style of painting got its name from the fact that it featured much curvier figures that actually resemble the vegetable called a yam, which is similar to a sweet potato.
“甘薯”石画风格因为其更加突出曲线形态而得名。这种形态实际上有点像一种叫甘薯的蔬菜，类似于甘甜的土豆。
The Modern paintings are interesting because they include paintings at the time of the first contact with European settlers.
“现代”风格的绘画很有趣，因为它们画出了第一次与欧洲移居者的接触。
Aborigines managed to convey the idea of the settlers' clothing by simply painting the Europeans without any hands, indicating the habit of standing with their hands in their pockets!
原住民试图以不画手的方式来体现移居者的服装，以体现后者站立时总是将手插在兜里的习惯！
Size is another characteristic.
大小是另一个特点。
The more recent images tend to be life size or even larger, but the Dynamic figures are painted in miniature.
近期的图像往往和真人一样大或者更大一些，但是“活力”风格的人物被描绘得很小。
Aboriginal rock art also records the environmental changes that occurred over thousands of years.
原住民的岩石绘画也记录了数千年来的环境变化
For example, we know from the Dynamic paintings that over 8,000 years ago, Aborigines would have rarely eaten fish and sea levels were much lower at this time.
例如，我们知道“活力”绘画时期是在8000多年前，土著居民在那时很少吃鱼而且海平面很低。
In fact, fish didn't start to appear in paintings until the Yam period along with shells and other marine images.
事实上，鱼的形象直到“甘薯”时代才和贝壳及其他海洋生物形象一起出现在画作中。

The paintings of the Yam tradition also suggest that, during this time, the Aborigines moved away from animals as their main food source and began including vegetables in their diet, as this feature prominently.
“甘薯”时期的画作也表明，这一时期原住民的饮食不再将动物作为主要食物来源，他们已经开始吃蔬菜。这些特点在画作中非常明显。
Freshwater creatures didn't appear in the paintings until the Modern period from 4,000 years ago.
淡水生物直到4000年前的“现代”时期才出现在画作上。
So, these paintings have already taught us a lot.
所以这些画作已经让我们了解了很多
But one image that has always intrigued us is known as the "Rainbow Serpent".
但是有一个图像总能引起我们的好奇，它就是“彩虹蛇”。
The Rainbow Serpent, which is the focus of my most recent project, gets its name from its snake or serpent-like body and it first appeared in the Yam period 4 to 6,000 years ago.
彩虹蛇是我近期研究项目的焦点，它因其蛇形的身躯而得名，并于4000至6000年前首次出现在“甘薯”时期。
Many believe it is a curious mixture of kangaroo, snake and crocodile.
许多人认为它是袋鼠、蛇、鳄鱼的奇妙组合。
But we decided to study the Rainbow Serpent paintings to see if we could locate the animal that the very first painters based their image on.
但我们决定研究彩虹蛇画作，目的是要找出最早的画者所依据的是何种动物。
The Yam period coincided with the end of the last ice age.
“甘薯”时期正好赶上最后一个冰河世纪的末期
This brought about tremendous change in the environment, with the sea levels rising and creeping steadily inland.
这导致环境发生了翻天覆地的变化，海平面不断上升并且向内陆蔓延。
This flooded many familiar land features and also caused a great deal of disruption to traditional patterns of life, hunting in particular.
这淹没了许多熟悉的地貌，同时对传统生活模式造成了极大的破坏，尤其是打猎。
New shores were formed and totally different creatures would have washed up onto the shores.
新的海岸形成了，完全不同的生物被冲向了海岸。
We studied 107 paintings of the Rainbow Serpent and found that the one creature that matches it most closely was the Ribboned Pipefish, which is a type of sea horse.
我们研究了107种关于彩虹蛇的画作，并且发现条纹海龙鱼是与之最匹配的生物，它是海马的一种。
This sea creature would have been a totally unfamiliar sight in the inland regions where the image is found and may have been the inspiration behind the early paintings.
内陆发现了这种完全陌生的海洋生物的图像，同时它可能激发了早期绘画的灵感。
So, at the end of the ice age there would have been enormous changes in animal and plant life.
所以在冰河世纪的末期，动植物发生了巨大的变化。
It's not surprising then, that the Aborigines linked this abundance to the new creatures they witnessed.
原住民将这些变化与他们目睹的这个新生物联系在一起也就不足为奇了
Even today, Aborigines see the Rainbow Serpent as a symbol of creation, which is understandable given the increase in vegetation and the new life forms that featured when the image first appeared.
即使在今天，原住民还是把彩虹蛇看作是创造力的象征，在它初次出现时增添了许多植物和新的生命形态。

109.title: C7-Test 1-Section 1
transcript: Hello, this is Land Transport information at Toronto Airport.
你好，这里是多伦多机场地面交通信息处
How may I help you?
我能帮你做什么？
Oh, good morning, I'm flying to Toronto Airport next week, and I need to get to a town called um, Milton.
早上好，我下周将飞往多伦多机场，我要去一个叫做米尔顿的城镇。
Could you tell me how I can get there?
你能告诉我怎么才能到那里吗？
Milton, did you say?
你说的是米尔顿吗？
Let me see. I think that's about 150 miles south-west of here.
让我看看，我想它在这里西南处的150英里左右。
In fact it's 147 miles to be exact, so it'll take you at least -say, three to four hours by road.
事实上，精确的说是147英里，所以路上走至少需要3到4小时的时间。
Wow! Is it as far as that?
天啊！这么远？
Yes, I'm afraid so.
恐怕是的。
But you have a number of options to get you there and you can always rent a car right here at the airport, of course.
但是你有很多方法可以到那里，你可以在机场租车去。
Right. Well, I don't really want to drive myself, so I'd like more information about public transport.
是的，但我不想自己开车去，所以我想了解更多公共交通的信息。
Ok. In that case the quickest and most comfortable is a cab and of course there are always plenty available.
好的，这样的话，最快、最舒适的方法是坐出租车，当然有很多车可以用。
But it'll cost you.
但是会花很多钱。
You can also take a greyhound bus or there's an airport shuttle Service to Milton.
你也可以乘坐长途汽车或是机场专车去米尔顿。
Hum, I think for that kind of distance a cab would be way beyond my budget.
我想这么远坐出租车可能会超出我的预算。
But the bus sounds OK.
但是坐汽车听起来不错。
Can you tell me how much that would cost?
你能告诉我这要花多少钱吗？
Sure. Let's see. That would be $15 one way, or $27.50 return.
当然。让我看看。长途巴士单程是15美元，或者往返27.50美元。
That's on the Greyhound.
那个是灰狗巴士的价格
Oh, that's quite cheap-great! But where abouts does it stop in Milton?
这很便宜，太好了！但是它在米尔顿的哪儿停呢？
It goes directly from the airport here to the City Centre and it's pretty fast.
它直接从机场开往市中心，非常快。
But you have to bear in mind that there is only one departure a day, so it depends what time your flight gets in.
但是每天只发一趟车，所以还得看你的飞机几点到达。
Oh,of course.
当然。
Hang on, we're due to get there at 11:30 am.
等等，我们是在上午11:30抵达。
Hmmm, too bad, the bus leaves at 3:45, so you would have quite a wait - more than 4 hours.
太糟了。汽车在3:45出发，所以你得等好久，超过4小时。
Oh, I see.
我明白了。
Well, what about the Shuttle you mentioned?
那么你说的专车呢？
OK. That's the Airport Shuttle that will take you from the airport right to your hotel or private address.
好的，机场专车将把你从机场带到你的酒店或者私人地址。
It's a door-to-door service and it would suit you much better, because there's one every two hours.
提供服务上门，所以这个更适合你，因为每两小时就有一辆。
So how much does that cost?
那么这个多少钱？
Let's see.
让我看看。
Yeah, that's $35 one way, $65 return, so I guess it's a bit more expensive than the Greyhound.
让我看看。单程是35美元，往返是65美元。所以我想它要比长途汽车贵一点儿。
Oh, that doesn't sound too bad, especially if it'll take me straight to the hotel.
听起来不错，特别是能把我直接送到酒店。
But you do need to reserve a seat.
但是你得预订座位。
OK. Is it possible to make a booking right now,through you?
好的，现在能通过你预约吗？
Sure.
当然。
OK. I just have to fill this form out for you.
好的，我为你填一张表。
So what date do you want to book this for?
你想什么时候预定？
The 16th of October - oh, no, sorry, that's my departure date.
10月16日，不，对不起，那是我的启程日期。
I arrive on the 17th, so book it for then, please.
我17日到，所以请定在那一天。
So, that's the Toronto Airport Shuttle to Milton.
多伦多机场专车到米尔顿。
And this is for just one person or...?
是一个人还是……
Yes, just me, please.
是的，就我一个人。
Right. And you said your expected time of arrival was 11:30?
好的，你说你预计的抵达时间是11:30？
So if I book your Shuttle for after 12:00 - let's say, 12:30 that should give you plenty of time to, you know, collect your baggage maybe grab a coffee?
所以如果我给你预约上12点的专车，比如12:30，你就有很多时间收拾行李或者喝杯咖啡。
Yeah, that sounds fine, as long as we land on time!
是的，听起来不错，只要我们能准时抵达。
Well, we'll take your flight details so you don't need to worry too much about that.
我们会记下你的飞机详情，所以你不用为此太过担心。
Now, what about the fare? What sort of ticket do you want? One way or?
现在，关于车费呢？你想要哪种票？单程还是……
Yes. That'll be fine, provided I can book the return trip once I'm there.
是的，单程就可以。只要我到那里后还能预约到回程的票。
No problem - just allow a couple of days in advance to make sure you get a seat.
没问题。只要提前几天确保你能订到座位。
And what's your name, please?
请问你叫什么名字？
Janet, Janet Thomson.
珍妮特，珍妮特·汤姆森。
ls that Thompson spelt with a "p"?
拼写上有P的汤姆森吗？
No, it's T-H-O-M-S-O-N.
不，T-H-O-M-S-O-N。
OK, and you'll be coming from the UK?
好的，你从英国来？
What flight will you be travelling on?
乘坐哪个航班？
Oh, it's Air Canada flight number AC936, from London Heathrow.
来自伦敦希思罗机场的加拿大航空公司AC936。
Right.
好的。
Now, do you know where you'll be staying?
你知道你将住在哪吗？
We need to give the driver an address.
我们要把地址给司机。
Yes, it's called the Vacation Motel-and I think it's near the town centre.
是的，假日旅馆，我想就在城镇的中心附近。
Anyway, the address is 24 Kitchener Street- that's k-i-t-c-h-e-n-e-r street.
地址是基奇纳街24号。KITCHENER街。
That's fine. Right.
好的
So that's $35 to pay please. Have you got your credit card number there?
请付35美金。你有信用卡号吗？
Yes, it's a VISA card, and the number is 3303 8450 2045 6837.
OK, well, that seems to be everything.
好的，就是这些。
Have a good trip and we'll see you in Toronto next week.
旅途愉快，下周在多伦多见。
Yes, bye-oh, thanks for your help!
好的，再见，谢谢你的帮助。

110.title: C7-Test 1-Section 2
transcript: Thank you all for coming to my talk this evening.
感谢你们能在今晚参加我的演讲，
It's nice to see so many people in the audience.
很高兴看到这么多人。
For those of you who don't know very much about PS Camping，
对于那些不是很了解PS露营公司的人来说。
let me start by giving you some background information about the company.
让我先向你们介绍一下公司的背景。
The company started twenty-five years ago.
公司成立于25年前，作为零售连锁店出售露营装备，
It actually opened as a retail chain selling camping equipment and then twenty years ago, it bought a small number of campsites in the UK, and began offering camping holidays.
然后在20年前，它在英国买下一小部分露营地，并且开始提供露营度假业务。
The company grew rapidly and has been providing holidays in continental Europe for the last fifteen years.
公司发展的很快，并且在过去的15年里在欧洲大陆提供假日活动。
If you book a camping holiday with us, you'll have a choice of over three hundred sites.
如果你在我们这里预定露营假日，你可以在300多个露营地中选择。
In Italy we now have some 64 sites that we either own, or have exclusive use of.
在意大利，我们现在拥有或单独使用的露营地有64个。
France is where we have the majority of sites, and we currently have a project to expand into Switzerland.
我们在法国有多数的露营地，并且我们目前计划扩展到瑞士去。
We also have a number of sites in Northern Spain, particularly in the mountainous region of Picos de Europa.
我们在西班牙北部也有许多露营地，特别是在欧罗巴山的山区。
We've upgraded all these Spanish sites, and improved them considerably from their original three-star rating.
我们已经升级了所有西班牙景点，使它们从原来的三星得到巨大提升。
We believe our holidays offer superb facilities for the whole family.
我们认为我们的假期会为整个家庭提供一流的设施服务。
Parents who want their children to be fully occupied for all or part of the day can take advantage of our children's activities.
那些想让孩子全天或半天有事可做的家长们可以利用我们的儿童活动项目。
These are organised by our well-qualified and enthusiastic staff.
我们称职而热情的员工将组织这些活动。
Each day kicks off with a sports match, perhaps football, or volleyball, followed by an hour of drama for everyone.
每天都以一个体育比赛开始，可能是足球或者排球，之后会有一个一小时的戏剧表演。
This may include singing or dancing, mime or other activities.
这可能包含唱歌跳舞哑剧或者其他活动。
In the afternoon, there's a different art activity for each day of the week including a poster competition or model making.
一整周，每天下午，会有一个不同的艺术活动，比如海报大赛或者建模型。
What's more, our sites are truly child-friendly, and, with this in mind, we operate a no-noise rule in the evenings.
另外，我们的场地真正为孩子着想。同时，在此基础上，我们晚上实行零噪音管制。
Children's evening activities usually finish at 9:30, or occasionally 10, and from 10:30 holiday-makers are expected to be quiet in the areas where there are tents.
孩子们的夜晚活动通常在9点半结束，偶尔到10点。从10点半起帐篷区才有望保持安静。
We want nothing to go wrong on a PS Camping holiday,
我们不希望PS露营假期出现任何差错，
but if it does, we also want all customers to be insured.
但是如果有问题，我们也希望所有的顾客必须参保。
If you haven't organised an annual insurance policy of your own you’ll need to take out the low-cost cover we offer
如果你没有办理年险，你需要参加我们提供的低价格保险，
and we require that you arrange this when you make your holiday reservation.
当你预订假期时，我们要求你加上这条。
There are many advantages to choosing PS Camping, and to recommending it to others.
选择PS露营并将它推荐给别人有很多优点。
As a regular customer, you'll be kept informed of special offers.
作为常客可以随时了解我们的特价活动信息，
And your friends can benefit from ten per cent off their holiday, or book a luxury tent for the price of a standard one.
你的朋友们在这里预订他们的旅行服务时可以享受9折，或者能以标准帐篷的价格使用高级帐篷。
In return, we'll send you a thank-you present, which you can choose from a list of high-quality items.
作为回报，我们会让你选择一份高质量的小礼物以作酬谢。
When it comes to our tents, these are equipped to the highest standard.
说到我们的帐篷，它们都是高质量的设备。
We really do think of every essential detail, from an oven and cooking rings fuelled by bottled gas, to mirrors in the bedroom areas.
我们考虑到每一个必不可少的细节，从烤箱、瓶装煤气点燃的烹饪灶到卧室的镜子。
If you don't want to cook indoors, you can borrow a barbecue if you ask in advance for one to be made available, and there's even a picnic blanket to sit on outside your tent.
如果你不想在室内就餐，你可以买烧烤架，如果你提前询问是否有可用的。
Inside, a box of games and toys can be found, and children's tents can be hired if required, all tents have a fridge,
里面有一盒游戏和玩具，如果需要的话，还可以租用儿童帐篷。所有的帐篷都有冰箱。
and if you want to spend the day on the beach, for example, ask for a specially designed PS Camping cool box, which will keep your food and drinks chilled.
但如果你想在海滩上消磨一天，可以要一个“PS露营”特别设计的冷藏箱，它能使你的食品和饮料保持冰凉。
There are excellent washing facilities at all our sites, with washing machines and clothes lines in the central areas, along with mops and buckets  in case your tent needs cleaning during your stay.
我们所有的景点都有非常棒的洗衣设备。中心区有洗衣机和晾衣绳，还有墩布和桶以便旅行期间你需要清理自己的帐篷。
All sites have a cafe and/or a shop for those who'd rather 'eat in' than dine at a local restaurant.
所有景点都有咖啡店或商店，为那些在家吃饭或在当地餐馆就餐的人提供服务。

111.title: C7-Test 1-Section 3
transcript: Well, you've both been looking at different styles of managing individuals, in companies and the workplace.
你们都对公司和工作场所不同风格的个人管理进行了调查，
How's the research going, Philip?
飞利浦，你的调查进行的怎么样？
Well, I've been looking at why individualism, I mean individual differences, are such an important area of management studies.
我关注于为何个人主义，也就是个体差异是管理研究的重要部分。
When you think about any organization, be it a family business or a multinational company, they are all fundamentally a group of people working together.
当你想到任何组织，家族企业或者跨国公司，它们都是一群人在一起工作。
But it's what these individuals contribute to their places of work that makes you realize how important they are.
但是这些个人对工作做出的贡献使你认识到他们的重要性。
Of course they bring different ideas,  but it's also their attitudes and their experiences of learning.
他们当然带来了不同的观点，但是还带来了他们的态度和学习经验。
Diversity is important in these areas too.
在这方面，多样化也很重要。
So why do people behave so differently from one another at work?
那么为什么人们在工作中表现的各不一样呢？
There are lots of reasons, but research has shown a lot of it comes down to personality.
有很多原因，但是调查显示多数源于性格。
And the other factor is gender.
另一个原因是性别。
It's a well-known fact that men and women do lots of things in different ways, and the workplace is no different.
众所周知，男人和女人的很多处事方式不同，而在工作场所也一样。
Did you look at the effects of this variation on companies?
你注意到这些变化对公司的影响了吗？
Yes, I did.
是的。
On the positive side, exposure to such diversity helps encourage creativity which is generally an asset to a company.
展现多样性的优点是激发创造性，这对公司来说是一种财产。
But unfortunately individual differences are also the root of conflict between staff and they can lead to difficulties for management, which can sometimes be serious.
但是不幸的是，个体差异也是员工冲突的根源，这会导致管理上的困难，有时会产生严重问题。
Thanks, Philip.
谢谢，飞利浦。
So now I guess the two main things to remember here are to identify individual talent and then to utilize it.
所以我想现在要记住的两件主要事情是辨别个人天赋，然后利用它。
So Janice, you were looking at identifying different talents in workers.
那么珍尼丝，你关注于辨别员工的不同才能，
Do you think this is easy for managers to do?
你觉得这对管理者来说容易吗？
Well, currently teamwork is in fashion in the workplace and in my opinion the importance of the individual is generally neglected.
当前，工作场合流行团队合作，在我来看，个人的重要性普遍被忽视了。
What managers should be targeting is those employees  who can take the lead in a situation and are not afraid to accept the idea of responsibility.
管理者应该重视那些主导形势，敢于担当责任的员工。
That's true.
没错。
Janice but unfortunately many managers think the entire notion of encouraging individuality amongst their staff is far too hard.
珍尼丝，但是不幸的是许多管理者认为在员工中间鼓励个性是很难的。
Yes.
是的。
That may be true but I think one of the most important tasks of managers is to consider the needs of the individual on one hand and group co-operation and conformity on the other.
也许是对的，但是我认为其中对管理者最难的事情是考虑个人需求和小组的合作一致。
It requires creative thinking on the part of management to avoid tension.
这需要创造性思维的管理，以避免冲突。
So Janice, what kind of people do you think companies should be looking for?
那么珍尼丝，你认为公司应该找什么样的人呢？
Well, it has to start from the very beginning when companies are looking for new employees.
这得从最初公司开始找新的雇员开始。
When the personal department is choosing between applicants they need to look for someone who's broken the mould and can think for themselves.
当人事部门选择申请人的时候应该考虑那些可以打破常规，自行思考的人。
Instead, people making these decisions often use a range of psychological tests to see if a person is a problem solver, or will do as they're told.
相反，人们作决定时通常使用一系列的心理测试，测试这个人是善于解决问题的人还是听话的人。
I'm not convinced these qualities are actually the most important.
我不确信这些品质是最重要的。
So do you think being a good team player is overrated?
你认为当一名好的团队合作者被高估了？
No, It's not overrated.
不，没有高估。
You do need to learn the rules and learn them fast.
你需要学习这些规则并且要快速学习。
No individual can get around this if you're working in an organization.
如果你在一个机构里面工作，没有人可以逃避这点。
So how should managers deal with this?
那么管理者如何处理这种情况？
Rewards. When an individual demonstrates, the behaviour the organisation expects, some kind of incentive can be given.
奖励。当个人行为符合组织期望时，应该给予个人某种激励。
What's important here is that this happens right at the beginning, so new recruits learn the rules of the system immediately.
重要的是这种情况刚好发生在最初，因此新招募的人员可以迅速领会系统的规章制度。
Also the incentive should be something the individual actually wants, and this isn't always just money.
同样激励物也应该是个人真正想要的，而不仅仅总是金钱激励。
To come back to you, Philip.
飞利浦，回到你这里。
You were saying that recognition of good performers is essential.
你说辨认出好的执行者很重要。
What else should managers be looking for?
管理者们还应该找寻什么？
Well, managing people means you not only have an understanding of your employees, but you also recognise the culture of the organization.
管理者不仅意味着你要理解你的雇员，你对组织文化也要有认识。
In fact, for some organizations creativity and individuality may be the last thing they want to see during working hours.
实际上，对于某些组织来说，在工作期间最不愿意看到的是创造性和个性！
Very true.
十分正确。
Yes, but managing people isn't as easy as it looks.
是的，管理人不像看起来那么容易。
For example, change in the workplace can be quite tricky, especially if there's a need to increase profit.
比如，在工作场所的改革就十分棘手，特别是在需要提高利润的时候。
And at times like these  managers may have to give priority to profit rather than individual staff needs.
在这种情况下，经理们可能不得不先考虑利润而不会先考虑个别雇员的需要。
Yes, and that creates difficult situations for people.
是的，这会使人们处境困境。
Yes, but what's important is that managers are able to deal with quite high levels of personal stress.
是的，但是重要的是经理们要有能力处理高强度的个人压力。
During times of change they should be thinking not only about the strain on their staff but take time out to think of themselves.
变革期间，他们不仅要考虑雇员的承受压力，也要考虑他们自己承受的压力。
Absolutely. So what are the implications of that for...
没错，那么这么做的影响……

112.title: C7-Test 1-Section 4
transcript: Good afternoon, everyone!
大家下午好！
This is the first seminar in preparation for our archaeological fieldwork in Namibia,
这是纳米比亚野外考古的第一场研讨会。
We are fantastically lucky to have received partial research funding for this trip from our institute, so I shall expect 200% attention and participation from you all.
非常幸运的是，学院为我们这次考察提供了部分研究资金，因此我希望大家能够以200%的热情去关注和参加。

First in this seminar, I'm going to give a brief introduction to contemporary research on rock art, and in the second part I'm going to give you some do's and don'ts for our fieldwork trip in April— so please listen very carefully.
在这次研讨会上，我想先针对当代石刻艺术给大家做一个简单的介绍，然后跟大家说一下关于四月野外考察的注意事情，所以请大家认真听。
I'm first going to focus on the interpretation of rock art in Namibia, we are very fortunate to be going to an area where you can find some of the most important sites in the entire world.
首先我打算重点解释一下纳米比亚的石刻艺术。我们很幸运能够去那里，因为那里有一些世界上最重要的遗址。
And I hope to show you how easy it is for everyone to make mistakes in looking at cultures which are different from our own- the first and most important lesson we have to learn.
我想让你们明白，当面对一个和自己完全不同的文化世界时，犯错是多么的在所难免，这是我们要学的第一课，也是最重要的一课。
In Namibia there are both paintings and engraving-that's where the surface of the rock is cut out.
在纳米比亚，岩石上雕刻出绘画和雕刻品。
Many of the engravings show footprints of animals and most scholars used to think that the purpose of these was simple and obvious.
许多雕刻品上刻有动物的足迹，许多学者认为其目的简单明了。
This rock art was like a school book with picture to teach children about tracks: which tracks belonged to which animal-giraffe, lion and so on.
这些石刻艺术就像带有图片的教科书一样可以教孩子们如何找寻踪迹。包括长颈鹿、狮子等等。
But there were some mysteries.
但是仍有一些难以理解的事情。
First, when you look at a typical Namibian painting or engraving, you see the tracks are repeated, there are dozens of tracks for the same animal.
首先，当你看到一个典型的纳米比亚绘画或雕刻时，你会看到重复的足迹，有许多相同动物的足迹。
You'd expect just one clear illustration if the reason-the aim-was to teach tracking.
如果目的是教追踪的话，你希望用一个清晰的图例。
Now there were two more problems, why are some of the engraving of animals very accurate as you'd expect-all clearly identifiable-and others quite unrealistic.
现在，还有两个问题。为什么有些雕刻的动物如你所想的那样非常精准，容易辨别，而另一种却非常不切实际。
And another mystery-some of these unrealistic animals-that's in the engravings- seem to be half human.
另一个神秘的事是，石刻上的一些动物是虚幻的，看上去半人半兽。
Some, for example, have got human faces.
例如，有些具有人的面孔。
Many researchers now think that these were pictures the wise men engraved of themselves.
许多研究者现在认为这些图片是智者雕刻出的自画像。
They believed they could use magic to control the animals they had drawn, so the hunters could then catch them for food.
他们认为自己可以用魔法控制他们画出的动物，所以狩猎者可以捕食它们。
This shows you some of the dangers of coming from one culture to another, as we'll be doing, without understanding it fully.
这表明在我们没有完全理解之前，从一种文化到另一种文化之间存在着某种危险。

Scholars imagined that children looked at rock art pictures to learn to track-just because they themselves had learnt skills from pictures, many researchers now believe that rock art had a much more complex purpose, and we'll talk more about it next week.
学者们认为孩子通过石刻艺术的图片学习踪迹，正是因为他们自己从图片上学到了技能；许多研究者现在认为石刻艺术有更复杂的用途。下周我们会详细地讲解！
Now before I invite you to join in a discussion in this second part of the seminar,I'd like to make some very important points about our fieldwork-and in fact any field trip to look at rock art.
现在，在我邀请你们进入到讨论会的第二个阶段前，我希望就我们的野外工作提出几点重要的看法，事实上是任何石刻艺术的实地考察。
We're going to a number of sites, and we won't always be together.
我们将参观许多景点，但是我们不总是在一起。
The single largest problem faced by people who manage the sites is-yes, I'm sure you've guessed-damage caused by visitors, even though it's usually unintentional.
我想你们也能猜到，景点管理者面临的最大问题是参观者带来的破坏，即使通常不是故意损坏的。
Whenever, you do go to a site, don't forget you can learn many things from observing at a distance instead of walking all over it.
无论你什么时候到一个景点，别忘了你可以通过在远处观察学到很多事情，而不是走近它。
This can really help to reduce visitor pressure.
这有助于减轻游客的压力。
People often say, "Well, there's only two of us and  just this one time", but maybe thousands of people are saying the same thing.
人们经常说“只有我们两个人，就这一次”。但是也许成千上万的人都会说同样的话。
And then some basic rules to guide you-we'll have our own camp near a village, but remember never to camp on a site if you go on your own.
之后有一些你们要遵守的基本原则，我们将在村庄附近露营，但是如果你一个人去的话，不要在景点露营。
It may be disrespectful to the people of that culture, and certainly don't make fires, however romantic it may seem,
这也许对那里的人很无礼，另外一定不要生火，即使这看起来很浪漫。
It's really dangerous in dry areas, and you can easily burn priceless undiscovered material by doing so.
这在干燥的地区很危险，而且你可能因此轻易烧毁了未发现的无价之宝。
So, how are we going to enjoy the rock art on our field trip?
那么，我们该如何享受这次石刻艺术的野外考察呢？
By looking at it, drawing it and photographing it-NEVER by touching it or even tracing it.
通过观看、绘画和拍照，绝不要碰它，甚至是描摹。
Rock art is fragile and precious.
石刻艺术很脆弱也很珍贵。
Remember that climbing on rocks and in caves can destroy in a moment what has lasted for centuries.
记住，爬到岩石上或到洞穴里可能会在顷刻毁掉长存数世纪的文物。
So no heroics in Namibia, please.
因此在纳米比亚不要逞英雄！
Try to be extra careful and help others to be too.
要格外小心，并且告诫别人也是如此。
And lastly please don't even move rocks or branches to take photographs- you should leave the site intact-I'm sure I can rely on you to do that.
最后请不要为拍照而挪动任何石雕或树干，你们要保持其完好无损。相信大家都能做到。

Well, that's about all I want to say before today's first discussion, but if you have any questions please ask them now-and don't forget you'll find some fascinating information about world-wide sites on the Internet.
这是今天首次讨论之前，我要说的话。但是如果你们有问题，请现在就问。别忘了，你将在网上找到一些关于全球景点的好消息。
Right, first question then?
好的，第一个问题是什么？

117.title: C7-Test 3-Section 1
transcript: Good morning!
早上好。
Oh, good morning.
早上好
Is this...er...room number 26?
这是26号房间吗？
Yes. That's right.
是的，没错。
So is this the Student Job Centre?
这里是学生工作中心吗？
It certainly is.
是的
How can I help you?
需要帮忙吗？
Well, actually I'm looking for a job-a part-time job.
事实上，我想找一份兼职工作。
Do you have anything available at the moment?
你现在有合适的工作吗？
Ah, yes...Are you a registered student?
是的，你是注册的学生吗？
I'm afraid this service is only available to full-time students.
恐怕这项服务只提供给全日制学生。
Yes...I am I'm doing a degree in Business Studies.
是的，我是。我在学商学位。
Here's my student card .
这是我的学生证。
Which year are you in?
你是哪年入学的？
Well...I've been at uni for four years but I'm in the Third Year because I took last year off.
我在这所大学已经有四年了，但是我现在是三年级，因为我去年休学了一年。
Right...well.
好的
Let's just have a look at what positions are available at the moment.
让我看看现在有什么合适的职位。
There's a job working at the reception desk at the Sports Centre for three evenings a week that's Wednesdays, Thursdays and Fridays.
有一个在运动中心做前台的工作，一周工作三个晚上，分别是周三、周四和周五。
That sounds like fun, but unfortunately I have evening lectures-so that's not possible, I'm afraid
听起来很有意思，但是不幸的是我晚上有课，所以我恐怕不能去了。
Is there anything during the day?
有在白天的工作吗？
OK.
好的。
That's no good then.
这个不合适。
Um. What about cleaning ?
清洁工作怎么样？
There's a position for a cleaner at the Child Care Centre.
有一个在幼儿中心的清洁工岗位。
Right.
好的。
But you'd need to be there at 6 am.
但是你得在早上6点前到。
Does that appeal?
这合你的心意吗？
Six o'clock in the morning!
早上6点！
Oh, that's far too early for me, I'm afraid. I'd never make it that early in the morning.
对我来说太早了，我恐怕从来没那么早起过。
Mmm...Well - there was a position going in the Computer Lab for three days a week that might be OK.
在计算机实验室有一个一周工作三天的工作可能合适。
Ah. Here it is!
在这里。
No. It's in the Library, not the Lab.
不，是在图书馆当文书助理，不是在实验室。
Clerical Assistant required-I think it mostly involves putting the books back on the shelves.
我想工作内容多是整理书架。
Oh no-hang on.
不，等等。
It's for Wednesday and Friday evenings again
又是周三、周五的晚上。
No - I can't manage that because of the lectures.
不行，我去不了，因为有课。
OK, I'm getting the idea.
好的，我知道了。
Look. I'll just get a few details from you anyway, and then we can check through the list and see what comes up.
无论如何，我得先记一些你的个人信息，然后我们再查查这张单子，看有什么工作适合你。
We'll fill in the personal details on this application form first,if that's OK?
我们先填一下申请表上的个人信息，好吗？
Yes, that's fine
好的。
Now, what's your name again?
你叫什么名字来的？
Anita Newman - that's N-E-W-M-A-N
安妮塔·纽曼。N-E-W-M-A-N。
And your address, Anita?
你的地址是什么，安妮塔？
I'm in one of the Halls of Residence for post-graduate students you know, International House.
我住在研究生宿舍，就是国际留学生宿舍。
OK - that's easy.
好的，明白。
What's your room number there?
你在那里的房间号是多少？
Room B569 - no Sorry B659.
B569号房间，不，对不起，是B659房。
I always get that wrong, I haven't been living there very long.
我总是搞错，我在那里住的时间不长。
Do you have any other skills?
你有其他技能吗？
Typing, languages, that sort of thing.
比如打字、语言之类的？
Well, I speak some Japanese.
我会说一点日语。
Right I'll make a note of that.
好的，我会记下来。
Now-let's see what else is available.
现在看看有什么合适的。
What do you think of administrative work?
你觉得行政工作怎么样？
There is a position for an Office Assistant at the English Language Centre.
在英语中心有一个办公室助理的岗位。
That sounds interesting.
听起来很有趣。
It's for 3 days a week—Monday, Friday and Saturday mornings, interested?
一周三天，周一、周五和周六的早上，感兴趣吗？
Mmm. I was hoping to have Saturdays free.
我希望周六有空。
But I need the work so...can you tell me what the job involves?
但是我需要工作，所以……你能告诉我工作的内容吗？
Yes, sure.
当然。
It says here that you'll be required to deal with student enquiries and answer the phone.
上面说你要负责处理学生的咨询并接听电话。
Oh I'm sure I can handle all that without a problem.
这些我肯定能做，没有问题。
Great, well, would you like me to arrange an interview for you?
太好了。你想让我帮你安排面试吗？
Say, Friday morning, around ten?
周五早上10点左右怎么样？
Could we make it a bit later?
晚点可以吗？
Unfortunately, I've got something to do at ten.
我在十点恐怕有点事情要做。
Would that be OK?
可以吗？
Not a problem, how about eleven thirty?
没问题。11:30怎么样？
Hope it works out for you Anita.
祝你顺利，安妮塔。
Me too. And thanks for all your help.
我也是。谢谢你的帮助。

118.title: C7-Test 3-Section 2
transcript: Good morning.
早上好
I'm very pleased to have been invited along to your club to talk about our Charity Sponsored Walking Holiday for Education Aid.
我很高兴被邀请到你们的俱乐部介绍我们的慈善徒步旅行为教育援助赞助的活动。
I'll start by giving you a brief overview of what it entails .
我先给大家介绍一下这次活动的内容。
First of all let me explain what we mean by sponsored here.
首先，我先解释一下“赞助”在这里的意思。
This is where people promise to donate money to the charity if you achieve your goal
如果你完成了目标，人们就会捐钱给慈善机构。
In this case to walk a certain number of miles.
步行到一定的公里数，
Basically we are organizing a ten—day holiday, from the sixth to the sixteenth of November.
基本上，我们组织了一个十天的假期，从11月6日至16日
With eight days actual walking trekking in the Semira Mountains.
其中有8天在Semira山脉爬山。
Let's have a look at some of the details.
让我们看看详细的情况。
We require you to raise sponsorship money of at least $3,200.
我们要求你们筹集至少3200美元的资助款
Paying $250 of it up front as a deposit and the rest in stages throughout the year.
其中先预付250美元作为押金，其余在全年分阶段付清。
Out of this about thirty—five per cent will go on your expenses and that leaves sixty-five per cent guaranteed to go to the charity.
其中35％是你们的花费，剩余的65％保证给慈善机构。
Which brings me to the most important part.
下面是最重要的部分。
This trek is being specifically organized to help Education in the Semira region.
组织这次徒步旅行主要是为了帮助Semira地区的教育。
Last year we helped train teachers for the disabled and this year we're focusing on the pupils.
去年，我们为残疾人培训老师，今年我们将重点放在学生身上。
Each of the walkers'sponsorship money will go to help an individual special needs pupil in one of the mountain schools.
每个参与这次步行活动的人获得的资助款都将用于帮助一个来自山区学校有特殊需要的学生。
In the second part of the talk I'll be giving you a lot more details, but back to the basic information.
在第二部分，我会告诉你更多详情，但是回到基础信息上，
Age limits. This is the second time we have run this kind of holiday and um on the first we even had an eighty-year old, but we found it was wise to establish limits this time.
年龄限制。这是我们第二次搞这种假期，第一次我们甚至有80岁高龄的人参加，但是我们发现这次设置限制是明智的。
You have to be at least eighteen and the top limit is now seventy, though you need to obtain a health certificate from your doctor if you are over sixty years old.
你必须是18岁以上至70岁以下。如果你超过60岁，就得提供医生开的健康证明。

Now, the Semira Mountains are among the highest in the world but you mustn't be too daunted we will mainly be trekking in the foothills only, although there will be spectacular views even in the foothills .
Semira山是世界上最高的山脉之一，但是大家不用害怕，我们只是在山脉的丘陵地带徒步旅行，这一地带的景色也很壮观。
However, you will need to be extremely fit if you aren't now and you're interested in coming with us.
然而，如果你现在身体不佳又很想跟我们去的话，你得变得非常健康才行。
You have plenty of time to get into shape.
你们有足够的时间调养身体。
You will be sleeping in tents so you must have quite a bit of equipment with you but you will be helped by local assistants.
你将睡在帐篷里，所以你得带一些装备，但是当地的助理会帮助你。
Your bedding and so forth will be carried by them .
他们会帮你拿着被褥之类的东西。
We ask that you only walk with a small rucksack with needs for the day.
我们要求你们在徒步旅行时只背小型背包。
I don't think I've really said enough about the marvellous area you'll be walking in .
关于途径的景色真是美不胜收，说之不尽。
Let's have a look at some of the sights you'll be seeing.
让我们看看你将看到的一些景色。
Apart from these spectacular snow-covered peaks and valleys, there are marvellous historic villages.
除了壮观的雪山峰顶和山谷，这里还有很多美丽的古老村庄。
The area has been famous for centuries for making beautiful carpets, although recently there has been a trend to move into weaving blankets and wood carving.
这里因制作精美的地毯而闻名几个世纪，尽管最近有转向编织毯和木雕的趋势。
The people are extremely friendly and welcoming .
这里的人非常友好、好客。
We deliberately keep the parties small in size to minimize disruption to people and landscape.
我们特意将活动人数减到最少，减低对这里的居民和风景的破坏。
I hope that there are still some people interested.
我希望还有一些人对此感兴趣。
I will be distributing leaflets at the end where you can find out more information.
我会在最后发宣传单，你们可以看到更多的信息，
but just for the moment I'll outline the itinerary, the main highpoints of the holiday.
但是现在我将概述一下这次假期的主要行程。
Obviously, you'll start by flying out to Kishba, the capital city, on Day One.
显然，第一天你们将飞往首都Kishba。
After a couple of days to acclimatize yourself, you'll start the trek on Day Three walking through the enormous Katiba Forest which will take the whole of the day.
经过几天的水土适应，你们将在第三天开始穿过广阔的Katiba森林，这要花一整天的时间。
Day Four takes us higher up going through the foothills past a number of villages and visiting a school for the disabled in Sohan.
第四天我们将往更高的地方走，经过丘陵地带穿过一些村庄，拜访在索汉的一所残障学校。
Then you have a rest day,that's Day Five,
第五天是休息时间
before going to the spectacular Kumi Temple with twelfth-century carvings ,set in a small forest by a lake and that's Day Six, the highlight for many.
最精彩的是第六天参观壮观的Kumi寺庙和12世纪的雕刻品，寺庙坐落在沿湖的小森林里。
We stay near there for Day Seven because then comes the hardest day, walking through very mountainous country, but culminating in a swim in the Parteh Falls
第七天我们还在附近，因为之后是最艰难的一天，我们将穿过多山的乡村，抵达最后的parte瀑布游泳。
This is the highest waterfall in the region .
它这里最高的瀑布。
Day Nine is much easier, with part of the day spent in a village where they make some of the gorgeous red blankets.
第九天会轻松很多，我们会花部分时间游览制作精美红毯的村庄。
Then back down to Kishba and the journey home.
之后回到Kishba，返回家乡。
So you can see it's a pretty packed timetable…
所以你可以看到时间表塞的满满的

119.title: C7-Test 3-Section 3
transcript: Thanks to all of you for coming along today to hear about how the robotic float project is helping with ocean research .
感谢各位的光临，今天主要讲解自动漂浮项目是如何有助于海洋研究的。
Well first of all we'll look at what a robotic float does and its use.
首先，我们将看看什么是自动漂浮和如何使用。
So let's start with the device itself.
所以，让我们先从设备本身开始。
It looks a bit like a cigar and it's about one and a half metres long.
它看起来有点像雪茄，大约有一米半长。
More importantly it's full of equipment that's designed to collect data.
更重要的是，它装满了用来收集数据的装备。
So it can help us in building up a profile of different factors which work together within the world's oceans.
所以它为了我们研究世界大洋而建立起一个大洋的多面体。
Sounds like a big project-isn't it too big for one country to undertake?
听起来像是一个大工程，由一个国家来完成的话是不是量太大了？
That's quite true but this project is a really good example of international co-operation .
没错，不过这个项目是国际合作的好例子。
Over the last five years scientists from thirteen countries have been taking part in the project and launching floats in their area of ocean control.
在过去的5年里，来自13个国家的科学家参与到这个项目中，并且将漂浮物发射到他们各自海洋掌控的地方。
And next year this number will rise to fourteen when Indonesia joins the project.
明年当印尼加入到项目中时，参与国就是14个了。
That's impressive.
听上去不错。
But let's move onto how floats work .
但是让我们回到漂浮物的工作原理上。
The operational cycle goes like this.
运作周期是这样的。
Each of the floats is dropped in the ocean from a boat at a set point and activated from a satellite.
每一个漂浮物都是在设定点从船上放入海里，并且由卫星激活。
Then the float immediately sinks about 2,000 metres, that's two whole kilometres down in the water.
然后，漂浮物立即下沉到约2000米的位置，那是水下两公里。
It stays at this depth for about 10 days and is carried around by the currents which operate in the ocean at this level .
它位于这个深度停留十天左右，随波逐流，在海洋的这个水位上开展工作。
During this time it's possible for it to cover quite large distances but the average is fifty kilometres.
它在这段时期的覆盖距离可能非常大，但是平均是50公里。
So what is it actually recording?
那么它到底在记录什么？
Well at this stage nothing, but as it rises to the surface it collects all sorts of data most importantly variations in salinity,
在这个阶段什么也不记录。但是当它升到水面时就会记录各种数据。最重要的是盐度变化、
that's salt levels and the changes In temperature a bit like underwater weather balloons.
含盐量和温度变化，有点像水下的气象气球。
Then when it gets back to the surface all the data it's collected is beamed up to the satellite .
之后，当它到达水面时，所有收集的数据都会发向卫星。
After about five hours on the surface the float automatically sinks, beginning the whole process again.
漂浮物在水面停留大约5小时后会自动下沉，整个过程再次开始。
What happens to the data?
怎么处理那些数据？
Well the information is transferred direct to onshore meteorological stations like our one in Hobart
信息直接传送到海岸气象站，就像我们在霍巴特的气象站。
and within four hours the findings can be on computers and they can be mapped and analysed.
4小时内，结论就能出现在电脑上，并进行定位和分析。
You say you're building models of the world's ocean systems but how're they going to be used and more importantly, when?
你说过你们在建立世界海洋系统的模型，但是它们是如何使用的，更重要的是在什么时候用？
Some of the data has already helped in completing projects.
一些数据已经用于帮助完成项目。
For example our understanding of the underlying causes of El Niňo events is being confirmed by float data.
比如，我们对厄尔尼诺现象内在原因的理解可以由漂浮物搜集到的信息来确认。
Another way we're using float data is to help us to understand the mechanics of climate change like global warming and ozone depletion.
另外，我们利用漂浮体数据帮助我们了解气候变化的运行方式，比如全球变暖和臭氧层空洞。
That's part of an ongoing variability study but the results are still a long way off.
这只是持续存在的可变性研究的一部分，但是离结果还很远。
However, this is not the case with our ocean weather forecasting.
但是，这与我们的海洋天气预报的情况不同。
Because we know from the floats what the prevailing weather conditions will be in certain parts of the ocean we can advise the navy on search and rescue missions.
因为我们从漂浮物可以得知海洋某一地区的主要天气条件，我们可以在搜查、营救任务中向海军提出建议。
That's happening right now and many yachtsmen owe their lives to the success of this project.
现在就是这样做的，而且许多游艇驾驶者因为这个项目的成功而获救。
In addition the float data can help us to look at the biological implications of ocean processes.
另外，漂浮体数据可以帮我们了解海洋的生物学意义。
Would that help with preserving fish stocks?
它能利于保护鱼类吗？
Yes, and advising governments on fisheries legislation.
是的，并且建议政府对渔业立法。
We're well on the way to completing a project on this.
我们正在完成这个项目。
We hope it will help to bring about more sustainable fishing practices.
我们希望这能有助于产生更多适当的捕鱼行为。
We'll be seeing the results of that quite soon.
我们很快会看到结果。
It sounds like the data from floats has lots of applications.
听起来漂浮体数据的用途很广。
Yes it does. It's also a powerful agricultural tool.
是的，它还是强大的农业工具。
If we were aware of what the weather would be like say, next year,
假设我们能提前知道明年的天气情况，
we could make sure that the farmers planted appropriate grain varieties to produce the best yield from the available rainfall.
我们就指导农民种植一些合适的农作物产品，以确保在可用的雨水下获得最好的产量。
That sounds a bit like science fiction, especially when now we can't even tell them when a drought will break.
这听起来有点像科幻小说，特别是目前我们还无法告诉他们干旱何时会停止。
I agree that this concept is still a long way in the future but it will come eventually and the float data will have made a contribution.
我同意这个概念在未来仍需很长一段时间去研究，但是它终究会实现，而且漂浮体数据也会为此出一份力。

120.title: C7-Test 3-Section 4
transcript: Good morning everyone.
大家早上好。
Today's lecture forms part of the Hospitality and Tourism module.
今天的讲座属于接待观光课程的一部分。
Last week I looked at the economy end of the hotel business.
上周我们讨论了酒店行业节约的方面，
This week I'm going to discuss the luxury end of the market.
这周我们讨论下该市场奢侈的方面。
Let's consider the following scenario.
让我们想想下面的情景。
You wake up in the middle of the night in a strange hotel miles away from home,disoriented most probably from jet lag, when even the most expensive surroundings can seem empty and dispiriting.
你在一个离家千里之外的午夜从一个陌生的旅店突然醒来，伴随着时差造成的眩晕感，即使最奢华的环境也让你感到空荡和失落。

You have paid a great deal of money to stay in this first-class hotel with its contemporary technology but according to recent research carried out by an international travel and public relations company, all is not well.
你付了一大笔钱住在一流的酒店享受现代化的科技，但是根据一个国际旅游与公共关系公司的调查发现事实并不理想。

The research suggests that even the most opulent luxurious hotels seem to have underestimated the most basic needs of their customers-be they travelling for work or pleasure the need to feel at home in surroundings which are both familiar and inviting.
这项调查显示最奢华的酒店也低估了顾客最基本的需求。无论他们是出差还是旅行都应让他们感到周围是熟悉、亲切的家的感觉。
Do these findings, however apply only to hotels situated in particular areas?
这项发现只适用于特定区域的酒店吗？
Is it possible that the external environment can affect a guest's well-being?
外部环境可能会影响客人的幸福感吗？
The company's research covered a whole range of different hotel types, both independent hotels and those which are part of large chains.
这家公司的调查覆盖各种各样的酒店类型，无论是私人酒店还是大型连锁酒店。
They investigated chic so-called boutique hotels in the heart of downtown business districts stately mansions located in the depths of beautiful countryside,
他们调查了位于商业中心所谓时髦的精品酒店，宏伟的大厦坐落在美丽乡村的深处，
And plush hotels built at the edge of tropical beaches surrounded by palm trees and idyllic blue ocean
豪华的酒店矗立在热带海滩的边上，周围围绕着棕榈树和恬静的大海。
And the research concluded that what was outside the hotel building simply didn't matter.
研究的结论是酒店的外部环境并不重要。
This is a fascinating revelation and those of you hoping to move into careers in the travel and leisure industry would be well advised to look at the findings in more detail.
这个发现很有意思，非常建议有志从事旅游业和休闲业的人仔仔细细地研究一下这份报告。
But back to the main point of this lecture the need to feel at home.
但是回到谈论的重点，客人需要有家的感觉。
What can the hotel industry do about it?
酒店业能做些什么来解决这个问题？
And is the very idea so subjective that it's impossible to do anything about it on a global basis?
同时这是一个极为主观的想法，是否在全球层面上都无法有所作为呢？
However, nothing stands still in this world.
然而，在这个世界上没有什么是止步不前的。
One company has come up with the slogan"Take Your Home With You", and aims to provide clients with luxury serviced apartments .
一个公司给出的口号是“有家相伴”，目的是为顾客提供奢华的酒店式公寓。
Those in the business travel industry maintain that these serviced apartments dispense with all the unwanted and expensive hotel services that business travelers don't want,
一些商业旅行业认为这些酒店式公寓去掉了所有商务旅客不想要的昂贵的服务，
while maximizing the facilities they do want .
最大化了他们需要的设施。
For example, not only sleeping and living accommodation but also a sleek modern kitchen that allows guests to cook and entertain if they wish at no additional cost.
例如，不仅是睡觉和生活的居住空间，还有整洁的现代化厨房，客人不用额外花费就能做饭和娱乐。

The attractions of such facilities are obvious and it'll be interesting to see whether the company manages to establish a trend all over the world and make a lasting impact on the luxury accommodation market.
这种设施的吸引力显而易见，看到这种公司是否会建立起一种遍及全球的趋势，对奢华住宿市场造成长久的影响是很有趣的。
Now, finally I want to consider the psychology underpinning the traditional holiday hotel industry.
最后，我想讨论一下支撑传统假日酒店的心理学问题。
As a hotelier, how do you go about attracting people to give up the security of their own home and entrust themselves to staying in a completely strange place and sleeping in an unfamiliar bed?
作为一名酒店老板，如何才能吸引客人，让他们撇开自己家中的安全舒适，放心地住在一个完全陌生的地方，睡在一张陌生的床上？
Firstly, hotels exploit people's need to escape the predictability of their everyday lives.
首先，酒店要利用人们渴望逃避日常生活预见性的需求。
For a few days, people can pretend they are free of responsibilities and can indulge themselves.
这几天，人们可以假装没有任何责任的束缚、尽情玩乐。
Secondly, there is something very powerful in our need to be pampered and looked after.
第二，我们有一种强烈的被宠爱、照顾的需求。
It's almost as if we return to being a baby,when everything was done for us and we felt safe and secure.
仿佛我们回到了婴儿时代，每件事都为我们做好了，我们感到很安全。
And not far removed from this is the pleasure in being spoilt and given little treats like the miniscule bottles of shampoo and tiny bars of soap,
这就像被宠爱、被给予的愉悦一样。比如会有小瓶洗发露、小块香皂，
the chocolate on your pillow at night—and we actually forget that we are paying for it all.
夜晚会有枕边巧克力，我们甚至忘了这些都是自己付钱的！
Next week I'm going to look at eco-hotels a fairly new phenomenon but increasingly popular...
下周，我们将探讨生态酒店，一种日益受欢迎的新现象……

120211012091954.title: C15-Test 3-Section 1
transcript: Good morning. Thanks for coming in to see us here at the agency, Joe. I'm one of the agency representatives, and my name's Sally Baker.
早上好。乔,十分感谢你来公司见我。我是机构的代表之一,我叫萨利·贝克。
Hi Sally. I think we spoke on the phone, didn't we?
你好,萨利。我想我们之前通过电话,对吗?
That's right, we did. So thank you for sending in your CV. We've had quite a careful look at it and l think we have two jobs that might be suitable for you.
没错。感谢你发来你的简历。我们已经认真地看过了。我觉得目前可能有两份适合你的工作。
OK.
好的。
The first one is in a company based in North London. They're looking for an administrative assistant.
第一份来自伦敦北部的一家公司。招聘的是行政助理。
OK. What sort of company is it?
好的。经营什么的公司?
They're called Home Solutions and they design and make furniture.
是一家设计制作家具的公司,叫做家装解决者。
Oh, I don't know much about that, but it sounds interesting.
是吗?我对它并不了解,但听起来不错。

Yes, well as I said, they want someone in their office, and looking at your past experience it does look as if you fit quite a few of the requirements. So on your CV it appears you've done some data entry?
是的,正如我刚才说到的,他们想要招聘一些办公室员工。你之前的经验看起来确实挺符合他们的要求的。简历上看,你之前做过数据录入工作,是吗?
Yes.
是的。
So that's one skill they want. Then they expect the person they appoint to attend meetings and take notes there ...
这也正是他们希望员工掌握的技巧。另外,他们希望他们招聘的员工能够岀席会议并做好会议笔记。
OK. I've done that before, yes.
好的。我之前也做过这些。
And you'd need to be able to cope with general admin.
而且你要能适应总务部的工作。
Filing, and keeping records and so on? That should be OK. And in my last job I also had to manage the diary.
归档,然后做记录这些?应该没问题。上一份工作还需要我整理工作日志。
Excellent. That's something they want here too. I'd suggest you add it to your CV - I don't think you mentioned that, did you?
太好了。这也是他们要求的工作技能。我建议你把这些经验加到你的简历中,我记得你之前好像没提到,是吗?
No.
没有。
So as far as the requirements go, they want good computer skills, of course, and they particularly mention spreadsheets.
说到要求,熟练的电脑技能当然也是必须的。他们还特别提到了电子制表软件。
That should be fine.
这些应该也没问题。
And interpersonal skills - which would be something they'd check with your references.
还要有人际交往能力,他们可能会在你的推荐信中确认这个。
I think that should be OK, yes .
我觉得没问题。
Then they mention that they want someone who is careful and takes care with details - just looking at your CV, I'd say you're probably alright there.
还有他们提到了希望应聘者细心认真,注意细节。看你的简历,我觉得你可能也很符合。
I think so, yes. Do they want any special experience?
我觉得也是。需要特别的工作经验吗?
I think they wanted some experience of teleconferencing.
我觉得他们希望应聘者有过电话会议工作的经验。
I've got three years' experience of that.
我之前有过三年的经验。
Let's see, yes, good. In fact they're only asking for at least one year, so that's great. So is that something that might interest you?
我看看,哦,是的。事实上,他们要求的是至少有一年的工作经验,太棒了。所以这份工作你感兴趣吗?
It is, yes. The only thing is, you said they were in North London so it would be quite a long commute for me.
是的。唯一的就是你刚才提到他们是在伦敦北部,所以对我来说通勤时间会很长。
OK.
理解了。
So the second position might suit you better as far as the location goes; that's for a warehouse assistant and that's in South London.
要是说地点的话,第二份工作也许更适合你。伦敦南部的一家公司在招聘仓库助理。
Yes, that would be a lot closer.
这样距离就近多了。
And you've worked in a warehouse before, haven't you?
你之前在仓库工作过,对吗?
Yes.
是的。
So as far as the responsibilities for this position go, they want someone who can manage the stock, obviously , and also deliveries.
说到这份工作的职责,很明显,他们希望应聘者能够管理库存和交货。
That should be OK. You've got to keep track of stuff, but I've always been quite good with numbers.
这应该没问题。这份工作需要员工记录数字,而我对数字一向很在行。
Good. that's their first requirement. And they want someone who's computer literate, which we know you are.
太好了。这是第一个要求。他们还希望应聘者精通电脑,我知道这对你来说也没有问题。
Sure.
确实。
Then they mention organisational skills. They want someone who's well organised.
然后他们还说到了组织技能。他们希望应聘者做事情要有条理。
Yes, I think I am.
好的,我觉得我还行。
And tidy?
还要利落。
Yes, they go together really, don't they?
是的,这两者是一致的,对不对?
Sure. Then the usual stuff; they want someone who can communicate well both orally and in writing.
确实。剩下的就是平常的要求了。他们希望应聘者有良好的口头和书面表达能力。
OK. And for the last warehouse job I had, one of the things I enjoyed most was being part of a team. I found that was really essential for the job.
知道了。我之前在仓库工作的时候,最享受的一件事儿就是成为团队的一员。我发现这对做好工作是十分重要的。
Excellent. Yes, they do mention that they want someone who's used to that, yes. Now when you were working in a warehouse last time, what sorts of items were you dealing with?
你说得太对了。是的,他们确实提到了希望应聘者能够习惯团队合作。好了。你上次在仓库工作是什么时候?你主要处理什么物品?
It was mostly bathroom and kitchen equipment, sinks and stoves and fridges.
主要是浴室和厨房设备,水槽、炉灶和冰箱。
So you're OK moving heavy things?
你能够搬重物吗?
Sure. I'm quite strong, and I've had the training.
当然。我身体很强壮,我之前接受过培训。
Good. Now as far as experience goes, they mention they want someone with a licence, and that you have experience of driving in London - so you can cope with the traffic and so on.
好的。说到经验,招聘单位希望应聘者有驾照,还要有在伦敦驾驶的经验。这样你可以应付交通情况等等。
Yes, no problem.
好的,没问题。
And you've got experience of warehouse work .. . and the final thing they mention is customer service. I think looking at your CV you're OK there .
你有过在仓库工作的经验.最后一项就是客户服务。我记得从你的简历上看你这方面还不错,
Right. So what about pay? Can you tell me a bit more about that, please ...
是的。薪酬怎么样呢?麻烦你告诉我一些关于报酬的信息...

120211012094607.title: C15-Test 3-Section 2
transcript: My guest on the show today is Alice Riches who started the Street Play Scheme where she lives in Beechwood Road. For those of you that don't already know -Street Play involves local residents closing off their street for a few hours so that children have a chance to play in the street safely.
今天我邀请来的嘉宾是爱丽丝·里凯斯。她住在比奇伍德路,在这里她发起了街头玩耍计划。为还不了解的观众解释一下,街头玩耍计划是指为了让孩子们有机会安全地在街上玩耍,当地居民封闭街道数小时。
She started it in her own street, Beechwood Road, and the idea caught on, and there are now Street Play Schemes all over the city. So when did you actually start the scheme, Alice?
她在自己居住的街道——比奇伍德路首先发起倡议。这种做法变得流行,现在全市都有街头玩耍项目了。那么爱丽丝,你实际上是从什么时候开始了这个项目的呢?

Well, I first had the idea when my oldest child was still a toddler, so that's about six years ago now - but it took at least two years of campaigning before we were actually able to make it happen . So the scheme's been up and running for three years now.
在我的长子还在学走路的时候,大约是6年前,我第一次有了这个念头。但是在我们实际能够实现计划之前我花了至少两年的时间进行宣传活动。所以到现在这个计划已经实行了三年了。
We'd love to be able to close our road for longer - for the whole weekend, from Saturday morning until Sunday evening, for example. At the moment it's just once a week.
我们希望能够将道路封闭更长时间——整个周末。比如从周六早上封闭到周日晚上。现在只是一周封闭一次。
But when we started it was only once a month. But we're working on it.
但是在项目一开始的时候一个月才封闭一次。我们还会不断努力。
So what actually happens when Beechwood Road is closed?
所以当比奇伍德路封闭的时候会发生什么?

We have volunteer wardens, mostly parents but some elderly residents too, who block off our road at either end. The council have provided special signs but there's always a volunteer there to explain what's happening to any motorists.
我们有志愿看守人,大部分都是孩子父母,但是也有年长的居民。他们在每个路口封锁车辆。议会提供了特别的指示标志,但是一直有志愿者向驾驶人解释发生了什么。
Generally, they're fine about it - we've only had to get the police involved once or twice.Now I should explain that the road isn't completely closed to cars.
司机们通常都没有问题。只有一两次需要找警察帮忙。在我应该解释一下道路并不是完全封闭不能通车。
But only residents' cars are allowed. If people really need to get in or out of Beechwood Road, it's not a problem - as long as they drive at under 20 kilometres per hour.
只有当地居民的车可以通过。如果人们真的需要在比奇伍德路进出的不是问题。只要司机的车速在每小时20公里以下即可。
But most people just decide not to use their cars during this time, or they park in another street. The wardens are only there to stop through traffic.
但是大部分人决定这段时间不用车或者他们会把车停在另外一条街。看守人只是在那里阻止车辆通行。
So can anyone apply to get involved in Street Play?
那么任何人都能申请参加街道玩耍项目吗?
Absolutely - we want to include all kids in the city - especially those who live on busy roads. It's here that demand is greatest.
当然了。我们希望全市的孩子都参与进来,尤其是那些住在繁忙街道的孩子。他们出来玩儿的渴望是最强烈的。

Obviously, there isn't such demand in wealthier areas where the children have access to parks or large gardens - or in the suburbs where there are usually more places for children to play outside. I'd recommend that anyone listening who likes the idea should just give it a go.
很明显,在相对富裕的地方,孩子们可以去公园或者大花园玩儿—或者在郊区,有很多可供孩子在户外玩耍的地方,所以这些区域想参与的人不多。我建议听众们如果喜欢这个主意的话都可以试一试。
We've been surprised by the positive reaction of residents all over the city. And that's not just parents.
全城范围内居民的积极反应特别让人惊喜。而且,反馈不光来自父母。是会有一些抱怨的人,但是他们只是少数。

There are always a few who complain but they're a tiny minority. On the whole everyone is very supportive and say they're very happy to see children out on the street - even if it does get quite noisy.
整体来说,虽然有的时候会很吵,但所有人都很支持,他们很高兴看到孩子能在街道上玩儿。

There have been so many benefits of Street Play for the kids. Parents really like the fact that the kids are getting fresh air instead of sitting staring at a computer screen, even if they're not doing anything particularly energetic.
街道玩耍项目对孩子很有好处。虽然孩子们没有做什么特别消耗体力的事儿,父母们还是很喜欢孩子们出去呼吸新鲜的空气而不是坐在电脑屏幕前。

And of course it's great that kids can play with their friends outside without being supervised by their parents -but for me the biggest advantage is that kids develop confidence in themselves to be outside without their parents. The other really fantastic thing is that children get to know the adults in the street - it's like having a big extended family.
当然了,孩子们在不被家长看管的情况下和伙伴们一起玩儿实在太好了。但是在我看来,最大的好处是孩子们能够培养自信,敢于在没有父母陪伴的时候到户外去。另一个美妙之处在于孩子们可以熟悉在这条街道生活的大人们,就像是有了更大的家庭一样。
It certainly does have a lot of benefits. I want to move on now and ask you about a related project in King Street.
这个项目确实有很多好处。我想要再问你一下国王街的相关项目。

Right. Well this was an experiment I was involved in where local residents decided to try and reduce the traffic along King Street, which is the busiest main road in our area, by persuading people not to use their cars for one day.
好的。这是我参与的一个实验。国王街是我们区最繁忙的一条主街。当地居民决定劝说人们,选择一天不开车来减少街上的车流。

We thought about making people pay more for parking - but we decided that would be really unpopular - so instead we just stopped people from parking on King Street but left the other car parks open. It was surprising how much of a difference all this made.
我们想过增加停车费,但是我们最终确定这种方式不会有人支持。所以,取而代之,我们只是不许司机在国王街停车,但是保留了其他的停车场。
As we'd predicted, air quality was significantly better but what I hadn't expected was how much quieter it would be - even with the buses still running.
正如我们所料,空气质量明显提升。但是我们没有预料到的是尽管公交车仍然在运行,街上变得特别安静。
Of course everyone said they felt safer but we were actually amazed that sales in the shops went up considerably that day - we thought there'd be fewer people out shopping - not more.
当然了,人们都说自己感觉更安全了。实际上,我们惊讶地发现当天街上商店的营业额大幅提升。我们本以为购物的人数会只减不增.
That's really interesting so the fact that ...
那确实很有趣，所以...

120211012100208.title: C15-Test 3-Section 3
transcript: Tom, could I ask you for some advice, please?
汤姆,请问我能向你征求一些意见吗?
Yes of course, if you think I can help. What's it about?
当然可以了,只要你觉得我能帮得上忙。关于什么的?
It's my first media studies assignment, and I'm not sure how to go about it. You must have done it last year.
是我的第一份关于媒体研究的作业,我不确定要怎么做。你去年一定做过这个。
Is that the one comparing the coverage of a particular story in a range of newspapers?
是比较不同报刊中的对特定故事报道的那个吗?
That's right.
就是那个。
Oh yes, I really enjoyed writing it.
对,我写过。我当时还写得很开心。
So what sort of things do I need to compare?
所以我需要比较哪些事情呢?
Well, there are several things. For example, there's the question of which page of the newspaper the item appears on.
有很多可比的。比如,关于报道出现在报纸的哪一版面。
You mean, because there's a big difference between having it on the front page and the bottom of page ten, for instance?
你的意思是,比方说,把它放在报纸头版和在第十页的底部是有很大差别的?
Exactly. And that shows how important the editor thinks the story is. Then there's the size - how many column inches the story is given, how many columns it spreads over.
你说对了。这还显示出编辑对报视程度。然后还可以讨论篇幅。报道的列长是多少,占了几列。
And I suppose that includes the headline.
我觉得还应该包括标题。
It certainly does. It's all part of attracting the reader's attention.
当然了。就是这部分吸引读者的注意力。
What about graphics - whether there's anything visual in addition to the text?
配图呢?文本旁边有没有加上任何可视化信息?

Yes, you need to consider those, too, because they can have a big effect on the reader's understanding of the story - sometimes a bigger effect than the text itself. Then you'll need to look at how the item is put together: what structure is it given? Bear in mind that not many people read beyond the first paragraph, so what has the journalist put at the beginning? And if, say, there are conflicting opinions about something, does one appear near the end, where people probably won't read it?
可以。你也需要考虑那些,因为它们会很大程度上影响读者对报道的理解,有的时候比文章本身的影响还要大。接着,你需要看看整个故事是怎么编排的:报道的结构是什么?你要记住很多人都只读第一段,所以记者在文章开头写了什么?还有,比如,假设有冲突的观点,如果它出现在报道结尾的话,大家是不是可能不会读到?
And newspapers sometimes give wrong or misleading information, don't they? Either deliberately or by accident. Should I be looking at that , too?
另外,新闻有时候会呈现错误或者有误导性的消息,对吗?不管是故意的还是不小心的。我要不要也说一下这个?

Yes, if you can. Compare what's in different versions, and as far as possible, try and work out what's true and what isn't. And that relates to a very important point: what's the writer's purpose, or at least the most important one, if they have several. It may seem to be to inform the public, but often it's that they want to create fear, or controversy, or to make somebody look ridiculous.
如果你能完成,可以。比较不同的版本,尽可能地区分开真实的和错误的信息。那么这就牵扯出一个非常重要的问题:作者的写作目的是什么。如果有好几个,至少找到最重要的那一个。看起来可能是为了向公众传递信息,但是他们经常想要制造恐慌或争议,或是想要让某些人出丑。
Gosh, I see what you mean. And I suppose the writer may make assumptions about the reader.
天哪,我懂你的意思。我认为作者可能做出关于读者的假设。

That's right - about their knowledge of the subject, their attitudes , and their level of education, which means writing so that the readers understand without feeling patronised. All of that will make a difference to how the story is presented.
是的,关于他们对这一题材的了解、态度和教育水平。这就意味着作者需要写出让读者理解的文章,而不是居高临下。所有这些都会影响报道的呈现。
Does it matter what type of story I write about?
我写什么类型的故事重要吗?

No - national or international politics, the arts ... Anything, as long as it's covered in two or three newspapers. Though of course it'll be easier and more fun if it's something you're interested in and know something about.
不重要,国內国际政治,艺术...什么都行,只要在两三份报纸上报道过就行。
And on that basis a national news item would be worth analysing - I'm quite keen on politics, so I'll try and find a suitable topic. What did you choose for your analysis, Tom?
这么说的话,一个国内的新闻报道应该值得分析。我很关心政治,所以我会尝试找到合适的话题。汤姆,你是怎么选择你的分析内容的?

I was interested in how newspapers express their opinions explicitly, so I wanted to compare editorials in different papers. but when I started looking. I couldn't find two on the same topic that I felt like analysing.
我对报纸如何明确表达观点很感兴趣,所以当时我想比较不同报纸的社论。但是,在我开始研究的时候,我找不到两篇我想分析的、讲述相同话题的报道。
In that case, I won't even bother to look.
那种情况下,我就不会去看了。

So in the end I chose a human interest story - a terribly emotional story about a young girl who was very ill, and lots of other people - mostly strangers - raised money so she could go abroad for treatment. Actually, I was surprised - some papers just wrote about how wonderful everyone was, but others considered the broader picture, like why treatment wasn't available here.
所以最后我选择了人们都感兴趣的话题,是一个特别引发人们情感的故事:一个小女孩生病了,很多人,大多数是陌生人给她捐款筹钱,所以她能够去国外接受治疗。实际上,我特别吃惊。因为一些报纸只说了大家是有多善良,但是其它的报纸从更高的角度考虑,比如讨论为什么在我们国家无法治疗。

Hmm, I usually find stories like that raise quite strong feelings in me! I'll avoid that. Perhaps I'll choose an arts topic, like different reviews of a film, or something about funding for the arts - I'll think about that.
嗯,我经常发现这样的故事会让我很激动。我会避开这一类。也许我会选择艺术,比如一部电影的不同评论。或者关于艺术基金的。我会好好考虑。
Yes, that might be interesting.
好的,应该会很有趣的。
OK, well thanks a lot for your help, Tom. It's been really useful.
好的,谢谢你的帮助,汤姆。你的建议真的很有用。
You're welcome . Good luck with the assignment, Hazel.
客气啦。希望你的作业完成得顺兹尔。

120211012101622.title: C15-Test 3-Section 4
transcript: Nowadays, we use different products for personal cleanliness, laundry, dishwashing and household cleaning, but this is very much a 20th-century development.
现如今,个人清洁,洗衣洗碗,房屋清洁的产品层出不穷。但是这些很大程度上都是20世纪的成果了。

The origins of cleanliness date back to prehistoric times. Since water is essential for life, the earliest people lived near water and knew something about its cleansing properties - at least that it rinsed mud off their hands.
清洁的起源可以追溯到史前时期。因为水是生命的根本,最早的人类临水而居,并且了解了水的清洁属性——至少能够将手上的泥巴洗掉。

During the excavation of ancient Babylon, evidence was found that soapmaking was known as early as 2800 BC. Archaeologists discovered cylinders made of clay, with inscriptions on them saying that fats were boiled with ashes.
在古巴比伦的挖掘过程中,考古学家发现证据证明早在公元前2800年人们就已经制作香皂。他们发现用陶土制作的圆柱上铭刻着,将灰烬和油脂放在一起煮。
This is a method of making soap, though there's no reference to the purpose of this material.
这是制作肥皂的一种方法,但是没有提到这种材料的用途。

The early Greeks bathed for aesthetic reasons and apparently didn't use soap. Instead, they cleaned their bodies with blocks of sand, pumice and ashes , then anointed themselves with oil, and scraped off the oil and dirt with a metal instrument known as a strigil.
早期的希腊人为了追求美而洗澡。很明显他们没有使用肥皂。但是,他们用成块的沙子、浮石和灰烬来清洁身体。他们以油擦身,然后用一种名为刮身板的金属器物把油和身上的污垢一起刮掉。
They also used oil mixed with ashes. Clothes were washed without soap in streams.
他们还把油和灰烬混合在一起。人们用溪水洗衣服,不用肥皂。
The ancient Germans and Gauls are also credited with discovering how to make a substance called 'soap', made of melted animal fat and ashes. They used this mixture to tint their hair red.
古时的德国人和高卢人被认为发现了如何用融化的动物脂肪和灰烬来制作“皂”。他们用这种混合物将头发染成红色。

Soap got its name, according to an ancient Roman legend , from Mount Sapo, where animals were sacrificed, leaving deposits of animal fat. Rain washed these deposits, along with wood ashes , down into the clay soil along the River Tiber. Women found that this mixture greatly reduced the effort required to wash their clothes.
根据古罗马的传奇故事,皂的名字来自于肥皂山。在那里动物作为祭品被杀掉,地面上形成了脂肪沉积。经雨水冲刷后的沉积物和木灰混合到了台伯河边的粘性土壤之中。妇女们发现这种混合物大大减少了洗衣服的工作量。
As Roman civilisation advanced , so did bathing. The first of the famous Roman baths, supplied with water from their aqueducts, was built around 312 BC.
随着罗马文明的进步,洗澡也在进步。第一个著名的罗马浴池是在公元前312年建造的,由渡槽供水。
The baths were luxurious, and bathing became very popular. And by the second century AD, the Greek physician Galen recommended soap for both medicinal and cleansing purposes.
浴室很豪华,洗澡也变得流行起来。到公元二世纪,希腊医生盖伦推荐将肥皂用于医药和清洁用途。

After the fall of Rome in 467 AD and the resulting decline in bathing habits, much of Europe felt the impact of filth on public health. This lack of personal cleanliness and related unsanitary living conditions were major factors in the outbreaks of disease in the Middle Ages, and especially the Black Death of the 14th century .
由于公元467年罗马沦陷和人们洗浴习惯的缺乏,欧洲大部分地区都感受到了污秽对公众健康的影响。个人清洁缺乏和相关的不洁的生活条件是中世纪疾病爆发的主要因素,特别是14世纪的黑死病。

Nevertheless, soapmaking became an established craft in Europe, and associations of soapmakers guarded their trade secrets closely. Vegetable and animal oils were used with ashes of plants, along with perfume, apparently for the first time.
尽管如此,肥皂剧制作在欧洲已成为一门老牌手艺肥皂制作者协会严密保守着他们的商业秘密。很显然,这是第一次将植物油、动物油、植物灰和香料一起用来制作。
Gradually more varieties of soap became available for shaving and shampoing, as well as bathing and laundering.
渐渐地,更多种类的肥皂可以用来剃须、洗头、洗澡和洗衣服。

A major step toward large-scale commercial soapmaking occurred in 1791, when a French chemist, Nicholas Leblanc, patented a process for turning salt into soda ash, or sodium carbonate. Soda ash is the alkali obtained from ashes that combines with fat to form soap.
1791年,法国化学家尼古拉斯·勒布朗发明了一种将盐转化为苏打灰或碳酸钠的工艺,这是迈向大规模商业化肥皂制造的重要一步。苏打灰是从灰烬中提取的碱,它与脂肪结合形成肥皂。
The Leblanc process yielded quantities of good-quality, inexpensive soda ash.
勒布朗生产了大量质量上乘、价格低廉的苏打灰。

Modern soapmaking was born some 20 years later, in the early 19th century, with the discovery by Michel Eugene Chevreul, another French chemist, of the chemical nature and relationship of fats, glycerine and fatty acids. His studies established the basis for both fat and soap chemistry, and soapmaking became a science.
现代肥皂制作诞生于大约20年后,即19世纪初,另位法国化学家米歇尔·恩谢夫鲁发现了脂肪、甘油和脂肪酸的化学性质和相互关系。他的研究为脂肪和肥皂化学奠定了基础,肥皂制造成为一门科学。
Further developments during the 19th century made it easier and cheaper to manufacture soap.
19世纪的进一步发展使肥皂的制造变得更容易和更便宜。

Until the 19th century, soap was regarded as a luxury item, and was heavily taxed in several countries. As it became more readily available, it became an everyday necessity, a development that was reinforced when the high tax was removed.
直到19世纪,肥皂一直被视为奢侈品,在一些国家被征收重税。随着它变得越来越易得,它成了日常生活的必需品。取消高额税收后,这一发展更加迅速。
Soap was then something ordinary people could afford, and cleanliness standards improved.
肥皂成了普通人买得起的物品,清洁标准也提高了。
With this widespread use came the development of milder soaps for bathing and soaps for use in the washing machines that were available to consumers by the turn of the 20th century.
随着这种广泛使用,20世纪初消费者们可以购买用于洗澡的较温和的肥皂和用于洗衣机的肥皂。

120211012105306.title: C15-Test 4-Section 1
transcript: Hello. Do you mind if I ask you some questions about your journey today? We're doing a customer satisfaction survey.
您好。您介不介意我问您一些关于今天的旅行的问题?我们正在做客户满意度调查。
Yes. OK. I've got about ten minutes before my train home leaves. I'm on a day trip.
没问题。你问吧。十分钟后我要搭乘火车回家。我参加的是一日行。
Great. Thank you. So first of all, could you tell me your name?
太好了。谢谢您。首先,请问您叫什么名字?
It's Sophie Bird.
我叫苏菲·伯德
Thank you. And would you mind telling me what you do?
谢谢。请问您可以告诉我您的职业吗?
I'm a journalist.
我是个记者。
Oh really? That must be interesting.
真的吗?那一定很有趣。
Yes. It is.
是的。的确是这样。
So was the reason for your visit here today work?
您今天来这里是为了工作吗?
Actually, it's my day off. I came here to do some shopping.
实际上,今天我休班。我来这里是为了购物。
Oh right.
知道了。
But I do sometimes come here for work.
但是我偶尔到这里工作。
OK. Now I'd like to ask some questions about your journey today, if that's OK.
嗯。现在如果可以的话,我想问您一些关于今天旅行的问题。
Yes. No problem.
你说吧。没问题。
Right, so can you tell me which station you're travelling back to?
好的,您能告诉我您要回到哪个车站吗?
Staunfirth, where I live.
