斯当佛斯,我在那里住。
Can I just check the spelling? S-T-A-U-N-F-I-R-T-H?
请问是这么拼吗?S-T-A-U-N-F-I-R-T-H对吗
That's right.
是的。
And you travelled from there this morning?
您是这个早上过来的吗?
Yes.
是的。
OK, good. Next, can I ask what kind of ticket you bought? I assume it wasn't a season ticket, as you don't travel every day.
好的,知道了。接下来,能问下您买的哪种票?我猜不是季票,因为您也不是每天都来。
That's right. No, I just got a normal return ticket. I don't have a rail card so I didn't get any discount. I keep meaning to get one because it's a lot cheaper.
是的。我买的就是普通的往返票。我没有铁路卡所以没有打折。我一直想要弄一张因为那样会便宜很多。
Yes - you'd have saved 20% on your ticket today. So you paid the full price for your ticket?
是的。如果有的话,您的票价今天可以打八折。所以您买的是全价票了?
I paid £23.70.
我花了23.70磅。
OK. Do you think that's good value for money?
知道了。您觉得物有所值吗?
Not really. I think it's too much for a journey that only takes 45 minutes.
并没有。我觉得对于一趟只有45分钟的行程来说太贵了
Yes, that's one of the main complaints we get. So, you didn't buy your ticket in advance?
是的,我们收到的最多的抱怨也是这个。那么,您没有提前买票吗?
No. I know it's cheaper if you buy a week in advance but I didn't know I was coming then.
没有。我知道提前一周买票会更便宜但是我那时候并不知道我会来。
I know. You can't always plan ahead. So, did you buy it this morning?
了解了。有的事儿很难提前计划。那么,您是今天早上买的票吗
No, it was yesterday.
不是,是昨天。
Right. And do you usually buy your tickets at the station?
好的。您经常在车站买票吗?

Well, I do usually but the ticket office closes early and I hate using ticket machines. I think ticket offices should be open for longer hours. There's always a queue for the machines and they're often out of order.
我确实经常在售票处买票,但是售票处很早就关门。我不喜欢用售票机。我觉得售票处的营业时间应该更长。售票机前很多人排队,有时很没有秩序。
A lot of customers are saying the same thing.
很多顾客和您说的一样。
So to answer your question ... I got an e-ticket online.
至于你刚才的问题。我在网上买的电子票。
OK. Thank you. Now I'd like to ask you about your satisfaction with your journey. So what would you say you were most satisfied with today?
了解了。谢谢您。现在我想问您一些关于旅行满意度的问题。今天的旅行您最满意的是什么?
Well, I like the wifi on the train. It's improved a lot. It makes it easier for me to work if I want to.
火车上提供无线网络,我很喜欢。网速提高很多。如果我要工作的话也更方便。
That's the first time today anyone's mentioned that. It's good to get some positive feedback on that.
今天还没有人说到这个。能得到一些积极的反馈真好。
Mmm.
嗯。
And, is there anything you weren't satisfied with?
今天有什么不满意的吗?
Well, normally, the trains run on time and are pretty reliable but today there was a delay: the train was about 15 minutes behind schedule.
平时火车总是准时到站,这一点很值得信赖。但是今天火车晚点了,晚了15分钟。
OK. I'll put that down. Now I'd also like to ask about the facilities at this station . You've probably noticed that the whole station's been upgraded. What are you most satisfied with?
好的。我会记下来。现在我想知道有关火车站设施的情况。您也许已经注意到了整个火车站已经翻新升级了。您最满意的是什么?

I think the best thing is that they've improved the amount of information about train times etc. that's given to passengers - it's much clearer - before there was only one board and I couldn't always see it properly- which was frustrating.
我觉得最好的就是他们增加了有关火车班次时间等等的信息。旅客们看时间看得更清楚了。之前只有一个板子,我经常看不清上面的字,这很令人沮丧。
That's good. And is there anything you're not satisfied with?
那就好。有什么不满意的吗?

Let's see ... I think things have generally improved a lot. The trains are much more modern and I like the new cafe. But one thing is that there aren't enough places to sit down, especially on the platforms.
我想想。我觉得普遍来说已经完善了很多。火车更加现代化了,我也喜欢新的咖啡厅。但是确实有一个不满意的地方,那就是车站,尤其是站台上的座椅不够。
OK - so I'll put 'seating' down, shall I, as the thing you're least satisfied with?
好的,我把它记成您最不满意的事项?
Yes. OK.
可以。没问题
Can I ask your opinion about some of the other facilities? We'd like feedback on whether people are satisfied, dissatisfied or neither satisfied nor dissatisfied.
我能问问您对其他设施的看法吗?我们希望得到客户的回应,不管是满意还是不满意还是无意见。
OK.
可以。
What about the parking at the station?
车站停车方便吗?
Well to be honest, I don't really have an opinion as I never use it.
说实话,因为我没用过,我也没什么看法。
So, neither satisfied nor dissatisfied for that then.
那就是无意见
Yes, I suppose so ...
是的，我觉得是
OK, and what about ... ?
好的，那……

120211012105949.title: C15-Test 4-Section 2
transcript: As chair of the town council subcommittee on park facilities, I'd like to bring you up to date on some of the changes that have been made recently to the Croft Valley Park. So if you could just take a look at the map I handed out, let's begin with a general overview.
作为镇政会关于停车场的小组委员会的委员长,我想要带大家了解一下克罗夫特山谷公园的最近的变化。请您看下我刚才发给您的地图,我先整体介绍一下概况.
So the basic arrangement of the park hasn't changed - it still has two gates, north and south, and a lake in the middle.
关于公园的基本规划没有改变,公园里还是有两个入口,在北面和南面。公园的中间有个湖。
The cafe continues to serve an assortment of drinks and snacks and is still in the same place, looking out over the lake and next to the old museum.
咖啡店还会提供各种各样的小吃和饮品。还是在老位置,紧挨着老博物馆,俯瞰中心湖。

We're hoping to change the location of the toilets, and bring them nearer to the centre of the park as they're a bit out of the way at present, near the adventure playground, in the corner of your map.
我们希望能够改变洗手间的位置,挪到离着公园中心更近的位置来。它们现在离得有点远,在探险游乐场附近,就在你地图的角落里。

The formal gardens have been replanted and should be at their best in a month or two. They used to be behind the old museum, but we've now used the space near the south gate - between the park boundary and the path that goes past the lake towards the old museum .
我们在规则式花园里重新种上了植物,一两个月之后正是长得好的时候。这些花园原来在老博物馆后面,但是现在挪到靠近南门的地方了。在湖的一侧有一条通往老博物馆的小路。花园就在小路和公园外墙中间。
We have a new outdoor gym for adults and children, which is already proving very popular. It's by the glass houses, just to the right of the path from the south gate.
对大人和儿童新开放的户外健身馆已经很有人气了。健身馆在玻璃房旁边,在从南门进来的小路的右边。
You have to look for it as it's a bit hidden in the trees.
因为健身房前面有树遮挡,您需要特别找一下。
One very successful introduction has been our skateboard ramp. It's in constant use during the evenings and holidays.
初次投入使用的滑板坡道特别成功。它经常在晚上和节假日使用。
It's near the old museum. at the end of a little path that leads off from the main path between the lake and museum.
坡道靠近老博物馆。有条小径从博物馆和湖泊中间的主路中拐出来。坡道就在小路的尽头。
We've also introduced a new area for wild flowers, to attract bees and butterflies. It's on a bend in the path that goes round the east side of the lake, just south of the adventure playground.
为了吸引蝴蝶和蜜蜂,我们还为野花开辟了一个新的区域。就在湖泊东侧小路的拐弯处,在探险游乐场的南侧。
Now let me tell you a bit more about some of the changes to Croft Valley Park.
现在让我为您介绍一些关于克罗夫特山谷公园的其它变化。

One of our most exciting developments has been the adventure playground. We were aware that we had nowhere for children to let off steam, and decided to use our available funds to set up a completely new facility in a large space to the north of the park.
最让人激动的改造之一就是探险游乐场了。我们意识到公园里没有地方能让孩子们来疯玩儿疯闹,所以决定使用现有资金在公园北侧的空地建造个全新的场所。

It's open year-round, though it closes early in the winter months, and entrance is completely free. Children can choose whatever activities they want to do, irrespective of their age, but we do ask adults not to leave them on their own there.
游乐场一整年都开放,但是在冬天的时候营业时间短。门票是完全免费的。不论什么年龄的孩子都可以选择想玩儿任何项目。但是我们要求家长们不要让孩子独自在那里玩耍。
There are plenty of seats where parents can relax and keep an eye on their children at the same time.
游乐场里有很多座椅,可以让家长休息的同时看护孩子。
Lastly, the glass houses. A huge amount of work has been done on them to repair the damage following the disastrous fire that recently destroyed their western side.
最后,为您介绍玻璃房。最近的一场灾难性的大火烧毁了玻璃房的西侧。为了修复被破坏的部分我们做了大量的工作。

Over £80,000 was spent on replacing the glass walls and the metal supports, as well as the plants that had been destroyed, although unfortunately the collection of tropical palm trees has proved too expensive to replace up to now. At present the glass houses are open from 1 0am to 3pm Mondays to Thursdays.
我们更换玻璃墙面和金属支架,还有重新栽种被烧毁的植物,花费超过八万英镑。但是很不幸的是,被破坏的热带棕榈树因为价格昂贵现在也没有重新栽种。现在玻璃房每周一到周四上午十点到下午三点开放。
And it's hoped to extend this to the weekend soon. We're grateful to all those who helped us by contributing their time and money to this achievement.
希望不久之后营业时间可以延长到周末。非常感谢为了这次修缮为我们提供了帮助、贡献了时和资金的人们。
The gardens have ...
花园有

120211012111353.title: C15-Test 4-Section 3
transcript: OK, Jack. Before we plan our presentation about refrigeration, let's discuss what we've discovered so far.
好了杰克。在我们准备关于冰箱的展示之前让我们讨论一下我们目前找到的信息吧。
Fine, Annie. Though I have to admit I haven't done much research yet.
好的,安妮。但是我得承认我还没有做很多的研究。

Nor me. But I found an interesting article about icehouses. I'd seen some 18th and 19th-century ones here in the UK, so I knew they were often built in a shady area or underground, close to lakes that might freeze in the winter. Then blocks of ice could be cut and stored in the icehouse. But I didn't realise that insulating the blocks with straw or sawdust meant they didn't melt for months. The ancient Romans had refrigeration, too.
我也没有。但是我发现了一篇关于冰室的文章,很有趣。我已经看了一些英国的18世纪和19世纪的冰室。所以我知道冰室经常建于阴凉的地方或者地下,靠近冬季可能结冰的湖泊。然后冰块可以被切割并且储存在冰室中。但是我没想到用稻草或者锯末来隔热可以使冰数月不化。古罗马也有制冷术。
I didn't know that.
我还不知道。

Yes, pits were dug in the ground, and snow was imported from the mountains—even though they were at quite a distance. The snow was stored in the pits. Ice formed at the bottom of it. Both the ice and the snow were then sold. The ice cost more than the snow and my guess is that only the wealthy members of society could afford it.
是的,先在地面挖坑,将远处山上的雪移过来。雪被储存在土坑中，在底部会形成冰。冰和雪之后会被卖掉。冰比雪的价格更贵。我猜测只有社会上的富裕阶层可以用得起。
I wouldn't be surprised. I also came across an article about modern domestic fridges. Several different technologies are used, but they were too complex for me to understand.
我觉得也不惊奇。我还看到一篇关于现代家用冰箱的文章。用到了许多不同的科技,但是对我来说很难理解。
You have to wonder what happens when people get rid of old ones.
你必须考虑一下旧冰箱被废弃的时候会发生什么。
You mean because the gases in them are harmful for the environment?
你的意思是因为冰箱中的气体会污染环境?
Exactly. At least there are now plenty of organisations that will recycle most of the components safely, but of course some people just dump old fridges in the countryside.
是的。至少现在有不少的组织都会安全回收其中的大部分零件,当然也有人直接把旧冰箱丢在乡下。
It's hard to see how they can be stopped unfortunately. In the UK we get rid of three million a year altogether!
糟糕的是很难明确应该如何阻止他们。在英国一年间一共要丢弃300万台冰箱。
That sounds a lot, especially because fridges hardly ever break down.
听起来真不少,尤其是因为冰箱几乎不会坏掉。

That's right. In this country we keep domestic fridges for 11 years on average , and a lot last for 20 or more. So if you divide the cost by the number of years you can use a fridge. they're not expensive. compared with some household appliances.
你说得对。在英国本地,平均每台冰箱的使用时间是11年,很多能用20年或者更久。所以如果把成本平均到冰箱的使用时间上,和其他家用电器比起来,冰箱的价格并不贵。
True. I suppose manufacturers encourage people to spend more by making them different colours and designs. l"m sure when my parents bought their first fridge they had hardly any choice!
是的。我觉得制造厂商通过制造不同颜色和设计的冰箱来鼓励大家购买。我确定我父母买第一个冰箱的时候几乎没有选择。
Yes, there's been quite a change.
是的,这确实是一个相当大的变化。
Right. let's make a list of topics to cover in our presentation. and decide who"s going to do more research on them. Then later. we can get together and plan the next step.
好了,让我们来列一个关于我们展示要谈到的话题的单子,然后决定谁将要再多做些研究。之后,我们再一起做下一步计划。
OK. How about starting with how useful refrigeration is. and the range of goods that are refrigerated nowadays? Because of course it's not just food and drinks.
好的。展示以冰箱的用途开始怎么样?再说下现在需要冷藏的各个种类的商品。因为冰箱里放的当然不止是食物和饮料。
No, I suppose flowers and medicines are refrigerated, too.
是的,我想鲜花和药品也要冷藏。
And computers. I could do that, unless you particularly want to.
还有电脑。我可以讲这个,除非你特别想做。

No, that's fine by me. What about the effects of refrigeration on people's health? After all, some of the chemicals used in the 19th century were pretty harmful, but there have been lots of benefits too, like always having access to fresh food. Do you fancy dealing with that?
我就不用了。聊聊冰箱对人的健康的影响怎么样?毕竟19世纪的冰箱用到的化学物质对人身体伤害特别大,但是冰箱也有很多好处,比如总能让人们吃到新鲜的食物。你想要做这个吗?
I'm not terribly keen, to be honest.
说实话,我不是很想做。
Nor me. My mind just goes blank when I read anything about chemicals.
我也不想。我一读到和化学的东西脑子就一片空白。

Oh, all right then. I'll do you a favour. But you owe me. Jack. OK. What about the effects on food producers, like farmers in poorer countries being able to export their produce to developed countries? Something for you , maybe?
噢,那就算了,我帮你这个忙吧。但是杰克,记得你欠我一次啊。我来做吧。关于冰箱对食品制作者的影响呢?比如较为贫穷国家的农民能够把他们的产品出口到发达国家去?也许你来做这个？
I don't mind. It should be quite interesting .
我没问题。应该还挺有趣的。
I think we should also look at how refrigeration has helped whole cities - like Las Vegas, which couldn't exist without refrigeration because it's in the middle of a desert.
我认为我们应该聊一下冰箱是如何帮助整个城市的,比如拉斯维加斯。如果无法制冷,拉斯维加斯就不会存在了,因为它是位于沙漠中的城市。
Right. I had a quick look at an economics book in the library that's got a chapter about this sort of thing. I could give you the title. if you want to do this section.
好的。我之前在图书馆翻阅过一本经济学的书,里面有一章就是讲这个的。如果你想负责这个部分的话,我可以把书名给你。
Not particularly, to be honest. I find economics books pretty heavy going, as a rule.
说实话,我并不是很想做。我发现通常来说经济学的书籍很难读。
OK. leave it to me. then.
那就交给我吧。
Thanks. Then there's transport, and the difference that refrigerated trucks have made. I wouldn't mind having a go at that.
谢谢啦。还有交通方面。冷藏车带来的变化。我愿意做这一部分。
Don't forget trains. too. I read something about milk and butter being transported in refrigerated railroad cars in the USA, right back in the 1840s.
别忘了还有火车。我之前读到早在19世纪40年代的美国,牛奶和黄油是用带有冷藏功能的火车运输的。
I hadn't thought of trains . Thanks.
我之前没想到火车。谢谢。
Shall we have a separate section on domestic fridges? After all, they're something everyone's familiar with.
我们要不要单用一个部分讲家用冰箱?毕竟,这是大家都熟悉的内容。

What about spliting it into two? You could investigate 19th- and 20th century fridges, and I'll concentrate on what's available these days, and how manufacturers differentiate their products from those of their competitors .
咱们把它分成两部分怎么样?你可以调查一下19和20世纪的冰箱。我来负责现代冰箱,还有冰箱制造厂商如何制造与竞争者不同的产品。
OK, that'd suit me.
可以，这个很适合我。

120211012112921.title: C15-Test 4-Section 4
transcript: Hi everyone, in this session I'll be presenting my research about the social history of Britain during the Industrial Revolution. I particularly looked at how ordinary lives were affected by changes that happened at that time.
大家好。我将在这个环节给大家呈现关于工业革命时期英国的社会历史的研究。我特别关注了那一时期的变革对普通人的影响。
This was a time that saw the beginning of a new phenomenon: consumerism - where buying and selling goods became a major part of ordinary people's lives.
工业革命时期见证了消费主义这种新现象的开端。消费主义是指购买和销售商品成为普通人生活的主要组成部分。

In fact, it was in the 19th century that the quantity and quality of people's possessions was used as an indication of the wealth of the country. Before this, the vast majority of people had very few possessions, but all that was changed by the Industrial Revolution.
事实上,在19世纪,居民所有物的数量和品质成为国家财富的指标。在此之前,大部分人只有很少的财产,但是这一都在工业革命时期发生了改变。

This was the era from the mid-18th to the late 19th century, when improvements in how goods were made as well as in technology triggered massive social changes that transformed life for just about everybody in several key areas.
从18世纪中到19世纪末这段时期,产品制作工艺的提升、技术的进步促使很多领域发生了巨大社会变革在几个关键领域改变了几乎每个人的生活。
First let's look at manufacturing. When it comes to manufacturing, we tend to think of the Industrial Revolution in images of steam engines and coal.
首先,我们来聊聊制造业。提到制造业,人们倾向于将工业革命与蒸汽机和煤炭联想起来。

And it's true that the Industrial Revolution couldn't have taken place at all if it weren't for these new sources of power.They marked an important shift away from the traditional watermills and windmills that had dominated before this.
的确，如果没有这些新的能源，工业革命根本就不可能发生。它们标志着传统水磨坊和风车在此之前的主导地位发生了重大转变。
The most advanced industry for much of the 19th century was textiles. This meant that fashionable fabrics, and lace and ribbons were made available to everyone.
在19世纪大部分时间里最先进的工业是纺织业。这意昧着任何人能都用时尚的材料、蕾丝和丝带。

Before the Industrial Revolution, most people made goods to sell in small workshops, often in their own homes. But enormous new machines were now being created that could produce the goods faster and on a larger scale, and these required a lot more space.
工业革命之前,大多数人都是在小作坊里,通常是在自己家里制造商品出售的。但是当时,巨大的新机器正在被创造出来,它们能够更快、更大规模地生产商品,而这需要更多的空间。
So large factories were built, replacing the workshops, and forcing workers to travel to work. In fact, large numbers of people migrated from villages into towns as a result.
于是人们建造了大工厂,取代了小作坊,迫使工人们去上班。事实上,大量的人因此从村庄迁移到城镇。

As well as manufacturing, there were new technologies in transport, contributing to the growth of consumerism. The horse-drawn stagecoaches and carts of the 18th century, which carried very few people and goods, and travelled slowly along poorly surfaced roads, were gradually replaced by the numerous canals that were constructed.
除了制造业,运输业也出现了新技术,促进了消费主义的发展。在18世纪时,驿站马车和马拉货车的运输能力很差,在粗糙的路面上行进速度很慢。这种交通方式逐渐被修建的众多运河取代。

These were particularly important for the transportation of goods. The canals gradually fell out of use, though, as railways were developed, becoming the main way of moving goods and people from one end of the country to the other.
运河对货物运输特别重要。因为铁路发展起来了,运河交通逐渐衰落。铁路跨越国土,从国家的一端跑到另一端,成为运输旅客和货物的主要方式。

And the goods they moved weren't just coal, iron, clothes, and so on - significantly, they included newspapers, which meant that thousands of people were not only more knowledgeable about what was going on in the country, but could also read about what was available in the shops. And that encouraged them to buy more.
运输的货物不仅仅是煤、铁、衣服等商品,更重要的是,报纸也在运输物品之列,这意味着成千上万的人不仅可以增加对这个国家的情况的了解,还可以阅读商店里商品的相关信息。这促进了销售。
So faster forms of transport resulted in distribution becoming far more efficient- goods could now be sold all over the country, instead of just in the local market.
因此,更快的运输方式促使配送效率大大提高——不仅仅是在当地市场,还可以在全国各地销售商品。

The third main area that saw changes that contributed to consumerism was retailing. The number and quality of shops grew rapidly, and in particular, small shops suffered as customers flocked to the growing number of department stores - a form of retailing that was new in the 19th century.
导致消费主义的第三个主要领域是零售业。商店的数量和品质迅速增加,小商店的情况更加糟糕,因为成群结队的顾客跑去百货商场购物。这是19世纪新出现的一种零售形式。

The entrepreneurs who opened these found new ways to stock them with goods, and to attract customers: for instance, improved lighting inside greatly increased the visibility of the goods for sale. Another development that made goods more visible from outside resulted from the use of plate glass, which made it possible for windows to be much larger than previously.
百货商店的老板们找到了新的方法来储存货物,招揽顾客。比如,增加室内照明来让售卖的商品变得更加显眼。另一个让商品从外面看起来更显眼的方式是使用平板玻璃。这使得窗户比以前大得多。

New ways of promoting goods were introduced, too. Previously, the focus had been on informing potential customers about the availability of goods; now there was an explosion in advertising trying to persuade people to go shopping.
新的促销方式也被引入。以前,人们关注的焦点是向潜在客户告知商品的供应情况。现如今激增的广告试图说服顾客购买商品。

Flanders claims that one of the great effects of the Industrial Revolution was that it created choice. All sorts of things that had previously been luxuries - from sugar to cutlery - became conveniences, and before long they'd turned into necessities: life without sugar or cutlery was unimaginable.
弗兰德斯声称,工业革命的一个重大影响是它创造了选择。所有以前是奢侈品的东西——从糖到餐具—变得更加易得。而且它们很快就变成了必需品,没有糖和餐具的生活是难以想象的。
Rather like mobile phones these days!
就像现在的手机。

120220602172241.title: C16-Test 1-Part 1
transcript: Hello. Children's Engineering Workshops.
你好。儿童工程工作室。
Oh hello. I wanted some information about the workshops in the school holidays.
哦，你好。我想了解一些关于学校假期的研讨会信息。
Sure.
当然。
I have two daughters who are interested. The younger one's Lydia, she's four - do you take children as young as that?
我有两个女儿感兴趣。小女儿叫莉迪亚，今年四岁 - 你们接受这么小的孩子吗？
Yes, our Tiny Engineers workshop is for four to five-year-olds.
是的，我们的 "小小工程师 "工作坊是为四到五岁的孩子准备的。
What sorts of activities do they do?
他们会做哪些活动？

All sorts. For example, they work together to design a special cover that goes round an egg, so that when it's inside they can drop it from a height and it doesn't break. Well, sometimes it does break but that's part of the fun!
各种各样的。例如，他们一起设计了一个特殊的套子，可以套在鸡蛋上。这样，当鸡蛋在里面时，他们可以从高处掉下来，而鸡蛋不会碎。好吧，有时它确实会碎，但这也是乐趣的一部分！
Right. And Lydia loves building things. Is there any opportunity for her to do that?
对。莉迪亚也喜欢建造东西。有什么机会让她做这个吗？
Well, they have a competition to see who can make the highest tower.You'd be amazed how high they can go.
嗯，他们有一个比赛，看谁能造出最高的塔。你会惊讶于他们能做多高。
Right.
很好。
But they're learning all the time as well as having fun.
他们一直在学习，同时也在享受乐趣。
For example, one thing they do is to design and build a car that's attached to a balloon,and the force of the air in that actually powers the car and makes it move along.
例如，其中一个活动是设计和建造一辆连接在气球上的汽车，空气的力量实际上为汽车提供了动力，使它向前移动。
They go really fast too.
他们的速度也非常快。
OK, well, all this sounds perfect.
好吧，这一切听起来很完美。
Now Carly, that's my older daughter, has just had her seventh birthday,so presumably she'd be in a different group?
现在来说说卡莉，也就是我的大女儿，刚刚过完她的七岁生日，所以她应该是在一个不同的小组里吧？
Yes, she'd be in the Junior Engineers. That's for children from six to eight.
是的，她会在初级工程师组。那是为六到八岁的孩子准备的。
And do they do the same sorts of activities?
他们做同样的活动吗？

Some are the same, but a bit more advanced.  So they work out how to build model vehicles, things like cars and trucks, but also how to construct animals using the same sorts of material and technique,and then they learn how they can program them and make them move.
有些是一样的，但要更高级一些。他们研究如何建造车辆模型，比如汽车和卡车，也研究如何用同样的材料和技术建造动物，然后他们学习如何给它们编程，让它们移动。
So they learn a bit of coding?
所以他们学会了一些编程？
They do. They pick it up really quickly.
是的。他们很快就能学会。
We're there to help if they need it, but they learn from one another too.
如果他们需要，我们会在那里提供帮助，但他们也会互相学习。
Right. And do they have competitions too?
真好。那他们也有比赛吗？
Yes, with the Junior Engineers, it's to use recycled materials like card and wood to build a bridge, and the longest one gets a prize.
是的，初级工程师的比赛是用回收的材料，如卡片和木头，来建造一座桥，最长的桥可以得到奖励。
That sounds fun. I wouldn't mind doing that myself!
这听起来很有趣。我不介意自己做这个 !
Then they have something a bit different, which is to think up an idea for a five-minute movie and then film it,using special animation software.
然后他们有一些不同的东西，那就是想出一个五分钟的电影的想法，然后用特殊的动画软件把它拍下来。
You'd be amazed what they come up with.
你会惊讶于他们想出来的东西。
And of course, that's something they can put on their phone and take home to show all their friends.
当然，这也是他们可以放在手机里，带回家给所有的朋友看的东西。
Exactly, And then they also build a robot in the shape of a human,and they decorate it and program it so that it can move its arms and legs.
没错。然后，他们还会建造了一个人类形状的机器人，他们对其进行装饰，并对其进行编程，使其能够移动手臂和腿。
Perfect. So, is it the same price as the Tiny Engineers?
完美。那么，它的价格和 "小工程师 "一样吗？
It's just a bit more: £50 for the five weeks.
只是多一点。五个星期是50英镑。
And are the classes on a Monday, too?
那么课程也是在星期一吗？
They used to be, but we found it didn't give our staff enough time to clear up after the first workshop,so we moved them to Wednesdays.
以前是的，但我们发现这样做没有给我们的员工足够的时间在第一次研讨会后进行清理，所以我们把它移到了星期三。
The classes are held in the morning from ten to eleven.
这些课程是在上午十点到十一点举行的。
OK. That's better for me actually.
好的，这对我来说其实更好。
And what about the location? Where exactly are the workshops held?
那地点呢？研讨会到底是在哪里举行的？
They're in building 10A - there's a big sign on the door,you can't miss it, and that's in Fradstone Industrial Estate.
他们在10A楼--门上有一个大牌子，你不可能错过它，那是在弗拉德斯通工业区。
Sorry?
什么？
Fradstone - that's F-R-A-D-S-T-O-N-E.
弗拉德斯通--就是F-R-A-D-S-T-O-N-E。
And that's in Grasford, isn't it?
那是在Grasford，对吗？
Yes, up past the station.
是的，在车站的前面。
And will I have any parking problems there?
在那里会有任何停车问题吗？
No, there's always plenty available. So would you like to enrol Lydia and Carly now?
不会，那里总是有很多停车位。那么，你现在想为莉迪亚和卡莉报名吗？
OK.
好的。
So can I have your full name ...
那么我可以知道你的全名吗...？

120220602174239.title: C16-Test 1-Part 2
transcript: Good morning, everyone, and welcome to Stevenson's, one of the country's major manufacturers of metal goods.
大家早上好，欢迎来到史蒂文森公司，这是全国主要的金属制品制造商之一。
Thank you for choosing us for your two weeks of work experience.
感谢你们选择我们进行为期两周的工作体验。
My name is Julia Simmons, and since the beginning of this year I've been the managing director.
我的名字是朱莉娅-西蒙斯，从今年年初开始，我就是总经理了。
Stevenson's is quite an old company.
史蒂文森公司是一个相当古老的公司。
Like me, the founder, Ronald Stevenson, went into the steel industry when he left school - that was in 1923.
像我一样，创始人罗纳德-史蒂文森在离开学校时进入了钢铁行业--那是在1923年。
He set up this company when he finished his apprenticeship, in 1926,
他在1926年完成学徒期时成立了这家公司，
although he actually started making plans two years earlier, in 1924.
尽管他实际上在两年前就开始制定计划，即1924年。
He was a very determined young man!
他是一个非常坚定的年轻人。
Stevenson's long-term plan was to manufacture components for the machine tools industry-although in fact that never came about-
史蒂文森的长期计划是为机床行业制造零部件--尽管事实上这从未发生过--
and for the automotive industry, that is, cars and lorries.
以及为汽车行业，即汽车和卡车制造零部件。
However, there was a delay of five years before that happened,because shortly before the company went into production,
然而，在这之前有五年的延迟，因为在公司投入生产前不久，
Stevenson was given the opportunity to make goods for hospitals and other players in the healthcare industry,so that's what we did for the first five years.
史蒂文森得到了为医院和医疗保健行业的其他参与者制造商品的机会。所以这就是我们在最初五年所做的事情。
Over the years, we've expanded the premises considerably-we were lucky that the site is big enough,so moving to a new location has never been necessary.
多年来，我们大大地扩大了厂房--我们很幸运，场地足够大，所以从来没有必要搬到一个新的地方。
However, the layout is far from ideal for modern machinery and production methods,
然而，对于现代机械和生产方法来说，这里的布局远远不够理想，
so we intend to carry out major refurbishment of this site over the next five years.
所以我们打算在未来五年内对这个场地进行重大整修。
I'd better give you some idea of what you'll be doing during your two weeks with us,so you know what to expect.
我最好给你们一些关于你们在我们这里的两周内会做什么的想法，这样你就知道该做什么。
Most mornings you'll have a presentation from one of the managers, to learn about their department,starting this morning with research and development.
大多数早上，你们会有一个经理的介绍，以了解他们的部门，今天早上将以研究和开发作为开始内容。
And you'll all spend some time in each department,
你们将在每个部门花一些时间，
observing what's going on and talking to people - as long as you don't stop them from doing their work altogether!
观察正在发生的事情，并与人交谈--只要你们不阻止他们做他们的工作就可以了!
In the past, a teacher from your school has come in at the end of each week to find out how the group were getting on,
在过去，你们学校的一位老师会在每周结束时来了解小组的进展情况，
but your school isn't able to arrange that this year.
但你们学校今年无法安排了。
OK, now I'll briefly help you to orientate yourselves around the site.
好的，现在我将简要地帮助你们熟悉周围的情况。
As you can see, we're in the reception area, which we try to make attractive and welcoming to visitors.
正如你们所看到的，我们在接待区，我们努力使接待区对来访者具有吸引力和欢迎性。
There's a corridor running left from here,and if you go along that, the door facing you at the end is the entrance to the coffee room.
从这里向左走有一条走廊，如果你沿着走廊走，尽头处面向你的那扇门就是咖啡室的入口。
This looks out onto the main road on one side, and some trees on the other, and that'll be where you meet each morning.
这里一边是主干道，另一边是一些树木，这将是你们每天早上见面的地方。
The factory is the very big room on the far side of the site.
工厂是场地最边上的那个很大的房间。
Next to it is the warehouse, which can be accessed by lorries going up the road to the turning area at the end.
旁边是仓库，卡车可以沿着路走到尽头的转弯处进入。
You can get to the warehouse by crossing to the far side of the courtyard, and then the door is on your right.
你可以穿过院子的远端到达仓库，然后门就在你的右边。
Somewhere you'll be keen to find is the staff canteen.
你会很想找到的地方是员工食堂。
This is right next to reception. I can confidently say that the food's very good, but the view isn't.
这就在接待处旁边。我可以自信地说，这里的食物非常好，但风景却不好。
The windows on one side look onto a corridor and courtyard, which aren't very attractive at all,and on the other onto the access road, which isn't much better.
一边的窗户看到的是走廊和院子，一点也不吸引人，另一边是通道，也没好多少。
You'll be using the meeting room quite often, and you'll find it by walking along the corridor to the left of the courtyard,and continuing along it to the end.
你会经常使用会议室，你可以沿着庭院左边的走廊走，然后继续走到尽头，就能找到它。
The meeting room is the last one on the right,and I'm afraid there's no natural daylight in the room.
会议室是右边的最后一间，恐怕房间里没有自然光。
Then you'll need to know where some of the offices are.
然后你需要知道一些办公室的位置。
The human resources department is at the front of this building, so you head to the left along the corridor from reception,
人力资源部门在这栋楼的前面，所以你从接待处沿着走廊向左走。
and it's the second room you come to. It looks out onto the main road.
这是你来到的第二个房间。从这儿可以看到主路。
And finally, the boardroom, where you'll be meeting sometimes.
最后是会议室，你们有时会在这里开会。
That has quite a pleasant view, as it looks out on to the trees.
这间房的风景相当不错，因为它可以看到树木。
Go along the corridor past the courtyard, right to the end.
沿着走廊经过院子，一直走到尽头。
The boardroom is on the left, next to the factory.
董事会办公室在左边，紧挨着工厂。
OK, now are there any questions before we ...
好了，现在有什么问题吗，在我们...

120220606102728.title: C16-Test 1-Part 3
transcript: How are you getting on with your art project, Tom?
汤姆，你的艺术项目进展如何？
OK. Like, they gave us the theme of birds to base our project on, and I'm not really all that interested in wildlife.
好的。是这样的，他们给了我们鸟类的主题作为我们项目的基础，而我对野生动物并不是很感兴趣。
But I'm starting to get into it. I've pretty well finished the introductory stage.
但我已经开始着手了。我已经很好地完成了入门阶段。
So have I. When they gave us all those handouts with details of books and websites to look at,I was really put off,
我也是。当他们给我们所有那些关于书籍和网站的详细资料时，我真的被劝退了，
but the more I read, the more interested I got.
但是我读得越多，我就越感兴趣。
Me too. I found I could research so many different aspects of birds in art-colour, movement, texture.
我也是。我发现我可以从艺术的角度研究鸟类的许多不同方面——颜色、运动和质地。
So I was looking forward to the Bird Park visit.
所以我很期待参观鸟类公园。
What a letdown! It poured with rain and we hardly saw a single bird.
真让人失望！倾盆大雨，我们几乎看不到一只鸟。
Much less use than the trip to the Natural History Museum,
比去自然历史博物馆少了很多价值。
Yeah. I liked all the stuff about evolution there.The workshop sessions with Dr Fletcher were good too, especially the brainstorming sessions.
是啊。我喜欢那里所有关于进化的东西。与弗莱彻博士的研讨会也很好，特别是头脑风暴会议。
Ah I missed those because I was ill. I wish we could've seen the projects last year's students did.
我错过那些是因为我病了。我真希望我们能看到去年学生做的项目。
Mm. I suppose they want us to do our own thing, not copy.
嗯。我想他们想让我们做出自己的东西，而不是模仿。
Have you drafted your proposal yet?
你的提案起草好了吗？
Yes, but I haven't handed it in. I need to amend some parts.
是的，但我还没交。我需要修改一些部分。
I've realised the notes from my research are almost all just descriptions,I haven't actually evaluated anything. So I'll have to fix that.
我已经意识到我的研究笔记几乎都是描述，我没有真正评估过任何东西。所以我得解决这个问题。
Oh, I didn't know we had to do that. I'll have to look at that too.
我不知道我们还得这么做。这个我也得看看。
Did you do a timeline for the project?
你为这个项目制定时间表了吗?
Yes, and a mind map.
是的，还有思维导图。
Yeah, so did I. I quite enjoyed that.But it was hard having to explain the basis for my decisions in my action plan.
是啊，我也是，我很喜欢。但我很难解释我在行动计划中做出决定的依据。
What?
什么？
You know, give a rationale.
你知道的，给出一个理由。
I didn't realise we had to do that. OK, I can add it now.
我不知道我们必须这么做。好的，我可以现在加上去。
And I've done the video diary presentation, and worked out what I want my outcome to be in the project.
我已经完成了视频日记的演示，并且弄清楚了我想在这个项目中得到的结果。
Someone told me it's best not to be too precise about your actual outcome at this stage,so you have more scope to explore your ideas later on.
有人告诉我，在这个阶段最好不要太精确你的实际结果，这样你以后就有更多的空间去探索你的想法。
So I'm going to go back to my proposal to make it a bit more vague.
所以我要回到我的提议，让它更模糊一点。
Really? OK, I'll change that too then.
真的吗?好的。那我也改一下。
One part of the project I'm unsure about is where we choose some paintings of birds and say what they mean to us.
这个项目有一部分我不确定，那就是我们从哪里选择一些鸟的图画来表达他们对我们意味着什么。
Like, I chose a painting of a falcon by Landseer. I like it because the bird's standing there with his head turned to one side,
比如，我选了一幅兰西尔的猎鹰画。我喜欢它是因为那只鸟站在那里，头转向一边，
but he seems to be staring straight at you. But I can't just say it's a bit scary, can I?
但它似乎在直直地盯着你。但我不能只是说这有点吓人，对吧?
You could talk about the possible danger suggested by the bird's look.
你可以谈谈鸟的外表可能暗示的危险。
Oh, OK.
噢,好的。
There's a picture of a fish hawk by Audubon I like.
我喜欢奥杜邦的一幅鱼鹰画。
It's swooping over the water with a fish in its talons,and with great black wings which take up most of the picture.
它的爪子里夹着一条鱼在水面上猛扑过来，黑色的大翅膀占据了大部分画面。
So you could discuss it in relation to predators and food chains?
所以你可以把它和捕食者与食物链联系起来讨论?
Well actually I think I'll concentrate on the impression of rapid motion it gives.
实际上，我想我会专注于它给人的动作迅速的印象。
Right.
没错。
Do you know that picture of a kingfisher by van Gogh-it's perching on a reed growing near a stream.
你知道梵高画的翠鸟吗?它栖息在小溪附近的芦苇上。
Yes it's got these beautiful blue and red and black shades.
是的，它有这些漂亮的蓝色、红色和黑色阴影。
Mm hm.I've actually chosen it because I saw a real kingfisher once when I was little,I was out walking with my grandfather,and I've never forgotten it.
嗯嗯。我之所以选择这只翠鸟，是因为我小时候和祖父一起出去散步时看到过一只真正的翠鸟，我从未忘记过它。
So we can use a personal link?
所以我们可以用个人联系吗?
Sure.
当然。
OK. There's a portrait called William Wells, I can't remember the artist but it's a middle-aged man who’s just shot a bird.
好的。有一幅画像叫威廉·威尔斯，我不记得是谁画的了，是一个中年男子，他刚射中了一只鸟。
And his expression, and the way he's holding the bird in his hand suggests he's not sure about what he's done.
他的表情，他手里捉着鸟的方式表明他不确定自己做了什么。
To me it's about how ambiguous people are in the way they exploit the natural world.
对我来说，它是关于人们在开发自然世界的过程中是如何模棱两可的。
Interesting. There's Gauguin's picture Vairumati. He did it in Tahiti.
有趣。这是高更的画Vairumati。他在塔希提岛完成的。
It's a woman with a white bird behind her that is eating a lizard,and what I'm interested in is what idea this bird refers to.
画的是一个女人身后有一只白色的鸟正在吃一只蜥蜴，我感兴趣的是这只鸟表达的是什么想法。
Apparently, it's a reference to the never-ending cycle of existence.
显然，它指的是永不停止的循环的存在。
Wow. I chose a portrait of a little boy. Giovanni de Medici.
哇。我选了一幅小男孩的肖像，乔瓦尼·德·美第奇。
He's holding a tiny bird in one fist, I like the way he's holding it carefully so he doesn't hurt it.
他一只手捧着一只小鸟。我喜欢他小心地拿着它，这样就不会弄伤它了。
Ah right.
对的。

120220606105952.title: C16-Test 1-Part 4
transcript: Ancient philosophy is not just about talking or lecturing, or even reading long, dense books.
古代哲学不仅仅是说话、讲课，甚至是读又长又厚的书。
In fact, it is something people have used throughout history - to solve their problems and to achieve their greatest triumphs.
事实上，这是贯穿人类历史上的工具——用来解决问题和取得最大胜利。
Specifically, I am referring to Stoicism, which, in my opinion, is the most practical of all philosophies and therefore the most appealing.
具体来说，我指的是斯多葛主义，在我看来，它是所有哲学中最实用的，因此也是最吸引人的。
Stoicism was founded in Ancient Greece by Zeno of Citium in the early 3rd century BC, but was practised by the likes of Epictetus, Cato, Seneca and Marcus Aurelius.
斯多葛学派是公元前3世纪早期由提提翁的芝诺(Zeno of Citium)在古希腊创立的，但爱比克泰德(Epictetus)、加图(Cato)、塞内加(Seneca)和马可·奥里利乌斯(Marcus Aurelius)等人都践行过。
Amazingly, we still have access to these ideas, despite the fact that the most famous Stoics never wrote anything down for publication.
令人惊讶的是，我们仍然可以触到这些思想，尽管最著名的斯多葛学派从来没有写过任何东西以供出版。
Cato definitely didn't. Marcus Aurelius never intended his Meditations to be anything but personal.
卡托绝对是没有的。马可·奥勒留从来没有想过让他的《沉思录》不是个人的。
Seneca's letters were, well, letters and Epictetus' thoughts come to us by way of a note-taking student.
塞内加的书信和爱比克泰德的思想都是通过一个学生的笔记传来的。
Stoic principles were based on the idea that its followers could have an unshakable happiness in this life and the key to achieving this was virtue.
斯多葛学派的原则是建立在这样一种观念上的，即其追随者可以在这一生中获得不可动摇的幸福，而实现这一幸福的关键是美德。

The road to virtue, in turn, lay in understanding that destructive emotions, like anger and jealousy, are under our conscious control - they don't have to control us, because we can learn to control them.
反过来说，通往美德之路，在于理解像愤怒和嫉妒这样的破坏性情绪是在我们的意识控制之下的--它们不必控制我们，因为我们可以学会控制它们。
In the words of Epictetus:"external events I cannot control, but the choices I make with regard to them, I do control'.
用爱比克泰德的话来说"我无法控制外部事件。而我对他们所做的选择我能控制”。
The modern day philosopher and writer Nassim Nicholas Taleb defines a Stoic as someone who has a different perspective on experiences which most of us would see as wholly negative
现代哲学家和作家纳西姆·尼古拉斯·塔勒布将禁欲主义者定义为对我们大多数人认为完全消极的经历持不同观点的人：
a Stoic 'transforms fear into caution, pain into transformation, mistakes into initiation and desire into undertaking'.
禁欲主义者“将恐惧转化为谨慎，将痛苦转化为转变，将错误转化为启蒙，将欲望转化为事业”。
Using this definition as a model, we can see that throughout the centuries Stoicism has been practised in more recent history by kings, presidents, artists, writers and entrepreneurs.
用这个定义作为模型，我们可以看到，在最近的历史中，国王、总统、艺术家、作家和企业家在几个世纪里一直奉行斯多葛主义。
The founding fathers of the United States were inspired by the philosophy.
美国的开国元勋都受到了这种哲学的启发。
George Washington was introduced to Stoicism by his neighbours at age seventeen,and later, put on a play based on the life of Cato to inspire his men.
乔治·华盛顿17岁时被他的邻居介绍了斯多葛学派，后来又上演了一部以卡托的生活为原型的戏剧来激励他的部下。
Thomas Jefferson kept a copy of Seneca beside his bed.
托马斯·杰斐逊在床边放了一本塞内加的书。

Writers and artists have also been inspired by the stoics. Eugene Delacroix, the renowned French Romantic artist (known best for his painting Liberty Leading the People) was an ardent Stoic,referring to it as his “consoling religion”.
作家和艺术家也受到了禁欲主义者的启发。德拉克罗瓦，著名的法国浪漫主义艺术家(以《自由领导人民》闻名)是一个热情的斯多葛主义者，并将其称为自己的“慰藉宗教”。
The economist Adam Smith's theories on capitalism were significantly influenced by the Stoicism that he studied as a schoolboy, under a teacher who had translated Marcus Aurelius' works.
经济学家亚当•斯密的资本主义理论深受斯多葛学派的影响，在他还是个小学生时，师从翻译过马可•奥勒留著作的老师。
Today's political leaders are no different, with many finding their inspiration from the ancient texts.
今天的政治领导人也不例外，许多人从古代文献中找到了灵感。
Former US president Bill Clinton rereads Marcus Aurelius every single year,and many have compared former President Obama's calm leadership style to that of Cato.
美国前总统比尔·克林顿每年都会重读马可·奥勒留的书，很多人将前总统奥巴马冷静的领导风格比作卡托的领导风格。
Wen Jiabao,the former prime minister of China, claims that Meditations is one of two books he travels with and that he has read it more than one hundred times over the course of his life.
中国前总理温家宝称，《禅修》是他随身携带的两本书之一，他一生中读了一百多次。

Stoicism had a profound influence on Albert Ellis, who invented Cognitive Behaviour Therapy, which is used to help people manage their problems by changing the way that they think and behave. It's most commonly used to treat depression.
禁欲主义对阿尔伯特·埃利斯产生了深远的影响。埃利斯发明了认知行为疗法，用来帮助人们通过改变思维和行为方式来管理他们的问题。
The idea is that we can take control of our lives by challenging the irrational beliefs that create our faulty thinking, symptoms and behaviours by using logic instead.
它最常被用来治疗抑郁症。主要思想是，我们可以通过使用逻辑来挑战那些导致我们错误思维症状和行为的非理性信念以控制我们的生活。

Stoicism has also become popular in the world of business. Stoic principles can build the resilience and state of mind required to overcome setbacks because Stoics teach turning obstacles into opportunity.
斯多葛主义在商界也很流行。斯多葛主义原则可以建立克服挫折所需的恢复力和心理状态，因为斯多葛主义教授了如何将阻碍转变为机会。
A lesson every business entrepreneur needs to learn.
这是每个企业家都需要学习的一课。
I would argue that studying Stoicism is as relevant today as it was 2,000 years ago,thanks to its brilliant insights into how to lead a good life.
我想声明的是，由于斯多葛主义在如何过好生活方面的杰出见解，它在今天和在2000年前一样重要。
At the very root of the thinking, there is a very simple way of living - control what you can and accept what you can't.
从这种想法的根源去看，有一种非常简单的生活方式是——控制你能做的，接受你不能做的。
This is not as easy as it sounds and will require considerable practice - it can take a lifetime to master.
这并不像听起来那么容易，需要大量的练习——可能需要一辈子的时间才能掌握。
The Stoics also believed the most important foundation for a good and happy life is not money, fame, power or pleasure,
斯多葛学派还认为，美好幸福生活最重要的基础不是金钱、名誉、权力或快乐，
but having a disciplined and principled character - something which seems to resonate with many people today.
而是拥有自律和有原则的性格——这一点今天似乎能与许多人产生共鸣。

120230706094210.title: C18-Test 1-Part 1
transcript: Man: Excuse me. Would you mind if I asked you some questions? We’re doing a survey on transport.
对不起。你介意我问你一些问题吗？我们正在做一个关于交通的调查。
Sadie: Yes, that’s OK.
好的，没问题。
Man: First of all, can I take your name?
首先，我可以知道你的名字吗？
Sadie: Yes. It’s Sadie Jones.
可以。我叫萨迪-琼斯。
Man: Thanks very much. And could I have your date of birth – just the year will do, actually. Is that all right?
非常感谢。能否告诉我你的出生日期--其实只需说出年份就可以了。可以吗？
Sadie: Yes, that’s fine. It’s 1991.
Man: So next your postcode, please.
那么接下来是你的邮编，谢谢。
Sadie: It’s DW30 7YZ.
DW30 7YZ。
Man: Great. Thanks. Is that in Wells?
很好。谢谢。这是在威尔斯吗？
Sadie: No it’s actually in Harborne – Wells isn’t far from there, though.
不，实际上是在哈伯恩，不过威尔斯离那里不远。
Man: I really like that area. My grandmother lived there when I was a kid.
我真的很喜欢那个地区。我小时候，我的祖母就住在那里。
Sadie: Yes, it is nice.
是的，那里很好。
Man: Right, so now I want to ask you some questions about how you travelled here today. Did you use public transport?
对了，现在我想问你一些问题，你今天是怎么来的。你是否使用了公共交通工具？
Sadie: Yes. I came by bus.
是的。我是坐公交车来的。
Man: OK. And that was today. It’s the 24th of April, isn’t it?
好的。那是今天。今天是4月24日，对吗？
Sadie: Isn’t it the 25th? No, actually, you’re right.
今天不是25号吗？不，实际上，你是对的。
Man: And what was the reason for your trip today? I can see you’ve got some shopping with you.
那你今天旅行的原因是什么？我看得出你拿了一些购物的东西。
Sadie: Yes. I did some shopping but the main reason I came here was to go to the dentist.
是的。我买了一些东西，但我来这里的主要原因是去看牙医。
Man: That’s not much fun. Hope it was nothing serious.
这可不好玩。希望不是很严重。
Sadie: No, it was just a check-up. It’s fine.
不，这只是一次检查。没事的。
Man: Good. Do you normally travel by bus into the city centre?
很好。你通常乘坐公共汽车到市中心吗？
Sadie: Yes. I stopped driving in ages ago because parking was so difficult to find and it costs so much.
是的。我很久以前就不开车来了，因为停车位太难找了，而且费用太高。
Man: I see.
我明白了。
Sadie: The bus is much more convenient too. It only takes about 30 minutes.
公交车也方便多了。只需要大约30分钟。
Man: That’s good. So where did you start your journey?
那很好。那么，你在哪里开始出发的？
Sadie: At the bus stop on Claxby Street.
在克莱斯比街的公交车站。
Man: Is that C-L-A–X-B-Y?
那是C-L-A-X-B-Y吗？
Sadie: That's right.
是的。
Man: And how satisfied with the service are you? Do you have any complaints?
那你对服务的满意度如何？你有什么抱怨吗？
Sadie: Well, as I said, it’s very convenient and quick when it’s on time, but this morning it was late. Only about 10 minutes, but still.
嗯，正如我所说的，当它准时到达时，它非常方便和快捷，但今天早上它迟到了。只有大约10分钟，但确实迟到了。
Man: Yes, I understand that’s annoying. And what about the timetable? Do you have any comments about that?
是的，我明白这很烦人。那时间表呢？你对此有什么意见吗？

Sadie: Mmm. I suppose I mainly use the bus during the day, but any time I’ve been in town in the evening – for dinner or at the cinema – I’ve noticed you have to wait a long time for a bus – there aren’t that many.
嗯。我想我主要是在白天使用公交车，但任何时候我晚上在城里--吃饭或看电影--我都注意到要等很长时间才能找到一辆公交车--没有那么多车。
Man: OK, thanks. So now I’d like to ask you about your car use.
好的，谢谢。那么现在我想问问你的用车情况。
Sadie: Well, I have got a car but I don’t use it that often. Mainly just to go to the supermarket. But that’s about it really. My husband uses it at the weekends to go to the golf club.
嗯，我有一辆车，但我不经常使用。主要是为了去超市。但这差不多就这些了。我丈夫在周末开车去高尔夫俱乐部。
Man: And what about a bicycle?
那自行车呢？
Sadie: I don’t actually have one at the moment.
我现在还没有。
Man: What about the city bikes you can rent? Do you ever use those?
那那些可以租的城市自行车呢？你曾经使用过这些自行车吗？
Sadie: No – I’m not keen on cycling there because of all the pollution. But I would like to get a bike – it would be good to use it to get to work.
没有--我不太喜欢骑行，因为污染问题。但我想买一辆自行车--用它来上班会很好。
Man: So why haven’t you got one now?
那你为什么现在还没买呢？
Sadie: Well, I live in a flat – on the second floor and it doesn’t have any storage – so we’d have to leave it in the hall outside the flat.
我住在一个公寓里，在二楼，没有任何存储空间，所以我们必须把它放在公寓外面的大厅里。
Man: I see. OK. Well, I think that’s all ...
我明白了。好的。好吧，我想这就是全部......

120230706094243.title: C18-Test 1-Part 2
transcript: Good evening, everyone.
晚上好，各位。
Let me start by welcoming you all to this talk and thanking you for taking the time to consider joining ACE voluntary organisation.
首先，我欢迎大家参加这次讲座，并感谢大家花时间考虑加入ACE志愿组织。
ACE offers support to people and services in the local area and we’re now looking for more volunteers to help us do this.
ACE为当地的人们和服务提供支持，我们现在正在寻找更多的志愿者来帮助我们完成这项工作。
By the way, I hope you’re all comfortable – we have brought in extra seats so that no one has to stand, but it does mean that the people at the back of the room may be a bit squashed.
顺便说一下，我希望你们都能感到舒适--我们已经带来了额外的座位，这样就没有人需要站着，但这确实意味着房间后面的人可能会有点挤。
We’ll only be here for about half an hour so, hopefully, that’s OK.
我们只在这里待半个小时左右，所以，希望问题不大。
One of the first questions we’re often asked is how old you need to be to volunteer.
我们经常被问到的第一个问题是，成为志愿者对年龄有什么要求。
Well, you can be as young as 16 or you can be 60 or over; it all depends on what type of voluntary work you want to do.
嗯，小至16岁，大到60岁或以上都可以；这完全取决于你想做什么类型的志愿工作。
Other considerations, such as reliability, are crucial in voluntary work and age isn’t related to these, in our experience.
其他考虑因素，如可靠性，在志愿工作中是至关重要的，而根据我们的经验，年龄与这些无关。
Another question we get asked relates to training.
另外一个我们经常被问到的问题与培训有关。
Well, there’s plenty of that and it’s all face-to-face.
嗯，有很多这样的培训，而且都是面对面的。
What’s more, training doesn’t end when you start working for us – it takes place before, during and after periods of work.
更重要的是，培训不会在你开始为我们工作时结束--它发生在工作之前、期间和之后。
Often, it’s run by other experienced volunteers as managers tend to prefer to get on with other things.
通常情况下，培训是由其他有经验的志愿者进行的，因为管理人员往往有其他事情要做。
Now, I would ask you to consider a couple of important issues before you decide to apply for voluntary work.
现在，我想请你们在决定申请志愿工作之前考虑几个重要问题。
We don’t worry about why you want to be a volunteer – people have many different reasons that range from getting work experience to just doing something they’ve always wanted to do.
我们不过问您为什么想成为一名志愿者--人们有许多不同的原因，从获得工作经验到只是做他们一直想做的事情。
But it is critical that you have enough hours in the day for whatever role we agree is suitable for you – if being a volunteer becomes stressful then it’s best not to do it at all.
但关键是您每天有足够的时间从事我们认为适合您的任何角色--如果做志愿者变得有压力，那么最好就不要做了。
You may think that your income is important, but we don’t ask about that.
您可能认为您的收入很重要，但我们不问这个问题。
It’s up to you to decide if you can work without earning money.
您可以自行决定是否可以在不赚钱的情况下工作。
What we value is dedication.
我们看重的是奉献精神。
Some of our most loyal volunteers earn very little themselves but still give their full energy to the work they do with us.
我们一些最忠诚的志愿者自己的收入很少，但仍然在和我们的协作中全力以赴。
OK, so let’s take a look at some of the work areas that we need volunteers for and the sort of things that would help you in those.
好了，让我们来看看我们需要志愿者的一些工作领域，以及在这些领域对您有帮助的事情。
You may wish simply to help us raise money.
你可能只是希望帮助我们筹集资金。
If you have the creativity to come up with an imaginative or novel way of fundraising, we’d be delighted, as standing in the local streets or shops with a collection box can be rather boring!
如果你有创造力，能想出一个富有想象力或新颖的筹款方式，我们会很高兴，因为拿着募捐箱站在当地街道或商店里可能会相当无聊！
One outdoor activity that we need volunteers for is litter collection and for this it’s useful if you can walk for long periods, sometimes uphill.
我们需要志愿者的其中一项户外活动是收集垃圾，如果你能长时间步行，有时还需要上坡，那就很有帮助。
Some of our regular collectors are quite elderly, but very active and keen to protect the environment.
我们的一些稳定的收集者是相当年长的，但非常活跃，热衷于保护环境。
If you enjoy working with children, we have three vacancies for what are called ‘playmates’.
如果你喜欢和孩子们一起工作，我们有三个被称为 "玩伴 "的岗位空缺。
These volunteers help children learn about staying healthy through a range of out-of-school activities.
这些志愿者通过一系列的校外活动帮助儿童学习保持健康。
You don’t need to have children yourself, but it’s good if you know something about nutrition and can give clear instructions.
你不需要自己有孩子，但如果你了解一些关于营养的知识并能给予明确的指导，那就更好了。
If that doesn’t appeal to you, maybe you would be interested in helping out at our story club for disabled children, especially if you have done some acting.
如果这对你没有吸引力，也许你会有兴趣在我们的残疾儿童故事俱乐部帮忙，特别是如果你之前做过一些表演。
We put on three performances a year based on books they have read and we’re always looking for support with the theatrical side of this.
我们每年根据他们读过的书进行三场演出，我们一直在寻找对戏剧方面的支持。
The last area I’ll mention today is first aid. Volunteers who join this group can end up teaching others in vulnerable groups who may be at risk of injury.
我今天要提到的最后一个领域是急救。加入这个团体的志愿者最终可以在弱势群体中教导其他可能有受伤风险的人。
Initially, though, your priority will be to take in a lot of information and not forget any important steps or details.
不过，在最开始的阶段，你的首要任务是吸收大量的信息，不要忘记任何重要的步骤或细节。
Right, so does anyone have any questions …
好了，有没有人有什么问题......

120230706094315.title: C18-Test 1-Part 3
transcript: Hugo: Hi Chantal. What did you think of the talk, then?
嗨，Chantal。那你觉得这次座谈怎么样？
Chantal: Hi Hugo. I thought it was good once I'd moved seats.
你好，Hugo。我想换了座位会很好。
Hugo: Oh – were the people beside you chatting or something?
哦，你旁边的人是在聊天还是什么？
Chantal: It wasn't that. I went early so that I'd get a seat and not have to stand, but then this guy sat right in front of me and he was so tall!
不是的。我去得很早，这样我就能找到一个座位，不用站着。但后来有个人就坐在我前面，他很高！
Hugo: It's hard to see through people's heads, isn't it?
要越过别人的头顶看过去很难，不是吗？
Chantal: Impossible! Anyway, to answer your question, I thought it was really interesting, especially what the speaker said about the job market.
做不到。总之，回答你的问题，我认为这真的很有趣，尤其是演讲者对就业市场的看法。
Hugo: Me too. I mean we know we're going into a really competitive field so it's obvious that we may struggle to get work.
我也是。我的意思是，我们知道我们要进入一个竞争非常激烈的领域，所以很明显我们可能很难找到工作。
Chantal: That's right – and we know we can't all have that ‘dream job'.
是的，我们知道我们不可能都有 "理想工作"。
Hugo: Yeah, but it looks like there's a whole range of … areas of work that we hadn't even thought of – like fashion journalism, for instance.
是的，但是看起来有一系列的......我们甚至没有想到的工作领域--比如说时尚新闻。
Chantal: Yeah – I wasn't expecting so many career options.
是的--我没想到会有这么多职业选择。
Hugo: Mmm. Overall, she had quite a strong message, didn't she?
嗯。总的来说，她传递了相当震撼的信息，不是吗？
Chantal: She did. She kept saying things like ‘I know you all think this, but …' and then she'd tell us how it really is.
是的。她一直在说 "我知道你们都这么想，但是......"，然后她会告诉我们事情的真实情况。
Hugo: Perhaps she thinks students are a bit narrow-minded about the industry.
也许她认为学生对这个行业有点狭隘的看法。
Chantal: It was a bit harsh, though! We know it's a tough industry.
不过，她有点刻薄！我们知道这是一个艰难的行业。
Hugo: Yeah – and we're only first years, after all. We've got a lot to learn.
是的，而且我们只是一年级学生，还有很多要学习的。
Chantal: Exactly. Do you think our secondary-school education should have been more career-focused?
是呀。你觉得我们中学的教育应该更加注重职业发展吗？
Hugo: Well, we had numerous talks on careers, which was good, but none of them were very inspiring. They could have asked more people like today's speaker to talk to us.
嗯，我们确实有很多关于职业的讲座，这是好的，但是没有一个非常激励人心。他们本可以请更多像今天的演讲者这样的专家来和我们交流。
Chantal: I agree. We were told about lots of different careers – just when we needed to be, but not by the experts who really know stuff.
我同意。我们被告知了很多不同的职业，只是在我们需要的时候，但并不是由真正了解这些行业的专家来告诉我们。
Hugo: So did today's talk influence your thoughts on what career you'd like to take up in the future?
那今天的讲座对你的未来职业规划的想法有影响吗？
Chantal: Well, I promised myself that I'd go through this course and keep an open mind till the end.
嗯，我答应自己要完成这门课程，并持开放的心态到最后。
Hugo: But I think it's better to pick an area of the industry now and then aim to get better and better at it.
但我认为最好是现在选择一个行业领域，并努力变得越来越好。
Chantal: Well, I think we'll just have to differ on that issue!
嗯，我想在这个问题上我们的观点可能会有所不同！
Hugo: One thing's for certain, though. From what she said, we'll be unpaid assistants in the industry for quite a long time.
但有一点是确定的。根据她的说法，我们在这个行业里将会做很长时间的无薪助理工作。
Chantal: Mmm.
嗯。
Hugo: I'm prepared for that, aren't you?
我做好了准备，你呢？
Chantal: Actually, I'm not going to accept that view.
实际上，我不会接受那种情况。
Hugo: Really? But she knows it's the case – and everyone else says the same.
真的吗？但她知道这是事实，而且其他人也都这么说。
Chantal: That doesn't mean it has to be true for me.
那并不意味着对我来说也是真的。
Hugo: OK. Well – I hope you're right!
好吧，希望你是对的！
Chantal: I thought the speaker's account of her first job was fascinating.
我觉得演讲者讲述她的第一份工作很有趣。
Hugo: Yeah – she admitted she was lucky to get work being a personal dresser for a musician. She didn't even apply for the job and there she was getting paid to choose all his clothes.
是的 - 她承认自己能得到一个音乐家的私人服装师的工作是很幸运的。她甚至没有申请这份工作，但她却因此而得到了报酬。
Chantal: It must have felt amazing – though she said all she was looking for back then was experience, not financial reward.
那肯定感觉很棒 - 尽管她说当时她只是在积累经验，而不是财务回报。
Hugo: Mmm. And then he was so mean, telling her she was more interested in her own appearance than his!
嗯。然后他却很刻薄地告诉她，她更关注自己的外表而不是他的！
Chantal: But – she did realise he was right about that, which really made me think. I'm always considering my own clothes but now I can see you should be focusing on your client!
但是 - 她确实意识到他在这方面是对的，这让我真的思考了很多。我总是考虑自己的衣服，但现在我明白应该更关注客户！
Hugo: She obviously regretted losing the job.
显然她后悔失去了那份工作。
Chantal: Well, as she said, she should have hidden her negative feelings about him, but she didn't.
嗯，正如她所说的，她本应该隐藏自己对他的负面情绪，但她没有做到。
Hugo: It was really brave the way she picked herself up and took that job in retail. Fancy working in a shop after that!
她能振作起来并在零售业找到那份工作的方式真的很勇敢。在那之后去一家商店工作，真是有点奇怪！
Chantal: Yeah – well, she recommended we all do it at some point. I guess as a designer you'd get to find out some useful information, like how big or small the average shopper is.
是的 - 她建议我们都应该有这样的经历。我想作为设计师，你可以了解到一些有用的信息，比如一般购物者的体型大小。

Hugo: I think that's an issue for manufacturers, not designers. However, it would be useful to know if there's a gap in the market – you know, an item that no one's stocking but that consumers are looking for.
我认为那是制造商的问题，而不是设计师的问题。不过，知道市场上是否存在空白 - 也就是说，没有人销售但消费者需要的物品 - 这对我们来说是有用的。
Chantal: Yeah, people don't give up searching. They also take things back to the store if they aren't right.
是的，人们不会放弃寻找。如果他们买到的东西不合适，他们也会把它们退回商店。
Hugo: Yeah. Imagine you worked in an expensive shop and you found out the garments sold there were being returned because they … fell apart in the wash!
是的。想象一下，你在一家昂贵的商店工作，然后发现那里销售的衣服在洗涤过程中竟然散架了！
Chantal: Yeah, it would be good to know that kind of thing.
是的，了解那种情况会很有用。

120230706094347.title: C18-Test 1-Part 4
transcript: For my presentation today I want to tell you about how groups of elephants have been moved and settled in new reserves.
今天我要在我的演讲中告诉大家关于如何将一群大象转移到新的保护区并安顿下来。
This is known as translocation and has been carried out in Malawi in Africa in recent years.
这被称为迁移，近年来在非洲的马拉维进行了这项工作。
The reason this is being done is because of overpopulation of elephants in some areas.
之所以这样做，是因为一些地区大象的过度繁殖问题。
Overpopulation is a good problem to have and not one we tend to hear about very often.
过度繁殖是一个问题，而且我们很少听到这样的问题。
In Malawi’s Majete National Park the elephant population had been wiped out by poachers, who killed the elephants for their ivory.
在马拉维的马杰特国家公园，大象种群曾经被偷猎者彻底消灭，这些偷猎者为了象牙而杀害大象。
But in 2003, the park was restocked and effective law enforcement was introduced.
但在2003年，该公园得到了重新补充种群，有效的执法措施也得以引入。
Since then, not a single elephant has been poached.
从那时起，没再有大象被偷猎。
In this safe environment, the elephant population boomed.
在这个安全的环境中，大象种群迅速增长。
Breeding went so well that there were more elephants than the park could support.
繁殖非常成功，以至于公园里的大象数量超过了公园所能承载的上限。
This led to a number of problems.
这导致了一些问题。
Firstly, there was more competition for food, which meant that some elephants were suffering from hunger.
首先，食物的竞争加剧，这意味着一些大象面临饥饿。
As there was a limit to the amount of food in the national park, some elephants began looking further afield.
由于国家公园的食物供应有限，一些大象开始四处寻找食物。
Elephants were routinely knocking down fences around the park, which then had to be repaired at a significant cost.
大象经常撞倒围绕公园的围栏，这就不得不以巨大的成本进行修复。
To solve this problem, the decision was made to move dozens of elephants from Majete National Park to Nkhotakota Wildlife Park, where there were no elephants.
为了解决这个问题，决定将大量的大象从马杰特国家公园转移到恩科塔科塔野生动物公园，那里没有大象。
But, obviously, attempting to move significant numbers of elephants to a new home 300 kilometres away is quite a challenge.
但是，显然，试图将大量的大象迁移到距离300公里远的新家是一个相当大的挑战。
So how did this translocation process work in practice?
那么如何实现迁移呢？
Elephants were moved in groups of between eight and twenty, all belonging to one family.
大象以八到二十只为一组进行转移，它们都属于同一个家族。
Because relationships are very important to elephants, they all had to be moved at the same time.
由于群体关系对大象非常重要，它们必须同时转移。
A team of vets and park rangers flew over the park in helicopters and targeted a group, which were rounded up and directed to a designated open plain.
一队兽医和公园护林员乘坐直升机飞越公园，并选择一群大象，将它们围捕并引导到指定的开阔地。

The vets then used darts to immobilise the elephants – this was a tricky manoeuvre, as they not only had to select the right dose of tranquiliser for different-sized elephants but they had to dart the elephants as they were running around.
然后，兽医使用镇定剂注射针来使大象昏迷 - 这是一个棘手的操作，因为他们不仅需要选择不同体型大象的正确镇静剂剂量，还必须在大象奔跑时注射。
This also had to be done as quickly as possible so as to minimise the stress caused.
这也必须尽快完成，以尽量减少造成的压力。
As soon as the elephants began to flop onto the ground, the team moved in to take care of them.
当大象开始倒下时，团队立即靠近并照料它们。
To avoid the risk of suffocation, the team had to make sure none of the elephants were lying on their chests because their lungs could be crushed in this position.
为了避免窒息的风险，团队必须确保没有一只大象仰卧在胸部，因为这个姿势可能会压迫它们的肺部。
So all the elephants had to be placed on their sides.
所以所有的大象都必须侧躺。
One person stayed with each elephant while they waited for the vets to do checks.
每只大象都有一个人陪伴，等待兽医进行检查。
It was very important to keep an eye on their breathing – if there were fewer than six breaths per minute, the elephant would need urgent medical attention.
密切观察它们的呼吸非常重要 - 如果每分钟呼吸少于六次，大象将需要紧急医疗救治。
Collars were fitted to the matriarch in each group so their movements could be tracked in their new home.
在每个组的首领大象身上安装了颈圈，以便在它们的新家中跟踪它们的活动。
Measurements were taken of each elephant’s tusks – elephants with large tusks would be at greater risk from poachers – and also of their feet.
还测量了每只大象的象牙长度 - 长牙的大象更容易受到偷猎者的威胁 - 以及它们的脚的尺寸。
The elephants were then taken to a recovery area before being loaded onto trucks and transported to their new home.
然后将大象带到恢复区域，然后装载到卡车上，运输到它们的新家。
The elephants translocated to Nkhotakota settled in very well and the project has generally been accepted to have been a huge success – and not just for the elephants.
迁移到恩科塔科塔的大象适应得非常好，这个项目被普遍认为是一个巨大的成功 - 不仅仅是对大象来说。
Employment prospects have improved enormously, contributing to rising living standards for the whole community.
就业前景有了很大的改善，为整个社区的生活水平提供了贡献。
Poaching is no longer an issue, as former poachers are able to find more reliable sources of income.
偷猎不再是问题，因为以前的偷猎者能够找到更可靠的收入来源。
In fact, many of them volunteered to give up their weapons, as they were no longer of any use to them.
事实上，他们中的许多人自愿放弃了武器，因为武器对他们已经没有任何用处。
More than two dozen elephants have been born at Nkhotakota since relocation.
迁移后，恩科塔科塔已经诞生了二十多只大象。
With an area of more than 1,800 square kilometres, there’s plenty of space for the elephant population to continue to grow.
占地超过1,800平方公里，对于大象种群继续增长来说有足够的空间。
Their presence is also helping to rebalance Nkhotakota’s damaged ecosystem and providing a sustainable conservation model, which could be replicated in other parks.
它们的存在还有助于恢复恩科塔科塔受损的生态系统，并提供一个可持续的保护模式，这种模式可以在其他公园复制。
All this has been a big draw for tourism, which contributes five times more than the illegal wildlife trade to GDP, and this is mainly because of the elephants.
所有这些对旅游业来说是一个巨大的吸引力，对国内生产总值的贡献是非法野生动物贸易的五倍，而这主要是因为大象。
There’s also been a dramatic rise in interest …
对此也产生了极大的兴趣...

120240703142102.title: C19-Test 3-Part 1
transcript: Hi Shannon – how are you settling into your new flat?
嗨，Shannon-在新公寓住得怎么样？
Really well, thanks.
非常好，谢谢。
You look like you're going shopping.
你看起来像是要去购物。
Yes, I am. My cousins are coming to stay for a couple of days, and I have to cook for them.
是的，我要去。我的表亲要来住几天，我得给他们做饭。
Well, there are plenty of places to buy food in Kite Place – it's the area by the harbour.
港口边上的风筝广场有很多地方可以买吃的。
Oh, OK, I'll find that on the map. Thanks.
哦，好的，我会在地图上找到的。谢谢。
What sort of food do you need to get?
你需要买什么食物？
Well, neither of them eats meat but they both like fish.
他们都不吃肉，但都喜欢吃鱼。
Well, there's a really good fish market there.
那里有个很不错的鱼市。
Oh great – where is it exactly?
太好了，具体在哪儿？
It's at the far end of Kite Place, so you have to go over the bridge and then it's on the right.
在风筝广场的最里面，你得过桥，鱼市就在右边。
OK – is it open all day?
好的-鱼市全天营业吗？
It doesn't close until four, but I'd recommend going earlier than that – it does run out of some things.
4点才关门，但我建议早点去，晚去的话有些东西也许卖完了。
Oh, I don't want that to happen.
哦，我可不想那样。
As long as you get there by 3.30, you should be fine. It's only 11 now, so plenty of time.
只要你在 3 点半前到，应该没问题。现在才 11 点，时间很充裕。
Right.
好的。
Do you need to buy vegetables too?
你需要买蔬菜吗？
I do, and I want to avoid all the plastic packaging in the supermarket!
我需要，而且我想避免超市里用的塑料包装！
Well, there's a really nice organic shop there. Now what's it called . . . it's the name of a flower. I know, it's ‘Rose'.
嗯，那里有一家非常不错的有机商店。它叫什么名字？……是一种花的名字，我想起来了，叫“玫瑰”。
That's a nice name.
这名字不错。
Yeah – it sells vegetables and quite a lot of other stuff.
是的，它卖蔬菜和很多其他东西。
And where's that?
这家店在哪儿呢？
Well, as you reach the market, you'll see a big grey building on your left
嗯，当你到市场时，你会看到左手边有一幢灰色的大房子-
I think it used to be a warehouse. Anyway, now it's a restaurant upstairs, but the ground floor has two shops either side of the entrance and it's the one on the left—
我想它以前是个仓库。总之，现在楼上是一家餐馆，但一层入口两侧各有一家商店，左边那家就是。
That's easy enough.
应该很好找。
You can't miss it – there's also a big sign on the pavement so you can look for that.
你不会错过的-人行道上也有个大路标，你可以留心一下。
Fine! I guess if I need anything else, I'll have to go to the supermarket.
好的！我想如果我还需要什么，就得去超市了。
Yeah – you should be able to get everything you need, but there's a minibus that goes to the supermarket if you need it. It's purple and the number is 289.
是的，你应该可以买到所有你需要的东西，但那有一辆迷你巴士可以去超市。车是紫色的，号码是 289。
Thanks, that's great.
谢谢，太好了。
So what do you need to get at the fish market? The salmon is always very good and the shellfish.
那么你需要在鱼市上买什么呢？鲑鱼和贝类总是很好吃。
I'm going to make a curry, I think, and I need about 12 prawns for that.
我打算做咖喱，我想需要 12 只虾。
They'll have plenty of those.
鱼市那边有很多。
OK.
好的。
Have you ever tried samphire?
你吃过海蓬子吗？
No – what's that?
没有-那是什么？
It's a type of seaweed. I just ask for a handful and you fry it in butter. It's delicious!
是一种海草。只要买一把，然后用黄油煎。很好吃的！
Oh, I might try that – how do you spell it?
哦，我可以试试-怎么拼？
It's S-A-M-P-H-I-R-E.
是 S-A-M-P-H-I-R-E。
Great – it's always good to try something different.
太好了-尝试不同的东西总是好的。
Yeah.
是的。
I'll see what beans they have in the organic shop and I think I'll get something for dessert there.
我应该还会去看看有机商店里有什么豆子，我想我会在那买些做甜点的食材。
How about a mango?
芒果怎么样？
I'm not sure – they're not always ripe. I'd prefer a melon – it's bigger too.
我不确定-芒果不一定熟。我更倾向甜瓜-它也更大。
Good idea. The owner also sells a lot of spices there that you can put in a curry, and things like coconut.
好主意。店主还卖很多可以放进咖喱里的香料，还有椰子之类的东西。
Oh, that's very helpful. I'll have a look.
哦，这很有帮助。我会去看看的。
No problem.
没问题。
I know bread doesn't really go with curry but I always like to have some in case.
我知道面包和咖喱不太搭配，但我总喜欢准备一些以备不时之需。
As I said – all the bread is home-made and there's lots of variety. I like the brown bread myself.
如我所说，所有面包都是自制的，种类很多。我自己喜欢黑面包。
Mm, sounds good.
嗯，听起来不错。
They sell other things there too.
那里还卖其他东西。
Like cakes? I love chocolate cake.
比如蛋糕？我喜欢巧克力蛋糕。
Well – not that, but they have a whole range of tarts and the best are the strawberry ones.
嗯-不是那个，但他们有各种蛋挞，最好吃的是草莓蛋挞。
Perfect – hopefully I won't even have to go to the supermarket!
太好了-顺利的话我都不用去超市了！

120240703150347.title: C19-Test 3-Part 2
transcript: The children's book festival is coming up again soon and here to tell us all about it is the festival's organiser, Jenny Morgan. So tell us what we can expect this year, Jenny.
儿童图书节马上又要开始了，在这里向我们做介绍的是儿童图书节的组织者珍妮·摩根。珍妮，请告诉我们今年有什么令人期待的活动。
Well, as usual we've got five days of action-packed exciting events for children, with writers coming from all over the country getting involved.
好的，和往年一样，我们为孩子们准备了五天精彩纷呈的活动，全国各地的作家都将参与其中。
Just to give you an idea of what's on offer in the workshops.
让我们来了解一下工作坊的活动内容吧。
First of all, there's a very special event called Superheroes.
首先，我们将举办一个名为 "超级英雄"的特别活动。
This is a chance for deaf children to share their reading experiences with author Madeleine Gordon, who is herself hearing impaired.
这是一个让聋哑儿童同作家马德琳·戈登分享阅读经验的机会，她本人也有听力障碍。
‘Just do it’ is a practical workshop led by the well-known illustrator Mark Keane.
“想做就做”是一个由著名插画家马克·基恩主持的实践工作坊。
He'll take participants on a magical journey to faraway lands with an opportunity for aspiring actors to do some role play.
他将带领参与者踏上前往远方的魔幻之旅，并为有抱负的演员提供角色扮演的机会。
‘Count on me’ is an inspiring and entertaining look at the issues of friendship for 13–14-year-olds.
“依靠我”活动将寓教于乐，带领13至14岁的青少年对友谊问题进行探究。
It looks at some of the friendships described in popular books and asks participants to compare these with their own experiences.
活动将探讨一些流行书籍中描述的友谊，并要求参与者将其与自己的亲身经历做比较。
‘Speak up’ is part of a series of workshops on the subject of mental health.
“大声说出来”是心理健康主题系列工作坊的一部分。
This is a creative writing workshop encouraging children to describe situations where young people experience loneliness.
这是一个创意写作工作坊，鼓励孩子们描述青少年经历孤独的情境。
A recent survey revealed that children can be lonely even when they're at home with their families.
最近的一项调查显示，即使孩子们和家人在一起，他们也会感到孤独。
‘Jump for joy’, as many of you will know, is the heart-warming, best-selling story by Nina Karan about a young girl's trip to visit her relatives in India.
“欢呼雀跃”，很多人都知道，尼娜·卡兰的畅销书《欢呼雀跃》讲述了一个小女孩去印度探亲的感人故事。
It recently received the gold medal at the Waterford Awards. Nina will get children to celebrate the word ‘joy' by writing a poem.
该书最近获得了沃特福德奖的金奖。尼娜将让孩子们写一首诗来赞美“欢乐”这个词。
‘Sticks and stones’is the beautifully illustrated picture book for young readers about a community who organise an African-Caribbean festival to help local children learn about their Jamaican roots.
《棍棒和石头》是一本针对小读者的图文并茂的图画书，讲述了一个社区组织了一个非洲-加勒比文化节来帮助当地儿童了解他们的牙买加血统的故事。
This will be a musical event where children will have the chance to play steel drums. This is bound to be very popular, so please book as soon as possible.
这个文化节也将是一次音乐盛会，孩子们将有机会演奏钢鼓。这本书一定会非常受欢迎，请尽快预订。
Thanks Jenny.That all sounds really interesting. I'm just wondering if you have a favourite book you could recommend for our readers?
谢谢珍妮。这一切听起来真的很有趣。我想知道您有没有一本最喜欢的书可以推荐给我们的读者？
It's hard to choose, but Alive and Kicking is definitely worth mentioning.
很难选择，但《活力四射》绝对值得一提。
You won't have heard of the writer as it's her first book – which is really impressive.
你可能没听说过这位作家，因为这是她的第一本书，一本令人印象深刻的书。
It's basically the teenage diary of a boy from Somalia who comes to live in the UK.
这本书记述了一个从索马里来到英国生活的少年的日记。
It deals with the serious issue of immigration and all the challenges the boy has to face at school and with the language barrier, etc.
这本书涉及移民问题这一严肃话题，以及男孩在学校适应和语言障碍等方面必须面对的挑战。
Usually, books like this are quite sad, but this one actually made me cry with laughter.
一般来说，这样的书都比较伤感，但这本书却让我笑出眼泪。
On each page, there are simple but hilarious black and white stick drawings of the boy with his friends and teachers.
在每一页上，都有描绘小男孩与朋友及老师相处过程的简洁有趣的黑白简笔画。
At the end of each diary entry, there are new English words the boy learns each day, which may help develop some children's vocabulary.
在每篇日记的末尾会有男孩每天学习的新英语单词，这可能有助于扩展一些孩子的词汇量。
I think my kids would enjoy that. What about any advice for parents on how to encourage their children to read more?
我想我的孩子会喜欢的。对于如何鼓励孩子多读书，您对家长有什么建议呢？
Well, this is something I get asked about a lot.
我经常被问到这件事。
There are so many distractions for kids these days that it can be hard to find time for reading.
如今，孩子们有太多的事情要分心，很难抽出时间来阅读。
One thing I'd say is to make time to sit down with your child and share books with them.
我想说的一件事是，家长应该抽出时间和孩子坐在一起，与他们分享书籍。
A lot of parents give up reading aloud to their children as soon as they learn to read independently, but this is a mistake.
很多家长在孩子学会独立阅读后就不再朗读，其实这是错误的。
It's good to read more advanced books to them as it helps to develop their vocabulary.
为他们朗读更高级的书籍很有好处，因为这有助于扩展他们的词汇量。
If you don't have time for this, then let them listen to audio books. Often, they'll want to read books they've listened to for themselves.
如果您没有时间，可以让他们听有声读物。通常，他们会想自己阅读已经听过的书。
I think it's a good idea to make a mental note of the type of books your child is reading – often they just read the same genre all the time, which can get a bit boring.
我认为，最好在头脑中记下您孩子正在阅读的书籍类型-他们往往总是阅读同一类型的书籍，这可能会让他们感到有些乏味。
You can introduce new authors and genres to them. Librarians should be able to help you with this.
家长可以向孩子们推荐新的作者和书籍类型。图书管理员应该能在这方面帮到家长们。
Well Jenny, I think that's really useful......
珍妮，我觉得这些建议真的很有用……

120240703175301.title: C19-Test 3-Part 4
transcript: In today's lecture, I'm going to be talking about microplastics.
在今天的讲座中，我将谈谈微塑料。
Microplastics are tiny pieces of plastic smaller than five millimetres in size.
微塑料是尺寸小于五毫米的塑料碎片。
Recently there's been a greater awareness that there are large quantities of plastic waste – big and small – in the environment.
最近，人们越来越意识到，环境中存在着大量大小不等的塑料垃圾。
The amount of plastic waste in the oceans has received widespread attention, but far less is known about the effects of microplastics in freshwater and particularly in soil.
海洋中的塑料垃圾数量已受到广泛关注，但人们对淡水中，尤其是土壤中微塑料的影响却知之甚少。
Microplastics can enter the environment via a number of different sources.
微塑料可通过多种不同来源进入大自然。
Threads and microfibres detach from synthetic clothing every time they're put in a washing machine, and these find their way into the water system.
每次将合成纤维衣物放入洗衣机中清洗，丝线和微纤维会从合成纤维衣物上脱落并进入水系统。
Other sources include big pieces of plastic waste that are already in the environment, and these break down into microscopic particles over a period of time.
其他来源包括已经存在于自然中的大块塑料废弃物，这些废弃物在一段时间后会分解成微小颗粒。
On a larger scale, factory waste is another route, as are tyres which wear down as cars, lorries and so on travel along road surfaces.
在更大范围内，工厂废料是另一个来源，汽车、卡车等在路面上行驶时受到磨损的轮胎也是一个来源。
We already understand some of the impacts of microplastics from studies involving fish and other animals.
通过对鱼类和其他动物的研究，我们已经了解了微塑料的一些影响。

There is evidence that microplastics harm small creatures in a variety of ways, such as by damaging their mouths, or by impairing their ability to feed, for example when microplastics get lodged in their digestive system.
有证据表明，微塑料会以各种方式对小型生物造成伤害，例如损坏它们的口腔，或损害它们的进食能力，如当微塑料卡在它们的消化系统中时。

Surprisingly perhaps, it is likely that humans consume microplastics, as these have been detected in a wide range of food and drink products, including bottled water, as well as in water that comes direct from the tap.
令人吃惊的是，人类很可能也食用微塑料，因为在各种食物和饮品（包括瓶装水和自来水）中都检测到了微塑料。
What's more, salt and many kinds of seafood have also been found to contain microplastics.
此外，盐和多种海产品也被发现含有微塑料。
However, it's important to underline that there is not yet conclusive proof that microplastics cause significant harm to people.
不过，必须强调的是，目前还没有确凿证据证明微塑料会对人体造成重大伤害。
In many countries, including here in the UK, there is legislation which prevents manufacturers from adding plastic microbeads to shower gels, facial cleansers and toothpaste.
包括英国在内的许多国家都有法律禁止制造商在沐浴露、洁面乳和牙膏中添加塑料微珠。

It is very difficult to accurately estimate the total amount of microplastic particles in the soil as they can be hard to detect, but we do know they are carried in the air and deposited in the soil by rain.
要准确估计土壤中的微塑料颗粒总量非常困难，因为它们很难被检测到，但我们确实知道这些颗粒被空气携带，并通过降雨沉积在土壤中。
What's more, many of the fertilisers used by both farmers and gardeners contain microplastics.
此外，农民和园丁使用的许多肥料都含有微塑料。
A team from the Anglia Ruskin University in Cambridge has carried out a study of the effects of microplastics on the digestive tracts of earthworms.
剑桥安格利亚鲁斯金大学的一个研究小组对微塑料对蚯蚓消化道的影响进行了研究。
These worms, which live in topsoil, are an essential component of our agricultural system.
这些生活在表土中的蚯蚓是我们农业系统的重要组成部分。
By feeding on soil, they mix nutrients into it, thereby making it more fertile.
它们以土壤为食，将养分混合到土壤中，从而使土壤更加肥沃。

The researchers set out to discover whether the introduction of microplastics into the soil– and the subsequent ingestion of these by earthworms – would impact soil quality and ultimately inhibit plant growth.
研究人员开始研究将微塑料引入土壤——以及蚯蚓随后摄入这些微塑料——是否会影响土壤质量并最终抑制植物生长。
The short answer was, yes, it did.
简单来说：会。
After placing three different types of microplastic particles into the soil, they planted perennial rye grass.
将三种不同类型的微塑料颗粒放入土壤后，他们种植了多年生黑麦草。
The particles of microplastic, which included biodegradable PLA and conventional high-density polyethylene, or HDPE, were then ingested by the earthworms in the soil.
这些包括可生物降解的聚乳酸 (PLA) 和传统的高密度聚乙烯 (HDPE) 微塑料颗粒，随后被土壤中的蚯蚓摄入。
The result was that the worms lost weight rapidly.
结果，蚯蚓的体重迅速下降。

What's more, a lower percentage than normal of the rye grass seeds germinated, and the researchers concluded that this was a direct result of the earthworms being unable to fulfil their normal role in making soil more fertile.
此外，黑麦草种子的发芽率也低于正常水平。研究人员得出结论，这是蚯蚓无法发挥其正常作用让土壤变得更加肥沃所导致的结果。
The team also discovered that there was an increase in the amount of acid found in the soil, and this was attributed mainly to the microplastic particles from conventional HDPE plastic.
研究小组还发现，土壤中的酸含量有所增加，这主要归因于传统高密度聚乙烯塑料中的微塑料颗粒。
The conclusions of the study make for very interesting reading – I've included the reference in the notes to give you at the end of this session.
这项研究的结论是非常有趣，我在本节课结束会给大家的笔记中附上了参考文献。
To summarise, the authors proposed the idea that we need to regard soil as we would regard any other process in nature.
概括一下，作者提出的观点是，我们需要像看待自然界中的任何其他过程一样看待土壤。
This means we should accept the implications of soil being dependent on decaying and dead matter constantly being passed through the bodies of earthworms.
这意味着我们应该接受“土壤的肥沃依赖于蚯蚓体内不断传递的腐烂和死亡物质”这一自然现象将会带来的影响。
That is, when soil becomes impoverished by the presence of microplastics, not only ecosystems but also the whole of society are negatively impacted.
也就是说，当土壤因为微塑料的存在而变得贫瘠时，不仅生态系统，整个社会都会受到负面影响。

120240710111912.title: C19-Test 3-Part 3
transcript: Hi Jake. How are you getting on with the practical teaching?
嗨，杰克。你的实践教学进展如何？
It's harder than I expected, but I've got some great classes. How about you?
比我预想的要难，但我上了几堂很棒的课。你怎么样？
Not brilliant. I'm really struggling with my Year 12 science class.
算不上好。12 年级的科学课上得很吃力。
Are they hard to control?
他们很不听话吗？
Well, I don't have discipline problems as such. It's just that they don't seem to think that science has anything to do with their lives. It's depressing.
嗯，我没有这方面的纪律问题。只是他们似乎不认为科学与他们的生活有任何关系。这让我很郁闷。
They listen to what I say, and I gave them a test last week and the results weren't too bad, but there's no real engagement.
他们听我讲课，然后我上周给他们做了个测试，结果还不错，但他们没有真正参与进来。
Right.
好吧。
And as part of my teaching practice, I have to design an experiment for them to do.
作为教学实践的一部分，我必须设计一个实验让他们做。
I was wondering about something on the children's diets . . . you know, asking them to record what they eat and maybe linking it to their state of health.
我在想关于孩子们饮食的问题……你知道的，让他们记录他们吃了什么，并将其与他们的健康状况联系起来。
Mmm. Let's think. So your methodology would involve the children recording what they eat.
嗯。让我们想想。所以你的方法是让孩子们记录他们吃了什么。
OK, but you'd also need to have access to the children's medical records and I don't think people would be happy about that; confidentiality would be an issue.
好的，但你还需要获得儿童的医疗记录，我想人们不会乐意这样做；保密会是个问题。
If you could get the right data, the conclusions might be significant, but I suspect it's not going to be easy.
如果你能得到准确的数据，结论可能会很有意义，但我担心这并不容易。
Right.
是的。
Have you thought about doing an experiment using animals?
你想过用动物做实验吗？
Wouldn't that be upsetting for the children?
那不会让孩子们感到不安吗？
Well, the animals don't have to be harmed in any way. It could just be an experiment where they're given a certain diet and the effects are observed.
嗯，动物不必受到任何伤害。可以只是给它们吃某种食物，然后观察其效果。
Would I have to get permission to use animals?
使用动物需要得到许可吗？
Yes, you'd have to submit an outline of the experiment and fill in a form, but it's quite straightforward.
是的，你必须提交一份实验大纲并填写一份表格，但这很简单。
But if we found out that, say, a particular diet affects the health of animals, the same thing wouldn't necessarily be true for people, would it?
但是，如果我们发现，比如说某种饮食会影响动物的健康，这在人身上却不一定适用，对吗？
No that's true, but the findings for any experiment are going to be limited. It's inevitable.
确实如此，但任何实验的结果都是有限制的。这是不可避免的。
I suppose so. So what animals could I use to investigate the effects of diet? Mice?
我想也是。那我可以用什么动物来研究饮食的影响呢？小白鼠？
Yes. You'd need experimental mice – ones that have been specially bred for experiments.
是的。你需要实验小鼠：专门为实验培育的小白鼠。
OK, so what will your experiment be investigating exactly?
好的，那你实验的具体内容是什么呢？
Well, something to do with nutrition. So maybe we could look at food supplements . . . things like extra iron and extra protein, and their impact on health.
嗯，跟营养有关。也许我们可以研究食物补充剂......比如额外的铁和额外的蛋白质，以及它们对健康的影响。
Mmm. That might be rather broad. Maybe just look at the effects of one supplement, like sugar, on the health of the mice?
嗯。这可能比较宽泛。也许可以只研究一种补充剂，比如说糖，对小白鼠健康的影响？
In fact, maybe the focus could be on whether mice can control their own diet. . . .
事实上，也许可以把重点放在小鼠能否控制自己的饮食上……
So, what happens when they have access to more sugar, that they don't really need?
那么，当小白鼠获得多于它们真正需要的糖时，会发生什么？
Exactly. Do they eat it or do they decide to leave it?
没错。它们会吃掉那些糖，还是决定不吃？
Great. Then later on, you could do a follow-up experiment adding another variable.
很好。之后你可以做一个后续实验，增加另一个变量。
Like, you could give some of the mice the chance to be more active, running on a wheel or something, and the others just sit around and don't do much.
比如，你可以给一些小白鼠活动的机会，让它们在轮子上跑什么的，而其他的小白鼠只是坐着，什么也不做。
Or I could repeat the experiment but change the type of food I provided . . . or use mice with a different genetic structure.
或者我可以重复这个实验，但改变我提供的食物类型……或者使用不同基因结构的小白鼠。
But I think your idea would be more interesting, I might think about that some more.
但我觉得你的想法更有趣，我可以再考虑一下。
So can I talk through a possible procedure for the experiment where mice are given a sugar supplement?
那么，我能谈谈给小白鼠糖分补充剂这一实验的可行步骤吗？
Sure. I did a similar experiment in college actually.
当然可以，其实我在大学时也做过类似的实验。
Great. So how many mice would I need?
很好。我需要多少只小鼠？
I'd say about 12. And all young ones, not a mixture of old and young.
大约 12 只。而且都是幼鼠，不能老幼混合。
OK. And I'd need two groups of equal sizes, so six in each group. And how would I tell them apart?
好的。我需要两组同样大小的老鼠，每组六只。我怎么区分它们呢？
I suppose I could put some sort of tag on one group . . . or just mark them in some way?
我想我可以给其中一组贴上某种标签......或者用某种方法标记它们？
You could use food colouring, that wouldn't hurt them.
你可以用食用色素，这不会伤害它们。
Perfect. Then each group would go into a separate cage, and one group, let's call them group A, would be the control group.
好极了。然后，每组将被关进一个单独的笼子里，其中一组，我们称之为 A 组，作为对照组。
So they'd just have ordinary mouse food. I suppose you can buy that?
它们只吃普通的食物，我想你能买到这种食物吧？
Yes, it comes in dry pellets.
是的，有干球状的。
And the other group would have the same as the first group, but they'd also have the extra sugar.
另一组的食物和第一组一样，但它们会有糖分补充剂。
Would you just give them straight sugar?
你会直接给它们糖吗？
It might be better to give them something like cereal with it.
给它们麦片之类的东西可能更好。
Mmm. Then you'd need to weigh the mice, I should think once a week. And you'd need an electronic balance.
嗯。那你就得给小白鼠称体重，我觉得应该一周一次。你还需要一个电子天平。
But we can't hold them on the balance, or it'd affect the reading.
但我们不能把它们放在天平上，否则会影响读数。
Exactly. So you need something called a weighing chamber to stop the mice from running away.
没错。所以你需要一个称重室来防止老鼠跑走。
It sounds complicated, but actually you can just use a plastic box with holes in the top.
听起来很复杂，但其实只要用一个顶部有孔的塑料盒就可以了。

OK. So once we've measured the weight gain of each mouse we can work out the average for each group, as well as the standard deviation. And then see where we go from there. That sounds cool, I think the students will enjoy it.
好的。一旦我们测量了每只小鼠的体重增长，就可以算出每组的平均值和标准偏差。然后再看看我们该怎么做。这听起来很酷，我想学生们会喜欢的。
Yes. One thing you must remember . . .
是的。有一件事你要记住……

120250702135720.title: C20-Test 1-Part 1
transcript: I've been meaning to ask you for some advice about restaurants－I need to book somewhere to celebrate my sister's 30th birthday and l liked the sound of that place you went for your mum's 50th.
我一直想问问你餐厅推荐的事——我需要订个地方给我姐姐庆祝30岁生日，听你说你去过的那家店（给你妈妈办50岁生日的地方）感觉挺不错的。
The Junction? Yeah, I'd definitely recommend that for a special occasion.
The Junction吗？是的，如果是特殊场合，我肯定会推荐那里。
We had a great time there.  Everyone really enjoyed it.
我们在那里度过了一段美好的时光。每个人都真的很享受。
Where is it again? l can't remember!
在哪儿来着?我记不清了。
It's on Greyson Street, only about a two minute walk from the station.
在格雷森街，从车站走过去大约两分钟。
Oh, that's good.  I'd prefer not to have to drive anywhere.
哦，那太好了。我可不想开车去任何地方。
But l don't want to have to walk too far either.
但我也不想走太远。
Yes, the location's perfect but that's not necessarily why I'd recommend it.
是的，地理位置很完美，但这不一定是我推荐它的原因。
The food's amazing.  lf you like fish it's probably the best restaurant in town for that.
