食物棒极了。如果你喜欢吃鱼，这里可能是城里最好的餐厅。
It's always really fresh and there are lots of interesting dishes to choose from.
它总是非常新鲜，而且有很多有趣的菜可以选择。
But all the food is good there.
但那里的食物都很好。
ls it really expensive?
真的很贵吗?
It's certainly not cheap but for a special occasion l think it's fine.
当然不便宜，但对于特殊场合来说，我觉得还不惜。
It's got a great atmosphere and before dinner you can go up on the roof and have a drink - it's really nice up there but you need to book.
那里气氛很棒，晚餐前你可以上楼顶喝一杯 -- 那里真的很棒，但你需要预订。
It's very popular as the views are spectacular.
它非常受欢迎，因为景色壮观。
Sounds good.  So that's definitely a possibility then.
听起来不错。那么，这确实是一种可能性。
ls there anywhere else you can think of?
你还能想到别的地方吗?
lf you want somewhere a bit less formal then you could try Paloma.
如果你想找个不那么正式的地方，可以试试帕洛玛，
Where's that? l haven't heard of it.
在哪儿?我没听说过。
No, it's quite new.  It's only been open a few months but it's got a great reputation already.
那家是新开的。虽然才开业几个月，但已经有不错的口碑了。
It's in a really beautiful old building on Bow Street.
它在波街上一栋非常漂亮的老建筑里。
Oh, I think l know where you mean - right beside the cinema?
哦，我想我知道你说的是哪里--就在电影院旁边吗?
Yes, that's it.  I've only been there a couple of times but l was really impressed.
是的，就是那里。我只去过那里几次，但真的印象深刻。
The chef used to work at Don Felipe's, apparently.
显然，那个厨师以前在费利佩先生那里工作过。
I was really sorry when that closed down.
那家店关门的时候我真的很难过。
So is all the food they serve Spanish, then?
那他们提供的所有食物都是西班牙风味的吗?
Yeah.  You can get lots of small dishes to share - which always works really well if you're in a group.
是啊。这里有很多小份的菜可以分享——如果是多人聚餐，这样安排特别合适。
Mmm.  Worth thinking about.
嗯，值得考虑。
Yeah.  There's a lively atmosphere and the waiters are really friendly.
是的，那里气氛很活跃，服务员也非常友好。
The only thing is that you need to pay a £50 deposit to book a table.
唯一的问题是，你需要支付 50 英镑的订金才能预订一张桌子。
A lot of restaurants are doing that these days.
现在很多餐厅都在这么做。
I should have a look at the menu to check there's a good choice of vegetarian dishes.
我应该看看菜单，看看有没有不错的素食菜单。
A couple of my friends have stopped eating meat.
我有几个朋友已经不吃肉了。
Not sure.  I'd say the selection of those would be quite limited.
不确定。我觉得那些菜的选择会相当有限。
I've just thought of another idea.
我刚刚想到了另一个主意。
Have you been to The Audley?
你去过奥德利吗?
No, don't think I've heard of it.  How's it spelt?
不，我想我没听说过。它是怎么拼写的?
A-U-D-L-E-Y.  You must have heard of it - there's been a lot about it in the press.
A-U-D-L-E-Y。你一定听说过，报纸上有很多关于它的报道。
l don't tend to pay much attention to that kind of thing.  So where is it exactly?
我一般不太关注这种事，那么，它具体在哪里？
It's in that hotel near Baxter Bridge - on the top floor.
在巴克斯特桥附近的那间酒店 -- 顶层。
Oh, the view would be incredible from up there.
哦，从那里看到的最色一定很棒。
Yeah.  I'd love to go.
是的，我很想去。
I can't think of the chef's name but she was a judge on that TV cookery show recently.
我想不起那个厨师的名字了，不过她最近是那个电视烹饪节目的评委。
And she's written a couple of cookery books.
她还写了几本烹饪书。
Oh, Angela Frayn.
哦，安吉拉·弗雷思。
That's the one.  Anyway, it's had excellent reviews from all the newspapers.
就是那个，不管怎样，所有报纸都给了很好的评价。
That would be a memorable place for a celebration.
那将是一个值得纪念的庆祝场所。
Definitely.  Obviously it's worth going there just for the view but the food is supposed to be really special.
确实如此。单是为了欣赏风景也值得专程前往，不过那里的美食才堪称一绝。
She only likes cooking with local products, doesn't she?
她只喜欢用本地食材做饭，对吧?
Yes.  Everything at the restaurant has to be sourced within a short distance and absolutely nothing flown in from abroad.
是的。餐厅的所有食材都必须在短距离内采购，绝对不用国外空运进口的原料。
l imagine it's really expensive though.
不过我想那一定很贵吧。
Well, you could go for the set lunch.
好吧，你可以去吃午餐的套餐。
That's quite reasonable for a top class restaurant.  £30 a head.
对于一家顶级餐厅来说，这个价格相当合理: 每人 30英镑。
In the evening l think it'd be more like ￡50.
晚上的话，我觉得大概要 50 英镑。
At least that, l should think.  But I'm sure everyone would enjoy it.
至少我是这么认为的，但我相信每个人都会喜欢的。
It's not the kind of place you leave feeling hungry though, is it? With tiny portions?
不会吃不饱的，分量也不会太小，是吧？
No, the reviews I've read didn't mention that.  l imagine they'd be average.
不，我看过的评论都没提过这点。我想应该是中等分量吧。
Well, that's all great, thanks.
嗯，那太好了，谢谢。

120250702141210.title: C20-Test 1-Part 2
transcript: Hello and welcome. My name's Heather McCallum and I'm one of the potters who work here at Edelman Pottery.
你好，欢迎光临。我叫希瑟·麦卡勒姆，是这里爱德曼陶艺工作室的陶艺师之一。
Before we go into the workshop, I just want to say a bit about the craft of pottery.
在我们进入工作室之前，我想先简单介绍一下陶艺这门手工艺。
Then we'll have a look at the equipment, and you can try making a pot of your own.
然后我们会看看设备，你们可以尝试自己制作一个陶罐。
Like many people, I'm sure you know that pottery as an art form is tens of thousands of years old, and we know this because it stands the test of time.
像很多人一样，我相信你们知道陶艺作为一种艺术形式已经有数万年的历史了，我们知道这一点是因为它经得起时间的考验。
Things like baskets and pictures don't survive in the earth in the same way that pots do.
篮子和画像这样的东西在土壤中无法像陶罐那样保存下来。
And even if ancient pots are found in small pieces, they still provide a lot of information about the past.
即使古代的陶罐被发现时是碎片，它们仍然能提供很多关于过去的信息。
There's no doubt that pottery has given archaeologists a fascinating insight into how ancient humans lived.
毫无疑问，陶艺为考古学家提供了关于古代人类生活的令人着迷的洞察。

The shape of an artefact may have been lost, but archaeologists can tell whether the pots were for, say, storage or cooking by examining the impressions on the clay, the scratches from tools; and the clay itself can reveal where the pots came from.
尽管文物的形状可能已经不复存在，但考古学家可以通过检查陶土上的印痕、工具留下的划痕来判断这些陶罐是用于储存还是烹饪；而陶土本身可以揭示陶罐的产地。
When I ask people why they want to take a pottery class with me, they sometimes talk about these things.
当我问人们为什么想参加我的陶艺课时，他们有时会谈到这些事情。

Like our ancestors, they hope that something they create will also last longer than they do, that their work – whether it is good or not – might say something about humanity many years after their death.
像我们的祖先一样，他们希望自己创造的东西也能比他们存在的更久，他们的作品——无论好坏——在他们去世多年后可能会对人类有某些启示。
Of course, you will all have your own reasons for coming here.
当然，你们每个人都有自己来这里的理由。
As far as I'm concerned, what I love most is the concentration you need to make a good pot. That focus takes you away from the stresses of everyday life.
对我来说，我最喜欢的是制作一个好陶罐时的专注。这种专注能让你远离日常生活的压力。
If you're elderly, it's also good exercise for hands and wrists and helps with arthritis and, of course, it's a fun activity for children because it's so messy!
如果你年纪较大，这也是锻炼手部和手腕的好运动，有助于缓解关节炎，当然，对孩子们来说这也是一项有趣的活动，因为会弄得很脏！
Here at Edelman Pottery, we show you some of the basic pottery techniques so that you can use these to create whatever you wish – a gift for a friend, perhaps.
在爱德曼陶艺工作室，我们会向你展示一些基本的陶艺技巧，这样你可以用这些技巧创造任何你想要的东西——也许是送给朋友的礼物。
Like nearly everyone who comes here, I'm sure this is the first time you will have tried the art, so we'll keep things simple today.
像几乎所有来这里的人一样，我相信这是你们第一次尝试这门艺术，所以今天难度不会太高。
Now, before we move on, can I just say a word about what you're wearing.
当然，在我们继续之前，我可以先说几句关于各位穿着的话吗？
As we said in our email, please remove any watches, necklaces, etc. and put them somewhere safe.
正如在邮件中所说，请摘下任何手表、项链等，并将它们放在安全的地方。
If you have long hair, do tie it back now. We'll provide aprons later, but I trust your clothes are old but comfortable – not your favourite T-shirt or jeans!
如果你头发长，请现在扎起来。我们稍后会提供围裙，但我相信大家穿的是旧且舒适的衣服——而不是你最喜欢的T恤或牛仔裤！
So now we're in the workshop. Have a look around – there's a lot going on.
现在我们在工作室里。你们可以四周看看——有很多活动在进行。
To make pottery that will last, you need a potter's wheel, a kiln – which is basically a very hot oven where you fire the pottery – and some tools.
要制作持久的陶器，你需要一个陶轮、一个窑——基本上是一个非常热的烤箱，用来烧制陶器——以及一些工具。
So first, the kiln. If you look over in the far corner, you'll see one of ours.
首先是窑。如果你看远处的角落，你会看到我们的一台窑。
Since their invention, kilns have changed very little, though in the past 20 years, a lot of progress has been made in temperature control.
自从窑的发明以来，它们几乎没有变化，尽管在过去的20年里，温度控制方面取得了很大进展。
Basically, a kiln removes the water from clay at temperatures of around a thousand degrees Celsius. This allows anything you've made to set permanently in shape.
基本上，窑在大约一千摄氏度的温度下去除陶土中的水分。这使得你制作的任何东西都能永久定型。
It's a pretty ugly, heavy object that's hard to keep in a house or flat so most people don't have one.
它是一个又丑又重的物体，很难放在家里或公寓里，所以大多数人没有。
You may think, "Can't I use my oven?" Well, that's possible but domestic ovens don't really get hot enough and eventually the clay will crack and fall apart.
你可能会想，“不能用我的烤箱吗？” 这当然是可能的，但家用烤箱实际上不够热，最终陶土会裂开并散架。
Some people fire pottery in a fire pit outside – but bear in mind that can be dangerous.
有些人在室外的火坑中烧制陶器——但请记住，这可能很危险。
You also need to know about safety procedures for kilns as they release toxic compounds into the air.
你还需要了解窑的安全程序，因为它们会释放有毒化合物到空气中。
Every potter needs a potter's wheel. This machine is used to shape the clay into an object with circular walls or sides – such as a bowl.
每个陶艺师都需要一个陶轮。这台机器用于将陶土塑造成具有圆形壁或侧面的物体——例如碗。
Its invention revolutionised the pottery industry, allowing multiple items to be produced in a day.
它的发明彻底改变了陶艺行业，使得一天内可以生产出多个物品。
Lastly, there are a number of different tools that potters use, depending on what they want to make.
最后，根据陶艺师想要制作的东西，他们使用许多不同的工具。
When you start, your hands can make all kinds of shapes and curves without relying on a sculpting tool.
当你开始时，你的双手可以在不依赖雕塑工具的情况下制作各种形状和曲线。
However, there are some basic tools that you will need to handle the clay on the wheel. Some look very strange and have even odder names that you may find hard to remember.
然而，有一些基本工具你需要用来在陶轮上处理陶土。有些看起来非常奇怪，名字更是难以记住。
Rather than go through them all now, I'll just name a few tools as we go along. We can provide these and I wouldn't recommend spending money on them yet.
我不会现在全部讲解，而是会在我们进行过程中提到一些工具。我们可以提供这些工具，不建议你现在花钱购买。
So let's try making a pot of your own …
所以让我们试着制作一个属于自己的陶罐吧……

120250702145934.title: C20-Test 1-Part 3
transcript: Shall we go through the notes we've made from our research into loneliness now, Dev?
我们现在来看看我们关于孤独的研究笔记吧，德夫？
OK, Tamara. It's been a real eye-opener. I had no idea that loneliness has been increasing steadily for the last twenty years.
好的，塔玛拉。这真是让人大开眼界。我完全不知道孤独在过去二十年里一直在增加。
I know. And it's the same all over the world – the downside of a modern lifestyle I guess.
我知道。而且全世界都是这样——我猜这是现代生活方式带来的负面影响。
Did you come to any conclusions about what the reasons for the increase are?
你有没有得出孤独为什么增加的结论？
Well, I'd assumed it was mainly an issue for the elderly – but in fact it's something which affects young people just as much.
我本来以为这主要是老年人的问题——但实际上，年轻人也同样受到影响。
Mmm. So nothing really to do with longer lifespans. What about social media?
嗯。所以这与寿命延长无关。那社交媒体呢？
In my case, far from making me feel isolated, it actually does the opposite.
对我来说，社交媒体不仅没有让我感到孤立，恰恰相反。
It definitely does more good than harm. I'd say loneliness has a lot to do with the way cities are designed – people living in high-rise flats with not much opportunity to speak to their neighbours.
它肯定利大于弊。我认为孤独与城市设计有很大关系——人们住在高层公寓里，几乎没有机会与邻居交谈。
I think you're right.
我认为你说的对。
Another possible reason is that people are having fewer children and don't live in large extended family groups.
另一个可能的原因是人们生育的孩子越来越少，不再生活在大家庭中。
But, in this country anyway, that all changed decades ago and yet loneliness is a more recent problem.
但是，至少在这个国家，这种情况几十年前就已经改变了，而孤独却是最近才出现的问题。
I suppose so. A more plausible explanation is that people are having to move around for work and often end up living miles away from their family and friends.
我想也是。一个更合理的解释是人们为了工作不得不搬来搬去，往往最终住在离家人和朋友很远的地方。
That's true.
确实如此。
Looking at the studies on health risks and loneliness, there are claims that loneliness has as much impact as smoking 15 cigarettes a day.
看看关于健康风险和孤独的研究，有人声称孤独的影响相当于每天吸15支香烟。
Or similar to the risks caused by obesity. But I'm not sure there's enough evidence for some of these claims.
或者类似于肥胖带来的风险。但我不确定这些说法是否有足够的证据支持。
Well–what about that one in Finland which showed that loneliness increased the risk of cancer by about ten per cent? And those findings have been supported by other studies too.
那么——芬兰的那项研究呢？它显示孤独会增加约10%的癌症风险。而其他研究也支持这些发现。
You're right about that one. I was actually thinking of the studies on dementia. Some found no association between loneliness and dementia and others found the opposite.
你说得对。我其实在想关于痴呆症的研究。有些研究发现孤独与痴呆症没有关联，而其他研究则发现相反的结果。
Not exactly reliable then. There's been a lot of research on cardiovascular disease and whether loneliness contributes to that.
那就不太可靠了。关于心血管疾病和孤独是否会导致心血管疾病的研究也很多。
Yes, I read that it was hard to reach a judgement as the definition of loneliness varied quite a lot and the responses from participants were too subjective.
是的，我读到很难得出结论，因为孤独的定义差异很大，参与者的回答也太主观了。
But there's no doubt that loneliness contributes to a weakened immune system.
但毫无疑问，孤独会导致免疫系统减弱。
Unquestionably. The data on that is sound.
毫无疑问。这方面的数据是可靠的。
What did you think about the evolutionary theory of loneliness?
你对孤独的进化理论怎么看？
Well, I thought the idea that loneliness evolved because it motivated people to be with other people quite convincing. Survival often depended on group cooperation.
嗯，我觉得孤独进化的原因是它促使人们与他人在一起，这个观点很有说服力。生存往往依赖于群体合作。
But I don't think there's enough evidence to claim that there must be a group of neurons in our brains which influence social behaviour, by making us feel bad when we're alone.
但我不认为有足够的证据表明我们大脑中一定有一组神经元会通过让我们感到孤独来影响社交行为。
There are a few studies which support the theory but not conclusively enough. More evidence is needed.
有一些研究支持这个理论，但还不够有说服力。还需要更多的证据。
Mmm. And anyway, this theory's not really useful when it comes to solving the problem of loneliness today.
嗯。而且无论如何，这个理论对解决今天的孤独问题并没有太大帮助。
True.
确实。
Should we look at the relationship between loneliness and mental health now?
我们现在来看看孤独与心理健康的关系吧？
OK. So, loneliness and depression are clearly related and that's been recognised by various governments around the world. But unlike depression, loneliness has no recognised clinical form.
好的。孤独和抑郁显然是相关的，这已经被世界各国政府认可。但与抑郁不同，孤独没有公认的临床形式。
There's no available diagnosis or effective treatment – and that's not likely to change.
没有可用的诊断方法或有效的治疗方法——而且这种情况不太可能改变。
I don't think so either. I was thinking we should start our presentation with an example from our own experience.
我也这么认为。我在想我们应该用我们自己的经历作为演讲的开头。
I'd like to talk about how lonely I was when I started university.
我想谈谈我刚上大学时有多孤独。
Being away from home for the first time and all that.
第一次离家等等。
Good idea. Everyone will be able to relate to that–although a lot of students were probably too embarrassed to admit to it.
好主意。每个人都能与此产生共鸣——尽管很多学生可能太尴尬而不愿承认。
Yeah. We could discuss ways of dealing with loneliness as well. Like just talking to strangers.
是啊。我们也可以讨论应对孤独的方法。比如与陌生人交谈。
Loads of studies have shown that interactions with shop assistants and bar staff make people feel more optimistic and relaxed.
很多研究表明，与店员和酒吧服务员的互动会让人感到更乐观和放松。
I don't know about that but it must make people feel more connected with their community.
我不确定，但这肯定会让人感到与社区更加联系在一起。
True. Although you need to be a certain kind of person to be able to just strike up a conversation.
确实。虽然你需要是那种能够随时随地与人交谈的人。
Good point. We should say something about solitude and how being alone and being lonely aren't the same thing.
好点子。我们应该说一下独处和孤独不是一回事。
It's strange the way some people can't stand being by themselves while others love it.
有些人不能忍受独处，而另一些人则喜欢独处，这很奇怪。

Yeah. The research shows a certain amount of solitude is beneficial for well-being, which I appreciate – but being alone isn't something I actually like. I'd never choose to go on holiday alone, for example.
是的。研究表明，适量的独处对健康有益，我能理解这一点——但独处并不是我喜欢的事情。例如，我绝不会选择一个人去度假。
Me neither.
我也不会。

120250703141507.title: C20-Test 1-Part 4
transcript: It's quite hard to think of a city that doesn't have a big river running through it.
很难想象一座没有大河流经的城市。
If you think about the major cities in the world, Shanghai, New York, Mumbai, London, they're nearly all built on rivers.
如果你想想世界上的主要城市，上海、纽约、孟买、伦敦，它们几乎都依河而建。
When these cities were established hundreds or even thousands of years ago, the rivers were a big part of people's lives.
当这些城市在几百年甚至几千年前建立时，河流是人们生活的重要组成部分。
In 16th-century London, the quickest way to get from one part of the city to another was by river.
在16世纪的伦敦，穿行于城市各处最快的方式便是通过水路。

But people also used the river for fishing, as the water then was relatively clean, and they would also go on boat trips up and down the river just for pleasure, as a relaxing escape from the noise and bustle of the city streets.
但人们也用河水捕鱼，因为当时的水质还算干净；他们还会乘船在河上游览，以此作为消遣，逃离都市的喧嚣。
But as industries developed and populations increased, city rivers suffered.
但随着工业发展和人口增长，城市里的河流也因此遭殃。
The rising number of people meant there was a huge increase in the amount of sewage discharged into the rivers.
不断增长的人口意味着排入河流的污水量急剧增加。
Rivers had always been used for this purpose, but when the number of inhabitants was so small that wasn't such a problem.
河流一直被用于排污，但在居民数量很少时，这并不是什么大问题。
However, as cities grew to over a million inhabitants, the impact on the rivers became more serious.
然而，当城市人口超过一百万时，对河流的影响就变得更加严重了。
In addition, other types of pollution increased as factories were built beside the river and discharged their waste materials into the water.
此外，随着工厂在河边建起并向水中排放废料，其他类型的污染也加剧了。
This got worse over time.
这种情况随着时间的推移而恶化。
As recently as 1957, scientists at London's Natural History Museum declared that the River Thames was ‘dead' in biological terms, as the water was too filthy to support any kind of life.
就在1957年，伦敦自然历史博物馆的科学家们宣布，泰晤士河在生物学意义上已经“死亡”，因为河水太过污浊，已无法支持任何形式的生命。
But in recent years, as rivers lost their industrial function, cities have begun to recognise their true value and to take steps to clean them up.
但近年来，随着河流失去了其工业功能，城市开始认识到它们的真正价值，并着手进行治理。
For example, the River Thames is now cleaner than it's been for a hundred and fifty years.
例如，泰晤士河如今的清洁程度是过去一百五十年来前所未有的。
These days you can see seals swimming in the water and recently people had to try to rescue a whale which had got lost and swum up the river from the sea by mistake.
现在你可以看到海豹在水中嬉戏，最近人们还紧急营救了一头从海里迷路游进河里的鲸鱼。
Unfortunately they didn't succeed, but the problem was disorientation rather than the quality of the water.
不幸的是，营救并未成功，但这主要是由于它迷失了方向，而非水质问题。
Then, all around the world, riverside areas are now seen as prime sites for development.
如今，世界各地的滨河区域都被视为开发的黄金地段。

Warehouses that were once used for storing goods are now being converted into expensive restaurants and also into apartments with river views, which are in great demand and sell for astronomical prices.
曾经用来储存货物的仓库，现在正被改造成昂贵的餐厅和备受欢迎的河景公寓，并以天价出售。

In Los Angeles, on the west coast of the USA, an architect has plans to revitalise the banks of the river and to make a park there which can provide facilities for sports as well as a natural environment for relaxing in.
在美国西海岸的洛杉矶，一位建筑师正计划复兴河岸，在那里建造一座公园，既能提供运动设施，也能营造一个可供休憩的自然环境。

It's also hoped that the riverside can be used for other purposes – it's been proposed that facilities could be provided for displaying projects related to various kinds of art that have been produced by local people, for example.
人们还希望滨河区域能有其他用途——例如，有人提议可以增建设施，用来展示当地居民创作的各类艺术项目。

In the city of Paris, during the summer months of July and August, all the traffic is banned from the roads by the sides of the river and the banks are transformed into beaches, where people can relax in deckchairs under potted palm trees, sunbathe, or buy a drink or a snack while enjoying the view.
在巴黎，每年七八月份的夏季，河岸边的所有道路都禁止车辆通行，河岸被改造成海滩，人们可以在盆栽棕榈树下的躺椅上放松、晒日光浴，或者边买小吃饮料边欣赏风景。

But to make the most of our rivers in our increasingly crowded cities, we need to allow them to regain their original purpose and be used as a means of transport, reclaiming our streets from cars and lorries.
但是，为了在我们日益拥挤的城市中充分利用河流，我们需要恢复其最初的运输功能，将街道从汽车和卡车的拥堵中解放出来。
To do this we'll have to shift more traffic back to the river, but this time cleanly and silently, making the most of modern technology.
为此，我们必须将更多的交通转移回河运，而这一次，要充分利用现代科技，做到清洁、安静。
Already more than two billion passengers use the ferry to travel in cities around the world like Istanbul, San Francisco and New York.
目前，在伊斯坦布尔、旧金山和纽约等世界各地的城市，已有超过二十亿人次乘坐渡轮出行。
And these numbers are set to rise further.
并且这个数字预计将进一步增长。
Admittedly, it's not a fast way of travelling, but neither is a car when it's stuck in traffic.
诚然，乘船算不上一种快速的出行方式，但当汽车困在拥堵的车流中时，也同样不快。
Of course, passenger traffic on roads might decrease as more people start working from home.
当然，随着越来越多的人开始在家办公，路面客运量可能会减少。
But another recent development, the huge rise in online shopping, has meant that another form of urban traffic just keeps on growing, and that's deliveries.
但近来在线购物的迅猛发展，也意味着另一种城市交通形式的持续增长，那就是快递派送。
Trucks and vans in the city pollute and double park while dropping off parcels.
城市里的卡车和货车在派送包裹时，不仅污染环境，还经常乱停乱放。
Imagine using the immense capacity of shipping to take these trucks off the road.
想象一下，利用水运的巨大潜力，让这些卡车从公路上消失。
One freight barge can replace 44 large trucks, uses far less energy and causes less pollution.
一艘货运驳船可以替代44辆大卡车，消耗的能源和造成的污染却要少得多。
When the barge docks at the riverside, the parcels could be taken the last few kilometres to their final destination on cargo bikes – electric ones, of course.
当驳船在河边靠岸后，包裹的最后几公里路程可以由货运自行车——当然是电动的——来完成。
This is already happening in the Dutch city of Amsterdam.
这在荷兰的阿姆斯特丹已经实现了。
And in future, the final stage could even be carried out by drone, although at present this isn't allowed.
未来，这最后阶段的运输甚至可能由无人机来执行，尽管目前还不被允许。
Wouldn't it be great to unblock our city centres in this way!
以这种方式来疏通我们的市中心，岂不妙哉！
Looking further ahead …
展望未来……

120250710101330.title: C20-Test 4-Part 1
transcript: Sandra – I seem to remember you had some family visitors staying with you recently.
Sandra，我记得你家里最近来了一些亲戚做客。
Yeah that's right. My brother and his family were here a couple of months ago.
是的，没错。我哥哥一家几个月前来过。
OK good. Well I wanted to ask your advice – I've got my cousin and her family visiting next month and as I don't have kids I've no idea where to take them.
好的，我想向你请教一下建议。下个月我表姐和她家人要来玩，但因为我没有孩子，所以完全不知道该带他们去哪里玩。
Right. What about accommodation? Are they going to stay with you in your flat?
明白。那住宿方面呢？他们会住在你家吗？
No, thankfully – there wouldn't be room – my cousin wants me to recommend a hotel. Do you know anywhere?
不，还好不用，我家没那么大。我表姐让我推荐一家酒店，你知道有什么合适的吗？
Yes, I do actually. I always recommend people stay at the Kings Hotel.
有啊，我通常都会推荐Kings Hotel。
Where's that near?
在哪儿附近？
It's about five minutes' walk from Murray Station – so nice and central. It's actually on George Street.
离Murray车站大约步行五分钟——位置很好、靠中心，在George街。
Oh yes I know. I think they're on quite a tight budget – so how much roughly is it to stay there?
哦，我知道那个地方。不过他们的预算好像不太高，住一晚大概多少钱？
If you book a family room it's about £125 per night. My brother paid for two double rooms in the end and I think that was around £95 for each room.
如果你订家庭房的话，大概每晚125英镑左右。我哥哥最后订了两间双人房，每间大概是95英镑左右。
Oh that's not too bad.
哦，这个价格还不错。
So how old are your cousin's kids?
你表姐的孩子多大啦？
Twelve and nine. So I want to organise some trips while they're here.
一个十二岁，一个九岁。我想趁他们在的时候安排一些活动。
I was thinking of doing a bus tour of the city centre – as none of them have been here before.
我原本打算带他们坐观光巴士游览市中心，因为他们都没来过这里。
Those bus tours are quite expensive. I think it's better to do a walking tour – it gives you a much better feel for the city.
观光巴士挺贵的。我觉得不如自己徒步旅游，这样更能感受(我们的)城市氛围。
There's one that starts from Carlton Square. It takes a couple of hours and doesn't cost that much.
有一个(旅行)从Carlton广场出发，大概两个小时，费用也不高。
Sounds good. I'll look that up – thanks.
听起来不错，我会查一下的，谢谢。
If the weather's nice, one thing you could do is visit the old fort. You can get there by boat. The whole trip takes half a day.
如果天气好，你们可以去旧堡垒参观，坐船过去。整个行程大概半天。
That's a really good idea. I'd like to do that myself.
这主意真不错，我自己也想去看看。
And if the weather's bad I was thinking they could go to the Science Museum. But maybe they could do that when I'm at work.
如果天气不好，我想着，让他们去科学博物馆。不过可能那天我在上班。
Yeah – don't forget it's closed on Mondays.
嗯，别忘了它周一关门。
They're here from Saturday for four nights – so Tuesday would be best, I think.
他们是星期六来的，一共住四晚，应该选周二去最好。
Mmm, and it won't be so crowded then. Saturdays are terrible.
嗯，那时候也不会那么拥挤。周六人最多。
I took my kids to the exhibition on old computers there and it was far too crowded. I wanted to go back but it's finished now.
我之前带我孩子去看那里看“老式电脑”展，结果太挤了。我想再去一次，但展览已经结束了。
That's a shame. My cousin's kids would have enjoyed that.
真可惜，我表姐的孩子肯定会喜欢那个展。
There's another one starting soon on space which looks really good too.
很快还有一个太空主题的新展，看起来也很棒。
OK, well I'll mention that to my cousin.
好，我会告诉我表姐的。
Have you thought about where to take them to eat?
你有没有想过带他们去哪儿吃饭？
Well I really like all the food stalls at Clacton Market. My cousin's vegetarian – I know it's one of the best places for that kind of food.
我很喜欢Clacton市场那边的小吃摊。我表姐是素食者，我知道那儿是吃这类食物最好的地方之一。
Definitely, and there'll be loads of choice for the kids, too.
对的，而且孩子们也会有很多选择。
You need to get there quite early though – at the weekend most of the stalls stop serving lunch at 2.30.
不过你要早点去，周末很多摊位中午2:30就不卖了。
Good point. It's all going to need careful planning! My cousin said she'd love to take the kids to a show at the theatre – but tickets are so expensive!
好建议。看来这些事都需要好好计划！我表姐说想带孩子们去看一场剧院演出，但门票实在太贵了！
I know. But you can get some good deals if you book online with bargaintickets.com for the following day – on some seats there's a 75% discount.
我知道。不过如果你在网上用bargaintickets.com预订第二天的票，有些座位可以打二五折。
Really?! I must try and get some.
真的吗？！我一定要试试看。
Yeah. There are lots of things you can do for free as well – no need to spend a fortune.
其实还有很多免费的活动可以参加，不需要花太多钱。
Like what?
比如什么？
They're coming next month, right? Well, check and see if it's the same weekend as the Roots Music Festival in Blakewell Gardens.
他们不是下个月来嘛？你可以查一下是不是正好赶上Blakewell Gardens的 Roots 音乐节。
R double-O-T-S?
R-O-O-T-S？
Yeah. Check it out online. It's always a family-friendly event and there's no entry charge.
对，就是这个名字。你可以上网查一下。这个音乐节一直都很适合家庭参加，而且入场免费。
That sounds perfect.
听起来太棒了。
And if you're in Blakewell Gardens – climb Telegraph Hill – you'll be able to look right down on the port.
还有，如果你去了Blakewell Gardens，记得爬Telegraph Hill，站在山顶可以看到港口的全景。
Everyone's always really impressed because it's so huge.
大家都觉得特别震撼，因为港口真的很大。
Oh yeah – I've been meaning to do that for ages. I've heard the view's amazing.
哦对，我一直想去那儿，听说风景真的很美。
Yeah. It's really worth the effort.
真的值得一看。
Well that's given me loads of ideas. Thanks so much …
你给了我好多好主意，真是太感谢了……

120250710102408.title: C20-Test 4-Part 2
transcript: Good morning, and welcome to City Football Club.
大家早上好，欢迎来到城市足球俱乐部。
I’d like to give you some useful information about your visit to the stadium today, and then we’ll start the tour of the areas of the stadium that are open to visitors.
在开始参观今天对外开放的场馆之前，请允许我向大家介绍一些实用信息。
I can see lots of children here today, so just to let mums and dads know a few things before we start.
我看到今天有很多小朋友在场，因此先请各位家长注意几个事项。
The stadium has lots of stairs, and the players’ tunnel is very dark.
体育场内楼梯很多，球员通道也非常昏暗。
Please don’t let your children wander off on their own, even for a minute. We don’t want any accidents or anyone getting frightened.
请不要让孩子单独离开身边，哪怕只是一分钟也不行。我们不希望发生任何意外，也不想让孩子受到惊吓。
Cameras are permitted everywhere, and you can take pictures of your child shooting a penalty.
整个场馆都可以拍照，你们可以拍下孩子罚点球的照片留作纪念。
Assistants are helping to organise this, and hopefully the queue won’t be too long – it’s very hot and sunny out on the pitch today.
有工作人员在现场协助组织拍照活动，希望排队的时间不会太长——毕竟今天场地外面阳光猛烈，天气非常热。
You can get food and drink at the café, and I really recommend the healthy lunch boxes for children.
你们可以在咖啡馆用餐和购买饮料，我特别推荐为孩子们准备的健康午餐盒。
Also in the café, children are invited to do a football-themed drawing.
此外，在咖啡馆里还邀请小朋友们参加以足球为主题的绘画活动。
We’ll pick the best one at the end of the afternoon, so don’t forget to put your name and contact details on the back.
我们会在下午结束前评选出最棒的作品，所以记得在画纸背面写上你的名字和联系方式。
That way, if you’ve left the stadium before then, we’ll send your prize, but sadly we can’t return drawings.
这样即使你们在公布结果前已经离开场馆，我们也可以把奖品寄给你，不过很抱歉，画作我们无法归还。
I’d like to mention some features of the tour.
接下来我想介绍一下本次参观的一些安排。
We’ll start with the three-sixty cinema experience, which has been very popular over the years,
我们将首先体验多年来一直广受好评的“360度电影院”。
and then I’ll take you to the players’ dressing rooms before going outside to the seating area and the pitch.
接着我们会参观球员更衣室，然后到室外的观众席和球场区域。
I should say, if you’d prefer your visit to be self-guided, please collect headphones from the reception and then you can listen to the pre-recorded information at your own speed.
另外需要说明的是，如果你希望自由安排参观节奏，可以前往前台领取耳机，收听预先录制好的讲解内容。
We’ve only just introduced this feature, and would appreciate your feedback.
这项服务是最近才推出的，我们非常欢迎你们提出反馈意见。
We’re thinking of offering tours in other languages in future so if you have any thoughts on that, we’d welcome those too.
我们考虑以后也提供其他语言的导览服务，欢迎大家对此提出建议。
If you plan to return another time, you might like to book one of our VIP tours – we’ve only just started offering these, and they can be booked online.
如果你打算以后再来参观，也可以提前预订我们的VIP导览服务——这种服务也是最近刚推出的，可以通过网络进行预订。
Now, the stadium you see today was built in 1989, as part of a three-year redevelopment project
现在，大家看到的这座体育场建于1989年，是为期三年的重建项目的一部分。
while that project was going on, the team had to play its matches at the ground of another club – apart from that, the club has been here on this site since 1870.
在那期间，球队不得不借用另一家俱乐部的场地进行比赛。除此之外，自1870年以来，俱乐部就一直位于这片场地上。
As some of you may know, that was the start of a really important decade in the history of football in this country.
可能有些朋友知道，1870年正是我国足球历史上一个非常重要的十年的开端。
For example, 1870 was also the year that football teams started to include a player whose role it was to guard the goal.
例如，就在这一年，足球队中首次出现了专门负责守门的球员角色。
It’s hard to imagine what the game must have been like without someone in that position, isn’t it?
很难想象当时没有守门员的情况下比赛是什么样子，对吧？
In 1872 and 73, many other clubs were established, both here and abroad, and the following year, in 1874, referees were allowed to send players off if they committed certain offences.
到了1872年和1873年，许多其他的俱乐部陆续成立，不仅是在国内，国外也开始出现足球俱乐部。紧接着在1874年，裁判被授权可以在某些违规情况下将球员罚出场外。
And also in that year, teams started having to swap ends at half-time.
同样是在那一年，比赛规定上下半场之间必须交换场地。

One fact I was interested to discover was that in early football games, the aim was for the scorer to get the ball between two flag posts, and later between sticks joined at the top with a piece of tape.
我还发现了一个有意思的历史事实：早期的足球比赛中，目标是让进球者把球射入两个旗杆之间的区域，后来则是用一根连接两根立柱顶部的带子作为球门。
In 1875, that tape was replaced with the solid crossbar that we’re familiar with today.
到了1875年，这根带子就被我们现在熟悉的坚固横梁所取代了。
1877 saw the founding of further new clubs, and the history books tell us that in the same year, all the clubs decided to set a limit of ninety minutes for each match.
1877年又建立了更多的新俱乐部，历史资料记载，同年各俱乐部达成一致，正式把每场比赛的时间定为90分钟。
Before that, it was a more casual arrangement, and this sometimes caused huge arguments – and sometimes fights – during matches,
在此之前，比赛时间比较随意，有时还会引发比赛期间巨大的争执，甚至打架，
when one team called the end of the game and the other team wanted to play on to try and score a winning goal.
因为一方球队认为比赛已经结束了，另一方却还想继续踢下去，试图进制胜一球。
By 1878, the number of teams in the football league increased again.
到了1878年，足球联赛中的球队数量再次增加。
In addition, referees started using whistles, and electric lamps were installed on certain pitches.
此外，裁判开始使用哨子，部分球场也安装了电灯。
This was a significant change, as games could then be played in the evenings all year round.
这一变化非常重大，因为从此之后，全年晚上也可以进行比赛了。
In 1880, clubs began to charge fans for admission to games, even though players were still amateurs and had other ‘proper’ jobs.
到了1880年，俱乐部开始对球迷收取入场费用，尽管那时的球员都还是业余选手，他们还有其他的“正式”工作。
That’s hard to imagine in the modern professional game where top players earn significant sums of money from both playing and commercial activities.
这在如今的职业足球世界里简直难以想象——现在的顶级球员不仅靠踢球获得可观收入，还能通过商业活动赚取大量报酬。

120250710103642.title: C20-Test 4-Part 3
transcript: How are you getting on with the assignment on handwriting?
你那篇关于书写能力的作业做得怎么样了？
Not too bad. You know, I hadn’t realised that children benefit in so many ways from learning to write.
还行。你知道吗，我以前不知道孩子们从学习书写中能获得这么多好处。
It’s such an important skill and yet most people think handwriting is less important than in the past because people hardly ever write by hand these days.
这是一项非常重要的技能，但大多数人认为现在书写已经没有以前那么重要了，因为现在几乎没人用手写字了。
Yes. And all the evidence suggests children should learn to write by hand before they learn to type. Not least because it helps their memory.
是的。而且所有证据都表明，孩子应该先学会手写，再学打字。其中一个主要原因就是这对他们的记忆力有帮助。
That’s right. The physical act of writing helps children to remember letters. That seems pretty obvious when you think about it.
没错。动手写字这个行为本身就能帮助孩子记住字母的形状。仔细想想其实挺明显的。
What’s less obvious is how it helps develop their concentration. They have to sit still and focus on one thing.
不太明显的是它如何帮助他们发展注意力。他们必须坐好，专注于一件事上。
Yeah. That aspect of handwriting had never occurred to me before.
对。这方面我以前真的没怎么想过。
Same here. I’m not sure I understand how it improves children’s imagination though.
我也是。不过我不太明白它是怎么提升孩子想象力的。
Well, there was that study which showed that primary aged children generated more ideas when they were writing by hand than using a keyboard.
嗯，有一项研究显示，小学生在手写时比用键盘能产生更多创意点子。
I would’ve guessed that would be the case.
我也觉得是这样。
Mmm, yeah. I’d never associated spatial awareness with handwriting either.
嗯，是的。我从来没把空间意识和手写联系在一起过。
I thought spatial awareness was more to do with knowing where you are in relation to objects or other people.
我一直以为空间意识是指你对自己在物体或他人周围位置的判断。
I thought that too. But good spatial awareness is essential for writing because you have to space words correctly.
我以前也这么想。但良好的空间意识对手写来说其实非常重要，因为你得正确地安排单词之间的间距。
It’s not just fine motor skills that improve through writing as I’d always assumed.
原来我以为只是精细动作技能会通过书写得到提高。
Handwriting is so much harder for children with dyspraxia, who have problems coordinating movement.
对于患有动作协调障碍的孩子来说，书写要困难得多，因为他们很难控制身体动作。
It’s good there are lots of things you can do in the classroom to help them.
幸好有很多方法可以在教室里帮助他们。
They need so much more support with letter formation – you need to play lots of games to help them distinguish letter shapes. It takes a lot of patience.
他们在字母构成方面需要更多的支持——需要玩很多游戏，以帮助他们区分不同字母的形状。这很考验耐心。
Yeah. I liked the idea of using one of those pens that lights up if you press too hard. That seems like a really simple solution.
嗯。我喜欢那个如果用力太大会亮灯的笔的设计，看起来是个很简单有效的办法。
Yes, absolutely. I’m not sure there’s much you can do about children with dyspraxia writing very slowly.
是的，确实如此。不过我也不确定有没有办法让患有动作协调障碍的孩子写得更快一些。
It’s more important to focus on accuracy and as they get more confident I think they eventually speed up.
更重要的是准确性，随着他们越来越自信，速度也会慢慢提上来。
One quite simple thing you can do is to use grid paper. So they write each letter in a box and that trains them to space the letters correctly.
有个比较简单的方法是使用格子纸，每个字母写在一个格子里，这样可以训练他们正确安排字母间距。
Indeed, that’s more important for legibility than trying to get them to write in a straight line.
没错，比起让他们写出一条直线，清晰的字母间距对辨识度更为重要。
For some children it might be better to teach them to write on a laptop rather than by hand. Like children with dyslexia.
对有些孩子来说，可能更适合教他们在笔记本电脑上打字而不是手写，比如像患有诵读困难症的孩子。
They often really struggle with handwriting and some just give up.
他们经常在书写方面遇到很大困难，有些人甚至直接放弃了。
Yeah. It’s not as frustrating for them if they get things wrong – on a keyboard they can be more willing to have a go. But I read that developing fluency isn’t any faster.
是的。对他们来说，当出错时，用键盘能更容易让他们愿意尝试。但我读到说，这样不能提高(写字)流利度。
That’s right. Did you read that article on the benefits of teaching print rather than cursive handwriting, where the letters are joined up?
没错。你有没有看过那篇关于教印刷体而非连笔草书的好处的文章？
Yes. Well in the past cursive writing was certainly considered more stylish and educated.
看了。过去人们普遍认为连笔书法更优雅、更有文化感。
But not anymore. Teachers’ attitudes have changed because it’s been proved that cursive is more difficult to learn –
但现在不是了。教师们的看法已经改变，因为事实证明连笔更难学——
especially for children with learning difficulties who find joining up letters really challenging.
特别是对那些学习困难的孩子，他们觉得把字母连起来特别难。
I agree. I was always worried that my poor handwriting affected my exam results. And now research shows that I was right to worry.
我同意这点。我一直担心我的糟糕字迹会影响考试成绩，现在研究证明我当时的担心是有道理的。
I’m sure a lot of students think it’s unfair that they’re being judged on their handwriting not just their knowledge.
我相信很多学生会觉得不公平，因为评判他们不仅看知识掌握程度，还要看字写得好不好。
Marks are definitely affected if examiners can’t read the script. That’s why it’s always been so important to teach children to write legibly….
如果阅卷老师看不懂字迹，分数肯定会受影响。这就是为什么一直强调教会孩子写清楚的重要性……
Do you think the role of handwriting will change in the future?
你觉得将来书写的角色会改变吗？
I can’t see that changing much. Touch typing still isn’t taught in most schools, which is a shame.
我看不会有多大变化。毕竟大多数学校仍然不教盲打，这挺可惜的。
But maybe that won’t be necessary in the future because people will also be able to write by hand on digital devices.
但也许未来这并不重要，因为人们也可以在数字设备上直接手写了。
Anyway, teachers understand the value of handwriting. It’s a basic life skill.
不管怎么说，老师们还是知道书写的价值的。它是一种基本的生活技能。
True. However, the fact is that people are writing by hand less and less and relying on digital devices. That does cause some problems.
没错。但现实是，人们越来越少用手写字，而是依赖数码设备。这也带来了一些问题。
You mean like note taking? There are lots of apps for that.
你是说像记笔记这种事吗？这类应用现在已经有很多了。
And for reading historical documents apparently. But my mum is shocked by my awful spelling and the fact that my punctuation is really inconsistent.
还有阅读历史文献的时候据说也有问题。但我妈妈总抱怨我拼写差得离谱，标点也非常混乱。
I think you can put that down to lack of practice.
我觉得这是因为缺乏练习。
I expect so. Personally, I miss writing by hand. I hardly ever write anything now. I remember my grandparents had such beautiful handwriting and it was so individual.
应该是这样的。我自己倒是怀念写字的感觉。我现在几乎什么都不写了。我还记得我祖父母那优美的字迹，每个人都有自己的风格。
Nobody I know would be able to identify my handwriting now. It’s a shame.
现在我认识的人几乎没有谁能看清我的书写了。真有点可惜。
I know. I feel the same way. I used to write a diary by hand and now I do that digitally. It just seems less effort to do it that way. So it’s not just a problem…
我也有同感。我以前还坚持用手写日记，现在改用电子方式了。感觉这样轻松多了。所以这不只是……

120250710104359.title: C20-Test 4-Part 4
transcript: We’ve been looking at different types of conflicts that may arise between wildlife and humans at the boundaries of protected areas such as national parks and animal sanctuaries.
我们一直在研究在保护区边界，比如国家公园和野生动物保护区附近，人类与野生动物之间可能出现的不同类型的冲突。
I’d like to illustrate this by telling you about some research that I’ve been involved in recently in the central African country of Zambia
我想通过最近我在赞比亚参与的一项研究来说明这个问题。这项研究是在赞比亚中部地区
in the area around the Chembe Bird Sanctuary, which contains over three hundred of the listed birds of Zambia.
一个名叫Chembe Bird Sanctuary的地方进行的。这个保护区拥有超过300种被列入保护名录的赞比亚鸟类。
These include a number of birds of prey, such as eagles, hawks and owls, that live by hunting and killing other birds and animals.
其中包括许多猛禽，例如鹰、隼和猫头鹰，它们以捕猎其他鸟类和动物为生。
Now, most of the people living in the local communities near to the bird sanctuary are small-scale farmers, and these birds of prey provide important social and ecological benefits to them.
目前居住在保护区附近的社区居民大多是小规模农民，而这些猛禽为他们提供了重要的社会和生态效益。
For example, a lot of damage can be caused to farmers’ crops by rodents such as rats,
例如，老鼠等啮齿类动物会对农作物造成严重破坏，
which would consume the crops as they grow in the fields as well as after harvesting if they weren’t hunted and killed by the birds.
如果这些鸟类不捕猎它们，老鼠不仅会在庄稼生长期间吃掉作物，在收获后也会继续破坏粮食。
And the predatory habits of these birds also protect farmers in other ways.
此外，这些猛禽的捕食行为还能从其他方面保护农民的利益。
For example, a major danger to rural workers is snakes, whose bite may be dangerous or even fatal, and birds of prey have a major role in keeping their populations under control.
比如，农村工人面临的一个主要危险是蛇咬伤，这种伤害可能是致命的。而猛禽在控制蛇的数量方面起着关键作用。
Local people have always been aware of these benefits and for years, even before the sanctuary was opened in 1973, the birds played a key role in the culture of the region.
当地居民一直都知道这些好处，甚至早在1973年保护区建立之前，这些鸟类就在当地的区域文化中扮演了重要角色。
However, more recently, the sanctuary and its birds have also become increasingly important to the community in economic terms,
然而，近年来，这些鸟兽保护区和里面的鸟类对社区来说也变得越来越具有经济上的意义。
because at present, after a relatively slow start, tourism has become an important source of revenue for them.
因为尽管旅游业起步较慢，但现在已经成为当地的一个重要收入来源。
However, although these birds of prey are protected by the government, their numbers are falling.
然而，尽管这些猛禽受到政府的法律保护，它们的数量却在下降。
Some of these deaths are accidental.
其中一些死亡是意外造成的。
Fatalities occur when birds alight on roads to catch and eat their prey, and are hit by fast-moving traffic.
当鸟类落在道路上捕捉并进食猎物时，可能会被高速行驶的车辆撞死。
Drivers in Zambia have to take special care at night, as birds may regard the quieter roads as safe places to sleep.
赞比亚的司机们晚上尤其需要小心，因为鸟类可能把夜间安静的道路当作安全的栖息地。
Accidental deaths may also occur if these birds fly close to high-voltage power lines, as they may be electrocuted.
此外，当这些鸟飞近高压电线时也可能发生意外触电身亡的情况。
This is a particular danger in the heavy rain which can occur in the region in the months from December to April.
这一点在每年12月至4月该地区降雨量较大的时候尤为危险。
And local farmers also pose a threat to these birds.
当地的农民也对这些猛禽构成了威胁。
As well as growing crops, small-scale farmers in the area also rear chickens.
除了种植农作物，该地区的农民还饲养鸡。
These provide food for the farmers’ families, as well as being an important source of income.
这些鸡为农民家庭提供食物的同时，也是重要的收入来源。
But they’re also an easy target for birds of prey, and so farmers may shoot these birds, which is illegal but understandable,
但这些鸡很容易成为猛禽的攻击目标，因此有些农民会非法射杀这些鸟类——虽然可以理解，
or they may poison the birds, which again is illegal and can have negative effects on the ecosystem.
或者投毒，这同样违法，并且会对整个生态系统造成负面影响。
So how else can farmers protect their chickens from birds of prey?
那么农民有没有其他办法可以保护他们的鸡免受猛禽侵害呢？
Some people believe that to prevent the predators from settling near the area where the chickens are kept it’s best to keep this area free from vegetation.
有些人认为，为了防止猛禽靠近鸡的活动区域，最好将这一带清理干净，不留下任何植被。
But in fact this is counter-productive as it means the chickens have no cover to hide in and they’ll be easier for the birds to see.
但实际上这样做适得其反，因为没有遮蔽物，鸡就没有地方躲藏，反而更容易被猛禽发现。
Another possibility would be to prevent the chickens from going outside at all, and to keep them safe from predators inside a building, but this would cost far too much to be a practical solution.
另一个方法是不让鸡去室外，而是把它们关在建筑物内以确保安全，但这成本太高，不太现实。
Nearly all the farmers reported that they spent a lot of time and effort trying to frighten off the birds of prey without actually harming them.
几乎所有受访的农民都表示，他们会花很多时间和精力试图吓跑猛禽的同时又不真正伤害它们。
Most of the farmers had at least one dog, and said this was a big help at scaring away the predators.
大多数农民家里至少有一只狗，他们表示狗对于驱赶这些捕食者很有帮助。
Some of the farmers also reported that during the breeding season, when the chickens were particularly vulnerable,
还有一些农民提到，在繁殖季节，尤其是小鸡最脆弱的时候，

they encouraged their children to watch over the chickens and to hit pans with a metal spoon so that the resulting noise would succeed in driving away birds that were trying to seize the young chicks.
他们会叫孩子们看守鸡群，并用金属勺子敲打锅具制造噪音，以此来吓走想抓小鸡的猛禽。
None of these methods was a hundred per cent effective, so as a result,
然而，这些方法都没有百分之百的效果。因此，
the village people told us that rather than just using one method, they were forced to use a combination for them to have any effect.
村民们告诉我们，与其单独使用某一种方法，他们通常会选择多种方法结合使用，才能起到一定的效果。
And even so, these birds of prey remain a major threat to the chickens’ survival and cause considerable economic loss to farmers.
即便如此，这些猛禽仍然是鸡生存的主要威胁，给农民带来了巨大的经济损失。
So we looked at the possibility of a longer-term solution to …
所以我们看看一个长期解决方案的可行性...

121.title: C7-Test 4-Section 1
transcript: Yes, what can I do for you?
是的，有什么可以帮你的？
My friend is in homestay...and she really enjoys it...
我的朋友住在寄宿家庭，她很喜欢那里，
so I'd like to join a family as well.
所以我也想住到寄宿家庭里。
Okay, so let me get some details.
好的。让我记一下详细的信息。
What's your name?
你叫什么？
My name is Keiko Yuichini
我叫Keiko Yuochini。
Could you spell your family name for me?
你能拼一下你的姓吗？
It's...Yuichini.
Yuichini，
That's Y—U—l—C—H—I—N—I.
Y-U-I-C-H-I-N-I。
And your first name?
你的名字是？
It's Keiko. K—E—I—K—O.
Keiko，K-E-I-K-O。
That's Keiko Yuichini...okay...and you're female.
Keiko Yuichini，好的。女性。
And your nationality?
你的国籍是什么？
I'm Japanese.
我是日本人
Right, and could I see your passport, please?
好的，我能看一下你的护照吗？
Here it is...
给你。
Okay...your passport number is JO6337...
好的，你的护照号是JO6337，
And you're how old?
你多少大了？
I'm twenty-eight years old.
我28岁了。
Now, you live at one of the colleges...which one?
现在你住在哪个大学里？
Willow College, umm...Room 21C.
柳木大学，21C房间。
Right, 21C Willow College,
好的，21C房，柳木大学。
And how long are you planning on staying with homestay?
你打算在寄宿家庭住多久？
About four months...longer if I like it...
大约4个月，如果我喜欢会住的更长。
And what course are you enrolled in?
你学的是什么课程？
Well, I've enrolled for twenty weeks in the...um...Advanced English Studies because I need help with my writing...
我报名参加了20周的高级英语课程，因为我想提高我的写作能力，
and I'm nearly at the end of my first five week course.
我快学完前5周的课程了。
Okay...Do you have any preference for a family with children or without children?
好的，你希望住宿的家庭里面有没有孩子呢？
I prefer...I mean I like young children, but I'd like to be with older people...you know...adults...someone around my age.
我喜欢小孩，但是我希望和大一点的人共处，成人或者和我同龄的人。
Okay, and what about pets?
好的，那么宠物呢？
I am a veterinarian so that's fine...the more the better.
我是一名兽医，所以没关系，越多越好。
All right, now what about you?
好的，那么你呢？
Are you a vegetarian or do you have any special food requirements?
你是素食主义者吗，或者你有特殊的食物要求吗？
No, I am not a vegetarian...but I don't eat a lot of meat...I really like seafood.
不，我不是素食主义者，但是我不怎么吃肉，我特别喜欢海鲜。
And what are your hobbies?
你有什么爱好？
I like reading and going to the movies.
我喜欢读书、看电影。
Do you play any sports?
你做什么运动吗？
Yes, I joined the handball team, but I didn't like that...so I stopped playing.
是的，我加入了手球队，但是我不喜欢，所以我退出了。
Now I play tennis on the weekend with my friends...
现在，我会和我的朋友在周末打网球。
All right, let's see, name, age, now the location.
好的，让我看看。名字、年龄，现在是位置。
Are you familiar with the public transport system?
你对公共交通系统熟悉吗？
No ...I'm not really because I have been living on campus...
不，因为我住在校园里，
I've been to the city a few times on the bus, but they are always late.
我乘公交去过城里几次，但是它们总是迟迟不来。
What about the trains?
那么火车呢？
I like catching the train...they are much faster...
我喜欢坐火车，它们很快。
Now, let me go check on the computer and see who I've got...listen, leave it with me...
现在，我在电脑上查一下，看有没有合适的家庭。交给我吧。
I'll check my records and I'll give you details this afternoon.
我会在今天下午告诉你详情。
Thank you for helping me...
谢谢。
It's a pleasure. Bye.
不客气。再见。
Bye.
再见。

122.title: C7-Test 4-Section 2
transcript: Welcome to all of you...can everybody see and hear me?
欢迎大家，每个人都能看见、听到我吗？
...Good...I'm Sally, your guide for this tour of the Bicentennial Park...
很好，我叫莎莉，是你们参观拜森泰尼亚尔公园的向导，
I hope that you're all wearing your most comfortable shoes and that you can keep up the pace.
我希望你们都穿着最舒适的鞋子，跟得上我们的步伐。
So let's get under way on our tour around this wonderful park.
那么，让我们开始游览这座美丽的公园。
I'll start today with some general background information.
我今天将从大概的背景知识开始说起。
There used to be a lot of factories in this area until the 1960s.
20世纪60年代以前，这里坐落着许多工厂。
Creating the park required the demolition of lots of derelict buildings on the site, so most of the exciting park space all around you was originally warehouses and storehouses.
园区的创建需要在该地拆除大量废弃的建筑物。所以大家所在的这座令人激动的公园的大部分空间都曾是仓库或货栈。
The idea of building a public park here was first discussed when a property developer proposed a high-rise housing development, but the local community wasn't happy.
当一位地产开发商打算建一座高楼时，修建公共公园的想法被首次提出，可是当地居民对此并不高兴。
If the land was to be cleaned up, they wanted to use the site for recreation.
如果这里要被清除，他们希望将这里用于娱乐。
Residents wanted open space for outdoor activities, rather than housing or even an indoor sports complex.
居民希望有户外活动的空地，而不是住房，甚至是室内运动场。
Now to the Bicentennial Park itself.
现在说说森泰尼亚尔公园，
It has two areas, a nature reserve and a formal park with man-made features and gardens.
这里有两个区域，一个是自然保护区，另一个是拥有人造特色以及花园的公园。
The tall blue-and-white building in front of us is called The Tower and is the centre point for the formal gardens.
你们面前的这座蓝白相间的高大建筑叫做塔楼，它是这座公园的中心。
It stands twelve metres high, so follow me up the stairs to where we can take advantage of the fantastic views.
它有12米高，所以跟我上楼看一看这里的美丽风景。
Well, here we are at the top of The Tower, and we're going to look at the view from each direction.
现在我们到了塔的顶部，我们将望望这里每一面的风景。
Out to the east, the large buildings about a kilometre away are on the Olympic site.
东边，一公里以外的高大建筑位于奥运会会址。
There's an indoor arena for gymnastics, a stadium for track and field and a swimming pool for races and synchronised swimming and also diving.
这里有一个室内体操馆、一个田径场和一个比赛型的游泳馆，可以用于花样游泳和跳水。
If you look carefully down there, you can see the train lines.
如果你仔细看，你会看到火车线。
The Olympic site has its own station to encourage the use of public transport.
奥运会会场有自己的车站，鼓励人们使用公共交通。
There is also a car park, but it only holds a limited number of cars.
这里还有一个停车场，不过只能容纳少数车辆。
The formal park has some specially-created water features.
公园有一些特殊创造的水景。
If you look out here to the south, you can see a circular ornamental pond.
如果你从这里往南看，你会看到一个圆形的装饰性池塘。
And around to the west, you can relax and sit on a bench to smell the flowers in the rose garden,
在西面，你可以坐在长椅上放松，闻着玫瑰园的花香。
and finally up to the north, if you look in front of you now, there's a lake with a small island in the centre, you can hire rowing boats at the boat shed, which you can't see from here,
最后是北面，如果你现在往前看，会发现湖中心有一座小岛。大家可以在船屋租划艇。你从这里看不到
but if you look through the trees, you can see the cafe, which has lovely views across the water.
但是如果你穿过树林就会看到一间咖啡馆，从那里可以看到美丽的水景。
OK, let's climb down now.
好的，现在我们往下走。
We will go now and have a look at the nature reserve section of the park, which has opened up natural wetland to the public.
我们看一看公园的自然保护区，这里向公众开放自然湿地。
The Mangroves have been made more accessible to visitors by the boardwalk built during the parks upgrade.
在给公园升级的时候，新建的木板路可以让游客更容易接近红树林。
You'd think that people would come here to look at the unusual plant life of the area, but in fact it's more often used for cycling and is very popular with the local clubs.
通常你会认为人们会来这儿观看不同寻常的植物，但事实上，这里更多用来骑脚踏车，而且这个活动风靡当地的俱乐部。
This is the far end of the park and over there you can see the Frog Pond, a natural feature here long before the park was designed.
这里是公园的尽头，你可以看到青蛙池塘，这个自然风景在建公园前就已经存在了。
Just next to it we have our outdoor classroom, a favourite spot for school parties.
它的旁边是我们的室外教室，学校聚会最热衷的地方。
The area is now most often used by primary schools for biology lessons.
这一区域现在主要用于小学的生物课程。
And finally let's pass by the Waterbird Refuge.
最后，让我们看看水鸟保护区。
This area is in a sheltered part of the estuary,that's why the park's viewing shelter is a favourite spot for bird watchers who can use it to spy through binoculars.
这里位于河口的庇护区。这就是为什么公园的观景棚是鸟类观察家用望远镜观察的最佳地点。
You can watch a variety of water birds, but most visitors expect to see black swans when they come to the shelter.
你可以看到各种各样的水禽，但是大多数游客来到这片庇护之地时，都希望看到黑天鹅。
You might spot one yourself right now!
你可能现在就能看到一只！
Well, here we are back at our starting point, the Visitor Centre...
现在我们回到了起点，游客中心。

123.title: C7-Test 4-Section 3
transcript: Remind me, Trevor...how long is the presentation?
特雷弗，提醒我一下，演讲要多长时间？
Dr White said three per hour.
怀特先生说一小时三个。
So about twenty minutes?
所以大约是20分钟。
Well...it'll be fifteen minutes per presentation.
每个演讲要15分钟。
And five minutes for questions.
另外5分钟用于提问？
And is this one going to be assessed?
这次要评分吗？
No...not this time round because it's the first one...you know.
不，这次不评分，因为这是第一次。
Good news.
好消息。
Well, Trevor, what are we going to include?
特雷弗，我们要说些什么？
Well...Do you think we ought to give some historical background?
你觉得我们应该说点历史背景吗？
Oh no...definitely not...we won't have time!
肯定不行，我们没时间了！
OK...but I think we ought to say something about the geographical location ...'cause not a lot of people know where the islands are...
好的，但是我想我们应该说一下地理位置，因为很多人不知道那些岛屿在哪儿。
Yes...OK...I'll take notes, shall I?
好的，我来记笔记吧？
Yeah, that'll be a help...
好的，这很有帮助。
So...geographical location...
所以，地理位置。
Then we ought to give an overview of the whole education system.
之后我们要介绍整个教育系统的概况。
Shouldn't we say something about the economy...
我们要不要提一下经济问题？
you know agricultural produce, minerals and so forth?
比如农产品、矿物质之类的？
Well...Dr. White said we shouldn't go into that sort of detail.
怀特博士告诉我们不要讨论那些细节。
But it's pretty important when you think about it... you know because it does influence the education system...
但是当你想到它的时候，你会发现这很重要，因为它的确影响教育系统。
Look...let's think about that one later shall we?
让我们之后再想这个吧？
Let's see how we're doing for time...
看看还有没有时间。
OK...so...general overview of education
好的，教育概述。
Of course ... and then the role of English language...
当然。然后是英文的角色。
Nope...that goes in the Language Policy Seminar...don't you remember?
不，那个将在语言政策讨论会上讲，你不记得了吗
Are you sure?
你确定吗？当然！
Positive.
好的，
All right...so those are the topics we're going to be...to be covering...
这些就是我们要讲的话题。
We need to think about what to prepare...
我们得想想要准备什么。
Dr. White said he wanted us to use plenty of visuals and things and we might as well try them out when we're not being assessed...
怀特博士说他想让我们多用一些视觉的东西，尤其是在不评分的时候还可以多尝试其他东西。
Well, the most important thing is the overhead projector...
最重要的是投影仪。
No problem...we'll get that from the media room...must remember to book it...
没问题，我们可以从媒体室拿，别忘了预定。
Well...we'll need a map of course
我们当然还需要一张地图。
Probably two...one of the islands...large scale.
可能要两大张。一张是岛屿的。
And one of West Africa.
另一张是非洲西部。
Well, the West African one is no problem...there's one in the Resources Room.
非洲西部的没问题，资料室就有一张。
Oh yeah, of course, the resources room; the islands are going to be more of a problem.
是的，资料室。岛屿那张可能有点难找。
Tell you what...there's a very clear map of Santiago in that tourist brochure I showed you last week.
告诉你吧，在我上周给你看的旅游手册上有一幅非常清晰的圣地亚哥的地图，
Don't you remember it?
你记得吗？
Oh yeah...that's right; we can just use the tourist brochure.
没错。我们可以用旅游手册。
We also need statistics...on several different things.
我们还得在一些不同的事情上找些统计资料。
Literacy rates.
识字率。
Yes, and school places.
是的，还有学校的地点。
How about the encyclopaedia?
《百科全书》怎么样？
Nah...not up-to-date enough!
不行，不够与时俱进！
Mmm...why don't we call the embassy?
我们干嘛不给大使馆打电话？
Oh...someone's enthusiastic!
有人相当有激情啊！
Well...if something's worth doing...
如果事情值得做的话。
I know...it's worth doing well...OK.
我知道它值得做好。好的。
We can find out statistics on school places from them as well.
我们还能从他们那里得到学校分布地点的统计资料。
Might as well.
最好如此。
Look, Julie, it's almost time for our tutorials...we can meet again on Monday...but we need to prepare some stuff before then...
朱丽，快到我们上课的时间了。我们可以周一再见，但是我们在这之前要准备一些材料……

124.title: C7-Test 4-Section 4
transcript: In today's lecture, I'm going to talk about Monosodium Glutamate, or MSG, as it's more commonly known.
在今天的课上，我将要讲解一下味精，或者说是更为大众所知的MSG。
Now, MSG, as you probably know, is a flavour enhancer which is used particularly in Chinese and Japanese cooking.
如今，正如你们所知，MSG是一种常被中国和日本用于烹调的增味剂。
Today I am going to explore why it is so popular in these cuisines and, more importantly, how does it enhance the flavour of food?
今天我将要探究一下为什么味精在烹饪中如此流行。更重要的是，为什么它可以增加食物的味道。
The main reason why MSG is more commonly used in Japanese meals is tradition.
味精常用于日本菜的主要原因是传统。
For many thousands of years the Japanese have incorporated a type of seaweed known as kombu in their cooking, as they discovered it had the ability to make food taste better.
数千年以来，日本将一种叫做海带的海草加入了他们的烹调，因为他们发现海带能够使食物尝上去更好吃。
But it wasn't until 1908 that the ingredient in kombu which was responsible for the improvement in flavour was actually discovered to be glutamate by scientists working there.
但是直到1908年，科学家才发现海带里面负责提味的成份是谷氨酸盐。
From 1908 until 1956, glutamate was produced commercially in Japan by a very slow and expensive means of extraction.
1908年至1956年，日本开始对味精进行商业开发，以一种缓慢又昂贵的提取方式获得味精。
It was in 1956 that the speed of the process was improved, and industrial production increased dramatically and still continues to increase to this day.
1956年，生产的速度提高了，工业产值大幅度增加，直到今天仍在继续增长。
In fact, hundreds of thousands of tonnes of MSG are produced all over the world today.
事实上，如今成千上万吨的味精在全世界生产。
So what exactly is MSG?
那么，味精到底是什么？
Well, Monosodium Glutamate contains seventy-eight point two per cent glutamate, twelve point two per cent sodium and nine point six per cent water.
味精富含78.2%的谷氨酸盐，12.2%的钠和9.6%的水。
Glutamate is an amino acid that can be found naturally in all protein-containing foods,
谷氨酸盐是一种氨基酸，在所有蛋白质食物中可以找到。
erm, so this includes food such as meat and cheese.
例如肉和奶酪。
It is widely known that Chinese and Japanese food contains MSG but many people don't seem to be aware that it is also used in foods in other parts of the world.
众所周知，中国和日本菜使用味精，但是很少有人意识到在世界其他地区的食物中味精也被广泛使用。
For example it is found in commercially made Italian pizzas, in American fast food and in Britain MSG is used in things like potato crisps.
例如，意大利比萨、美国快餐、英国炸薯条等。
So, how exactly does MSG work?
那么，到底味精是如何起作用的呢？
Well, in the Western world, we commonly talk of four 'tastes', and I'm sure you're all familiar with the concepts of sweet, sour, bitter and salt.
在西方世界，我们通常会说四种味道，即甜、酸、苦、咸。
Well, in 1908, Kikunae Ikeda identified a fifth 'taste'.
在1908年，池田菊苗发现了第五种味道。
And it is thought that MSG intensifies this naturally occurring taste in some food.
味精很自然地加强了一些事物的味道。
It does make perfect evolutionary sense that we should have the ability to detect or taste glutamate because it is the amino acid which is most common in natural foods.
因为谷氨酸盐是在自然食物中很常见的氨基酸，所以我们能够发现并尝出它的味道，从进化的角度看，这是说得通的。
John Prescott, an associate professor at the University of Chicago, suggests that this fifth taste serves a purpose just as the other tastes do.
芝加哥大学副教授，约翰·普雷斯科特认为这第五种味道和其他的味道作用相同。
He suggests that it signals to us the presence of protein in food, in the same way that sweetness indicates that a food contains energy-giving carbohydrates.
他认为这表示食物中蛋白质的存在，就像甜味表示食物中包含补充能量的碳水化合物。
Bitterness, he says, alerts us to toxins in the food, while sourness warns us of spoilage and saltiness signals the presence of minerals.
他说，苦味提醒我们食物有毒，酸味警告我们食物变质，而咸味表示矿物质的存在。
So, what else do we know about this fifth taste...
那么，对于第五种味道，我们还知道什么……

125.title: C6-Test 1-Section 1
transcript: Good morning, oh sorry, it's gone 12, I'll start again, good afternoon, Kingswell Sports Club, how can I help you?
早上好，对不起，都过了12点了。我重来一遍
Oh, good afternoon. I was wondering if you could give me some information about membership and facilities.
下午好，我在想你能不能向我介绍一下那里的会员资格和设施。
Of course.What would you like to know?
没问题，你想了解什么
Do you have tennis courts, for example?
比如，你那里有网球场吗？
No, I'm afraid we don't. We're primarily a golf club.
恐怕我们没有，我们主要是一个高尔夫球俱乐部。
What about football? I heard you had a team.
足球呢？我听说你们有一个球队。
No, I'm sorry. Perhaps you're thinking about Fresham Sports Centre.
哦，不对 你说的是Freshham运动中心吗
Oh, right.I know it.I've played badminton there.
是的 我知道那里，我在那里打过羽毛球
Have you?
是吗
They've got a lot of facilities we don't have and vice versa.
他们有很多我们没有的设施，反之也是一样。
We do have a keep - fit studio, which is very popular with members, and then as well as that there's swimming, of course.
我们有一个很受欢迎的健身房，在那里还可以游泳。
That's good.I like to swim every day.
不错 我喜欢天天游泳
We have a range of classes too.
我们还有一系列的课程。
Do you have judo classes?I'm keen to learn.
你们有柔道课程吗？我很想学。
Well, at the moment we offer kick - boxing. We're planning to add judo and stretch classes soon.
现在我们有拳击 很快会有柔道和伸展
We're currently running a range of yoga classes, too.
我们目前也开设了一系列瑜伽课。
What about relaxing after exercise? I assume you have a restaurant or something.
运动后的休息呢？我想你们有餐馆或者别的设施？
At the moment, we've got a salad bar which is very popular.We'll also have a fully - licensed restaurant by the end of the year.
现在，我们有一个很受欢迎的沙拉吧。今年年底，我们还会有一个经营项目齐全的餐馆。
Sounds good!
不错
What kind of membership are you interested in?
你想办理成为哪种会员。
Um, not really sure.What are the options?
不是特别确定 都有什么选择？
Well, there are three different membership schemes.
有三种不同的会员制。
I see.What's the difference?
懂了，有什么区别吗
Well, the first one's called Gold, and you can use all the facilities at any time of the day or week.
首先是金牌会员，你可以在一天或一周的任何时间使用所有设施。
You can also join in as many classes as you like for free.
你可以免费加入你喜欢的很多课程。
That sounds good.Is it very expensive?
听起来不错 贵吗
Well, you pay a ￡250 joining fee and then it's ￡450 - oh no, I'm sorry, it's just gone up by ￡50, sorry about that - it's now ￡500 for the annual subscription fee.
你要交纳250英镑的会费，然后是450英镑……不，对不起，涨了50英镑。对不起，现在的年费是500英镑
Right, got that.And what's the next type?
懂了 下一步呢
Well, that's Silver - it's the same as Gold except you have to pay a small fee of ￡1.00 per lesson for any you do and you can only use the centre at certain times.
是银牌会员，它和金牌一样，只是你要花一点钱，你上的每节课要花一英镑而且你只能在特定的时间使用中心的设施。
I see. So when exactly?
懂了，具体什么时候
You can only use the facilities between 10 am and 4.30 pm.
你只能在上午10点到下午4:30之间使用这里的设施。
So I couldn't use the pool at 8 in the morning or evening, then?
所以我不能在早上8点或晚上使用游泳池了？
That's right.
没错。
OK.And the price for that?
好的，价格呢？
Is the joining fee the same as for Gold?
会费和金牌会员一样吗？
Actually, it's slightly less than the ￡250 - it's ￡225, but the annual fee is only ￡300.
事实上，比250英镑少点，是225英镑。但是年费只有300英镑。
Does that sound more like what you want?
这是你想要的吗？
Well, it's still rather more expensive than I thought. I'm a student here in England and I'm only here for six months.
这还是比我想的价格高。我在英格兰只是学生，而且我在这里只待6个月。
Ah, then the Bronze scheme would probably suit you best.
那么，铜牌会员制可能更适合你。
How is that different?
这有什么不同？
Well, some of the facilities have restricted use.
一些设施被限制使用
And do I have to pay for classes?
我上课需要交钱吗？
Yes, it's ￡3 for each class you join.
3磅一节
I see.And what are the hours then?
懂了。时间呢？
Between 10.30 and 3.30 weekdays only and you pay a ￡50 joining fee.
只能在工作日的10:30到3:30，会费是50英镑。
The annual fee is ￡180 - it works out at ￡15 a month, so that would be quite a lot cheaper.
年费是180英镑，一个月15英镑，这个很便宜。
Oh, that should be all right.I could come in my free periods.What do I have to do if I want to join?
这个可以 我有空的时候就去。如果我想加入，我还要做些什么？
Well, we book you in for an assessment with an instructor, who will show you how to use all the equipment.
我们为你预定一个教练进行评估，他会告诉你如何使用这些设备。
If you want to organise a trial session and look around the centre, you'll need to speak to David Kynchley.
如果你想上试听课并参观一下中心，你需要找大卫·芬奇利。
Hmm. Could you spell that please?
拼一下？
Yes, David K-Y-N-C-H-L-E-Y.
好的。
I'll give you his direct line number.
我给你他的直线电话。
It's O—four—five—eight—nine—five—three—double one.
Thanks.
谢谢
Thank you for calling Kingswell Sports Club.
谢谢你拨打金斯韦尔健身房。

126.title: C6-Test 1-Section 2
transcript: And here on Radio Rivenden we have Lynne Rawley, the Public Relations Officer of our own Rivenden City Theatre.
这里是里弗顿广播，我们请来了里弗顿剧院的公关主任
Hello, Lynne.
你好林恩。
Hello.
你好
Now, the theatre is reopening soon after its three - year redevelopment programme, isn't it?
在3年的重建计划之后，现在，剧院很快要重新开张了吧？
That's right, and there are a lot of improvements.
是的，有了很多的改进。
The first thing people will see when they go in is that the foyer has been repainted in the original green and gold.
人们进入后首先看到的是大厅被重新刷成了最初的绿色和金色。
Then the box office has been reoriented with its own access from the side of the building instead of through the foyer, which means it can be open longer hours, and has more space, too.
售票处的入口改建到建筑物的侧面，而不通过大厅，这意味着它可以开放更长的时间并具有更大的空间。
The shop that used to be in the foyer, which sold books and CDs, is the one part of the redevelopment which isn't yet complete.
曾经在大厅出售书籍和光盘的商店是重建的一部分，但还没有完成。
The plan is to find new premises for it near the theatre, and we've had difficulty finding somewhere suitable.
计划是为它在剧院附近找新的地点，但很难找到合适的。
We hope to reopen the shop in the next few months.
我们希望商店能在接下来的几个月重新开张。
Will audiences find any difference in the auditorium?
礼堂会让观众感到不一样吗？
Yes, we've increased the leg-room between the rows.
是的 所以我们把两排之间的空间增加了。
This means that there are now fewer seats but we're sure audiences will be much happier.
这意味着座位变少了，但是我们确定观众肯定会更高兴。
And we've installed air conditioning, so it won't get so hot and stuffy.
我们还装了空调，所以里面不会又热又闷。
We already had a few seats which were suitable for wheelchair users, and now there are twice as many, which we hope will meet demand.
我们曾有一些为轮椅使用者提供的座椅，现在这种座椅增加了两倍，我们希望可以满足需求。
Something else that will benefit audiences is the new lifts.
还有一项使观众受益的是新电梯。
The two we used to have were very small and slow.
我们曾经用的两个电梯又小又旧，
They've now gone, and we've got much more efficient ones.
它们现在已经没再用了，我们有了更好用的。
Anything for the performers?
关于表演者呢？
Yes, we've made a number of improvements backstage.
是的，我们对后台进行了很多改进。
The small, dark dressing rooms we used to have have been converted into two large airy rooms, so they're much more comfortable now.
我们过去用的小化妆间变成了两间通风很好的大房间，所以他们现在应该会感到很舒适。
And the state - of - the - art electronic sound and lighting systems have been installed.
而且还装上了最先进的电子音响和照明设施。
OK, so what's the first play that audiences can see when the theatre reopens?
好的，当剧院重新开张的时候，观众看到的第一场演出是什么？
We've got a very exciting production of Peter Shaffer's Royal Hunt of the Sun, which is currently touring the country.
我们有一个非常激动人心的节目《彼得·谢弗的皇家狩猎——太阳》，这是目前在全国巡演的剧目。
That starts on October the 13th and runs till the 19th.
它于10月13日开始，直到19日结束。
We're experimenting a bit with the time the curtain goes up.
我们对演出的开始时间做了一点尝试。

We used to start all our performances at 7:30, but that made it difficult for people to go home by public transport, so instead we're beginning at 7, because at 9:45, when it finishes, there are still buses running.
我们过去通常在7:30开始演出，但是人们在之后很难乘坐公共交通回家，所以我们改为7点开始，因为当9:45结束时，人们仍可以乘坐公交车。
Tickets are already selling fast.
票卖的很快。
The Friday and Saturday performances sold out almost immediately and, in fact, now there are only tickets for Monday and Thursday.
周五、周六的演出票几乎立即售空，事实上，现在只有周一和周四的票了。
How much are they?
票价是多少？
We've introduced a simpler price structure.
我们引进了一套更简单的价格体系。
Ticket prices used to range from ￡6 to ￡30 but now they're all ￡18.
票价的范围曾是6英镑到30英镑，但是现在都变为18英镑。
They're available from the box office, in person, by phone, fax or post, or online.
人们可以通过电话、传真、邮寄或网上订票。
OK, Lynne, now if you'd like to give the contact details for the theatre...
好的，林恩，现在如果你愿意给出剧院的联系方式……

127.title: C6-Test 1-Section 3
transcript: Hi, can I help you?
你好，需要帮忙吗？
I was told to come here, because I'd like to talk to someone about taking a management course.
有人告诉我来这里，因为我想咨询关于管理课的事。
Right. I'm one of the tutors, so I should be able to help you.
是的，我是其中一位老师，所以我可以帮你。
Oh, good.
好的。
My name's Brian Ardley.
我叫Brian Ardley。
I've decided to enroll on a part - time management course.
我想参加在职管理课程。
A friend of mine took one last year, and recommended it to me.
我的一个朋友去年参加了这个课程并推荐给我。
Right.
好的。
Is there anything I should do before the course, like reading or anything?
课之前我有什么要做的吗，比如阅读或其他之类的？
We prefer to integrate reading with the course, so we don't give out a reading list in advance.
我们更喜欢将阅读和课程结合在一起，所以我们不提前发放阅读书目。
But we like people to write a case study, describing an organisation they know.
但是我们希望同学写案例，描述一个他们了解的组织。
I've already done that, as my friends told me you wanted one.
我已经写了，我的朋友说过你们需要这个。
But would it be possible to sit in on a teaching session, to see what it's like?
但是可以让我去教学课试听看看吗？
I haven't been a student for quite a while.
我不当学生很久了。
Fine.
好的
Just let me know which date, and I'll arrange it with the tutor.
告诉我合适的时间，我会为你安排老师。
Now, could I ask you about the college facilities, please?
现在我能问一下学校的设施怎么样吗？
Anything in particular?
具体想问什么？
Well, the course is one day a week, all day, isn't it?
一周有一天的课程，是全天的吧？
So presumably it's possible to buy food?
那么应该有地方吃饭吧？
Yes, the refectory's open all day.
是的，食堂全天开放。
Does it cater for special diets?
能满足特殊的饮食要求吗？
I have some food allergies.
我对一些食物过敏。
Provided you warn the refectory in advance, it won't be a problem.
我们会提前通知餐厅人员的，所以不用担心。
Good.
好。
What about facilities for young children?
小孩用的设施呢？
I'd like to bring my daughter here while I'm studying.
我想在我上课的时候把我的女儿带来。
How old is she?
她多大？
Three.
三岁。
Then she's eligible to join the nursery, which is supervised by a qualified Nursery Nurse.
她可以去育儿室，由合格的保育员负责看护。
The waiting list for a place is quite long though, so you ought to apply now.
候补名单上的人很多，所以你现在就该申请了。
OK.
好的。
I don't know if our careers advice service would be of any interest to you?
我不知道你对我们的职业咨询服务感兴趣吗？
Yes, it might help me decide how to develop my career after the course.
是的。它也许能帮我在结课后决定如何发展我的事业。
The centre has a lot of reference materials and staff qualified to give guidance on n one - to - one basis.
中心有很多参考资料而且员工也能会为你提供一对一的指导。
I noticed a fitness centre next to the college.
我注意到大学旁边有一个健身中心，
Is that for students?
是为学生准备的吗？
It's open to everyone, but students pay an annual fee that's much less than the general public pay.
那里对每一个人开放，但是学生付的年费要比大众付的钱少很多。
And presumably the college library stocks newspapers and journals, as well as books?
大学图书馆里应该不但存有很多书，还有报纸、杂志？
Yes, and there's also an audio-visual room, for viewing and listening to videos, cassettes, and so on.
是的，这里还有视听教室供大家看视频、听磁带等等。
Is there also access to computers?
在这里可以用电脑吗？
Yes, your tutor will need to arrange with the technical support team for you to get a password, so ask him or her about it when you start the course.
是的，你的老师会安排技术支持组帮你拿到密码，所以在你开课的时候咨询他/她。
OK.
好的。
By the way, do you know about our Business Centre?
对了，你知道我们的商务中心吗？
No.
不知道。
What's that?
那是什么？
It's a training resource—a collection of materials for people to study on their own, or use in their own organisations.
它是培训资源，帮助学生自学或用在他们自己的公司上的资料。
Uhuh.
额
You mean books and videos?
你是说书和视频？
Yes and manuals for self - study.
是的，还有自学手册。
Plus a lot of computer - based materials, so people can work through them at their own speed, and repeat anything they aren't sure about.
另外还有很多计算机基础教材，所以人们可以根据自己的速度学习并且在不懂得地方反复地看。
And you can hire laptops to use in your own home or workplace as well as printers that you can take away.
你可以租一个笔记本回家用或在办公室用，还可以带走一台打印机。
Does it have anything that I could use to improve my study skills?
有什么可以帮我提高学习技巧的吗？
I don't have much idea about report writing, and I'm sure I'll need it on the course.
我对撰写报告不太了解，我肯定在课堂上会用到它。
Oh yes, there's plenty of useful material.
是的，这方面有很多资料。
Just ask one of the staff.
问下工作人员就行。
Does the centre cover all the main areas of business?
中心覆盖商业的主要领域吗？
Yes, topics like finance, and of course marketing that's a popular one.
是的，像金融，还有市场营销都是比较火的类别
Local managers seem to queue up to borrow the videos!
本地的管理者好像已经排队借阅录像了！
So it isn't just for students, then?
那么，这不只是为学生准备的？
No, it's for members only, but anyone can join.
不，只为会员提供，但是任何人都可以加入。
How much does it cost?
这要花多少钱？
￡100 a year for a company, and ￡50 for an individual - with no discount for students, I'm afraid.
公司每年100英镑，个人是50英镑，恐怕对学生没有折扣。
That's very helpful.
这很有帮助。
Well, I think that's all.
我想就是这样了。
I'd better go home and fill in the enrollment form.
我最好回家去填报名表了。
Thanks for all your help.
谢谢你的帮助。
You're welcome.
不客气。
Goodbye.
再见。
Goodbye.
再见。

128.title: C6-Test 1-Section 4
transcript: In the last few weeks, we've been looking at various aspects of the social history of London, and this morning we're continuing with a look at life in the area called the East End.
在过去的几周，我们一直在看伦敦社会史的各个方面。这个早上，我们继续看一看东伦敦的生活情况。
I'll start with a brief history of the district, and then focus on life in the first half of the twentieth century.
我先简单介绍一下这个地区的历史，然后主要关注那里20世纪上半叶的生活。

Back in the first to the fourth centuries AD, when the Romans controlled England, London grew into a town of 45,000 people, and what's now the East End - the area by the river Thames, and along the road heading north - east from London to the coast - consisted of farmland with crops and livestock which helped to feed that population.
公元一世纪至公元四世纪，当罗马帝国统治英格兰时，伦敦发展成了一个拥有4万5千人的城镇，也就是现今的东伦敦。它位于泰晤士河附近，沿着伦敦东北走向的公路一直通向海岸，这里的耕种及畜牧业是当时人民赖以为生的方式。

The Romans left in 410, at the beginning of the fifth century, and from then onwards the country suffered a series of invasions by tribes from present - day Germany and Denmark, the Angles, Saxons and Jutes, many of whom settled in the East End.
罗马于五世纪初的410年离开，从那以后国家遭受了一系列部落的入侵，从如今的德国、丹麦到盎格鲁人，撒克逊人和朱特人。他们中的许多人居住在东伦敦。
The technology they introduced meant that metal and leather goods were produced there for the first time.
他们引进的技术意味着金属和皮革品首次在那里制造。
And as the East End was by the river, ships could transport goods between there and foreign markets.
东伦敦沿河，船只可以在这里和国外市场之间运输货物。
In the eleventh century, in 1066 to be precise, the Normans conquered England, and during the next few centuries London became one of the most powerful and prosperous cities in Europe.
在11世纪，确切的说是1066年，诺曼人征服了英格兰。在接下来的几个世纪，伦敦变成了欧洲最强大繁荣的城市之一。

The East End benefited from this, and because there were fewer restrictions there than in the city itself, plenty of newcomers settled there from abroad, bringing their skills as workers, merchants or money - lenders during the next few hundred years.
东伦敦受益于此，而且当地的限制并没有伦敦市内多。许多人在接下来的几个世纪中从国外来这里定居，有工人、商人或是放贷者。
In the sixteenth century the first dock was dug where ships were constructed, eventually making the East End the focus of massive international trade.
在16世纪，第一个码头被挖建，在那里建造船只，最终使伦敦东区成为大规模国际贸易的中心。
And in the late sixteenth century, when much of the rest of England was suffering economically, a lot of agricultural workers came to the East End to look for alternative work.
16世纪末期，英格兰大部分地区经济不景气，很多农业劳动者来到伦敦东区寻找替代工作。
In the seventeenth century, the East End was still a series of separate, semi - rural settlements.
在17世纪，伦敦东部仍然是一块偏僻的、半乡村的居住地。
There was a shortage of accommodation, so marshland was drained and built on to house the large numbers of people now living there.
那时房屋紧缺，因此沼泽地被抽干用来建造房屋，为那里的很多人提供了住处。
By the nineteenth century London was the busiest port in the world, and this became the main source of employment in the East End.
到19世纪，伦敦成为世界上最繁忙的港口，这也成了东伦敦主要的就业来源。

Those who could afford to live in more pleasant surroundings moved out, and the area became one where the vast majority of people lived in extreme poverty, and suffered from appalling sanitary conditions.
那些能够负担得起更好居住环境的人搬走了，这个地区成为了极贫困人群的居住地，并遭受着恶劣的卫生问题的折磨。
That brief outline takes us to the beginning of the twentieth century, and now we'll turn to housing.
这些描述带我们来到了20世纪初，现在让我们看看住房情况。
At the beginning of the century, living conditions for the majority of working people in East London were very basic indeed.
在这个世纪初，东伦敦的大多数工人的居住条件都非常简单。
Houses were crowded closely together and usually very badly built, because there was no regulation.
房子都挤在一起，通常房屋的质量很差，因为当时没有相关的管理规定。
But the poor and needy were attracted by the possibility of work, and they had to be housed.
但是那些贫困和生活艰难的人被这里的工作机会吸引而来，而且他们也需要有地方住。
It was the availability, rather than the condition, of the housing that was the major concern for tenants and landlords alike.
对房东及房客来说，最重要的问题是有没有房子住，而不是房子的条件如何。
Few houses had electricity at this time, so other sources of power were used, like coal for the fires which heated perhaps just one room.
时很少有房子能用上电，因此人们开始利用其他能源，例如用煤点火，但那也只能为一个房间供暖。
Of course, the smoke from these contributed a great deal to the air pollution for which London used to be famous.
当然，伦敦也因为这些烟雾造成的空气污染而闻名。
A tiny, damp, unhealthy house like this might well be occupied by two full families, possibly including several children, grandparents, aunts and uncles.
这样一个狭窄、潮湿、不宜健康的房子也许会住着两大家子人，可能包括几个孩子，祖父母，姑妈和叔叔。
Now, before I go on to health implications of this way of life, I'll say something about food and nutrition.
现在，在我谈论这种生活方式对健康的影响之前，我先讲讲食物和营养的问题。

13.title: C13-Test 3-Section 1
transcript: Hello, Linda speaking.
你好，我是琳达。
Oh hi, Linda. This is Matt Brooks.
哦,嗨,琳达。这是马特·布鲁克斯。
Alex White gave me your number.
亚历克斯·怀特给了我你的电话号码。
He said you’d be able to give me some advice about moving to Banford.
他说你可以给我一些关于搬到班福德的建议。
Yes, Alex did mention you. How can I help?
是的，亚历克斯提到过你。我能帮什么忙吗?
Well, first of all - which area to live in?
嗯，好的。首先，住在哪个地区呢?
Well. I live in Dalton, which is a really nice suburb - not too expensive, and there’s a nice park.
好。我住在道尔顿，这是一个很好的郊区——不太贵，而且有一个很好的公园。
Sounds good. Do you know how much it would be to rent a two bedroom flat there?
听起来不错。你知道在那里租一套两居室要多少钱吗?
Yeah, you should be able to get something reasonable for 850 pounds per month.
哦，是的，你应该可以以每月850英镑的合理价格租到房子，
That’s what people typically pay.
一般都是这个价格。
You certainly wouldn’t want to pay more than 900 pounds.
你肯定不会想租超过900英镑的房子，
That doesn’t include bills or anything.
而且还不包括账单或其它东西。
No. That sounds alright I’ll definitely have a look there.
是的，听起来不错，我一定会去看看的。
Are the transport links easy from where you live?
你住的地方交通方便吗?
Well, I’m very lucky.
嗯,我很幸运。
I work in the city centre so I don’t have to use public transport. I go by bike.
我在市中心工作，所以我不用乘坐公共交通工具。我骑自行车。
Oh, I wish I could do that. Is it safe to cycle around the city?
哦，我希望我也是这样，在城里骑自行车安全吗?
Yes, it’s fine. And it keeps me fit.
是的,很安全。骑行让我保持健康。
Anyway, driving to work in the city centre would be a nightmare because there’s hardly any parking.
无论如何，开车去市中心上班将是一场噩梦，
And the traffic during the rush hour can be bad.
高峰时段的交通也很糟糕。
I’d be working from home but I’d have to go to London one or two days a week.
我在家工作，但我每周得去伦敦一、两天。
Oh, that’s perfect. Getting to London is no problem.
哦，那很好呀。去伦敦没有问题。
There’s a fast train every 30 minutes which only takes 45 minutes.
每30分钟有一班快车，只要45分钟。
That’s good.
这就好。
Yeah, the train service isn’t bad during the week.
工作日的火车服务并不差，
And they run quite late at night It’s weekends that are a problem.
而且它们会开很晚，只是周末会有问题。
They’re always doing engineering work and you have to take a bus to Hadham and pick up the train there, which is really slow.
周末总是会有工程问题，你必须坐公共汽车去哈德汉姆，从那里搭火车，那真的很慢。
But other than that, Banford’s a great place to live.I’ve never been happier
但除此之外，班福德是一个很好的居住地。我从来没有这么开心过。
There are some nice restaurants in the city centre and a brand new cinema which has only been open a couple of months.
市中心有几家不错的餐馆，还有一家新开了几个月的电影院。
There’s a good arts centre too.
也有一个很好的艺术中心。
Sounds like Banford’s got it all.
听起来班福德什么都有。
Yes! We’re really lucky.
是的，我们非常幸运，
There are lots of really good aspects to living here.
生活在这里真的好处多多。
The schools are good and the hospital here is one of the best in the country.
学校也不错，这里的医院是全国最好的医院之一。
Everyone I know who’s been there’s had a positive experience.
我认识的每个人都有过积极的经历。
Oh, I can give you the name of my dentist too in Bridge Street, if you’re interested.
哦，如果你感兴趣的话，我可以把布里奇街牙医的名字告诉你，
I’ve been going to him for years and I’ve never had any problems.
我去他那看牙已有好几年了，自此什么问题都没有发生过。
Oh ok, thanks!
哦，好的，谢谢！
I’ll find his number and send it to you.
我找到他的号码就发给你。
Thanks, that would be really helpful.
谢谢，那你真的帮了大忙了。
Are you planning to visit Banford soon?
你打算很快就来班福德吗?
Yes. My wife and I are both coming next week.
是的。我和我妻子下周就要去了。
We want to make some appointments with estate agents.
我们想和房地产经纪人约个时间见面。
I could meet you if you like and show you around.
如果你愿意，我可以见见你，然后带你去四处看看。
Are you sure? We’d really appreciate that
你确定吗？我们会感激不尽的。
Either a Tuesday or Thursday is good for me, after 5.30.
5点半以后，星期二或星期四对我来说都可以。
Thursday’s preferable - Tuesday I need to get home before 6 pm.
星期四比较好——星期二我需要在下午6点以前回到家。
OK. Great. Let me know which train you’re catching and I’ll meet you in the café outside.
好的，那太好了，告诉我你要乘坐哪趟车，我就在外面的咖啡馆等你，
You can’t miss it. It’s opposite the station and next to the museum.
你不会错过它的，它就在车站的对面，博物馆的旁边。
Brilliant. I'll text you next week then. Thanks so much for all the advice.
太棒了。那我下周给你发短信。谢谢你的建议。
No problem. I’ll see you next week.
好的，下周见。

133.title: C6-Test 3-Section 1
transcript: Good morning.I'd like to open a bank account, please.
早上好。我想开个户。
Certainly.If you'd like to take a seat, I'll just get some details from you.It won't take long.
好的。你先坐，我先录入点信息不会耽误太久。
Thanks.
谢谢。
Is it a current account or a deposit account you wanted?
活期还是定期？
A current account.
活期。
Right.I've got the application form here then.
好的。我这里有一张申请表。
We have different types—I see you've got our leaflet there.
我们有很多种类，我看到你已经拿了一张宣传单了。
I've decided on the one called 'Select'.
我决定选这款“Select”。
Right, that's fine, so, first of all, can I have your full name please?
好的，首先，你能告诉我你的全名吗？
Yes, it's Pieter Henes.That's P-I-E-T-E-R.
是的。皮特·亨尼斯。PIETER。
Is it H-E-double N-E-S?
是H-E-N-N-E-S吗？
Uh, only one N actually.It's a less common spelling of the name.
只有一个N这样的拼写不常见
Oh, right.
好的。
OK.
好的。
And what's your date of birth please?
生日？
The twenty - seventh of the first, nineteen seventy - three.
Right.And will this be a joint account?
好的。要共同账号吗？
No, just myself.
不需要，就我自己。
OK, fine.And where are you living, Mr.Henes?
好的，你住哪里？
15, Riverside.
15号河畔。
Is that all one word?
一个词吗？
Yes.
是的。
Exeter?
Exeter？
Yes.
是的。
How long have you been at your present address?Er, is it more than two years?
住了多久了？超过两年了吗？
Ah, just two weeks actually.I only arrived in the country a month ago.I'm from Holland.
