就两周，我上个月才来到这里，我来自荷兰。
Oh, that's fine.But we normally ask for a previous address in that case.
没关系，我们通常要问下住址
Oh yes, well, it's Rielsdorf 2.That's R-I-E-L-S-D-O-R-F 2,Utrecht..
好的，地址是：R-i-e-l-s-d-o-r-f 2。R-i-e-l-s-d-o-r-f 2，
Holland OK.Thank you.Do you have a daytime telephone number?
荷兰 好的。谢谢有白天能打通的电话吗
Yes, I think the number at my office is six - oh - six - two - nine - five, Um, just a minute.I'd better check.
是的，我想我办公室的电话是606295，我最好确定一下
Oh, no sorry, six - one - six.I'm not used to it yet.
不是，是616，我还不习惯。
Would you like my home number too?
要我家的电话吗
Yes please.
是的，请说
It's seven - nine - six - four - three - one.
Are they both local numbers?
都是当地号码？
Yes.
是的
Right.And your occupation?
好的。职业？
Well, I'm in Britain as a project manager, but that's not my main job.I'm an engineer by profession.
我在英国做项目经理，但是这不是我的主要工作，我是一名工程师。
I see.I think I'll put that then.It's shorter!
明白，我写那个吧，比较短
Now we usually ask for a piece of information which we can use to check your identity, for security reasons.
出于安全考虑，我们通常要询问一些用来核对身份的信息。
You know, if you phone us.
如果你给我们打电话的话，
Like, erm, my wife's first name?
比如我妻子姓什么
Mother's might be better.It's less likely to be known.
最好是母亲姓什么，更少有人知道。
OK, Hers is Siti.
好，叫SITI
Siti?
SITI
Yes, S-I-T-I.It's Indonesian.
SITI 是印尼姓氏。
Fine.And how much would you like to open your account with? We usually ask for a minimum sum of￡50.That's about €75.
好，你要用多少钱开账户最少50英镑，大概75欧元。
Well, I'm going to transfer €2,000 from my Dutch account, just till I get paid.
在我收到付款后，我会从我的荷兰账户转来2000欧元。
In fact, I wanted to ask you about that.What's the best way to do it?
事实上我想问你什么是最好的的办法
It depends which bank you're with.
这取决于你是哪家银行。
It's the Fransen Bank in Utrecht.
乌特勒支的弗朗森银行。
OK, fine.I'll check that in a minute.
好的。我马上查一下。
If we have links with them we can do a direct transfer.But it's not a big problem either way.
如果我们有链接，我们可以直接为你转账这都不是问题。
Um, let's see.How often would you like to receive statements?
我看看你希望多久收到结算清单？
I haven't really thought.Um, what's the usual thing?
还没想过，通常多久？
It's up to you.Some people like them weekly.
看你，有些人喜欢一周一次。
Oh, no, that's too often.
太频繁了。
Can I have them sent, um, once a month?
一个月一次可以吗？
Yes, that's fine.Is there anything else?
可以的，还有其他事情吗？
I was thinking of registering for your internet service at some stage.
我想注册你们的网络服务。
Oh, yes.
包括了的。
Would you like me to send you information about that?
要我把相关信息发给你吗？
Please, yes.
好的。
And would you like to receive information about the bank's other services - insurance, loans, anything like that?
你想接收银行的其他服务信息吗，比如保险、贷款之类的？
Hmm, I don't think so. Thanks.
不用了，谢谢。
That's OK then.
好的。
And one last thing, if you agree...
如果你同意，下一件事是……

134.title: C6-Test 3-Section 2
transcript: Welcome, everybody, to the lovely house and gardens of Rosewood, once the home of the famous writer, Sebastian George.
欢迎大家来到美丽的Rosewood花园别墅，这里曾经是著名作家塞巴斯蒂安·乔治的家。
He bought the house in 1902 although he had first seen it two years earlier.
他在1902年买下这座房子，尽管他在两年前就见过这里了。
At that time the owners let it out to a tenant because George was too slow making up his mind to buy it.
那时房主将房子用于出租，因为乔治迟迟无法下定决心买下它。
When it came back on the market, there was no hesitation and he bought it immediately, for￡9,300, even though the house had no bathroom, no running water upstairs, and no electricity.
当房子重新出现在市场上时，他毫不犹豫地立即以9300英镑的价格买下了它，尽管房子没有浴室，楼上没有自来水，也没有电。
When he came here, he'd been married for ten years.
他来到这里时已经结婚十年了。
During that time, he'd become one of the most famous writers in the English - speaking world.
那时，他已经成为英语世界中最著名的作家之一。
His professional success was enormous, but his personal life wasn't as successful.
他的事业极其成功，但是他的个人生活却不那么成功。
He was no longer on speaking terms with his brother and had been devastated by the death at the age of seven of his elder daughter, Josephine.
他和他兄弟的关系不好，而且他7岁的大女儿约瑟芬的离世对他的打击极大。
Moving to Rosewood allowed the family to start a new life.
搬到Rosewood使这个家的生活有了新的开始。
George regarded Rosewood as a pure example of a traditional country house of this part of England and did some of his most successful writing here.
乔治将Rosewood看作是这一带英国传统乡村房屋的典范，而且他在这里写出了最成功的著作。
The house and its grounds became the family haven and their escape to privacy and quiet.
这座房子和这片土地成了家人的避难所，带他们躲入隐秘、幽静之中。

The walls, and the mullioned windows were built of the local sandstone, the tiles on the roofs and the bricks of the chimney stack were baked from local clay, and the wooden structures inside came from oak trees which grow around here.
墙壁和竖框窗户由当地的砂岩建造，房顶的瓦片和烟囱的砖块由当地的黏土烧制而成，屋内的木制结构来自周围的橡树。屋内的木制结构来自周围的橡树。
Now, please look at the map I've given you of the house and gardens.
现在，请看我给你们的关于房屋和花园的地图。
We're here at the Information Centre.
我们现在位于信息中心。
Follow the path marked with the arrow and the first area you come to is the orchard on your left.
根据箭头所指的路，首先你将看到左边的果园。

As you go further down the path, there's the kitchen garden on the right and as you go round the first sharp corner you will find, to your left, an area where different types of pear tree have been planted as well as some lovely flowers, and this is known as Pear Alley - designed by George himself.
当你再往前走时，你会看到右边的菜园。拐过第一个急弯后，你会看到左边种着不同种类的梨树和鲜花。这就是乔治自己设计的梨巷
Next to this is the greenhouse where some exotic plants and fruits are grown.
紧接着是温室，那里种植着一些异国的植物和水果。
Follow the path round the second corner and on your right, you will see the entrance to the Mulberry Garden with its 500 - year - old tree.
小路的第二个转角，你会发现你的右手边是桑树园的入口，那里有一株500年的古树。
Past the Mulberry Garden, follow the path until you reach the front of the house.
走过桑树园，沿着小路走，直到房子的前面。
I suggest you spend a good hour wandering around this lovely building.
我建议你们花些时间仔细看看这座美丽的房子。
A guide takes visitor groups round every two hours.
导游会每两小时带观光团游览一番。

If you would like to purchase any of George's books or other souvenirs, then leave the house by the side entrance, where you will find our shop, which is situated between the house and the garage which contains the magnificent old Rolls - Royce car which used to belong to George.
如果你想买一些乔治的书或其他纪念品，那么你从房子的侧门出去后就会看见我们的商店,位于房子和车库之间，车库里有一辆曾经属于乔治的华丽的老劳斯莱斯汽车。
I expect by this time you may also be in need of a rest and some refreshment.
我想这个时候你们也想休息一下，吃些茶点了。
Most visitors are, so why don't you visit the tea room on the far side of the garage?
那么你们为什么不去参观一下茶水间呢，它在车库的另一侧。
If you have time, there is a lovely walk down towards the River Dud well.
如果你还有时间，走过一段小路后你会看到Dudwell河。
For me, this is the best part of the estate.
对于我来说，那是这里最美的地方。
This isn't on the map but it is all clearly signposted.
地图上没有画，但是路上设有明显的路标。
You cross the field which spreads along the banks of the river.
你可以穿过沿着河畔的田野。
In spring, this area is well worth a visit.
在春天，这里非常值得参观。
Spend a minute or two watching the water pass by underneath as you cross the footbridge and then continue along the River Walk through the woodland.
当你走过桥上，花一两分钟观看脚下的河水，然后继续在树林中沿溪流而行。
On a hot summer's day the trees along this path provide welcome shade.
在炎热的夏季，这条路上的树木会带来大片的树荫。
Eventually you come to the water mill which used to provide the electricity for the house - only about four hours every evening - in George's time.
最后，你会看到用来给房屋供电的水车，在乔治居住的时候每晚只能供应4小时的电。

And, finally, for those of you who would like to see stunning views of the surrounding countryside and who are a little bit more energetic, when you return from the mill take the first turning on your left and climb up to the viewpoint.
最后，那些想看周围美丽的乡村景色和精力充沛的人，当你们从水车那里回来时，在第一个拐弯处左转并爬上去观看吧。
You won't regret it.
你不会后悔的
Enjoy your visit!
尽情享受吧！

135.title: C6-Test 3-Section 3
transcript: Lucy, we really need to get working on this marketing assignment.
露西，我们真的需要着手做这份市场调查了。
We've only got five weeks left to the end of term to design it, carry it out, and then write up the results.
我们距离期末只有五周时间去设计、实施和写下调查结果了。
Sure.
没问题。
Well, let's get started right now.
现在就开始
Let's go over the instructions.
先看下说明
What exactly do we have to do?
我们到底应该做些什么？
Well, it says here we have to look at one area of the entertainment industry.
上面写着我们必须调查娱乐业的一部分。
There's a list of the different types.
还有一份不同类型的列表。
What are they?
是什么
Music, cinema, theatre, sport, and eating out.
音乐，电影，戏剧，运动和外出就餐
Is that all?
就这些？
Looks like it.
看起来是的
So we choose one branch of the industry and then we look at how two different groups of people use it?
所以我们选择某一部分，然后调查两组不同的人是如何使用它的？
Is that right?
对吗
Yes.
是的。
And do we have to use any particular method to get our data?
我们需要用特殊方式获取数据吗？
Can we mail out a questionnaire, or do face - to - face interviews, or maybe even observations?
我们可以寄出问卷调查、面对面采访或者甚至是观察吗？
Well actually, it looks like we don't have a choice.
事实上，我们没有选择。
We have to do telephone interviews.
我们必须通过电话采访。
OK, so at least we don't have to waste time deciding between the different methods.
好的，至少我们不用在选择不同的方式上浪费时间。
Yeah, that's right.
没错
Oh, and the other requirement is the number of interviewees.
没错。另一个要求是受访者的数量。
Not too many, I hope.
希望不要太多
Ten?
十个
Twenty?
二十个
Well, we have to do two groups, remember, and it looks like we have to interview fifteen for each group.
我们要调查两组人，记得吗。看起来每一组我们要采访15个人。
That's thirty altogether then.
一共30人
It's going to take ages.
这要花很长时间
Yes, but remember we're working on this together, so we'll only have to do fifteen each.
是的，但是我们是一起工作，所以每个人只需负责十五人。
OK, so those are all the requirements?
好的，所以这些就是所有的要求了？
Yes, looks like it.
看起来是这样的
So, first, which area are we going to choose?
那么首先，我们要选择哪个领域？
My preference would be cinema, since that's where I spend most of my money.
我倾向于电影，因为我的多数钱都花在这上面。
Hmm, I don't think that's such a good idea.
我觉得这不是一个好主意。
I don't think there are huge differences in the market there.
这个市场的差异性不大。
I mean you get young and old, male and female, rich and poor all going to the same movies.
我是说年轻人、老年人，男人、女人，富人、穷人都会去看同一个电影。
Yeah, maybe you're right.
是的，也许你是对的。
Let's make it music then.
那么我们转向音乐
Right.
好的。
So, what two groups will we compare and contrast?
那么我要比较哪两组？
Male and female?
男人女人？
No.
没有。
Most of my female friends like the same music as me.
我的许多女性朋友喜欢的音乐和我一样
Different age groups would be much more likely to show up differences, I think.
我觉得不同的年龄更能体现出差异。
Yeah, I suppose you're right again.
是的，我想你又说对了。
I'll take some notes, shall I?
我来记笔记吧
So...age Groups.
年龄组
Well.
嗯
What do you think?
你怎么想
Maybe twenty - five or under for one group, and forty - five or over for the other group?
也许25岁或者以下是一组，45岁或以上是另一组？
That should show up differences.
这就能看出差异了
Right.
好的。
OK.
好的。
Next.
下一个。
How about the kind of music they like - let's give them some choices and then we can just tick boxes.
他们喜欢什么样的音乐？我们给他们一些选择，然后我们来做标记。
OK.
好的。
Let's have pop, jazz, folk, easy listening...what else?
那我们写上流行，爵士，民谣，轻音乐
Well, we should include classical.
应该包含古典音乐
Some people like it, you know.
有些人喜欢听它
OK.
好的。
OK.
好的。
And then we should have how they listen to music.
我们应该写上他们是如何听音乐的的
The medium.
媒介
Right. Let's include radio, CD and then I guess there's TV.
对，那写上收音机，光盘还有电视
What about concerts?
音乐会呢
You know, in pubs and halls.
像酒吧和礼堂里的那种
Oh yeah, we should include live music of course.
是的，我们应该加上现场音乐
OK, we're on a roll now!
好的，我们现在做的很顺利。
Next point could be about where they actually get their music.
下一个应该是他们从哪买音乐。
You mean like, do they buy it in music shops, or department stores?
你的意思是他们是在音像店还是百货公司买？
Yes, or download it from the Internet.
是的，或者从网上下载。
Right.
好的。
That could be for recorded music.
是的，可以是录的唱片。
Then we need another section for live music.
然后，我们需要调查现场音乐的部分。
Where do they go for that?
他们去哪听
OK.
好的。
Let's say disco, pub, club, concert hall...
好的，让我们写上舞厅、酒吧、俱乐部和音乐厅？
Or opera house!
歌剧院
And I guess we should include karaoke bars.
应该加上歌厅
Not many of them in this city!
在这个城市里面不多呀。
OK. We'll leave that out then.
好的，我们删去它。
So, what's left to do?
还剩什么
That's it.
就这些？
Well, now we can make a time - scale for doing it.
现在我们应该做一个时间表

136.title: C6-Test 3-Section 4
transcript: Good morning everyone.
大家早上好。
Last week we were looking at the hunter - gatherers in Ireland, across the Irish Sea from England.
上周我们介绍了与英格兰隔爱尔兰海相望的爱尔兰的原始狩猎族。
Today, we're going to move on to the period between four and six thousand years ago, known as the Neolithic period, which is when a total farming economy was introduced in Ireland.
今天，我们介绍4千至6千年前的新石器时代，正当农耕经济引入爱尔兰。
Now, there are several hypotheses about the origins of the first Neolithic settlers in Ireland but most of these contain problems.
现在有几种关于首批爱尔兰新石器时代定居者的假设，但是多数都存在问题。
For instance, there are considerable archaeological difficulties about the theory that they came from England.
有很多考古学上的困难，比如他们是如何从英格兰迁徙的。
The evidence doesn't really add up.
证据有些说不通。
But there are even greater practical problems about the theory that they came directly from continental Europe.
但是还有更大的实际问题是他们直接从欧洲大陆来。
For one thing, it's not clear just how sufficient numbers of men and women could have been transported to Ireland to establish a viable population.
首先，我们不知道这些大量的男男女女是如何抵达爱尔兰并繁衍生息的。
As you know, the hunter - gatherer economy which existed beforehand was based on small scattered groups.
如你所知，之前狩猎经济的存在是以小型分散族群为基础。
The farming economy would almost certainly have required much larger communities to do all the work needed to plant and tend sufficient crops to sustain them through the year.
农耕经济需要更多的人来种庄稼和打理庄稼，以维持他们一年的生活。
The early farmers kept various animals, including cattle and sheep.
早期的农民饲养各种动物，包括牛和羊。
There's also evidence of pigs, but it is possible that these could have been descended from the native wild species.
证据表明还有一些猪存在，但是可能是当地野生物种的后裔。
Now, we know from modern farming that if the level of breeding stock falls below about three hundred females, the future of the species locally is at risk.
现在，我们从现代农业得知，如果良种畜的水平低于300头雌性，那么当地的未来物种便岌岌可危。

So we must assume that from the beginnings of Neolithic farming the number of breeding sheep would have considerably exceeded three hundred, and the national cattle herd must have been of a similar size.
所以我们必须假定新石器时代农业开端的繁殖种羊在数量上超过300头，并且全国牛群的数量也必定相似。
The question is how these were brought to the area and where they came from.
问题是它们是如何引入到这里，而它们又来自何方。
It's usually suggested that the Neolithic settlers used skin - covered boats to transport livestock.
通常假设新石器时代的定居者使用动物皮肤制成船然后运输牲畜。
But this method would have severely restricted the range of the colonising fleets.
但是这种方法可能会严重影响移民舰队的规模。
The sheer volume of animal transport necessary means it's unlikely that this livestock could have been brought from anywhere further than England.
动物运输量意味着这些牲畜不可能来自于英格兰以外更远的地方。
What about crops?
那农作物呢
Well, two main cereal crops were introduced to Ireland during this time: wheat and barley, both in several varieties.
两种主要的谷物作物在这一时期引入爱尔兰：各种品种的小麦和大麦。
The main evidence for their presence consists of impressions on pottery, where a cereal grain accidentally became embedded in the surface of a pot before it was fired.
它们出现的重要证据来源于陶器上的压痕，谷粒在陶器没有烧制之前碰巧嵌在表面。
The grain itself was destroyed by the firing, but it left an impression on the pot which could be studied and identified by botanists.
大火破坏了谷粒，但是陶器上留下的印痕可以供植物学家研究、辨认。
Let's turn our attention now to the farming technology available at that time.
让我们现在看看那时的农耕技术。
Before the cereal crops could be planted, it would have been necessary to clear the forest and to break the ground by ploughing.
在谷物作物种植前要清理森林，用犁开垦土地。
The stone blade of a plough has been discovered during excavation in County Mayo in western Ireland.
在爱尔兰西部的梅欧郡发掘出了石刀的犁。
The body of the plough would have been of wood and could have been drawn by people, but it's also likely that cattle were used.
犁是木制的，可能由人拉着耕地，但是也可能是用牛犁地。
Now, the cultivation of crops and the husbandry of livestock brought about changes in people's lifestyle such as the type of shelters they made.
谷物的耕种和牲畜的饲养给人们的生活方式带来了变化，比如房屋的建筑类型。
For one thing, instead of moving from place to place they needed permanent dwellings.
首先，他们需要永久住所而不是不断的搬家。
The stone axes used to chop down trees to make these dwellings were far superior to any that the Stone Age hunter - gatherers used.
用于砍伐树木建造房屋的石斧要优于任何石器时代原始狩猎族用的工具。
To make the axes, sources of suitable stone had to be found and systematically exploited.
做斧子需要找到合适的石头来源和有计划的开发利用。
These so - called 'axe factories' were really quarries rather than factories, as the manufacture of the axes wasn't regularly performed on the quarry site.
这些所谓的“斧子工厂”实际上是一些采石场而不是工厂，这些斧子的制作没有规律。
However, after the axe had been chipped into shape, they needed water and sand for grinding and polishing, so a high mountainside wouldn't have been an appropriate place for this.
然而，在斧子打造成型后就需要水和沙来磨光，所以高的山坡不适合做此事。
So this final stage of the manufacture must have been carried out close to water and sure enough, there's ample evidence of this at coastal sites.
所以制造的最后一步要在水边进行，大量证据表明是在沿海地区。
Now it's clear that these Neolithic axes were transported all over Ireland, as well as to Scotland and the south of England.
现在很显然，这些新石器时代的斧子被运往爱尔兰的各处，还送到了苏格兰和英格兰南部。
It's not really surprising that axes from 'axe factories' in England have also been found in Ireland.
英格兰工厂出的斧子能在爱尔兰找到也就不新奇了。
At the very least, this indicates that there was a link between the two islands during that period.
至少这表明在那个时期两个岛有联系。
One of the most useful innovations of the colonisers was pottery making, which was quite unknown to Irish hunter - gatherers.
其中一个殖民者最有用的发明是陶艺制作，这对爱尔兰的原始狩猎族来说一无所知。
The pottery was probably made by shaping clay into a ball with the hand, and then hollowing it until the walls were the right thickness.
陶瓷可能是靠手将粘土弄成球形，然后掏空中间直到陶瓷壁达到合适的厚度。
After firing, the outside was often polished.
烧制后，外侧通常要抛光。
This would have helped the pots to retain water, as they weren't glazed.
这能让那些罐子存水，因为它们还没上过釉。
Now we know that the clay used usually came from local sources, which suggests that manufacture was on a fairly small scale, even though thousands of fragments are usually found at Neolithic sites.
现在我们知道粘土通常来自当地的资源，这意味着只是小范围的制造，
In the course of time decoration began to appear.
随着时间的推移，装饰开始出现。
At first, this looked like a series of stitches and was just around the tops of the pots.
最初看起来像是在陶器的顶端围绕着一系列的针脚。
This could have been an imitation of earlier vessels which were made of leather sewn onto wood.
这可能是模仿早期的一些容器，把皮子缝到木头上。
Then eventually pots with decoration all over...
然后最终出现了全身都有装饰的罐子……

14.title: C13-Test 3-Section 2
transcript: So if you're one of those people who hasn't found the perfect physical activity yet -
如果你也还没有找到最适合自己的体育活动，
here are some things to think about which might help you make the right decision for you.
以下是一些需要考虑的事情，可以帮助你做出正确决定。
The first question to ask yourself is whether you would enjoy training in a gym.
首先要问自己的问题是，是否喜欢在健身房锻炼。
Many people are put off by the idea of having to fit a visit to the gym into their busy day -
许多人因为想到在忙碌了一天之后还得去健身房而不愿去 -
you often have to go very early or late as some gyms can get very crowded.
健身房还经常人满为患，只能早点去或晚点去。
But with regular training you'll see a big difference in a relatively short space of time.
但是只要定期锻炼，在短期内就可以看到明显的效果。
Running has become incredibly popular in recent years.
近几年来，跑步变得非常受欢迎。
That's probably got a lot to do with the fact that it's a very accessible form of exercise - anyone can run
这很可能是因为这是一种很方便的锻炼方式
even if you can only run a few metres to begin with.
即使一开始只能跑很短的距离。
But make sure you get the right shoes – it's worth investing in a high quality pair and they don't come cheap.
但是一定要确保穿对鞋子 - 一双高质量的鞋子是非常值得的，虽然这种鞋子价格并不便宜。
Another great thing about running is that you can do it at any time of day or night - the only thing that may stop you is snow and ice.
跑步的另一个好处就是，不论是白天还是晚上，任何时候都可以跑步 - 是非是雨雪天气。
Swimming is another really good way to build fitness.
游泳是另一种健身的好方法。
What attracts many people is that you can swim in an indoor pool at any time of year.
很多人青睐游泳的原因是一年四季室内游泳池都可以游泳。
On the other hand it can be quite boring or solitary - it's hard to chat to people while you're swimming lengths.
另一方面，游泳可能有点无聊或者孤独 - 你游泳时，很难与人聊天。
Cycling has become almost as popular as running in recent years.
近年来，骑自行车几乎变得和跑步一样流行。
That's probably because as well as improving their fitness, many people say being out in the fresh air in a park or in the countryside can be fun, provided the conditions are right of course —
这可能是因为除了健身，很多人认为骑自行车可以让人呼吸到公园或乡村的新鲜空气，
only fanatics go out in the wind and rain!
只要不刮风或下雨 - 除非是自行车迷。
Yoga is a good choice for those of you looking for exercise which focuses on developing both a healthy mind and body.
对于那些正在寻找锻炼项目的人来说，瑜伽是一个很多的选择，瑜伽主要是培育健康的身心。
It's a good way of building strength and with the right instructor,
这是一种锻炼力量的好方法，并且有个好教练，
there's less chance of hurting yourself than with other more active sports.
相对于其他更剧烈的运动，练瑜伽身体受伤的可能性更小。
But don't expect to find it easy - it can be surprisingly challenging, especially for people who aren't very flexible.
但是不要以为瑜伽很简单 - 它可能会出乎意料的难，尤其是对那些身体不太灵活的人来说。
Getting a personal trainer is a good way to start your fitness programme.
由私人教练指导是健身的好方法。
Obviously there can be significant costs involved.
虽然费用很高。
But if you've got someone there to encourage you and help you achieve your goals, you're less likely to give up.
但是如果有个人在旁边鼓励你、帮助你，那么你就更不可能放弃。
Make sure you get someone with a recognised qualification, though, or you could do yourself permanent damage.
尽管如何，一定要确保私教有合格资历，否则可能会受到永久性伤害。
Whatever you do, don't join a gym unless you're sure you'll make good use of it.
无论你是做什么工作的，除非你确定健身房适合你自己，否则不要随意加入。
So many people waste lots of money by signing up for membership and then hardly ever go.
很多人花大价钱办理了会员，结果几乎一次也没去过。
What happens to their good intentions?
他们的出发点哪去了？
I don't think people suddenly stop caring about improving their fitness, or decide they have more important things to do.
我不认为这些人突然对健身不关心了，或者认为有更要的事情要做。
I think people lose interest when they don't think they're making enough progress.
我认为，当人们认为自己进展缓慢，可能就不愿去了。
That's when they give up hope and stop believing they'll ever achieve their goals.
这时他们就会放弃希望，不再相信自己可以实现目标。
Also, what people sometimes don't realise when they start is that it takes a lot of determination and hard work to keep training week after week and lots of people don't have that kind of commitment.
而且，人们在开始的时候并没有意识到，需要很大的决心以及大量的努力才能坚持日复一日、周复一周的锻炼，而许多人没有这种决心和毅力。
One thing you can do to help yourself is to set manageable goals - be realistic and don't push yourself too far.
你可以做得一件事是设定可管理的目标 - 现实一点，不要制定自己无法企及的目标。
Some people advise writing goals down, but I think it's better to have a flexible approach.
有人建议把目标写下来，但我认为灵活变通一点会更好。
Give yourself a really nice treat every time you reach one of your goals.
每次达到目标后，好好奖励一下自己。
And don't get too upset if you experience setbacks - it's a journey
如果遇到挫折，不要太难过，
there are bound to be difficulties along the way.
一段旅程里一定会遇到困难的。

141.title: C5-Test 1-Section 1
transcript: Good afternoon, Dreamtime Travel.
下午好，梦幻时光旅行社
How can I help you?
我能帮你做什么
Oh hello.
你好
I'm interested in the holidays you offer along the coast near here.
我对你们提供的海岸假期感兴趣
Yes.We operate several tours up the coast.Where in particular did you want to go
对，我们沿着海岸线经营几条旅程，你想去哪一个？
Well, I like the sound of the holiday that mentioned whales.
我喜欢有一个关于鲸鱼的假期
Was it Whale 'Watching'?
有一个“观看鲸鱼”的路线吗？
Oh, that's our Whale Watch Experience.
那是我们的观看鲸鱼之旅
It's very popular and it's based in a lovely little town with nice beaches.
它很受欢迎而且是在一个漂亮的小镇上，有着美丽的海滩。
Oh right, and how long does it last?
好的，这趟旅行要多长时间？
It's two days - that includes four hours' travel time each way from here.
两天时间，其中包括4个小时从这里启程的时间。
Good, I don't want to be away any longer than that.
很好，我不想离开的时间比这个更久。
So is that by coach?
那么是乘坐长途汽车去吗
Actually it's by minibus.
事实上是坐小巴去
We like to keep those tours small and personal so we don't take a whole coachload of people.
我们希望提供小型、私人化的行程，所以我们不会载着一整辆长途汽车的人。
In fact, we only take up to fifteen people on this tour, although we do run it with just twelve or thirteen.
事实上，我们的这项旅行只接纳15人，但是12或13个人也可以。
Oh, right.
好的
So do you run these tours often?
那么你们经常有这些旅行吗
Well it depends on the time of year.
这取决于一年中的时间
Of course in peak times like the summer holidays we do them every weekend, but at the moment it's usually once a month at most.
在旺季，如夏日暑假的时候，我们当然每周末都有，但是平时我们最多一个月一次。
And when is the next one going?
下一次是什么呢
Mmm, let me see.
让我看看
Um, there's one in three weeks' time which is April the 18th, and then we don't have another one until June the 2nd.
三周后有一次是在4月18日，之后直到6月2日才有了，
All right, and is April a good time to go?
好的，四月是去那里的好时间吗？
Pretty good, though the really good time is later in the year.
很不错，尽管最好的时间是一年中的晚些时间。
I have to say though that the whale sighting is only one of the many things offered.
不得不说观看鲸鱼只是众多项目中的一个
Really?
真的吗
Yes.
是的
The hotel itself where you stay has great facilities.
你住的酒店有很棒的设施
It's called The Pallisades.
它叫做The Pallisades
The Paris what?
巴黎什么
No, it's actually The Pallisades, P - A - L - L - I - S - A - D - E - S.
不，它叫做The Pallisades，P-A-L-L-I- S-A-D-E -S，
It's right on the main beach there.
它就在海滩上
Oh, I see.
我懂了
All of the rooms have nice views and the food is really good there too.
所有房间都能看到很好的景色，吃的东西也不错
Oh right.
好
And what about the other things?
那么其他的事情呢
You know, that are included in the price.
你知道，包括在这个价钱里的事情。
Oh, there are lots of things.
还有很多事情

If you don't want to do the whale watch cruise, your guide will take anyone who is interested either on a bushwalk through the national park near the hotel, and there's no extra charge for that, or on a fishing trip.
如果你不想乘船观看鲸鱼，你的导游将带你到酒店附近的国家公园漫步，这项活动不收取额外费用，或者带你去钓鱼。
That's an extra $12 I think.
我想这个会收取额外的12美元
And there's also a reptile park in town - that costs more or less the same.
另外在小镇上还有爬行动物公园，价钱跟这个差不多。
No, I think I'd prefer whales to snakes.
不，我想比起蛇，我更喜欢鲸鱼。
Yeah.And if you just want to relax you are free to sit by the hotel pool or go down the beach.
如果你想休息，你可以免费去酒店的泳池或海滩，
Oh, and they also have tennis courts at the hotel, but you have to pay for those by the hour.
酒店还有网球场，但是你得按小时付费。
But there are table tennis tables downstairs and they're part of the accommodation package.
但是楼下有兵乓球桌，住宿费里包括这一项，
Just speak to your guide.
所以只要和导游说一声就行
Well that sounds good.
听起来不错
So how much is the basic tour price?
那么基础旅游价格是多少
At this time of year it's usually around $300 but let me check.
一年中的这个时节是在300美元左右，但是让我查查，
Er...oh，it's actually $280.
事实上是280美元
And the next tour, are there any places on that one?
下一趟旅行还有空缺吗
How many people is it for?
要去多少人
There are two of us.
-两个人
Yes, that should be fine.
好的，没问题。
Can I just mention that we require all bookings to be made at least fourteen days before you travel to avoid cancellations of tours.
为了防止取消行程，我们要求所有预定至少在旅行前的14天做好。
And if you cancel within seven days of departure you will have to pay 50% of your total booking.
如果你在出发前7天内取消，你将不得不付50%的预定费，
OK.
好的
And you also need to pay a 20% deposit at the time of booking.
预订时，你还得付20%的押金。
Can I pay that by credit card?
我能用信用卡支付吗
Yes, you can.
可以的
All right, what I'll do is I'll talk to my partner and get back to you.
好的，我跟我的伙伴说一声，然后再打给你。
Fine.
好的
So I'll make a provisional booking, shall I?
那么我先做一个临时预定
Two for the Whale Watch Experience.
两个观看鲸鱼之旅
Let me issue you with a customer reference number for when you call back.
我给你一个客户咨询号以便你下次打回来查询
Do you have a pen?
你有笔吗？
Yes.
有
OK, it's three nine seven, four five, T.
That's T for tango.
Tango（探戈舞）的那个T
When you call back, ask to speak to the Tour Manager, that's me, Tracy.
再打来时就找领队，就是我，特蕾西，-好的，
Fine, I will.
我会的

142.title: C5-Test 1-Section 2
transcript: Hello and welcome to today's 'Buyer Beware' programme, where we give you some tips on how to spend your money wisely.
你好，欢迎来到今天的《购物要当心》节目。我们将向你介绍几点如何明智花钱的技巧。
Now, in today's show we're looking at beds for children and babies.
我们将在今天的节目介绍婴儿床
Let's start by looking at baby cots, that's for children of up to three years old.
让我们从3岁小孩的婴儿床开始。
We tested three different cots all in the budget price range and, as usual, we will feature the good points, the problems and our verdict.
我们在预算价格内测试了3种不同的小床。像平时一样，我们将重点介绍它们的优点、问题和我们的结论。
The first cot we looked at was by Baby Safe and it had several good points to recommend it.
我们看的第一张小床出自“婴儿安全”，它有几点好处值得推荐。
Our testers liked the fact that it had four wheels, so it was easy to move around.
我们的测试者喜欢它有四个轱辘，所以很好移动。
The only slight problems with this cot were that it had no brakes, but they didn't think that mattered too much.
唯一的小缺憾是这张小床没有刹车，但是他们认为这不太重要。

At first they were a bit concerned about the side bar, because they felt babies could trap their fingers in it, but our testers felt that this was unlikely to happen so they have given this one a verdict of 'satisfactory'.
开始他们有点担心侧栏，因为他们感觉婴儿的手指可能会卡在里面，但是我们的测试者认为这是不可能的，所以他们对这张床的评价是“满意”。
The next cot was by Choice Cots and this time our testers were pleased to find a cot which is simple to put together - unlike others we looked at.
第二张小床来自“精选床”，这次我们的测试者很高兴的发现这个床不像其他床一样，这个很容易组装。
On the minus side, our testers did not like the fact that the side of the cot did not drop down, making it difficult to pick up newborn babies.
从坏的方面讲，我们的测试者不喜欢小床的两侧不能降下来，这很难抱起新生儿。
However, the real problem with this cot was the space between the bars; our testers found they were too wide and a baby could easily trap his head.
然而，真正的问题是小床侧栏间的距离。我们的测试者发现它们太宽了，婴儿的头很容易卡在里面。
We felt this was a real safety hazard and so we have labelled this one dangerous, I'm afraid.
我们觉得这是一个安全隐患，恐怕我们对它的结论是“危险”。
And finally better news for the Mother's Choice cot.
最后，好消息是来自“母亲的选择”的小床。
This cot was slightly different in that, although the side bar did not drop down, the base could be raised or lowered into two different positions making it safe as well as convenient.
这张小床有点不同，尽管侧栏不能下降，但是它的底座可以朝两个不同的方向升高或降低，使小床安全又方便。

The negatives for this one were quite minor; the only niggle everyone had was the fact that it has no wheels and the only other problem anyone could find, was that there were pictures which were simply stuck on and so could easily become detached.
它的缺点微不足道，每个人都能挑出的问题是它没有轮子。另一点是产品上面的图片只是简单的黏贴，容易脱落。
The makers have now promised to discontinue this practice.
制造商已经承诺终止这种做法
As this cot will then be safe in every way, we have made the Mother's Choice cot our best buy.
这张小床从各方面来看都是安全的，我们认为“母亲的选择”是最佳产品。
Congratulations Mother's Choice!
恭喜“母亲的选择”
So, what features should you look for in a baby's cot?
那么，你应该挑选什么样的婴儿床呢？
Well obviously safety is a very important factor as well as comfort and convenience.
显然，安全、舒适和便捷是最重要的因素。
We recommend that, if you are buying a cot, do make sure that any metal present is not rusted or bent in any way.
如果你要买一张小床，我们建议你确保任何金属不能生锈或弯曲。
You should ensure your cot has only rounded or smooth edging without any sharp edges, this is especially important for wooden cots.
你还要确定小床的边缘是圆的或平滑的，没有任何锋利的边缘，这对于木床来说尤为重要。
And now on to beds for toddlers...
现在，对于初学走路的婴儿床……

143.title: C5-Test 1-Section 3
transcript: Excuse me, I was told to come here for advice about, erm, Management Diploma courses?
打扰一下，我被告知来这里咨询管理文凭的课程？
You've certainly come to the right place.
你来对地方了。
Hi, my name is Monica.
你好，我叫莫尼卡。
Nice to meet you.
见到你很高兴。
My name is Andrew, Andrew Harris.
我叫安德鲁，安德鲁·哈里斯。
So, Andrew, have you seen our diploma course prospectus yet?
那么安德鲁，你看我们课程的招生简章了吗？
Yes, I've already looked at it.
是的，我看了。

In fact, I thought the information on course content was really useful, but I'm afraid I'm a bit confused by all the different ways you can do the course: full - time intensive, part - time and so on.
事实上，我认为课程内容的介绍十分有用，但是我恐怕对上课的形式有点疑问：全日制、非全日制课程等等。
Well, let's see if I can help.
让我看看能否帮到你。
I think each course type has its advantages and disadvantages, so it really depends on you - your own study habits, and your financial circumstances, of course.
我认为每种课程的形式各有利弊，所以这取决于你，你的学习习惯，经济状况。
Are you working at the moment?
你是在工作吗？
Yes.
是的。
I've been working in the administration section of the local hospital for the last three years.
我过去的3年一直在当地医院的管理部门工作。
And before that I worked in the office of a computer engineering company for two years.
在此之前的两年，我在电脑公司的办公室工作。
So, I've got about five years of relevant work experience.
所以我在相关方面有5年的工作经验。
And what I'm hoping to focus on is Personnel Management.
我希望重点放在人事管理方面。
I see.
我明白了。
And are you planning to leave your current job to study, or are you thinking about just taking a year off ?
你打算辞去现在的工作去学习还是请一年的假？
I want to know what my options are, really.
我想知道我能有什么选择。

I don't want to quit my job or anything and my employers are keen for me to get some more qualifications but obviously, it would be better if I could do a course without taking too much time away from work.
我想知道我能有什么选择我不想辞职，我的老板想让我获得更多的资质，但是显然如果我不用占用很多工作时间又能上课是最好的了。
Right, so you don't really want to do the full - time course, then?
那么你不想上全日制课程了？
No, not really.
不。
It's also a question of finances.
还有一个资金问题。

You see, my office have agreed to pay the cost of the course itself, but I would have to take unpaid leave if I want to study full - time and, well, I don't think I could afford to support myself with no salary for a whole year.
我的公司同意付课程费，但是如果我上全日制课程，我得请无薪假。我觉得我无法一整年都没有薪水的生活下去。
OK.
好的。

Well, you have two other possibilities: you could either do the part - time course - that would be over two years and you wouldn't have to take any time off work - or you could do what we call a 'modular' course.
你有两种选择。你可以上非全日制课程，这不会占用你的工作时间，两年完成，或者你可以上模块化课程。
You could do that in eighteen months if you wanted - or longer, it's quite flexible and it would be up to you.
你可以用18个月完成或更久的时间，这个很灵活，完全取决于你。
Mmm.
额。
So what does the part - time course involve?
那么非全日制课程包括什么
For that you would join an evening class and have a lecture twice a week.
你可以上晚间课程，每周有两个讲座。
Then, you'd have to attend a seminar or discussion workshop one weekend a month.
之后，你会在每月的一个周末参加讨论会或研讨班。
What kind of coursework would I have to do?
我要做什么作业
Well, it's a mixture.
各种各样的作业
You'd be expected to write an essay each month, which counts towards your final assessment.
你每月要写一份论文，这将记入你的最终评定，
You have a case study to do by the end of the course, which might involve doing a survey or something like that, and also you need to hand in a short report every four weeks.
课程结束的时候你要做案例研究，这包括调查之类的事情。另外每四周你要交一份短片报告。
So, that's quite a lot of work, then, on top of working every day.
所以除了每天工作之外，还有很多作业要做。
It sounds like a lot of studying - and really tiring.
听起来有很多要学的，真累人。
Yeah, you certainly wouldn't have much free time!
是的，你肯定没有很多空闲时间了。
What about the 'modular' course?
模块化课程怎么样？
What would I have to do for that?
我要做些什么？
That's where you get the opportunity to study full - time for short periods.
你将有机会在短时间内完成全日制的课程。
That way you can cover a lot of coursework and attend lectures and seminars during the day.
那样你可以完成很多课程，白天要参加讲座和研讨班。
And each module lasts for one term, say, about twelve weeks at a time.
每个模块课程持续一学期，大约一次是12周。
There are obvious advantages in this - the main one being that you can study in a much more intensive way, which suits some people much better.
这显然有很多优点，主要的一点是你可以集中强化学习，这点对一些人很适合。
And how many of these 'modules' would I have to do to get the diploma?
上这种模块化课程，我应该做些什么才能获得学位？

The current programme is two modules - and then you have to choose a topic to work in more depth - but you can base that on your job, and so you don't need to be away from the office, and how long it takes is up to you.
目前的计划是两种模块，你要选择一个主题进行深入研究，但是你可以参考你的工作。你不用离开办公室，上多久也取决于你。
The important thing is that you don't have to study and work.
重要的是你不用既上班又学习
You can focus on one thing at a time.
你可以一次专注于一件事
Yes, I can see that.
是的，我明白了
It certainly sounds attractive!
听起来很诱人
It would be more expensive, though.
但是也更贵一些
I mean, I'd have to support myself without pay for each module.
我的意思是，每个模块我都得无薪养活自己。
That's true, so that might be a problem for you.
没错
Look, why don't you talk this over with your employers and...
所以这对你来说是一个问题，那么，为什么你不跟你的老板说......

144.title: C5-Test 1-Section 4
transcript: OK, so we've been looking at the attitudes of various social and cultural groups towards the management of their personal finances - how important they feel it is to save money, and what they save their money for.
好的，我们一直在调查不同社会和文化群体对个人理财的态度，关于他们存钱的重要性和存钱的目的。
One aspect that we haven't yet considered is gender.
我们还没有考虑过的一方面是性别
So if we consider gender issues we're basically asking whether men and women have different attitudes towards saving money, and whether they save money for different things.
所以如果我们考虑性别问题，我们基本上会问男人和女人是否对存钱有不同的态度，是否他们会为不同的事存钱。

Back in 1928 the British writer George Bernard Shaw wrote in his Intelligent Women's Guide to Socialism and Capitalism that 'A man is supposed to understand politics, economics and finance and is therefore unwilling to accept essential instruction.'
在1928年，英国作家乔治·萧伯纳写了一本《智慧女性之社会主义和资本主义指南》，他指出男人了解政治、经济与金融，所以之后他们就不再愿意接受指导。
He also said, 'A woman, having fewer pretensions, is far more willing to learn'.
他还说，女人没有那么自负，往往更爱学习。

Now, though these days people might question a lot of the assumptions contained in those statements, recent research does suggest that there are some quite fundamental differences between men and women in their attitudes to economic matters.
现在，尽管人们会质疑这其中包含的大量假设，但是最近的研究显示男女在经济问题的态度上有根本的不同。
Let's look at what men and women actually save for.
让我们看看男人和女人究竟为了什么存钱
Research studies of women in North America have found that women are far more likely to save for their children's education and they are also more likely to save up in order to buy a house one day.
关于对北美女人的调查发现，女人更愿意为了孩子的教育存钱。另外，她们还会为了某天能买上一所房子而存钱。
The same studies have found that men, on the other hand, tend to save for a car, which by the way takes a surprisingly large amount of the household budget in North America.
另一方面，同样在北美的调查发现男人往往为了车而存钱。令人惊讶的是，这笔钱会占用大部分的家庭预算。
But the other main priority for men when saving money is their retirement.
但是男人存钱的另一个主要原因是为了他们的退休生活
When they're earning, they're far more likely to put money aside for their old age than women are.
当他们赚钱的时候，他们比女人更知道存钱防老。
Now this is rather disturbing, because in fact the need for women to save for their old age is far greater than for men.
这点使人不安，因为事实上女人比男人更需要存钱防老。
Let's consider this for a moment.
让我们考虑一下这个问题
To start with, it is a fact that throughout the world, women are likely to live many years longer than men, so they need money to support them during this time.
首先，从全世界上看，女人往往比男人活的时间更长，所以她们在这段时间需要钱养活自己。
Since women are likely to be the ones left without a partner in old age, they may therefore have to pay for nursing care, because they don't have a spouse to look after them.
既然女人在老年更可能失去配偶独自一人的生活，那么她们就会因为没有伴侣的照顾而花护理费。
Furthermore the high divorce rates in North America are creating a poverty cycle for women.
另外，北美的高离婚率也给女人造成了贫困的循环。
It is the divorced women who will most often have to look after the children and thus they need more money to look after not just themselves but others.
离婚女人经常要照看孩子，所以她们不仅需要更多的钱照顾自己还要照顾别人。
So what can be done about this situation?
那么这种情况下该怎么做？
The population in North America is likely to contain an increasing number of elderly women.
北美的人口组成呈现女人老龄化发展
The research indicates that at present for women it takes a crisis to make them think about their future financial situation.
调查指出，目前对女人们来说，她们需要经历一场危机才会去考虑未来的经济状况。
But of course this is the very worst time for anyone to make important decisions.
但是这当然是任何人做出重要选择的糟糕时间
Women today need to look ahead, think ahead - not wait until they're under pressure.
现在，女人应该高瞻远瞩，不要等到压力来了才想。
Even women in their early twenties need to think about pensions, for example, and with increasing numbers of women in professional positions there are signs that this is beginning to happen.
即使是在女人二十多岁的时候就应该考虑养老金的问题了。另外越来越多的女性从事专业化岗位，这也是良好开始的标志。
Then research also suggests that women avoid dealing effectively with their economic situation because of a lack of confidence.
研究还表明女人由于缺乏自信而回避有效的处理她们的经济状况
The best way for them to overcome this is by getting themselves properly informed so they are less dependent on other people's advice.
克服这种情况最好的方法是给她们提供适当的指导，这样她们就不用依靠别人的意见。
A number of initiatives have been set up to help them do this.
许多方法可以帮助她们
This College, for example, is one of the educational institutions which offers night classes in Money Management, and increasing numbers of women are enrolling on such courses.
例如这所大学是其中一家提供理财夜间课程的教育学院，越来越多的女性报名参加这门课程。
Here, they can be given advice on different ways of saving.
在这里，她们可以得到关于各种存钱的建议。
Many women are unwilling to invest in stocks and shares, for instance, but these can be extremely profitable.
例如许多女人不愿意投资股票与证券，但是这些可以非常盈利。
It is usually advised that at least 70% of a person's savings should be in low - risk investments but for the rest, financial advisors often advise taking some well - informed risks.
通常建议至少70%的养老存款用于低风险投资，但是理财顾问建议用剩下的钱进行一些了解的风险投资。
Initiatives such as this can give women the economic skills and knowledge they need for a comfortable, independent retirement.
这种方案可以为女性提供经济技能和知识，使她们有一个舒适、独立的退休生活。
The increasing proportion of elderly women in the population is likely to have other economic consequences…
不断增长的女性老龄化人口可能带来另一个经济后果……

15.title: C13-Test 3-Section 3
transcript: OK, Jim. You wanted to see me about your textile design project.
好的,吉姆。你是想和我谈谈你的纺织设计项目，是吧。
That's right. I've been looking at how a range of natural dyes can be used to colour fabrics like cotton and wool.
是的。我一直在研究如何使用一系列的天然染料来给像棉花和羊毛这样的织物上色。
Why did you choose that topic?
你为什么选择这个话题?
Well, I got a lot of useful ideas from the museum, you know, at that exhibition of textiles.
我从博物馆获得了很多有用的想法，你知道，在那个纺织品展上。
But I've always been interested in anything to do with colour
我一直对任何与颜色有关的东西都很感兴趣。
Years ago, I went to a carpet shop with my parents when we were on holiday in Turkey, and I remember all the amazing colours.
多年前，我们在土耳其度假时，我和父母一起去了一家地毯店。那些神奇的颜色一直留在我的脑海当中。
They might not all have been natural dyes.
它们可能并非都是天然染料。
Maybe not, but for the project I decided to follow it up.
也许不是，但为了这个项目，我决定跟进。
And I found a great book about a botanic garden in California that specialises in plants used for dyes.
我在加利福尼亚发现了一本关于植物园写得很棒的书，这本书是专门研究用于染料的植物。
OK. So, in your project, you had to include a practical investigation.
好的。所以，在你的项目中，你必须包含一个实际的调查。
Yeah. At first I couldn't decide on my variables.
是的。起初我还无法决定我的变量。
I was going to just look at one type of fibre for example, like cotton ...
我打算先去看一种纤维材料，例如棉花。
…and see how different types of dyes affected it?
然后看看不同类型的染料是如何影响它的?
Yes. Then I decided to include others as well, so I looked at cotton and wool and nylon.
是的，然后我决定也要去看看其它的材料，然后我就去看了棉花，羊毛和尼龙。
With just one type of dye?
只用一种染料吗?
Various types, including some that weren't natural, for comparison.
各种各样的类型，包括一些不是天然的材料，用于作比较。
OK.
好的。
So, I did the experiments last week. I used some ready-made natural dyes,
我上周就做了实验。我用了一些现成的天然染料，
I found a website which supplied them, they came in just a few days, but I also made some of my own.
我找到了一个提供这些染料的网站，它们在几天内就送来了，但我也自己做了一些。
That must have taken quite a bit of time.
那一定花了不少时间。
Yes, I'd thought it'd just be a matter of a teaspoon or so of dye, and actually that wasn't the case at all.
是的，我以为这只是一茶匙左右的染料，其实根本不是这样。
Like I was using one vegetable, beetroot, for a red dye, and I had to chop up a whole pile of it.
就像我用甜菜根做红色染料一样，我必须切碎一堆甜菜根。
So it all took longer than I'd expected. One possibility is to use food colourings.
所以这一切花费的时间比我预期的要长。另外一种可能是使用食品色素。
I did use one. That was a yellow dye, an artificial one.
我的确使用了一种，那是一种黄色的人造染料。
Tartrazine?
柠檬黄？
Yeah. I used it on cotton first It came out a great colour but when I rinsed the material, the colour just washed away.
是的。我先把它用在棉花上，颜色效果很好，但当我冲洗材料时，颜色就被冲走了。
I'd been going to try it out on nylon, but I abandoned that idea.
我本来打算用尼龙做的，但我放弃了这个想法。
Were you worried about health issues?
你担心健康问题吗?
I'd thought if it's a legal food colouring, it must be safe.
我想如果这是一种合法的食用色素，它一定是安全的。
Well, it can occasionally cause allergic reactions, I believe.
嗯，我相信它偶尔会引起过敏反应。
So what natural dyes did you look at?
所以你看了什么天然染料?
Well, one was turmeric. The colour's great, it's a really strong yellow.
嗯,一个是姜黄。那颜色效果很棒，是一种很浓的黄色。
It's generally used in dishes like curry.
它通常用在像咖喱这样的菜里头。
It's meant to be quite good for your health when eaten, but you might find it's not permanent when it's used as a dye - a few washes, and it's gone.
它对你的健康并无坏处，但是当它被用作染料时，你可能会发现它并不是永久的—只要洗几次，它就消失了。
Right. I used beetroot as a dye for wool. When I chop up beetroot to eat I always end up with bright red hands,
是的。我用甜菜根作了羊毛染料。当我切甜菜根吃的时候，
but the wool ended up just a sort of watery cream shade. Disappointing.
最后我总是满手鲜红，但是羊毛最后只是一种水状的奶油色。太令人失望了。
There's a natural dye called Tyrian purple. Have you heard of that?
有一种叫做泰尔紫的天然染料。你听说过吗?
Yes. It comes from a shellfish, and it was worn in ancient times but only by important people as it was so rare, I didn't use it.
是的。它来自一种贝类，在古代用作衣服染料，但只有大人物才能穿，因为它太稀有了，所以我没有使用它。
It fell out of use centuries ago, though one researcher managed to get hold of some recently.
它在几个世纪前就已经不再使用了，不过最近有一位研究人员设法找到了一些。
But that shade of purple can be produced by chemical dyes nowadays.
但是现在化学染料可以生产那种紫色。
Did you use any black dyes?
你用过黑色染料吗?
Logwood. That was quite complicated.
洋苏木，过程很复杂。
I had to prepare the fabric so the dye would take.
我得把布料准备好，这样子染料才会起作用。
I hope you were careful to wear gloves.
我希望你得小心一点，要带上手套。
Yes. I know the danger with that dye.
是的。我知道那种染料的危险。
Good. It can be extremely dangerous if it's ingested.
好的。如果它被摄入，这会非常危险。
Now, presumably you had a look at an insect-based dye? Like cochineal, for example?
现在，假设你已经看过昆虫染料? 例如,像胭脂红?
Yes. I didn't actually make that, I didn't have time to start crushing up insects to get the red colour and anyway they're not available here,
好的。实际上我并没有这样做，我没有时间去碾碎昆虫来得到这种红颜色。总之，这里没有这种颜色，
but I managed to get the dye quite easily from a website.
但是我很容易地就从网站上买到了这种染料。
But it cost a fortune. I can see why it's generally just used in cooking, and in small quantities.
但它花了一大笔钱。我明白为什么它通常只用于烹饪，而且数量很少了。
Yes, it's very effective, but that's precisely why it's not used as a dye.
是的，它非常有效，但这正是为什么它不被用作染料的原因。
I also read about using metal oxide.
我还读到过要用金属氧化物呢。
Apparently you can allow iron to rust while it's in contact with the fabric, and that colours it.
显然，当铁与织物接触时，它就能生锈，这样子它就变色。
Yes, that works well for dying cotton.
是的，这对染色棉很有效。
But you have to be careful as the metal can actually affect the fabric and so you can't expect to get a lot of wear out of fabrics treated in this way.
但是你必须小心，因为金属实际上会影响织物，所以你不能指望用这种方式处理许多织物衣物。
And the colours are quite subtle, not everyone likes them.
颜色很奇妙，但不是每个人都喜欢它们。
Anyway, it looks as if you've done a lot of work ...
不管怎样，看起来你好像做了很多工作……

16.title: C13-Test 3-Section 4
transcript: Last week, we started looking at reptiles, including crocodiles and snakes.
上周，我们开始研究爬行动物，包括鳄鱼和蛇。
Today, I'd like us to have a look at another reptile - the lizard - and in particular,
今天，我们将研究另一种爬行动物 - 蜥蜴 -
at some studies that have been done on a particular type of lizard whose Latin name is tiliqua rugosa.
特别是，对一种特殊类型的蜥蜴所做的一些研究，这种蜥蜴的拉丁名字是tiliqua rugosa。
This is commonly known as the sleepy lizard, because it's quite slow in its movements and spends quite a lot of its time dozing under rocks or lying in the sun.
它通常被称为“瞌睡蜥蜴”，因为它的动作非常缓慢，并且相当多的时间都是在岩石下面或躺在阳光下打瞌睡。
I'll start with a general description.
我先大概介绍一下。
Sleepy lizards live in Western and South Australia, where they're quite common.
“瞌睡蜥蜴”生活在澳大利亚西部和南部，它在这两个地区很常见。
Unlike European lizards, which are mostly small, green and fast- moving sleepy lizards are brown,
不像大部分体型小、绿色和移动迅速的欧洲蜥蜴，“瞌睡蜥蜴”身体是棕色的，
but what's particularly distinctive about them is the colour of their tongue, which is dark blue, in contrast with the lining of their mouth which is bright pink.
但最明显的区别是它的舌头颜色“深蓝色”与嘴巴里面的粉红色形成鲜明对比
And they're much bigger than most European lizards.
而且它们比大多数欧洲蜥蜴都要大得多。
They have quite a varied diet including insects and even small animals, but they mostly eat plants of varying kinds.
它们的饮食多种多样，包括昆虫，甚至小动物，但它们大多吃各种各样的植物。
Even though they're quite large and powerful, with strong jaws that can crush beetles and snail shells, they still have quite a few predators.
尽管他们体型巨大，并且力量强大，它们的下颌可以压扁甲虫和蜗牛壳，但它们仍然有相当多的捕食者。
Large birds like cassowaries were one of the main ones in the past, but nowadays they're more likely to be caught and killed by snakes.
像食火鸡这样的大型鸟类是过去的主要鸟类之一，但现在它们更有可能被蛇捕获并杀死。
Actually, another threat to their survival isn't a predator at all, but is man-made -
实际上，他们生存的另一个威胁根本不是捕食者，而是人类
quite a large number of sleepy lizards are killed by cars when they're trying to cross highways.
很多瞌睡蜥蜴是在试图穿越高速公路时被汽车压死的
One study carried out by Michael Freake at Flinders University investigated the methods of navigation of these lizards.
弗林德斯大学的Michael Freake进行的一项研究调查了这些蜥蜴的导航方法。
Though they move slowly, they can travel quite long distances.
虽然它们移动缓慢，但它们可以走很远的距离。
And he found that even if they were taken some distance away from their home territory,
而且他发现，即使它们被带离家乡有一段距离，
they could usually find their way back home as long as they could see the sky - they didn't need any other landmarks on the ground.
它们通常也能找到回家的路，只要他们能看到天空 - 他们不需要地面上的任何其他地标。
Observations of these lizards in the wild have also revealed that their mating habits are quite unusual.
对这些蜥蜴在野外的观察也表明，它们的交配习性是非常不寻常的。
Unlike most animals, it seems that they've relatively monogamous, returning to the same partner year after year.
与大多数动物不同，它们似乎相对一夫一妻制，年复一年地回到同一个伴侣身边。
And the male and female also stay together for a long time, both before and after the birth of their young.
而且雌雄蜥蜴在它们的幼子出生前后都会长时间呆在一起。
It's quite interesting to think about the possible reasons for this.
想一想可能的原因是很有趣的。
It could be that it's to do with protecting their young – you'd expect them to have a much better chance of survival if they have both parents around.
这可能与保护他们的幼子有关 - 如果父母都在身边，幼子的生存几率就更高。
But in fact observers have noted that once the babies have hatched out of their eggs, they have hardly any contact with their parents.
但事实上，观察人员指出，一旦从卵子里孵化出来，幼子几乎不会与父母有任何接触。
So, there's not really any evidence to support that idea.
所以，没有任何证据支持那个观点。
Another suggestion's based on the observation that male lizards in monogamous relationships tend to be bigger and stronger than other males.
另一个建议是基于观察到一夫一妻制关系中的雄性蜥蜴往往比其他雄性更大更强壮。
So maybe the male lizards stay around so they can give the female lizards protection from other males.
所以也许雄蜥蜴呆在附近，就是为了让雌性蜥蜴免受其他雄性的侵害。
But again. we're not really sure.
但是，我们同样并不确定。
Finally, I'd like to mention another study that involved collecting data by tracking the lizards.
最后，我想提及另一项涉及通过跟踪蜥蜴来收集数据的研究。
I was actually involved in this myself.
我自己也参与其中。
So we caught some lizards in the wild and we developed a tiny GPS system that would allow us to track them, and we fixed this onto their tails.
我们把自己开发的一个小型GPS系统安装在它们的尾巴上，以进行跟踪。
Then we set the lizards free again, and we were able to track them for twelve days and gather data,
然后我们把蜥蜴放了，跟踪12天后收集数据，
not just about their location, but even about how many steps they took during this period.
不仅仅是关于它们的位置，甚至是这段时间它们走了多少步。
One surprising thing we discovered from this is that there were far fewer meetings between lizards than we expected -
我们从中发现的一件令人惊讶的事情是，
it seems that they were actually trying to avoid one another.
蜥蜴之间的会面比我们想象的要少得多 - 看起来他们实际上是在试图回避对方。
So why would that be? Well, again we have no clear evidence,
那这是为什么呢？好吧，我们又没有明确的证据，
but one hypothesis is that male lizards can cause quite serious injuries to one another,
但有一个假设是，雄性蜥蜴可能会对彼此造成相当严重的伤害，
so maybe this avoidance is a way of preventing this - of self-preservation, if you like.
所以也许这种回避是一种自我保护方式。
But we need to collect a lot more data before we can be sure of any of this.
但是我们需要收集更多的数据，然后才能确定这一点。

17.title: C13-Test 4-Section 1
transcript: Hi Alex. It’s Martha Clines here.
嗨,亚历克斯。我是玛莎·克莱恩。
James White gave me your number.
詹姆斯·怀特给了我你的电话号码。
I hope you don’t mind me calling you.
你应该不介意我给你打电话吧。
Of course not. How are you, Martha?
当然不会。你好吗？玛莎。
Good thanks.
好的谢谢。
I’m ringing because I need a bit of advice.
我打电话是因为我需要一些建议。
Oh yeah. What about?
哦，好的，关于什么方面的呢？
The training you did at JPNW a few years ago.
几年前你在JPNW做的培训。
I’m applying for the same thing.
我也在申请相同的项目。
Oh right Yes, I did mine in 2014. Best thing I ever did，I’m still working there.
哦，是的，我在2014年做的。这是我做过的最棒的事，我还在那里工作。
Really? What are you doing?
真的吗?你在做什么?
Well, now I work in the customer services department
嗯，我现在在客户服务部门工作，
but I did my initial training in Finance.
但是我最初是接受金融方面的培训。
I stayed there for the first two years and then moved to where I am now.
我在那里呆了两年，然后搬到现在的地方。
That’s the same department I’m applying for.
那就是我要申请的部门，
Did you enjoy it?
你喜欢这份工作吗?
I was pretty nervous to begin with.
一开始我是很紧张。
I didn’t do well in my exams at school and I was really worried because I failed Maths.
我在学校的考试成绩不好，我真的很担心，因为我数学不及格。
But it didn’t actually matter because I did lots of courses on the job.
但这并不重要，因为我在工作上又学了很多课程。
Did you get a diploma at the end of your trainee period?
你在实习结束后拿到文凭了吗?
I’m hoping to do the one in business skills.
我希望拿到商业技能文凭。
Yes. That sounds good.
是的。听起来不错。
I took the one on IT skills but I wish I’d done that one instead.
我选的是IT技能，但我希望我能选那个。
OK, that’s good to know.
那也挺好的。
What about the other trainees? How did you get on with them?
其他学员呢?你和他们相处得怎样?
There were about 20 of us who started at the same time and we were all around the same age -
我们大约有20个人同时开始，我们都差不多大——
I was 18 and there was only one person younger than me, who was 17.
我18岁，只有一个比我小的人，17岁。
The rest were between 18 and 20.
其余的人年龄在18到20岁之间。
I made some good friends.
我也交了一些好朋友。
I’ve heard lots of good things about the training at JPNW.
我听说了很多关于JPNW培训的好处。
It seems like there are a lot of opportunities there.
这样子说来那里有很多机会。
Yeah, definitely. Because of its size you can work in loads of different areas within the organisation.
是的,当然。由于它的规模，你可以在组织内不同的领域工作。
What about pay?
工资怎么样?
I know you get a lower minimum wage than regular employees.
我知道你的最低工资比普通员工还低。
That’s right - which isn’t great.
是的，这并不是很好。
But you get the same number of days’ holiday as everyone else.
但是你的假期和其他人一样多。
And the pay goes up massively if they offer you a job at the end of the training period.
如果你在实习结束时获得一份正式工作，那么你的工资就会大幅上涨。
Yeah, but I’m not doing it for the money – it’s the experience I think will be really useful.
好的，但我不是为了钱才这么做的——我是冲着有用的实习经验去的。
Everyone says by the end of the year you gain so much confidence.
每个人都说在年底结束的时候，你会信心大增。
You’re right. That’s the most useful part about it.
是的。这也是最有用的部分。
There’s a lot of variety too. You’re given lots of different things to do.
这里也有很多变化，你有机会尝试各种各样不同的东西。
I enjoyed it all - I didn’t even mind the studying.
我喜欢这一切——我甚至不介意学习。
Do you have to spend any time in college?
你必须在大学里待一段时间吗?
Yes, one day each month.
是的，每个月一天，
So you get lots of support from both your tutor and your manager.
所以你会从导师和经理那里获得很多支持。
That’s good. And the company is easy to get to, isn’t it?
很好耶。这样子公司也很容易找到，不是吗?
Yes, it’s very close to the train station so the location’s a real advantage.
是的，它离火车站很近，所以公司的地理位置真的非常好。
Have you got a date for your interview yet?
你面试的日期确定下来了吗？
Yes, it’s on the 23rd of this month.
嗯嗯，就在本月23日。
So long as you’re well prepared there’s nothing to worry about.
只要你准备充分，就没有什么可担心的。
Everyone’s very friendly.
每个人都很友好。
I am not sure what I should wear. What do you think?
我不知道该穿什么。你觉得呢?
Nothing too casual - like Jeans, for example.
不要穿太随意的衣服，比如牛仔裤。
If you’ve got a nice jacket, wear that with a skirt or trousers.
如果你有一件漂亮的外套，那就再配一条裙子或一条裤子。
OK. Thanks. Any other tips?
好的，感谢。还有什么其它建议吗？
Erm, well I know it’s really obvious but arrive in plenty of time.
嗯，还有一个就是要提前很多时间到场，
They hate people who are late.
他们讨厌迟到的人。
So make sure you know exactly where you have to get to.
所以一定要提前弄清楚你要去哪里？
And one other useful piece of advice my manager told me before I had the interview for this job - is to smile.
我面试这份工作之前，我的经理告诉我的另一个有用的建议—微笑。
Even if you feel terrified. It makes people respond better to you.
即使你感到害怕。微笑会让人对你有一个更好的反应。
I’ll have to practise doing that in the mirror!
我得在镜子里练习微笑！
Yeah - well, good luck.
好吧，祝你好运。
Let me know if you need any more information.
如果你需要更多的信息，请告诉我。
Thanks very much.
非常感谢。

18.title: C13-Test 4-Section 2
transcript: Hi everyone, welcome to the Snow Centre.
大家好，欢迎来到Snow Centre。
My name's Annie.
我叫安妮。
I hope you enjoyed the bus trip from the airport -
我希望大家会喜欢从机场出发的巴士之旅 -
we've certainly got plenty of snow today!
有很漂亮的雪景等着大家。
Well, you've come to New Zealand's premier snow and ski centre,
欢迎大家来到新西兰首屈一指的滑雪中心，
and we've a whole load of activities for you during your week here.
我们为大家准备了为期一周的丰富活动。
Most visitors come here for the cross-country skiing,
大多数游客都来这里越野滑雪，
where you're on fairly flat ground for most of the time rather than going down steep mountainsides.
这里大部分路段都是平坦的雪场，而不是陡峭的山坡。
There are marked trails, but you can also leave these and go off on your own and that's an experience not to be missed.
有做了标记的雪径，但是你也可以另辟蹊径，也是不错的体验。
You can go at your own speed – it's great aerobic exercise if you really push yourself,
你可以按照自己喜欢的速度滑行 - 速度快的话，这是一项不错的有氧运动，
or if you prefer you can just glide gently along and enjoy the beautiful scenery.
你也可以慢慢滑，享受四周美丽的景色。
This afternoon, you'll be going on a dog-sled trip.
今天下午会有一次狗拉雪橇之旅。
You may have seen our dogs on TV recently racing in the winter sled festival.
最近电视上就播放了一次我们的冬季狗拉雪橇比赛。
If you want, you can have your own team for the afternoon and learn how to drive them, following behind our leader on the trail.
如果大家愿意，下午可以自己组队，在教练的指导下学习如何拉雪橇。
Or if you'd prefer, you can just sit back in the sled and enjoy the ride as a passenger.
或者就坐在雪橇上，享受乘客的旅程。
At the weekend, we have the team relay event, and you're all welcome to join in.
周末，我们举办了团队接力活动，欢迎大家加入。
We have a local school coming along, and a lot of the teachers are taking part too.
我们有一所当地学校，还有很多老师也参加了。
Participation rather than winning is the main focus, and there's a medal for everyone who takes part.
胜负不是关键，重在参与，每个参与者都能获得一枚奖章。
Participants are in teams of two to four, and each team must complete four laps of the course.
参赛者两到四人组成一队，每队必须完成四圈的任务。
For your final expedition, you'll head off to Mount Frenner wearing a pair of special snow shoes which allow you to walk on top of the snow.
旅程的终点是Frenner山，穿上一双特殊的雪鞋，在雪地上行走。
This is an area where miners once searched for gold, though there are very few traces of their work left now.
这是矿工们曾经淘金的地方，尽管现在已经丝毫看不到以前的痕迹了。
When the snow melts in summer, the mountain slopes are carpeted in flowers and plants.
当夏天积雪融化时，山坡上铺满了花草。
It's a long ascent though not too steep, and walkers generally take a couple of days to get to the summit and return.
这是一次漫长的攀登，虽然不是太陡峭，但是步行者通常需要几天时间才能登顶并返回。
You'll spend the night in our hut half-way up the mountain.
在半山腰上的小屋里过夜。
That's included in your package for the stay.
这包括在旅行套餐里面了。
It's got cooking facilities, firewood and water for drinking.
屋里面有烹饪设施、柴火和饮用水，
For washing, we recommend you use melted snow, though, to conserve supplies.
我们建议大家用融雪洗涤，以便节省存的水
We can take your luggage up on our snowmobile for you for just ten dollars a person.
行李可以放在雪地车上，每人只需十美元。
The hut has cooking facilities so you can make a hot meal in the evening and morning, but you need to take your own food.
小屋有烹饪设施，所以可以在晚上和早上做顿热饭菜，但是要自己带食材。
The weather on Mount Frenner can be very stormy.
弗伦纳山上的天气可能有很大的暴风雨。
In that case, stay in the hut - generally the storms don't last long.
在这种情况下，留在小屋 - 通常暴风雨不会持续很长时间。
Don't stress about getting back here to the centre in time to catch the airport bus – they'll probably not be running anyway.
不要着急回到中心赶机场巴士 - 反正车也不能开。
We do have an emergency locator beacon in the hut but only use that if it's a real emergency, like if someone's ill or injured.
我小屋里面有一个应急定位信标，但只有在真正的紧急情况下才能使用，比如有人生病或受伤时，
Now, let me tell you something about the different ski trails you can follow during your stay here.
现在，让我告诉你一些不同的滑雪路线。
Highland Trail's directly accessible from where we are now.
Highland Trail可以从我们所在的位置进入。
This trail's been designed to give first-timers an experience they'll enjoy regardless of their age or skill
这条雪径旨在为初学者提供无论年龄或技能如何都能享受的体验
but it's also ideal for experts to practise their technique.
但它也是专家练习技术的理想选择。
Then there's Pine Trail …if you're nervous about skiing, leave this one to the experts.
然后是Pine Trail ……如果你对滑雪感到紧张，请不要轻易进入这条适合专家的雪径。
You follow a steep valley looking right down on the river below - scary!
沿着一条陡峭的山谷望着下面的河 - 吓人！
But if you've fully mastered the techniques needed for hills, it's great fun.
但是，如果你已经完全掌握了爬山所需的技术，那非常有趣。
Stony Trail's a good choice once you've got a general idea of the basics.
一旦你对基础知识有了总体的了解，Stony Trail是一个不错的选择。
There are one or two tricky sections, but nothing too challenging.
有一两个棘手的部分，但没有太大的挑战性。
There's a shelter half-way where you can sit and take a break and enjoy the afternoon sunshine.
半路有一个庇护所，可以坐下休息，享受午后的阳光。
And finally, Loser's Trail.
最后，Loser's Trail。
This starts off following a gentle river valley
从温和的河谷开始，
but the last part is quite exposed so the snow conditions can be challenging. .
最后一段经常会有下雪天气，因此挑战性巨大
if it's snowing or windy, check with us before you set out to make sure the trail's open that day.
如果是下雪或多风天气，出发前要提前跟我们确认一下道路是否开通
Right, so now if you'd like to follow me, we'll get started …
所以，如果大家现在想跟我来，我们就开始……

19.title: C13-Test 4-Section 3
transcript: I've still got loads to do for our report on nutritional food labels.
我还要为咱们的营养食品标签报告做很多工作。
Me too. What did you learn from doing the project about your own shopping habits?
我也是。从这个项目当中，你对自己的购物习惯有了多少了解？
Well, I've always had to check labels for traces of peanuts in everything I eat because of my allergy.
因为过敏，所以我每次吃东西都要检查标签上有没有含有花生的说明。
But beyond that I've never really been concerned enough to check how healthy a product is.
但除此之外，我从没有真正关注过一个健康的食品应该是什么样子的。
This project has actually taught me to read the labels much more carefully.
这个项目实际上教会我要更仔细地阅读标签。
I tended to believe claims on packaging like low in fat'.
我倾向于相信包装上比如“低脂肪”的说法。
But I now realise that the ‘healthy' yoghurt I've bought for years is full of sugar and that it's actually quite high in calories.
但我现在才意识到，我买了多年的“健康”酸奶糖分高，而且实际上它的卡路里含量还相当高。
Ready meals are the worst …comparing the labels on supermarket pizzas was a real eye-opener.
快餐是最糟糕的…拿超市标签相比较，你就会发现披萨的着实让人大开眼界。
Did you have any idea how many calories they contain?
你知道它们含有多少卡路里吗?
I was amazed.
我是很震惊。
Yes, because unless you read the label really carefully, you wouldn't know that the nutritional values given are for half a pizza.
是的，除非你非常仔细阅读标签，否则你不会知道上面所提供的营养价值是半个披萨才能够提供的。
When most people eat the whole pizza. Not exactly transparent is it?
当大多数人吃披萨的时候。所以这不是很透明，是吧。
Not at all. But I expect it won't stop you from buying pizza?
一点也不透明。但是这不会阻止你购买披萨吧？
Probably not, no! I thought comparing the different labelling systems used by food manufacturers was interesting.
可能不会。我认为对不同食品制造商使用的标签系统进行比较真的很有趣。
I think the kind of labelling system used makes a big difference.
我真的觉得标签系统的使用真的会很有用。
Which one did you prefer?
你更喜欢哪一种？
I liked the traditional daily value system best - the one which tells you what proportion of your required daily intake of each ingredient the product contains.
我更喜欢传统的每日价值体系——它会告诉你每天所需摄入的每种原料成分的比例是多少。
I'm not sure it's the easiest for people to use but at least you get the full story.
我不确定这对人们来说是最容易的，但至少你得到了完整全面的信息。
I like to know all the ingredients in a product - not just how much fat, salt and sugar they contain.
我喜欢了解一种产品的所有成分——而不仅仅是它们含有多少脂肪、盐和糖。
But it's good supermarkets have been making an effort to provide reliable information for customers.
只有不错的超市还在一直努力为顾客提供可靠的信息。
Yes. There just needs to be more consistency between labelling systems used by different supermarkets, in terms of portion sizes, etc.
是的。只是需要不同超市使用的标签系统之间能有更多的一致性，比如份量等。
Mmm. The labels on the different brands of chicken flavour crisps were quite revealing too, weren't they?
嗯。不同牌子的鸡肉味薯片上的标签也很能说明问题，不是吗?
Yeah. I don't understand how they can get away with calling them chicken flavour when they only contain artificial additives.
是的。我不明白为什么他们只添加了人工添加剂也可以把它叫做鸡肉味。
I know. I'd at least have expected them to contain a small percentage of real chicken.
我知道。我至少希望它们能包含一小部分真正的鸡肉。
Absolutely.
是的。
I think having nutritional food labeling has been a good idea, don't you?
我认为营养食品标签是个好主意，不是吗?
I think it will change people's behaviour and stop mothers, in particular, buying the wrong things.
我认为这将改变人们的行为，特别是阻止母亲购买错误的东西。
But didn't that study kind of prove the opposite?
但这项研究不是证明了相反的结果吗?
People didn't necessarily stop buying unhealthy products.
人们并不一定会停止购买不健康的产品。
They only said that might be the case.
他们只说可能是这样。
Those findings weren't that conclusive and it was quite a small-scale study. I think more research has to be done.
但这些发现并不是决定性的，这只是一个小规模的研究。我认为还需要做更多的研究。
Yes, I think you're probably right.
是的，我想你可能是对的。
What do you think of the traffic-light system?
你觉得红绿灯系统怎么样?
I think supermarkets like the idea of having a colour-coded system - red, orange or green - for levels of fat, sugar and salt in a product.
我觉得超市喜欢用红色 橙色或绿色等颜色编码的系统表明产品中脂肪、糖和盐的含量。
But it's not been adopted universally. And not on all products.
但它并没有被普遍采用。并非所有产品都是如此。
Why do you suppose that is?
你认为为什么会出现这种情况？
Pressure from the food manufacturers.
来自食品制造商的压力。
Hardly surprising that some of them are opposed to flagging up how unhealthy their products are.
毫不奇怪，他们中的一些人反对指出他们的产品有多不健康。
I'd have thought it would have been compulsory.
我本以为这是强制性的。
It seems ridiculous it isn't.
这似乎很荒谬，但事实并非如此。
I know. And what I couldn't get over is the fact that it was brought in without enough consultation
我知道。还有一个我无法忘怀的是，这系统是在没有充分咨询的情况下被引入的——
a lot of experts had deep reservations about it.
——很多专家对此持保留态度。
That is a bit weird.
这有点奇怪。
I suppose there's an argument for doing the research now when consumers are familiar with this system.
我想，当消费者熟悉这个系统的时候，进行研究是有理由的。
Yeah, maybe.
也许吧。
The participants in the survey were quite positive about the traffic-light system.
参与调查的人对红绿灯系统的态度是非常积极的。
Mmm. But I don't think they targeted the right people.
嗯。但我不认为他们针对的是正确的人群。
They should have focused on people with low literacy levels because these labels are designed to be accessible to them.
他们应该关注低识字率的人，因为这些标签正是为他们设计的。
Yeah. But it's good to get feedback from all socio-economic groups.
是的。但从所有的社会经济群体那里得到反馈是件好事。
And there wasn't much variation in their responses.
他们的反应并没有存在太大差异。
No. But if they hadn't interviewed participants face-to-face, they could have used a much bigger sample size.
不。但是，如果他们没有面对面的采访参与者，他们本可以获得更大的样本数量。
I wonder why they chose that method?
我想知道他们为什么选择那种方法?
Dunno. How were they selected?
不知道，他们是怎么选出来的？
Did they volunteer or were they approached?
他们是主动参加还是调查的人主动去接近？
I think they volunteered.
我觉得他们是自愿的。
The thing that wasn't stated was how often they bought packaged food
没有说明的一点是他们购买包装食品的频率，
all we know is how frequently they used the supermarket.
我们只知道他们去超市的频率。

20.title: C13-Test 4-Section 4
transcript: In my presentation, I'm going to talk about coffee, and its importance both in economic and social terms.
在我的报告中，我将谈论咖啡，以及它在经济和社会方面的重要性。
We think it was first drunk in the Arab world, but there's hardly any documentary evidence of it before the 1500s,
我们认为这最早是在阿拉伯世界被饮用，但在十六世纪之前几乎没有任何文件证据，
although of course that doesn't mean that people didn't know about it before then.
当然这并不意味着人们在此之前不知道咖啡。
However, there is evidence that coffee was originally gathered from bushes growing wild in Ethiopia, in the northeast of Africa.
然而，有证据表明，咖啡最初是从非洲东北部的埃塞俄比亚野生灌木丛中采集的。
In the early sixteenth century, it was being bought by traders,
在十六世纪初期，它被商人买下，
and gradually its use as a drink spread throughout the Middle East.
并逐渐在整个中东地区用作饮料。
It's also known that in 1522, in the Turkish city of Constantinople, which was the centre of the Ottoman Empire,
我们也知道，1522年，在土耳其的奥斯曼帝国中心君士坦丁堡市，
the court physician approved its use as a medicine.
法院医生批准将其用作药物。
By the mid-1500s, coffee bushes were being cultivated in the Yemen
到了十六世纪中叶，也门正开始种植咖啡灌木丛，
and for the next hundred years this region produced most of the coffee drunk in Africa and the Arab world.
并且在接下来的一百年里，这个地区生产了非洲和阿拉伯世界的大部分咖啡。
What's particularly interesting about coffee is its effect on social life.
咖啡最有趣的地方是它对社会生活的影响。
It was rarely drunk at home, but instead people went to coffee houses to drink it.
人们很少在家里喝咖啡，而是要去咖啡厅喝
These people, usually men, would meet to drink coffee and chat about issues of the day.
这些人，通常是男人，会聚一聚喝咖啡，聊天谈论当天的事情。
But at the time, this chance to share ideas and opinions was seen as something that was potentially dangerous,
但是在那个时候，分享想法和意见的机会被认为是一种潜在的危险，1
and in 1623 the ruler of Constantinople demanded the destruction of all the coffee houses in the city,
1623年君士坦丁堡的统治者要求摧毁所有的咖啡厅。
although after his death many new ones opened, and coffee consumption continued.
在这个城市，尽管在他死后，开设了许多新的咖啡厅，咖啡的消费也在继续。
In the seventeenth century, coffee drinking spread to Europe, and here too coffee shops became places where ordinary people, nearly always men, could meet to exchange ideas.
在十七世纪，喝咖啡流传到欧洲，在这里咖啡也变成了普通人，几乎所有男人都可以见面交流思想的地方。
Because of this, some people said that these places performed a similar function to universities.
正因为如此，有人说咖啡厅与大学有类似的功能。
The opportunity they provided for people to meet together outside their own homes and to discuss the topics of the day had an enormous impact on social life,
他们为人们在家外聚会并讨论当天的主题提供了机会，对社会生活产生了巨大的影响，
and many social movements and political developments had their origins in coffee house discussions.
许多社会运动和政治发展起源于咖啡厅的讨论。
In the late 1600s, the Yemeni monopoly on coffee production broke down and coffee production started to spread around the world, helped by European colonisation.
在17世纪后期，也门对咖啡生产的垄断被打破，咖啡生产开始在世界各地传播，这得益于欧洲殖民化。
Europeans set up coffee plantations in Indonesia and the Caribbean and production of coffee in the colonies skyrocketed.
欧洲人在印度尼西亚和加勒比地区建立了咖啡种植园，而殖民地的咖啡产量也猛增。
Different types of coffee were produced in different areas, and it's interesting that the names given to these different types,
不同类型的咖啡产于不同的地区，有趣的是，这些不同类型的名称，
like Mocha or Java coffee, were often taken from the port they were shipped to Europe from.
如摩卡咖啡或爪哇咖啡，通常是从他们从他们被装运的港口取得的。
But if you look at the labour system in the different colonies, there were some significant differences.
但是如果你看看不同殖民地的劳动制度，就会有一些显著的差异。
In Brazil and the various Caribbean colonies, coffee was grown in huge plantations and the workers there were almost all slaves.
在巴西和加勒比各个殖民地，咖啡种植在大片种植园里，那里的工人几乎都是奴隶。
But this wasn't the same in all colonies: for example in Java, which had been colonised by the Dutch,
但是，在所有的殖民地中，这一点并不一样：例如在荷兰殖民的爪哇，
the peasants grew coffee and passed a proportion of this on to the Dutch, so it was used as a means of taxation.
农民种咖啡，并将其中一部分给了荷兰人，所以它被用作税收手段。
But whatever system was used, under the European powers of the eighteenth century, coffee production was very closely linked to colonisation.
但无论采用何种制度，在十八世纪的欧洲列强下，咖啡生产与殖民化密切相关。
Coffee was grown in ever-increasing quantities to satisfy the growing demand from Europe,
为了满足欧洲日益增长的需求，
and it became nearly as important as sugar production, which was grown under very similar conditions.
咖啡的种植量不断增加，而它的重要性几乎与在类似条件下种植的糖一样重要。

However coffee prices were not yet low enough for people to drink it regularly at home, so most coffee consumption still took place in public coffee houses and it still remained something of a luxury item.
然而，咖啡价格还不足以让人们在家里定期饮用咖啡，所以大多数咖啡消费仍然发生在公共咖啡厅，并且仍然是一种奢侈品。
In Britain, however, a new drink was introduced from China, and started to become popular,
然而，在英国，从中国引进了一款新的饮料，并开始流行起来，
gradually taking over from coffee, although at first it was so expensive that only the upper classes could afford it.
逐渐取代咖啡，尽管一开始它太贵了，只有上层阶级才能喝得起。
This was tea, and by the late 1700s it was being widely drunk.
这就是茶。到1700年代末，大部分地区都开始了喝茶。
However when the USA gained independence from Britain in 1776, they identified this drink with Britain,
然而，当美国在1776年从英国获得独立时，他们将这种饮料与英国联系在一起，
and coffee remained the preferred drink in the USA, as it still is today.
咖啡仍然是美国的首选饮料，就像今天一样。
So, by the early nineteenth century, coffee was already being widely produced and consumed.
所以，到十九世纪初，咖啡已经被广泛生产和消费。
But during this century, production boomed and coffee prices started to fall.
但在本世纪，咖啡产量猛增，咖啡价格开始下跌。
This was partly because new types of transportation had been developed which were cheaper and more efficient.
这在一定程度上是因为开发了更便宜，更高效的新型运输方式。
So now, working people could afford to buy coffee - it wasn't just a drink for the middle classes.
所以现在，工人们可以买得起咖啡了 - 它已经不仅仅是中产阶级的饮料了。
And this was at a time when large parts of Europe were starting to work in industries.
而此时正值欧洲大部分地区开始工业领域制造的时候。
And sometimes this meant their work didn't stop when it got dark:
有时候，这意味着他们的工作在天黑时还没有完成：
they might have to continue throughout the night.
他们可能不得不整夜继续工作。
So, the use of coffee as a stimulant became important —
所以，使用咖啡作为兴奋剂变得很重要 -
it wasn't just a drink people drank in the morning, for breakfast.
不仅仅是人们早上喝的早餐。
There were also changes in cultivation ...
种植方面也发生了变化……

275.title: C14-Test 1-Section 1
transcript: Good morning. What can I do for you?
早上好。我能为你做什么？
I want to report a theft. I had some things stolen out of my bag yesterday.
我要举报一起偷窃案。昨天我包里有些东西被偷了。
I'm sorry to hear that. Right, so I'll need to take a few details. Can I start with your name?
很遗憾听到这个消息。好吧，我需要了解一些细节。我能从你的名字开始吗？
Louise Taylor.
路易斯·泰勒。
OK, thank you. And are you resident in the UK?
好的，谢谢。你是英国居民吗？
No, I'm actually Canadian, though my mother was British.
不，我其实是加拿大人，尽管我的母亲是英国人。
And your date of birth?
那你的出生日期呢？
December 14th, 1977.
So you're just visiting this country?
那你是来这个国家访问吗？
That's right. I come over most summers on business. I'm an interior designer and I come over to buy old furniture, antiques you know.
没错。我大多数夏天都是来出差的。我是一个室内设计师，我来这里采购旧家具，古董，你知道的。
There are some really lovely things around here, but you need to get out to the small towns. I've had a really good trip this year, until this happened.
这周遭有一些很可爱的东西，但我需要到小城镇去。我今年的旅行很愉快，直到这一切发生。
OK. So you've been here quite a while?
我明白了。所以你在这里有一段时间了？
Yes, I'm here for two months. I go back next week.
是的，我要在这里待两个月。我下周就回去了。
So may I ask where you're staying now?
请问你现在住哪儿？
Well at present I've got a place at Park Apartments, that's on King Street.
现在我在国王街的公园公寓落脚。
I was staying at the Riverside Apartments on the same street, but the apartment there was only available for six weeks so I had to find another one.
之前我住在同一条街上的河岸公寓里，但是那里的公寓只有六个星期的租期，所以我只能另找一间。
OK. And the apartment number?
好的。公寓号码呢？
Fifteen.
Right.
好的。
Now, I need to take some details of the theft. So you said you had some things stolen out of your bag?
现在，我需要了解一些这起偷窃的细节。你说你的包里有东西被偷了？
That's right.
没错。
And were you actually carrying the bag when the theft took place?
偷窃发生时，你确实带着你的包吗？
Yes. I really can't understand it. I had my backpack on. And I went into a supermarket to buy a few things and when I opened it up my wallet wasn't there.
是的。我真的不明白。我背着我的背包，然后去超市买了一些东西，但当我打开它的时候，我的钱包却不在了。
And what did your wallet have in it?
你的钱包里有什么？
Well, fortunately I don't keep my credit cards in that wallet - I keep them with my passport in an inside compartment in my backpack.
好吧，幸好我没有把信用卡放在钱包里--我把它们和护照放在背包里的一个夹层里。
But there was quite a bit of cash there...about ￡250 sterling, I should think.
但那里有相当一部分现金，大概有250英镑。
I withdrew ￡300 from my account yesterday, but I did a bit of shopping, so I must have already spent about ￡50 of that.
我昨天从我的账户里取出了300英镑，但我买了点东西，所以我一定花了大约50英镑。
OK.
好的。
At first I thought, oh I must have left the wallet back in the apartment, but then I realised my phone had gone as well.
一开始我想，噢，我一定是把钱包忘在公寓里了，但后来我意识到我的手机也不见了。
It was only a week old, and that's when I realised I'd been robbed. Anyway at least they didn't take the keys to my rental car.
这手机才买了一周，然后我就意识到自己被抢了。不过至少他们没有拿走我租的车的钥匙。
Yes. So you say the theft occurred yesterday?
好的。你说你是昨天被偷的？
Yes.
是的。
So that was September the tenth. And do you have any idea at all of where or when the things might possibly have been stolen?
所以那是九月十日。你知道这些东西可能在哪里或什么时候被偷吗？
Well at first I couldn't believe it because the bag had been on my back ever since I left the apartment after lunch.
一开始我不敢相信，因为自从我午饭后离开公寓，包就一直在我背上。
It's just a small backpack, but I generally use it when I'm travelling because it seems safer than a handbag. Anyway, I met up with a friend, and we spent a couple of hours in the museum.
这只是一个小背包，但我通常在旅行时用它，因为它看起来比手提包安全。我遇到了一个朋友，我们在博物馆逛了几小时。

But I do remember that as we were leaving there, at about 4 o'clock, a group of young boys ran up to us, and they were really crowding round us, and they were asking us what time it was, then all of a sudden they ran off.
但我记得，当我们离开那里的时候，大约在4点钟，一群年轻的男孩跑过来，他们挤在我们周围，问我们现在是什么时候，接着他们突然就跑了。
Can you remember anything about them?
你还记得关于他们的任何特征吗？
The one who did most of the talking was wearing a T-shirt with a picture of something...let's see... a tiger.
那个说话最多的人穿着一件印着某样东西的T恤……让我想想……一只老虎。
Right. Any idea of how old he might have been?
好。你知道他大概多大了吗？
Around twelve years old?
大约12岁？
And can you remember anything else about his appearance?
]你还能想起什么他的外貌特征吗？
Not much. He was quite thin...
记不太清了。他很瘦……
Colour of hair?
发色呢？
I do remember that - he was blond. All the others were dark-haired.
这我还记得，他是金发。其他人都是黑头发。
And any details of the others?
还有关于其他人的细节吗？
Not really. They came and went so quickly.
不太记得了。他们来得太快也跑得太快。
Right. So what I'm going to do now is give you a crime reference number so you can contact your insurance company.
好的。现在我要给你一个犯罪参考号码，这样你就可以联系你的保险公司了。
So this is ten digits: 87954 82361
Thank you. So should I ...
谢谢。那么我应该...

276.title: C14-Test 1-Section 2
transcript: Good morning everyone. My name's Janet Parker and I'm the human resources manager.
大家早上好，我叫Janet Parker。我是人力资源部经理。
We're very happy to welcome you to your new apprenticeship. I hope that the next six months will be a positive and enjoyable experience for you.
我们非常高兴地欢迎你们参加实习。我希望接下来的六个月对你们来说是一次积极愉快的经历。
I'd like to start with some general advice about being an apprentice.
我想从当实习生的一些建议开始。
Most of you have very little or no experience of working for a big organisation and the first week or so may be quite challenging.
你们中的大多数人只有很少或根本没有在大公司工作的经验，因此第一周对于你们来说可能是相当具有挑战性的。
There will be a lot of new information to take in but don't worry too much about trying to remember everything.
你们会有很多新的信息需要接受，但是不要太担心自己记不住所有的事情。
The important thing is to check with someone if you're not sure what to do - you'll find your supervisor is very approachable and won't mind explaining things or helping you out.
重要的是，如果你们不确定该做什么，就去问问别人，你们会发现你们的主管很平易近人，他们不会介意为你们解释或提供帮助。
You're here to learn so make the most of that opportunity.
你们是来学习的，所以要好好利用这个机会。

You'll be spending time in different departments during your first week so make an effort to talk to as many people as possible about their work - you'll make some new friends and find out lots of useful information.
在第一周，你们会在不同的部门呆上一段时间，所以要努力和尽可能多的人谈论他们的工作--你们会结交一些新朋友，并且会找到很多有用的信息。
As well as having a supervisor, you'll each be assigned a mentor.
除了一位主管外，你们每人还将有一位导师。
This person will be someone who's recently completed an apprenticeship and you'll meet with them on a weekly basis.
导师们将是最近刚结束实习期的人，你们将每周与他们见面。
Their role is to provide help and support throughout your apprenticeship.
他们会在你们的整个实习期间提供帮助和支持。

Of course, this doesn't mean they actually do any of your work for you - instead they'll be asking you about what goals you've achieved so far, as well as helping you to identify any areas for improvement.
当然，这并不意味着他们会为你做任何具体的工作，他们会问你们到目前为止你实现了什么目标，以及帮助你们明确任何需要改进的领域。
You can also discuss your more long-term ambitions with them as well.
你们也可以和他们讨论自己更长远的抱负。
Now I just want to run through a few company policies for our apprenticeship scheme with you. Most importantly, the internet.
现在我想和你们过一遍我们的实习计划的一些公司政策。最重要的是，互联网。
As part of your job you'll be doing some research online so obviously you'll have unlimited access for that but please don't use it for personal use - you'll have your own phones for that.
你们将在网上做一些调查，这是你们工作的一部分，所以很明显，你们可以无限制地使用网络，但是请不要将它用于个人用途，你们会有自己的手机。
Some of you have already asked me about flexible working.
你们中有些人已经问过我关于弹性工作的问题了。
After your probationary three-month period - some of you will be eligible for this - but it will depend on which department you're in and what your personal circumstances are.
在试用三个月后--你们中的一些人将有弹性工作的资格--但这将取决于你们所在的部门和你们的个人情况。
So please don't assume you'll automatically be permitted to do this.
所以请不要认为你们会自动变成弹性工作制。
I want to make sure there's no confusion about our holiday policy.
我想确保你们对我们的假日政策没有疑惑。
Apart from any statutory public holidays we ask that you don't book any holidays until after your six-month apprenticeship has finished.
除任何法定公众假期外，我们要求你们在完成六个月的实习前，不要计划任何假日。
Time off should only be taken if you are unwell. Please speak to your supervisor if this is going to be a problem.
只有当你们身体不舒服的时候，才可以请假。如果这一点对你来说有问题，请向你的主管咨询。
You'll be expected to work a 40-hour week but there may be opportunities to do overtime during busy periods.
你们应该每周工作40小时，但是在繁忙的时间里会有加班的可能性。
Although you're not required to do this, it can be a valuable experience - so we advise you to take it up if possible.
