And your address?
您的地址呢？
24 	Ardleigh Road.
Ardleigh 路 24号
Can you spell that?
您能拼读吗？
A-R-D-L-E-l-G-H Road.
ARDLEIGH 路。
Postcode?
邮编是多少呢？
BH5 2OP
BH5 2OP。
Thanks.And can I have your phone number?
谢谢。请您留个电话号码好吗？
Is a mobile alright?
手机号可以吗？
Fine.
可以。
Thank you.
谢谢
"And can I ask you where you heard about World Tours? From a friend? Or did you see an advert somewhere?
好的，谢谢您。您方便告诉我您是怎么知道我们国际旅行社的吗？朋友介绍的还是在哪里看到的广告？"
No	 I read about you in the newspaper.
都不是，我是在报纸上看到你们的消息的。
OK	 I'll get the brochures in the post to you but can I give you some information over the phone.What kinds of things do you want to do on your holiday?
好的。我马上把宣传册拿到邮局寄给您。不过，我也可以在电话里先给您介绍一些信息。您在假期的时候打算做些什么呢？
I'm interested in going to California with my family.
我打算和家人一起去加州。
I've got two children and we want to hire a car.
我有两个孩子，而且我们想租一辆车。
OK.We have a couple of self-drive tours there visiting different places of interest in California.
我们在加州有好几种自驾游可以参观不同的景点。
The first one begins in Los Angeles and there's plenty of time to visit some of the theme parks there.
第一种是从洛杉矶出发的，有大量时间可以参观当地的一些主题公园。
That's something on my children's list so I'd want to include that.
我的孩子们想去那里，所以我想把这个项目包括在内。
Good.Then you drive to San Francisco.
很好。然后就开车去旧金山。
From San Francisco you can drive to Yosemite Park where you spend a couple of nights.
你们可以从旧金山开车去优胜美地国家公园，然后在那儿待几天。
You can choose to stay in a lodge or on the campsite.
在那期间你们可以选择住在旅馆里或者露营。
I don't like the idea of staying in a tent.
我不喜欢住在帐篷里，
It'd be too hot.
太热了。
Right. And the tour ends in Las Vegas.
对。这趟旅程的终点是拉斯维加斯。
OK.
好的。
"The other trip we can arrange is slightly different. It starts in San Francisco. Then you drive south to Cambria.
我们可以安排的另一种自驾游稍微有点不同。它是从旧金山出发，然后一路南下开到坎布里亚。"
Someone told me there's a really nice castle near Cambria.
有人说坎布里亚附近有个很棒的城堡，
Will we go near that?
我们会去那附近吗？
Hearst Castle is on that road so you could stop there.
赫氏古堡就在那条路上，你们可以在那停下。
Good.I'd like to do that.
太棒了，我很乐意这么做。
Does this trip also go into the desert?
这个旅行路线也会去沙漠里吗？
No	 it continues to Santa Monica where most people like to stop and do some shopping.
不会，离开坎布里亚之后就继续去圣塔莫尼卡，很多人都喜欢在那儿停下然后购物。
We have enough of that at home so that doesn't interest us.
我们在家就有足够的地方可以购物，所以我们对这个没什么兴趣。
OK.
好的。
Well you could go straight on to San Diego.
好吧，那你们可以直接去圣地亚哥。
That's good for beaches isn't it?
那里有很棒的海滩，对吗？
That's right	 that's a good place to relax and your children might like to visit the zoo before flying home.
没错，那里是个放松的好地方，而且你的孩子们还可以在坐飞机回家之前去一趟动物园。
I don't think so.
我觉得还是算了吧，
We want some time for sunbathing and swimming.
我们想多留些时间晒太阳浴和游泳。
So how many days are the trips and how much do they cost?
那这趟旅程总共有多少天？费用是多少呢？
The first one I told you about is a self-drive tour through California which lasts twelve days and covers 2020 kilometres.
我跟你讲过的第一种穿过加州的自驾游，总共持续12天，总里程2020公里。
The shortest journey is 206 km and the longest is 632 kilometres.
最短的行程是206公里，最长的有632公里。
The cost is ￡525 per person.
费用是每人525英镑。
That includes accommodation	 car rental and a flight but no meals.
这其中包含了住宿、租车费和机票，不过不含餐食。
OK.
好的
And the other trip?
那另一个呢？
That lasts nine days but you spend only three days on the road.
第二种自驾游总共历时9天，不过在路上的时间只有3天。
You cover about 980 kilometres altogether.
总里程980公里。
So is that cheaper then?
所以这个要便宜一点吗？
"Yes	 it's almost a hundred pounds cheaper.It's ￡429 per person	 which is a good deal.
没错，几乎便宜了一百英镑。第二种的费用是每人429英镑，非常划算。"
So that covers accommodation and car hire.
费用里包括了住宿和租车费，
What about flights?
那机票呢？
They aren't included.But these hotels offer dinner in the price.
机票不包含在内。不过这些旅馆免费提供晚餐。
OK.Well	 thank you very much.
这样啊，好的，非常感谢。
I'll be in touch when I've had a chance to look at the brochure.
我看过宣传册之后就跟你联系。
I'm pleased to help.
很乐意为您效劳
Goodbye.
再见。
Goodbye.
再见。

57.title: C10-Test 1-Section 2
transcript: On behalf of LP Clubs, I'd like to welcome you all here today.
今天我谨代表LP俱乐部，欢迎各位的到来。
My name's Sandy Fisher and I'm one of the fitness managers here.
我的名字叫Sandy Fisher，是这里的健身经理之一。
"Before we start our tour of the club I'll just run through some basic information about the facilities we have here, including recent improvements, and explain the types of membership available.
在我们开始参观俱乐部之前，我先给大家介绍一些关于我们健身设施的基本信息，包括最近的一些设备升级，然后再给你们解释一下现有的几种会员类型。"
Our greatest asset is probably our swimming pool which at 25 metres isn't Olympic-sized, but now we've expanded it to eight lanes, it's much wider.
我们这配置最好的可能就是游泳池了，这座游泳池之前只有25米宽，算不上奥运标准，但是我们已经增加了8条泳道，现在宽了很多。
This means there are rarely more than a couple of people at a time in each lane.
也就是说每次使用同一条泳道的人基本上不会超过两三个。

"Unfortunately, there isn't space for an outdoor pool here but the glass roof on the swimming pool is partly retractable, which means you can enjoy something of the open-air experience on warmer days.
虽然很可惜我们这儿没有地方可以建造一个室外游泳池，但是我们游泳池的玻璃屋顶是部分可伸缩的，也就是说如果天气好的话，大家同样可以体验到露天游泳的乐趣。"
Our recently refurbished fitness suite has all the latest exercise equipment including ten new running machines, and a wide range of weight-training machines.
我们最近新装修的健身房里的运动器材都是最新的，包括十台全新的跑步机和各种不同的重量训练机。
Each member is given full training in how to operate the equipment and there is always a trainer on duty to offer help and advice.
我们会充分指导每位会员使用这些器材，而且随时都有当值的健身教练可以给大家提供帮助和建议。
Although we do have adult-only times after 6 and at certain times at weekends, children are well catered for.
虽然每天六点之后和周末的特定时段只有成年人才能过来，但是我们也能很好地满足孩子的需求。
Older children continue to benefit from a wide range of tuition; anything from trampolining to yoga.
大一些的孩子可以继续享受各种授课：从跳床到瑜伽，可供选择的种类非常广泛。
One thing all our members appreciate about us is that we take very good care of them.
所有会员都对我们很满意的一件事就是我们贴心的服务。
This starts on day one with your personal assessment.
从第一天你们要做的个人评估开始。
You are asked to fill in a questionnaire giving details of any health problems.
我们会让你们填一份调查问卷，任何关于健康问题的细节都会包括在内。
One of our personal trainers will then go through this with you.
接着一位私人教练会带着你们从头到尾把问卷过一遍。
The trainer will then take you through the safety rules for using the equipment in the fitness suite.
然后这位教练就会把使用健身房里所有器材的安全规则都告诉你们。
During your next exercise session a personal trainer will work with you to make sure you understand these.
在下一个运动环节期间，会有一位私人教练在一旁指导你们，以确保你们弄懂了这些规则。
It's very important to do this because we really do want to avoid having any sports injuries.
这一点至关重要，因为我们真的不希望大家出现任何运动损伤。
There's a lot more to looking after yourself than simply lifting weights!
锻炼身体不是仅仅举重就可以了，照顾好你们的身体要更重要得多。
At the end of the personal assessment, the trainer will draw up a plan, outlining what you should try to achieve within a six-week period.
在个人评估阶段的最后，教练会写出一份计划表，概述你们在六个周的时间里应该试着达到什么目标。
This will then be reviewed at the end of the six weeks.
这份计划表在六个周之后会被再次拿出来检查你们的锻炼成果。
Now, I'll just quickly run through the types of membership we have available.
现在我快速地给你们介绍一下我们现有的几种会员类型。
All members must pay a joining fee of ￡90 in addition to the rates for the monthly membership fees.
除了每个月的会员费之外，所有的会员都要交一笔90英镑的入会费。
Gold membership entitles you to free entry at all LP Clubs.
金卡会员可以免费进入所有的LP俱乐部。
There are now LP clubs in all major cities and towns so if you travel a lot this will be a great advantage.
我们在各大城市都有LP俱乐部，所以如果你们经常旅行的话，这种会员是很有好处的。
Individual gold membership costs ￡50 a month and joint membership for you and your partner will cost ￡75.
个人金卡会员每个月要交50英镑，而两人的共同会员每个月交75英镑。
"Premier membership is for professional people whose work commitments make it difficult for them to use the club during the day and so LP gives booking preferences to Premier members at peak times.
顶级会员是专为那些工作很忙，白天难得来俱乐部的专业人士打造的，LP会给顶级会员在高峰期优先预约的特权。"
This means you will find it easier to book the sessions at times that suit you.
也就是说成为顶级会员之后，要在你们方便的时间预约运动环节会更容易。
Reciprocal arrangements with other LP Clubs are available to Premier members.
顶级会员还能获得在其他LP俱乐部的对等安排。
Premier membership is for individuals only, but you will be sent passes for guests every month.
顶级会员只限个人办理，不过每个月都有机会可以免费邀请朋友同行。
The monthly fee is ￡65.
每月会费是65英镑。
You don't have to have any special clothes or equipment when you visit the club.
来俱乐部的时候，你们不需要带任何特殊衣物或者装备。
"We provide robes and hairdryers in the changing rooms, but it's very important to remember your photo card because you won't be able to get in without it.
我们在更衣室会提供浴袍和吹风机，但是千万不要忘记带有照片的证件卡，因为没有它你们就进不来了。"
For people who aren't working during the day then.
对那些不在白天工作的人来说......

58.title: C10-Test 1-Section 3
transcript: Erm . hello Professor, I'm John Wishart.
教授您好，我叫 John Wishart。
I'm working on my entry for the Global Design Competition.
我在为参加全球设计大赛做准备。
My tutor said you might be able to help me with it.
我的指导老师说您也许能帮帮我。
Ah, yes, I got a copy of your drawings.
啊，对，我这儿有你的画的复印件。
Come in and tell me about it.
快进来跟我说说。
What sort of competition is it?
对了，你参加的是个什么比赛来着？
Well, it's an international design competition and we have to come up with a new design for a typical domestic kitchen appliance.
它是一个国际性的设计比赛，我们需要为一种典型的家庭厨房用具想出一个全新的设计。
"I see, and are there any special conditions? Does it have to save energy for example?
我知道了，还有什么特殊要求吗？比如要节能什么的？"
Actually that was the focus in last year's competition.
实际上那是去年比赛的重点。
This year's different.We have to adopt an innovative approach to existing technology, using it in a way that hasn't been thought of before.
今年的不一样。我们要采用一种创新的方法来使用现有技术，要以一种别人之前都没想到过的方法使用它。
I see, that sounds tricky.
这样啊，听起来挺棘手的。
And what kitchen appliance have you chosen?
你选了什么厨房用具？
Well, I decided to choose the dishwasher.
我选择了洗碗机。
Interesting, what made you choose that?
有意思，你为什么会选这个呢？
Well, they're an everyday kitchen appliance in most Australian houses but they're all pretty boring and almost identical to each other.
首先，它们是大部分澳大利亚家庭每天都要用的厨房用具，然后这些洗碗机的外形都很单调，基本上没什么区别。
I think some people will be prepared to pay a little extra for something that looks different.
我想会有人愿意稍微多付一点钱来购买外形独特一些的产品的。
That's a nice idea.
想法不错。
I see you've called your design 'the Rockpool'; why is that?
我看见你把你的设计取名叫做“ the Rockpool”, 怎么会想到这个名字呢？
Basically because it looks like the rock pools you find on a beach.
主要是因为它看起来就像海滩上的潮水潭一样。
The top is made of glass so that you can look down into it.
它的顶端是玻璃做的，所以可以低头往里面看。
And there's a stone at the bottom.
底部还有一块石头，
Is that just for decoration?
那只是个装饰吗？
Actually it does have a function.
实际上它是有作用的。
Instead of pushing a button, you turn the stone.
这款洗碗机不需要按按钮，只要转一下这块石头就可以了。
So it's really just a novel way of starting the dishwasher.
那这确实是一种启动洗碗机的新奇方法。
That's right.
没错。
It's a really nice design, but what makes it innovative?
这个设计真的很棒，不过它创新在哪里呢？
Well, I decided to make a dishwasher that uses carbon dioxide.
我决定把这台洗碗机设计成使用二氧化碳来洗碗。
In place of water and detergent?
用来代替水和清洁剂？
How will you manage that?
你是怎么做到的？
The idea is to pressurise the carbon dioxide so that it becomes a liquid.
原理是把二氧化碳加压至液体状态，
The fluid is then released into the dishwasher where it cleans the dishes all by itself.
然后这个液体就会流入洗碗机中独自完成洗碗的工作。
"Sounds like a brilliant idea! Your system will totally do away with the need for strong detergents.
你这个想法听起来真是太妙了！有了你的这套系统，我们就再也不需要强力清洁剂了。"
So what happens once the dishes are clean?
那这些碗碟洗干净之后怎么办？
Well, to allow them to dry, the liquid carbon dioxide and the waste materials all go to an area called the holding chamber.
为了把这些碗碟弄干，液体二氧化碳和那些废料都会进入一个叫做“保存室”的地方。
That's where the liquid is depressurised and so it reverts to a gas.
在那里，液体二氧化碳会被减压，然后变成气体。
Then the oil and grease are separated out and sent to the waste system.
而那些油脂会被分离出来送到废物系统中去。
It sounds like you've thought it all out very thoroughly.
看起来你似乎把各个方面都考虑到了，很全面嘛。那，
So, what happens to the carbon dioxide once the process is complete?
这个过程结束之后二氧化碳要怎么处理呢？
Not wasted I hope.
不会就那么浪费了吧？
Actually, that's where the real savings are made.
事实上，这才是真正节能的地方。
The carbon dioxide is sent back to the cylinder and can be used again and again.
二氧化碳会被送回气缸中，可以一直循环使用。
What a terrific idea.
你这个想法太好了。
Do you think it will ever be built?
你觉得这种洗碗机真的能被制造出来吗？
Probably not, but that's OK.
可能制造不出来吧，不过也没关系。
Well, I'm sure a lot of positive things will come out of your design.
我相信从你的设计中会产生很多积极的东西的。
Now, you seem to have thought about everything so what exactly did you need me to help you with?
你似乎把所有的事情都考虑到了，那你具体需要我帮助你什么呢？
"Well, my design has made it to the final stage of the competition and, in a few months' time. I have to give a presentation, and that's the part I was hoping you could help me with.
我的设计已经进入决赛了，再过几个月的时间我就要做一个陈述，而这就是我希望您能帮我的地方。"
Right, well that should be easy enough.
好吧，这应该蛮简单的。
What have you managed to do so far?
你到目前为止都准备了什么？
Well, I've got detailed drawings to show how it will work and I've also written a 500- word paper on it.
我画了一幅很具体的图来展示洗碗机的具体原理，还写了一篇500字的文章。
I see.
我知道了。
Well, if you want to stand a good chance of winning you really need a model of the machine.
如果你想稳操胜券的话，真的还是要准备一个机器的模型。
Yes, I thought I might but I'm having a few problems.
对，我想过可能需要这么做，但是我遇到了一些麻烦。
What is the main difficulty so far?
目前你最主要的困难是什么？
Let me guess - is it the materials?
让我猜一下，是不是材料问题？
Yes.
是的。
I want it to look professional but everything that's top quality is also very expensive.
我想让它看起来专业一些，但是所有的优质材料都很贵。
Look, projects like this are very important to us. They really help lift our profile.
你看，像这样的项目对我们来说非常重要，它们真的能帮我们把个人简介提升一个档次。
So why don't you talk to the university about a grant?
所以你为什么不跟学校申请一笔补助呢？
I can help you fill out the application forms if you like.
你要是想的话，我可以帮你填一下表格
That would be great.
那太好了
You'd better show me this paper you've written as well.
你最好把你写的那篇文章也给我看看。
For a global competition such as this you need to make sure the technical details you've given are accurate and thorough.
像这样的全球性比赛，你得确保给出的所有技术性细节都是精确全面的。
That would be a great help.
您真是帮了我一个大忙。
Is there anything else I can do?
还有别的事情需要我帮忙吗？
Well, I'm really .

59.title: C10-Test 1-Section 4
transcript: Today we continue our series on ecology and conservation with a look at a particularly endangered member of the black bear family.
我们继续生态与保护系列话题，今天我们要讨论的是黑熊家族中的一个尤其濒危的成员。
One in ten black bears is actually born with a white coat, which is the result of a special gene that surfaces in a few.
事实上，十只黑熊中就有一只生来就是白色的皮毛，这是由一种出现在少数黑熊中的特殊基因导致的。
Local people have named it 'the spirit bear'.
当地人把这种熊称为“白灵熊”，
And according to the legends of these communities, its snowy fur brings with it a special power.
而根据一些部落传说，这种熊雪白的皮毛使它具有了一种特殊能力。
Because of this, it has always been highly regarded by them - so much that they do not speak of seeing it to anyone else.
受到这些传说的影响，当地人对这种熊十分崇敬，以至于他们不会对任何外人讲起它的存在。
It is their way of protecting it when strangers visit the area.
这是他们在陌生人来到这片区域的时候，保护白灵熊的一种方式。
The white bear's habitat is quite interesting.
这种白熊的栖息地相当有趣。
The bear's strong relationship with the old-growth rainforest is a complex one.
白灵熊和原始热带雨林之间存在着一种复杂的紧密联系。
The white bear relies on the huge centuries-old trees in the forest in many ways.
白灵熊在很多方面都依赖于雨林中的参天古木。
For example, the old-growth trees have extremely long roots that help prevent erosion of the soil along the banks of the many fish streams.
比如，这些古老的树长着特别长的根，能够帮助防止溪流两岸的土壤被侵蚀，而这些溪流中都生活着很多鱼。
Keeping these banks intact is important because these streams are home to salmon, which are the bear's main food source.
保持这些溪流两岸不受损非常重要，因为在这些溪流中生活着熊的主要食物来源——鲑鱼。
In return, the bear's feeding habits nurture the forest.
作为回报，熊的进食习惯也滋养着森林。
As the bears eat the salmon, they discard the skin and bones in great amounts on the forest floor, which provide vital nutrients.
这些熊在吃鲑鱼的时候，会把大量的富含营养的鱼皮和骨头丢弃在森林地面。
These produce lush vegetation that sustains thousands of other types of life forms, from birds to insects and more.
在这些鱼皮和骨头被丢弃的地方会长出茂密的植被，供养着从鸟类到昆虫等数千种其他类型的生物。
Today, the spirit bear lives off the coast of the province of British Columbia on a few islands.
如今白灵熊生活在大不列颠哥伦比亚省的一些岛上，远离海岸而居。
There is great concern for their survival since it is estimated that less than two hundred of these white bears remain.
由于科学家估算现存的白灵熊不超过两百只，它们的存亡引起了广泛关注。
The best way to protect them is to make every effort to preserve the delicate balance of their forest environment - in other words, their ecosystem.
要保护它们最好的办法就是努力维护它们生存的森林环境的微妙平衡，换句话说，就是要保护它们的生态环境。
The greatest threat to the bear's existence is the loss of its habitat.
白灵熊生存的最大威胁就是它栖息地的丧失。
Over many years, logging companies have stripped the land by cutting down a large number of trees.
多年以来，伐木公司砍倒了大批树木，把土地变得光秃秃的。
In addition, they have built roads which have fractured the areas where the bear usually feeds, and many hibernation sites have also been lost.
另外，他们还建造了许多公路，破坏了白灵熊经常进食的地区，还使它失去了很多冬眠的场所。
The logging of the trees along the streams has damaged the places where the bears fish.
砍伐溪流沿岸树木的行为毁坏了白灵熊捕鱼的地方。
To make matters worse, the number of salmon in those streams is declining because there is no legal limit on fishing at the moment.
使得问题更加严重的是，那些溪流中的鲑鱼数量也在减少，因为当时对捕鱼并没有法律限制。

All these influences have a negative impact on the spirit bear's very existence, which is made all the more fragile by the fact that reproduction among these bears has always been disappointingly low.
所有这些影响对白灵熊的生存都产生了负面作用，而这些熊的繁殖率一直都比较低这一事实更是使得它们愈加脆弱。
And so, what's the situation going forward?
那么，未来的情况又将如何呢？
Community organizations, environmental groups and the British Columbia government are now working together on the problem.
社区组织、环境保护团体和大不列颠哥伦比亚省政府现在都在共同解决这一问题。
The government is now requiring logging companies to adopt a better logging method, which is a positive step.
政府目前正要求伐木公司采取一种更好的伐木手段，这是一个积极的措施。
However, these measures alone may not be sufficient to ensure a healthy population of the spirit bear in the future.
但是，只采取这些措施也许并不足以保证未来白灵熊的数量呈一个健康的态势。
Other steps also need to be taken.
其他措施也需要付诸实行。
While it is important to maintain the spirit bear's habitat, there also needs to be more emphasis on its expansion.
虽然维护白灵熊的栖息地也很重要，但是还需要更加强调栖息地的扩张。
The move is justified as it will also create space for other bears that are losing their homes.
这种方法是非常合理的，因为它也会给其他正失去家园的熊创造出生存空间......

68.title: C10-Test 4-Section 1
transcript: Thorndyke's.
您好，这里是Thorndyke's。
Good morning. Is that Mr Thorndyke?
上午好，请问您是Thorndyke先生吗？
Speaking. How can I help?
是的，有什么能为您做的？
I've got quite a few things which need painting and fixing in the flat and I wonder whether you'd be able to do the work.
我的公寓里有一些东西需要粉刷和维修，我想知道你能干这些活吗？
I'm sure I'd be able to help but let me take down a few details.
我相信这些活我都能干，不过我得先了解一下具体信息。
Yes, of course.
好的，当然可以。
Well	firstly how did you hear about us?
首先，您是怎么知道我们的？
It was my friend May Hampton ... you did some excellent work for her a couple of years ago. Do you remember?
我是通过我的朋友May Hampton知道你们的......您几年前给她家干过活，效果特别棒，您还记得吗？
Oh yes, that was in West Park Flats, lovely lady.
哦，对，在West Park Flats，很可爱的一位女士。
Yes, she is.
是的，她确实很可爱。
And what's your name, please?
不好意思，怎么称呼您？
It's Edith Pargetter.
我叫Edith Pargetter。
Edith ... can you spell your surname, please?
Edith......不好意思，您能拼读一下您的姓氏吗？
It's P-A-R-G-E double T-E-R.
好的，我的姓氏是Pargetter。
Double T, right.
双写T，好的。
And do you live in West Park Flats as well?
好的。您也住在West Park Flats吗？
No, actually it's East Park，Flat 4.
不，实际上我住在East Park，四号楼。
Oh right,	 that's over the road. I seem to remember quite difficult to get to.
哦，好的，我好像记得那里是在马路对面，路挺难走的。
Yes. it's at the back of the library.
是的，在图书馆的后面。
Right, I know.
好的，我知道了。
And what's your phone number?
您的电话号码是多少？
875934, but I'm out a great deal in the afternoons and evenings.
875934，不过我下午和傍晚经常都在外面。
So would the best time to ring you be in the morning?
所以我最好在上午给您打电话对吗？
Yes.
是的。
Fine. I've made a note of that.
好的，我已经记下来了。
Can I just ask I'll be in a van and I know parking's rather difficult round your flats.
我想问的是，我到时候会开着面包车过去，我知道您家附近不太好停车，
Where would you recommend?
您有可以停车的地方推荐吗？
Well	 I always tell people in larger vehicles to park by the postbox on the other side of the road from the entrance.
我经常告诉开大型车辆的人把车停在邮箱旁边，就在入口那条路的另一边。
Good, thanks.
好的，多谢。
And will you be able to give me a full itemised quote?
您能不能给我一个完整的明细报价？
Oh yes. I'll list all the jobs separately with individual prices.
可以的，我会把所有的活儿分开列出来，然后每个都单独写上价格。
That'd be a great help.
那真是帮了我大忙了。
No problem.
没问题。
Now	 what would you like me to do?
那么您都需要我做些什么呢？
Firstly and most urgently is in the kitchen.
第一个也是最紧急的一个活是在厨房，
With all the weather damage	 the glass in the door has cracked and I'd need that fixing ...
由于一直以来受到天气的破坏，厨房门上的玻璃已经裂了，我想要您把它修......
I presume you mean replacing?
你意思是换一下？
Oh yes.
是的
And as soon as possible ...
越快越好
What I'll do is come round tomorrow morning and do that immediately.
我明天上午过去，马上帮您换掉。
Thank you so much.
谢谢你
The other things aren't so urgent but ...
其他的活没有那么紧急，不过......
Now, I'll make a note of everything you want doing.
您把需要我干的活都说一遍吧，我把它们统统记下来。
Well, in the kitchen I'd like some painting doing.
我想把厨房粉刷一下。
All the kitchen walls?
厨房里所有的墙都要刷吗？
Just the area over the cooker.
只是厨灶附近的墙，
It's very greasy ...
那里太油了......
Right ... it does tend to get that way!
是的，那个地方总是那样！
Yes!
没错！
Well	if you want a proper job done what I'd need to do is strip the old paint and plaster it about a week before I paint it.
如果您想要效果好一点的话，我就需要把墙上旧的涂料扒下来，然后在重新粉刷之前抹上灰泥晾一个星期左右。
Of course.
没问题。
Now, May tells me you also do work in the garden.
May跟我说，花园里的活您也会干。
That's right.
没错。
Well I'd like you to replace a fence.
我想要您帮我换一个栅栏。
Just one?
只换一个？
Yes, at the far end.
对，就是最末尾的那个。
Fine. Shouldn't be a problem.
可以，应该没问题。
And that's the lot.
就这些活。
Fine.
好的，
Yeah, as I say I can come round tomorrow morning to look over things with you.
就像我之前说的，我明天上午会过去一趟，跟你一起大致看看情况。
That's great, thank you.
那太好了，谢谢您。
So I'll look forward to seeing you tomorrow at .
那我们明天见......

69.title: C10-Test 4-Section 2
transcript: Welcome to Manham Port, where a thousand years of history are brought to life.
欢迎来到Manham港口，千年的历史会在这里重现。

All the family can enjoy a day out at Manham: visit our copper mine, see models of the machinery it used, have your photo taken in nineteenth century costume, experience at first hand how people lived at different stages throughout history, and especially how children studied, worked and played.
所有的家庭在Manham都能尽情享受出游的这一天：你们可以参观这里的铜矿、看看矿井里使用的机器的模型、穿着十九世纪的服装拍照、亲身体验人们在不同历史时期的生活状态，尤其是孩子们是如何学习、工作和玩耍的。

The port of Manham is located in beautiful and peaceful countryside, on a bend in the great River Avon, and developed here because it's the highest navigable point of the Avon- boats can go no higher up this river - and proved a handy place to load and unload cargo to and from the sea, which is over 23 miles away.
Manham港口位于美丽平静的乡间，地处伟大的埃文河河湾。这里能够发展起来主要得益于它是埃文河最高的通航点（这条河上的船只最高只能航行到这里），而且事实证明，由于这里离海岸只有23英里，是个给来往船只装卸货物的极佳地点。

A small port was already established here when, about 900 years ago, tin was discovered nearby, though it wasn't until the Industrial Revolution, when a tremendous need for metals of all kinds developed, that Manham expanded to become one of the busiest ports in the country.
不过直到工业革命时期，各类金属的需求急剧增加之后，Manham才扩张成为国内最繁忙的港口之一。早在900多年前锡矿在这附近被发现时，这里就已经建立了一个小港口，

And because it was already so busy, prospectors began to look for other minerals, and by the end of the nineteenth century, lead, copper, manganese and arsenic were added to the cargos leaving Manham.
由于这个港口已经十分繁忙了，勘探者便开始寻找其他矿物，到十九世纪末，铅、铜、锰和砷都被开采了出来，成为了Manham对外输出的货物之一。
In the early days, the ores had been smelted or processed in the same area they were mined.
早期的时候，矿石是在开采地区就近冶炼或者加工的。

But, as demand grew, the smelting process required huge factory furnaces or fires to melt the metal from the rock and there was not enough coal in the local area, so the rocks containing minerals had to be shipped long distances.
但是随着需求的增加，冶炼过程中需要巨大的工厂焚化炉或火力来熔化岩石中的金属，可当地并没有足够的煤炭可用，于是矿石就被运到了其他遥远的地区。
Sadly, in the twentieth century, the great port of Manham declined, and thousands of workers were forced to emigrate out of the area.
遗憾的是，二十世纪时，Manham港口规模缩小，数以千计的工人不得不出海迁往别处。
The building at the port fell into disrepair, and the place became almost forgotten.
港口的建筑年久失修，这个地方也几乎被人遗忘。
But then, the Manham Trust was formed to conserve the historical resources of the area.
不过，后来人们成立了一个Manham基金会来保护当地的历史资源。
It organised scores of local volunteers to remove undergrowth to find the original outlines of the installations.
基金会组织了大量的当地志愿者清除矮木丛，以弄清那些装置的原本轮廓。
It then brought in paid professionals to match installations with maps of the original port complex and to set about reconstructing it.
然后又雇请了一些专家按照原本港口建筑群的地图来匹配这些装置，并准备重建。
Today you can see the results of this ambitious programme of restoration.
今天你们就能看见这个宏大的重建项目的成果。
The intention, and we believe this will be realised before the end of the year, is to return Manham Port to the condition it reached at its peak as 'the greatest copper port in the country'.
这个项目的目标是把Manham港口恢复到它在黄金时期被誉为“英国最大的铜矿港口”时的状态，我们相信在今年年底之前这个目标就能实现。
But what can you do and see on your visit today?
那么各位在今天的旅程中都能做些什么、看到些什么呢？
Here are just a few highlights.
下面是我们列出的一些重要景点，
We suggest you start with the visit to the copper mine.
我们建议各位从游览铜矿开始。
Travel on converted mining trains and journey into the depths of the mountain along seams once worked by hundreds of miners.
你们可以坐在改造过的矿车中，沿着数百名工人过去挖出的甬道一路游览至大山深处。
Watch out especially for the great pumping machines which rid the mine of water.
在这期间你们可以特别注意寻找一下当初用来在矿井中排水的大水泵。
But please be warned that, like all mines, ours is very dark and closed in and we do say that children under five and also dogs should NOT be taken into the mine.
但是请注意，和所有的矿井一样，我们的矿井是一个很暗的密闭空间，所以我们建议各位不要把五岁以下的孩子和狗狗带进矿井。
The next recommended visit is to the village school.
我们推荐的第二个景点是村里的学校。
While looking round the classrooms, take a special look at our display of games, which is one of the largest in the world.
在各位参观教室的时候，可以特别看看我们的游戏展览，这是世界上规模最大的游戏展览之一。
And it's recommended that you time your visit to coincide with a guided tour.
我们还建议大家可以跟着有导游的游客一起游览。
This will give you the opportunity to ask lots of questions.
这样你们就有机会可以问很多问题了。
Near the school is the beautiful old sailing ketch called 'The George'.
学校附近有一艘叫做George号的旧帆船非常漂亮，
You are welcome to board the boat and look round the cabins.
你们可以上船参观一下船舱。
Look out for the ship's wheel which was missing until only five years ago when it was dredged out of the silt by a local fisherman.
大家可以注意一下帆船的舵轮，这个舵轮之前一直处于遗失状态，直到五年前才被一位当地渔民从淤泥中挖掘出来。
We have no idea how it got there but it's been polished and proudly restored to its original place on the boat.
它是如何掉进淤泥里的，我们无从得知，不过我们已经给它抛光并骄傲地将它重新装回了船上原本的位置。
Please take care going down the ladders if you wish to visit the lower deck we don't recommend you allow young children to use them.
如果各位想要去下层甲板上参观的话，下楼梯的时候请千万小心，我们不建议你们让小孩儿使用这些楼梯。
So we hope you have a memorable visit to Manham Port and will tell your friends all about us.
祝各位在Manham港口有一段难忘的旅程，同时我们也希望大家能替我们多多宣传。

70.title: C10-Test 4-Section 3
transcript: Hi, Laura could you spare a few minutes to talk about the work placement you did last summer?
Laura，你好，你能不能抽几分钟时间跟我讲一讲你去年暑假做的实习？
I'm thinking of doing one myself ...
因为我自己也在考虑做个实习。
Hi, Tim.
你好，Tim，
Sure.
当然没问题。
Didn't you do yours at an environmental services company?
你去年是不是在一家环境服务公司实习的？
That's right ...
是的

It's only a very small company and they needed someone to produce a company brochure, and I wanted to get some business experience because I'm interested in a career in occupational psychology in a business environment.
那只是一家小公司，他们当时需要一个人制作公司宣传册，而我也想获得一些商务经验，因为我对与商务环境下的职业心理学相关的职业比较感兴趣。
It was good because I had overall responsibility for the project.
而且我可以对那个项目全权负责，所以还不错。
What kind of skills do you think you developed on the placement?
你觉得你通过这次实习获得了什么能力吗？
I mean, apart from the ones you already had ...
当然了，除去你本身就有的那些能力之外......
Did you have to do all the artwork for the brochure, the layout and everything?
你当时要自己做宣传册的插图、排版和所有事情吗？
We hired the services of a professional photographer for that.
我们雇了一位专业的摄影师来做那些，
I did have to use my IT skills to a certain extent because I cut and pasted text from marketing leaflets, but that didn't involve anything I hadn't done before.
我在某种程度上确实用到了IT能力来从营销传单上把文字内容裁剪下来，粘贴到宣传册上，不过这些事情我之前就做过。
Do you think you got any better at managing your time and prioritising things?
那你觉得这次实习结束之后，你在管理时间和分清事情的先后次序方面有没有变得更好呢？
You always used to say you had trouble with that ...
你以前说你在这方面总是遇到问题......
Oh, definitely.
哦，必须的。
There was so much pressure to meet the project deadline.
要赶在项目截止日期之前完工压力太大了。
And I also got better at explaining things and asserting my opinions, because I had to have weekly consultations with the marketing manager and give him a progress report.
而且我也更加擅长解释事情和发表看法了，因为我每个周都要和市场部经理进行一次讨论并且交给他一份进度报告。
It sounds as if you got a lot out of it then.
听起来你收获颇多啊。
Absolutely.
当然了，
It was really worthwhile ...
这次实习太值得了.....
But you know, the company benefited too ...
不过，我也给公司带来了好处啊......
Yes, they must have done.
对，肯定的。
After all, if they'd used a professional advertising agency to produce their brochure instead of doing it in house, presumably they'd have paid a lot more?
毕竟如果他们请了一家专业的广告公司来制作宣传册，而不是自己在公司里做的话，可能就要多花很多钱了。
Oh, yes.
是的。
I worked it out it would have been 250 per cent more.
我算过了，那样的话会多花百分之二百五的钱。
And I thought the end result was good, even though we did everything on site.
而且虽然我们所有的东西都是在网上做的，但是我觉得最终结果也还不错啊。
The company has quite a powerful computer and I managed to borrow some scanning software from the university.
公司里有配置超棒的电脑，而且我还想办法从学校里借了一些扫描软件。
The new brochure looks really professional; it enhances the image of the company straight away.
新的宣传册看起来非常专业，立马就提升了公司形象。
So in the long run it should help them to attract clients, and improve their sales figures?
所以，长期来看的话，这个会帮助他们吸引客户增加营业额，对吗？
That's the idea.Yeah.
就是这样，没错。
Well, all in all it sounds very positive I think I will go ahead and apply for a placement myself.
总的听起来效果还是很积极的，我想我也着手申请一个实习吧。
How do I go about it?
但是要怎么做呢？
It's easy enough to do, because there's a government agency called STEP S T E P that organises placements for students.
很简单的，因为有一个叫做STEP的政府机构会为学生组织实习。
You should start by getting their booklet with all the details I expect you can download one from their website.
你应该先从看一下他们的手册开始，那上面什么信息都有，我想你从他们的官网上就能下载一份。
Actually, they've got copies in the psychology department I've seen them there.
实际上，心理学系办公室就有那些手册，我在那儿看见过。
I'll just go to the office and pick one up.
我等会儿直接去办公室拿一份儿就行了。
Right.
好的。
And then if I were you, after I'd looked at it I'd go over all the options with someone ...
可以啊。然后，如果我是你的话，在看完了手册之后，我会和某个人一起讨论一下所有的选择。
I suppose I should ask my tutor's advice.
我想我应该问问我导师的意见，
He knows more about me than anyone.
他比任何人都要了解我。
One of the career officers would be better, they've got more knowledge about the jobs market than your personal tutor would have.
就业指导老师可能更好一点，比起你的个人导师，他们对职场会更了解一些。
OK ...
And then when you know what you want you can register with STEP you'll find their address in the booklet.
等你了解了自己的选择之后，就可以在STEP上注册了，手册里有他们的网址。
And once you've registered they assign you to a mentor who looks after your application.
注册了之后，他们会把你分配给一位指导者，他会处理你的申请的。
And then I suppose you just sit back and wait till you hear something?
然后就光坐着等消息？
They told me at the careers office that it's best to be proactive, and get updates yourself by checking the website for new placement alerts.
就业指导办公室的老师告诉我最好要积极主动一点，你要自己查看网站上的新实习提示，多更新信息。
Your mentor is supposed to keep you informed, but you can't rely on that.
你的指导者本应通知你这些的，但是你不能完全依赖他。
I don't suppose it's a good idea to get in touch with companies directly, is it?
我猜直接和公司联系应该不是个好主意，对吧？
Not really ...
也不见得......
But it is the company who notifies you if they want you to go for an interview.
不过如果公司想要你去参加面试的话，他们会直接通知你的。
You get a letter of invitation or an email from personnel departments.
你会收到人事部门给你的邀请或者邮件。
And do I reply directly to them?
那我要直接回复他们吗？
Yes, you do.
嗯，要。
STEP only gets involved again once you've been made a job offer.
只有当你获得了工作机会之后，STEP才会介入。
Right ...
So, once you've had an interview you should let your mentor know what the outcome is?
那，一旦面试结束，是不是就要告诉指导者面试结果呢？
I mean whether you're offered a job, and whether you've decided to accept it?
像你有没有得到这个工作，还有你决定接不接受它之类的？
That's right.
没错。
They'll inform the careers office once a placement has been agreed, so you don't have to do that.
一旦实习申请通过了，他们就会通知就业指导办公室的，所以你不需要这么做。
Is that all then?
还有其他的事情吗？
More or less.
差不多就这些吧。
Only once you've accepted an offer you'll probably have to supply a reference, because the placement will be conditional on that.
差不多就这些吧。不过一旦接受了这个工作机会，你可能就要交一个介绍信，因为实习的工作会根据这个而定。
And that's something you should ask your own tutor to provide.
而这就是需要叫你的导师来做的事情了。
He knows about your academic ability and also about your qualities, like reliability.
他了解你的学术能力以及你的品性，比如可靠性。
Well, thanks very much for the information I'm starting to look forward ...
哦，太谢谢你跟我分享这些信息了，我已经开始期待......

71.title: C10-Test 4-Section 4
transcript: Today we're going to look at an important area of science, namely nanotechnology.
今天我们要来看看一个重要的科学领域，也就是纳米技术。
So what is it?
纳米技术是什么呢？
Nano means tiny, so it's science and engineering on the scale of atoms and molecules.
纳米指的是很小的东西，所以纳米技术是在原子和分子范围内的科学工程。
The idea is that by controlling and rearranging atoms, you can literally create anything.
想法是这样的：通过控制和重新排列原子，我们能创造出任何事物。
However, as we'll see, the science of the small has some big implications affecting us in many ways.
可是，如我们将看到的，这项关于小东西的科学在很多方面对我们产生了巨大的影响。
There's no doubt that nanotechnology promises so much for civilisation.
毫无疑问纳米技术在文明发展方面充满前景。
However, all new technologies have their teething problems.
但是所有的新科技在发展初期都会出现问题
And with nanotechnology, society often gets the wrong idea about its capabilities.
而对于纳米技术，人们经常对它的能力产生错误的想法。

Numerous science-fiction books and movies have raised people's fears about nanotechnology with scenarios such as inserting little nano robots into your body that monitor everything you do without you realising it, or self replicating nano robots that eventually take over the world.
很多科幻小说和电影使人们对纳米科技充满了恐惧，这些小说和电影中经常会出现这样的情节：有人会在你的身体里植入小小的纳米机器人来监视你的一举一动，而你却完全没有意识到这件事；或者自我复制的纳米机器人最后掌控了世界。
So how do we safeguard such a potentially powerful technology?
那么我们面对这么一种可能威力巨大的科技该如何做好保护措施呢？
Some scientists recommend that nano particles be treated as new chemicals with separate safety tests and clear labelling.
一些科学家建议把纳米粒子当做新的化学物质对待，用不同的安全测试对它们进行检测，并用清晰的标签来标示它们。
They believe that greater care should also be taken with nano particles in laboratories and factories.
他们认为在实验室和工厂内对待纳米粒子要更加小心。
Others have called for a withdrawal of new nano products such as cosmetics and a temporary halt to many kinds of nanotech research.
其他人要求召回像化妆品这样的新的纳米产品，并且暂时停止很多种纳米技术研究。
But as far as I'm concerned there's a need to plough ahead with the discoveries and applications of nanotechnology.
但是我认为还是需要继续研究和应用纳米技术的。
I really believe that most scientists would welcome a way to guard against unethical uses of such technology.
我真的相信大部分科学家都会对能够防止不道德地使用这种技术的方法表示欢迎。
We can't go around thinking that all innovation is bad, all advancement is bad.
我们不能一直认为所有的创新都是坏的，所有的进步都是不好的。
As with the debate about any new technology, it is how you use it that's important.
至于对任何新技术的争议，我只想说真正重要的是如何使用这些技术。
So let's look at some of its possible uses.
我们来看看几种可能的用途吧。
Thanks to nanotechnology, there could be a major breakthrough in the field of transportation with the production of more durable metals.
多亏了纳米技术，我们才能生产出更加持久耐用的金属，才能在交通运输领域做出重要的突破。
These could be virtually unbreakable, lighter and much more pliable leading to planes that are 50 times lighter than at present.
这些纳米金属几乎不会破损、重量更轻、柔韧性也更好，用这些金属制造出的飞机的重量是现有飞机的五十分之一。
Those same improved capabilities will dramatically reduce the cost of travelling into space making it more accessible to ordinary people and opening up a totally new holiday destination.
这些改善的金属性能会大大降低太空旅行的成本，使一般人也能有机会体验太空旅行，为大家开发出一个全新的度假地点。
In terms of technology, the computer industry will be able to shrink computer parts down to minute sizes.
在技术方面，计算机行业将能够把电脑零件缩到微小尺寸。
We need nanotechnology in order to create a new generation of computers that will work even faster and will have a million times more memory but will be about the size of a sugar cube.
我们需要用纳米技术来为电脑开创一个新的时代，那时的电脑工作速度更快、内存是现在的数百万倍，但是却只有一块方糖的大小。
Nanotechnology could also revolutionise the way that we generate power.
纳米技术还会变革我们产生能量的方式。
The cost of solar cells will be drastically reduced so harnessing this energy will be far more economical than at present.
太阳能电池的价格将会急剧下降，所以使用这种能源会比现在实惠得多。
But nanotechnology has much wider applications than this and could have an enormous impact on our environment.
不过纳米技术的应用范围要比这个更加广泛，它对我们的环境也会产生巨大的影响。
For instance, tiny airborne nano robots could be programmed to actually rebuild the ozone layer, which could lessen the impact of global warming on our planet.
比如，我们可以给小小的空中纳米机器人编程，让它们去复原臭氧层，这样就能减轻全球变暖对地球的影响了。
That's a pretty amazing thought, isn't it?
这个想法太令人惊叹了，对吧？

On a more local scale, this new technology could help with the clean up of environmental disasters as nanotechnology will allow us to remove oil and other contaminants from the water far more effectively.
我们把范围缩小一点来说，这项新技术能够帮助我们清理生态灾害，因为纳米技术会让我们更有效地从水中清除油脂和其他污染物。
And, if nanotechnology progresses as expected as a sort of building block set of about 90 atoms then you could build anything you wanted from the bottom up.
因为纳米技术会让我们更有效地从水中清除油脂和其他污染物。我们就可以彻底建造出任何想要的东西。
In terms of production, this means that you only use what you need and so there wouldn't be any waste.
在生产方面，纳米技术意味着我们只需要使用必要的材料，这样就不会产生任何浪费了。
The notion that you could create anything at all has major implications for our health.
能够创造任何东西这个概念对我们的健康有重要的影响。
It means that we'll eventually be able to replicate anything.
这就意味着我们最终可以复制出任何事物，
This would have a phenomenal effect on our society.
这在我们的社会中会产生非凡的影响。
In time it could even lead to the eradication of famine through the introduction of machines that produce food to feed the hungry.
假以时日，在能够制造食物填饱饥饿的机器诞生之后，这项技术甚至能消除饥荒。
But it's in the area of medicine that nanotechnology may have its biggest impact.
但是也许在医药领域，纳米技术才能发挥最大的作用。
How we detect disease will change as tiny biosensors are developed to analyse tests in minutes rather than days.
由于开发出的微小生物传感器能在几分钟而不是几天之内对测试进行分析，我们发现疾病的方式也会发生变化。
There's even speculation nano robots could be used to slow the ageing process, lengthening life expectancy.
甚至还有人推测纳米机器人能被用于减缓衰老的过程，延长寿命。
As you can see, I'm very excited by the implications that could be available to us in the next few decades.
正如你们所见，我对未来几十年里可能会产生的结果感到非常兴奋。
Just how long it'll take, I honestly don't know.
只是究竟还要多久，说实话我并不知道。

72.title: C9-Test 1-Section 1
transcript: Good evening.King's restaurant.
晚上好，这里是国王饭店。
Good evening.
晚上好
I'm ringing about the job I understand you have vacant?
我打电话是想询问你们那里的一个工作空缺
Oh, yes.
好的
I'd like to find out a few more details if I may.
我想多了解一些详情。
Yes, of course.
当然
Can I take your name?
我能问一下你的名字吗
It's Peter Chin.
彼得·陈
Okay Peter.
好的
Well, if you want to ask about the job and then if we're both still interested, we could arrange for you to come for an interview.
彼得，如果你想了解这份工作，并且之后我们双方仍感兴趣的话，我们可以安排你来面试，
Great, thanks.
太好了，谢谢
I'm afraid I missed the advert for the job but heard about it from a friend.
恐怕我错过了招聘广告，但是我从一个朋友那里听说了
That's no problem at all.
完全没有关系
What would you like to know?
你想了解什么
Well, um, what sort of work is it- washing up?
那是什么样的工作，洗碗吗？
It's answering the phone.
是接听电话
Oh, right, fine.
好的，可以
And not waiting at table.
不能招待用餐
That'll be good.
那挺好
And how many nights a week would it be?
很好。一周要工作几个晚上？
Well, we're really only busy at the weekend.
我们只会在周末很忙
So two nights?
那么是两个晚上吗
Three actually, so it would work out 12 hours a week.
事实上是三个，所以一周要工作12个小时。
That'd be fine.
很好
It wouldn't interfere with my studies.
这样就不会干扰我的学习了
Are you in the university?
你在上大学吗
Yes, first year Physics student.
是的，物理系一年级学生
Oh, right.
好的
Um, and because I'm not an EU national, would I need a work permit?
因为我不属于欧盟国家，所以我需要一张工作许可吗？
Yes, you would.
是的
Just get you tutor to sign it.
让你的导师签字就行
That wouldn't be a problem if I were to get the job.
如果我能得到那份工作就没有问题
Um, where exactly is the restaurant?
饭店具体在哪儿
Well, we have two branches- the one we're recruiting for is in Hillsdunne road.
我们有两家分支，我们招募的那家在Hillsdunne路。
I don't know that.
我不知道那里
How do you spell it, please?
请问怎么拼写
It's H-I-double L-S-D-U- double N-E Road.
Hillsdunne路
Got that, thanks.
记下了
Is it near a bus stop?
谢谢，附近有公交车站吗，
Yes, the nearest one would probably be just beside the library.
有。最近的一个可能就在图书馆旁边。
Oh, yes, I know it.
是的，我知道
That'll be fine for me.
对我来说很合适
And could I ask about the pay?
我能问一下工资吗
We're offering 4.45 pounds an hour.
我们按4.45磅一小时付
That's very good.
很好
My last job was 3.95 an hour.
我上一份工作是每小时3.95英镑
We feel it's pretty good and we also offer some good fringe benefits.
我们觉得这很不错，而且我们还提供一些附加福利，
Really?
真的吗
Well, we give you a free dinner, so you eat well.
我们为你提供免费的晚餐，所以你会吃的很好。
Right.
好的
Better than hostel food.
比招待所的食物要好
We certainly hope so.
我们希望如此
And we also offer extra pay for working on national holidays.
另外在国家法定假日工作，我们还会多付薪水，
Oh, that's a really good perk, isn't it?
这是一笔很好的额外收入，不是吗？
Yes, we think so.
是的
And then because of the difficulties of getting public transport, if you're working after 11 o'clock, we drive you home.
我们是这么想的，如果你工作到11点以后，由于难以搭上公共交通，我们会开车送你回家，
Oh, that's good to know.
那太好了
Well, we'd certainly be interested in inviting you for an interview, if you're still interested?
我们希望邀请你来面试，如果你还感兴趣的话？
Oh, yes, certainly.
当然
Could I just also ask what qualities you're looking for?
我能问一下你们有什么要求吗
Well, for this particular job, we want a clear voice, which you obviously do have.
这份工作需要有一副清楚的嗓音，你肯定合格！
Thanks.
谢谢。
And you must be able to think quickly, you know.
你还得思维敏捷。
Well, I hope I'd...
我希望我……
So, when could you come in for an interview?
那么你什么时候可以来面试
We're actually quite quiet tonight.
其实我们今晚这里比较安静
Sorry, I couldn't come tonight or tomorrow, I'm afraid.
对不起，我恐怕今晚或者明天都不能去
Thursday is ok, that'd be 22nd of October.
周四可以，也就是10月22日
Fine, after 5 pm?
好的，晚上5点以后
Yes, fine.
可以
Would 6 o'clock be ok?
6点行吗
Perfect.
好极了
And could you bring along the names of two referees?
你能告诉我两名推荐人的名字吗
Yes, that's fine. No problem.
好的，没问题。
Good, I look forward to seeing you.
很好，期待与你见面。
Oh, by the way, who should I ask for?
顺便说一下，我应该找谁呢？
Oh, yes, of course, sorry.
是的，当然，抱歉
My name is Samira Manuja.
我叫莎米拉·马努扎
Can you spell that please?
你能拼一下吗？
M-a-n-u-j-a
Manuja
Okay, I've got that.
好的，我记下了
Thanks very much.
非常感谢
Look forward to seeing you...
很好，期待与你见面。

73.title: C9-Test 1-Section 2
transcript: Now we go to Jane who is going to tell us about what's happening in town this weekend.
现在我们交给简，她会告诉我们这个周末城镇上将会发生什么。
Right, thanks, Andrew, and now on to what's new, and do we really need yet another sports shop in Bradcaster?
好的，谢谢安德鲁，现在有什么新鲜事，我们在Bradcaster真的需要另一家体育用品商店吗？

Well, most of you probably know Sports World - the branch of a Danish sports goods company that opened a few years ago-it's attracted a lot of custom, and so the company has now decided to open another branch in the area.
你们中的多数人可能知道丹麦体育用品公司的分店“体育天地”在几年前开业，并吸引了大量的客人，所以这个公司现在决定在该地区开另一家分店。
It's going to be in the shopping center to the west of Bradcaster, so that will be good news for all of you who found the original shop in the North of the town hard to get to.
它将在Bradcaster西边的购物中心开业，对于那些觉得原先在城镇北面的商店太远的人来说是一个好消息。
I was invited to a special preview and I can promise you, this is the ultimate in sports retailing.
我应邀预先参观，我向你们保证这将是体育零售业的旗舰店。
The whole place has been given a new minimalist look with the company's signature colors of black and red.
公司标志性的颜色黑色和红色带给整个地方一种全新的极简主义外观
The first three floors have a huge range of sports clothing as well as the equipment.
下面三层有大量的体育服装和器材
And on the top floor, there is a cafe and a book & DVD section.
顶层有咖啡，书店和光盘区域
You'll find all the well-known names as well as some less well-known ones.
你会找到所有知名和一些鲜为人知的东西。
If they haven't got exactly what you want in the stock, they promised to get it for you in 10 days, unlike the other store, where it can take up to 14 days.
如果他们没有你要找的东西，他们保证会在10天内为你找到。不像另一家商店要等上14天。
They cover all the major sports, including football, tennis, and swimming, but they particularly focus on running and they claim to have the widest range of equipment in the country.
他们覆盖所有主要的运动，包括足球、网球、游泳，但是他们特别关注于跑步，他们声称是全国拥有器材最为广泛的一家公司。
As well as that, a whole section of the third floor is devoted to sports bags, including the latest designs from the States - If you can't find what you want here, it doesn't exist.
除此之外，整个三层致力于运动包的销售，包括来自美国的最新设计。如果你不能在这里找到想要的，那么它就不存在！

The shop will be open from 9 am this Saturday and if you go along to the opening then you'll have the chance to meet the national 400 meters running champion Paul King, who's coming along to open the shop, and he will be staying around until about midday to chat to any fans who want to meet him and sign autographs.
商店将于这周六早上9点开业，如果你去开幕式，你将会遇到国家400米跑步冠军保罗·金。他也将一起为商店开张，他将待到中午左右与粉丝聊天、签名。
Then there will be a whole range of special attractions all weekend.
然后在所有周末将会有大量吸引人的特殊项目

There will be free tickets for local sporting events for the first 50 customers, and also a special competition open to all, just answer 15 out of 20 sports questions correctly to win a signed copy of Paul King's DVD 'Spring Tips', while the first person to get all the questions correct gets a year's free membership of the Bradcaster Gym.
对于前50名的客人将会免费赠予当地体育赛事的门票，另外还有一个特殊的竞赛向公众开放。只要从20道体育问题中回答正确15题，就能赢取保罗·金的签名光盘《Spring Tips》，第一位将所有问题回答正确的人将免费得到Bradcaster体育馆的全年会员资格。
All entrants will receive a special Sports calendar with details of all Bradcaster fixtures in the coming year.
所有参赛者都将获得一份特殊的体育日历，上面标有明年所有Bradcaster体育活动的详细信息。
One of the special opening offers is a fitness test- a complete review of your cardiac fitness and muscle tone, actually done in the shop by qualified staff.
其中一个特殊的开业特价是健康测试，由商店合格的工作人员全方面检查你的心脏健康和肌肉紧张度。
This would normally cost 30 pounds but is available at half price for this month only.
这通常要花费30英镑，但是只在这个月半价。
There are only a limited number of places available for this, so to make a booking phone 560341.
这项活动的地方有限，所以要拨打560341进行电话预约。
In addition, if you open an account you get lots more special offers including the chance to try out the equipment at the special opening evenings...
另外，如果你开一个账户，你会得到更多的特殊优惠，包括在特殊的开放日夜晚体验器材的机会。

74.title: C9-Test 1-Section 3
transcript: Before we start, Spiros and Hiroko, thanks for coming in today to talk about your recent study experiences and congratulations to you both in doing so well in your first semester exams!
在我们开始之前，感谢Spiros和Hiroko能在今天来这里讲讲你们最近的学习经历，并祝贺两位在第一学期的考试中取得了好成绩！
I'd like to discuss with you the value of the English for Academic Purposes course you did here last year before starting your university course.
我想与你们探讨在你们上大学之前，去年在这里学的学术英语课程的价值。
Spiros, if I could start with you, what parts of the programme have now proved to be particularly valuable to you?
Spiros，如果可以从你开始的话，项目的哪部分现在证明对你很有价值？
I think that having to do a seminar presentation really helped me.
我觉得研讨会演讲对我很有帮助
For example, a couple of weeks ago in our marketing subject, when it was my turn to give presentation, I felt quite confident.
例如，几周前当我们的营销专业轮到我演讲时，我感觉很自信。
Of course, I was still nervous but because I had done one before, I knew what to expect.
当然，我仍感到紧张，但是因为我之前做过，所以我知道会发生什么。
Also, I know I was well prepared and I had practised my timing.
而且我知道我准备充分，并练习控制时间。
In fact, I think that in relation to some of the other people in my group, I did quite a good job because my overall style was quite professional.
事实上，我认为相比我们组的其他人，我做得很好。因为我的整体风格都很专业。
What about you Hiroko?
你呢 Hiroko
Mmm, that's interesting.
很有趣
In my group, I was really surprised by the way students did their presentations - they just read the notes aloud.
在我的小组，我对同学们做演讲的方式感到惊讶，他们只是大声地读出他们的笔记，
Can you believe that?
你能相信吗
They didn't worry about their presentation style or keeping eye contact with their audience - and I remember that these things were really stressed to us in the course here.
他们不担心自己的演讲风格，也不和听众做眼神交流。我记得在这里上课时，这些事情对我们都很重要。
So, how did you approach your presentation Hiroko?
那么，Hiroko，你是如何演讲的？
Well, to speak frankly, I read my notes too.
坦白地说，我也读了我的笔记！
At the time, it was a relief to do it this way, but actually when I had finished I didn't feel any real sense of satisfaction.
当时这么做让人感觉很放松，但是其实当我结束后，我没有感到任何的满足感。
I didn't feel positive about the experience at all.
我并不觉得这是个积极的经历
That's a pity.
真是一种遗憾

You know although I was pleased with my presentation, I'm not so pleased with my actual performance right now in the tutorials - during the whole semester I've not said anything in our tutorial discussions.
尽管我对我的演讲满意，但是我不满意我在课堂上的实际表现。整个学期我在讨论课上一言未发。
Not a word.
一个字也没说过。
Really, Spiros,why's that?
是吗？为什么会这样？
Do the other students talk too much?
其他学生说的太多了吗？
It's partly that, but it's mostly because I have had no confidence to speak out.
有一部分是这个原因，但是多数是因为我没有勇气畅所欲言。
Their style of speaking is so different-it's not the style we were used to during the course.
他们的讲话风格与我们上课时的风格很不一样
They use so many colloquialisms, they're not very polite and sometimes there seems to be no order in their discussion.
他们用很多的口语，他们不是很有礼貌，有时他们的讨论似乎没有秩序。
Also they are very familiar with each other, so because they know each other's habits, they can let each other into the discussion.
而且，他们对彼此很熟悉，所以他们知道彼此的习惯，也就很容易让对方加入讨论。
You're right, Spiros.
你说得对
I've experienced that too.
Spiros，我也有这种体会，
For most of this semester, I've said absolutely nothing in tutorials.
在这个学期的多数时间里，我在课上都没有说过话。
But recently I've been trying to speak up more and I just jump in.
但是最近我试着多发表意见
And I've noticed an interesting thing.
我投入到讨论中并发现了一件有趣的事情
I've noticed that if they thought my point was interesting and new, then the next time they actually asked for my opinion, and then it was much easier for me to be part of the discussion.
我发现如果他们认为我的观点很有趣或新颖，下一次他们就会询问我的意见，这就使我顺利的加入到讨论中去。
That's great, Hiroko.
太好了
I hope that happens for me next semester-I'll have to work hard to find some interesting points.
希望下学期我也能这样，我得努力学习找出一些有趣的观点，
What help you to find these ideas?
我得努力学习找出一些有趣的观点
I think that one thing that helped me with this was the reading.
我认为是读书帮了我
I've had to do so much reading this semester just to help me to make sense of the lectures.
这学期我不得不读很多的书，以帮我理解讲课的内容。
At first I couldn't understand what the lecturers were talking about, so I had to turn to the books and journals.
起初，我不明白老师在讲什么，所以我不得不看书和期刊。
Every night I read for hours, using the lists of references that were given, and I made pages of notes.
每晚我都照着参考书单读几个小时的书，我还做了很多页的笔记。
At breakfast, I read and read my notes again.
早餐时，我一遍遍地读我的笔记。
This habit has helped me to follow the ideas in the lectures, and it's also given me some ideas to use in the tutorials.
这个习惯帮我跟上了讲课的思路，并且在讨论课上带给我许多启发。
But I did so much readings anyway-I don't think there's any time left over for anything extra.
但是我也做了许多阅读，我认为没有什么时间做这些额外的事情。
My reading speed is still quite slow, though I'm much better at dealing with vocabulary than I used to be.
我的读书速度还是很慢，尽管我现在比过去在处理词汇上要好很多。
What else do you think we could add to the course program to help with this reading problem?
你们认为我们在课堂项目上可以加点什么来解决阅读问题呢
There's not really anything because it's my problem.
没有什么，因为这是我的问题。
I remember we were given long articles to read.
我记得我们曾经要读长篇文章
We didn't like that but now I realise that reading those long articles was good preparation for the things I need to read now.
我那时不喜欢，但是现在我意识到读长篇文章对于我现在的阅读是一种很好的准备。
Also, in class we regularly had speed-reading tasks to do, and we kept a record of our reading speed, so the teachers were encouraging us to work on that.
另外，我们在课上定期会有快速阅读要做，我们对阅读的速度计时，因此老师鼓励我们这样做。
That's true, Spiros, but what we read could have been different.
没错，Spiros，但是我们读得本可以不同。

Sometimes in the English class I felt frustrated when I had to read articles about the environment or health or education, because I wanted to concentrate on my own field, but we didn't read anything about engineering.
有时在英语课上，当我读环境、健康或教育的文章时，我总感到受挫，因为我想专注于我的领域，但是我们读不到任何关于工程学的文章。
So, I think I wasted some time learning vocabulary I didn't need.
所以，有时我觉得我在浪费时间学一些我用不着的词汇。
But surely this strategies you were taught for dealing with that vocabulary were helpful.
但是教你处理词汇的方法肯定是有用的。
Yes, but psychologically speaking, I would have felt much better working on reading from my own field.
是的，但是从心理上说，我更喜欢读自己领域的文章
What do you think Spiros?
Spiros，你觉得呢？
I agree:that would have helped my confidence too, and I would have been more motivated.
我同意，这也会提高我的自信心，使我更有动力。
And it was good though that we could work on our own topics when we wrote the research assignments.
当我们写研究作业的时候，这对于我们研究的题目也有帮助。
Okay, let's move on to writing now...
好的，现在谈谈写作……

75.title: C9-Test 1-Section 4
transcript: Good afternoon everyone.
大家下午好。

Well, with some of you about to go out on fieldwork, it's timely that in this afternoon's session I'll be sharing some ideas about the reasons why groups of whales and dolphins sometimes swim ashore from the sea right onto the beach and, most often, die in what are known as 'mass strandings'.
你们中的一些人即将去实地考察，是时候在今天下午的课上讲讲为什么成群的鲸鱼和海豚会从大海游上海岸，并经常死于大规模的搁浅了。
Unfortunately, this type of event is a frequent occurrence in some of the locations that you'll be travelling to, when sometimes the tide goes out suddenly, confusing the animals.
不幸的是，这种事情经常发生在你去游览的地方。有时，那里的潮汐突然退去会使动物感到困惑。
However, there are many other theories about the causes of mass strandings.
然而，还有许多其他关于大规模搁浅成因的理论。
The first is that the behavior is linked to parasites.
首先是这种行为与寄生虫有关。
It's often found that stranded animals were infested with large numbers of parasites.
经常发现搁浅的动物受到大量寄生虫的感染。
For instance, a type of worm is commonly found in the ears of dead whales.
例如，一种虫子经常能在死鲸鱼的耳朵里找到。
Since marine animals rely heavily on their hearing to navigate, this type of infestation has the potential to be very harmful.
由于海洋动物主要靠听力导航，这种感染的潜在危害很大。
Another theory is related to toxins, or poisons.
另一种理论与毒素或毒药有关。
These have also been found to contribute to the death of many marine animals.
这些也导致了许多海洋生物的死亡。
Many toxins, as I'm sure you're aware, originate from plants, or animals.
我想你们也知道，许多毒素来源于植物和动物。
The whale ingests these toxins in its normal feeding behavior but whether these poisons directly or indirectly lead to stranding and death, seems to depend upon the toxin involved.
鲸鱼在它们的日常摄食行为中摄取了这些毒素，但是这些毒药是否直接或间接导致搁浅和死亡似乎要取决于涉及的毒素本身。

In 1988, for example, fourteen humpback whales examined after stranding along the beaches of Cape Cod were found to have been poisoned after eating tuna that contained saxitoxin, the same toxin that can be fatal in humans.
例如，1988 年，在科德角海滩搁浅的 14 头座头鲸经检查后发现，它们是因为食用了含有蛤蚌毒素（这种毒素对人类也可能是致命的）的金枪鱼而中毒的。
Alternatively, it has also been suggested that some animals strand accidentally by following their prey ashore in the confusion of the chase.
另外，也有人提出，一些动物是在追捕猎物时陷入混乱，跟着猎物上岸，从而意外搁浅的。
In 1995 David Thurston monitored pilot whales that beached after following the squid ashore.
1995年，大卫·瑟斯顿监控到巨头鲸在追踪岸边的鱿鱼后搁浅。
However, this idea does not seem to hold true for the majority of mass strandings because examination of the animals' stomach contents reveal that most had not been feeding as they stranded.
然而，这种想法并不适用于大多数大规模的搁浅，因为当检查搁浅的动物时，发现多数动物的胃里没有食物。
There are also some new theories, which link strandings to humans.
还有一些新理论将搁浅与人类相连。
A growing concern is that loud noises in the ocean cause strandings.
海洋噪音引发搁浅日益受到人们的关注。
Noises such as these caused by military exercises are of particular concern and have been pinpointed as the cause of some strandings of late.
例如军事演习的噪音特别受到关注，而且作为一些搁浅的原因被指出。
One of these, a mass stranding of whales in 2000 in the Bahamas coincided closely with experiments using a new submarine detection system.
其中一个发生在2000年的巴哈马群岛，一次大规模的鲸鱼搁浅与新型水下探测系统的实验同时发生。
There were several factors that made this stranding stand out as different from previous strandings.
有一些因素表明这次搁浅与以往的搁浅不同。
This led researchers to look for a new cause.
这让研究人员开始寻找新的原因。
For one, all the stranded animals were healthy.
首先，所有搁浅的动物都很健康。
In addition, the animals were spread out along 38 kilometers of coast, whereas it's more common for the animals to be found in a group when mass strandings occur.
另外，动物在海岸上散布了38公里，然而当大规模搁浅发生时，动物往往是成群的被发现。
A final theory is related to group behaviour, and suggests that sea mammals cannot distinguish between sick and healthy leaders and will follow sick leaders, even to an inevitable death.
最后一个理论与团体行为有关，海洋哺乳动物不能区分生病和健康的领导者，它们会跟随患病的领导者，即使是导致一场不可避免的死亡。
This is a particularly interesting theory since the whales that are thought to be most social-the toothed whaled- are the group that strand the most frequently.
这是一个很有意思的理论，因为鲸鱼（齿鲸）被认为是最爱群居的动物，它们最频繁的成群搁浅

The theory is also supported by evidence from a dolphin stranding in 1994. Examination of the dead animals revealed that apart from the leader, all the others had been healthy at the time of their death.
1994年海豚的搁浅也证实了这个理论。检查动物的死尸时发现，除了领导者以外，其他所有动物在死时都很健康。
Without one consistent theory however it is very hard for us to do anything about this phenomenon except to assist animal where and when we can.
然而，没有一个统一的理论很难让我们改变这个现象，除了帮助那些我们在何时何地发现的动物。
Stranding networks have been established around the world to aid in rescuing animals and collecting samples from those that could not be helped.
全世界成立了搁浅网络，旨在救助动物并从那些无法获救的动物身上提取样本。
I recommend John Connor's Marine Mammals ashore as an excellent starting point if you're interested in finding out more about these networks, or establishing one yourself.
如果你们想找到更多这方面的网络或建立自己的网络，我推荐约翰·康纳的“海洋哺乳动物上岸”作为一个极好的出发点。

76.title: C9-Test 2-Section 1
transcript: Good morning.
早上好
Can I help you?
需要帮忙吗
Yes.
是的
I've just been accepted on a course at the university and I'd like to try and arrange accommodation in the hall of residence.
我刚入学，我想安排一间大学宿舍
Yes, certainly.
当然
Please sit down.
请坐
What I'll do is fill in a form with you to find out a little more about your preferences and so forth.
我会帮你填表，了解一些你的喜好等等
Thank you.
谢谢
So first of all, can I take your name?
首先，你叫什么名字
It's Anu Bhatt.
安努·巴特
Could you spell your name please?
请问你能拼一下吗
Yes.
好的
A-N-U...B-H-A double T.
A-N-U-B-H-A-T-T
Thanks, and could I ask your date of birth?
谢谢，我能问一下你的出生日期吗
31st March 1972.
Thank you.
谢谢
And where are you from?
你来自哪里
India.
印度
Oh right.
好的
And what will you be studying?
你学的是什么
I'm doing a course in nursing.
我学的是护理课
Right, thank you.
好的，谢谢。
And how long would you want to stay in hall, do you think?
你想在大学宿舍住多久
Well, it'll take three years but I'd only like to stay in hall for two.
课程要学3年，但是我只在大学宿舍住两年。
I'd like to think about living outside for the third year.
第三年我想住外面
Fine.
好的
And what did you have in mind for catering?
关于饮食你是怎么考虑的
Do you want to cook for yourself or have all your meals provided, that's full board?
你是想自己做饭还是想三餐全在学校吃
Is there something in between?
有没有居中的方式
Yes.
有
You can just have evening meal provided, which is half board.
你可以只在学校吃晚饭，这是半食宿制
That's what I prefer.
我更喜欢这样
Yes, a lot of students opt for that.
是的，很多学生这么选
Now, with that in mind, do you have any special diet, anything we should know about?
现在，考虑到这一点的话，你有没有忌口，有什么是我们要了解的吗？
Yes, I don't take red meat.
是的，我不吃红肉，
No red meat.
不吃红肉
Now thinking about the room itself, we have a number of options.
现在关于房间，我们有很多选择。
You can have a single study bedroom or you can have a shared one.
你可以选择一间单人房也可以和别人共享
These are both what we call simple rooms.
这就是我们说的简易房
The other alternative is to opt for a single bedsit, which actually has more space and better facilities.
另一种选择是单人小套房，它的空间更大，设施更好。
There's about ￡20 a week difference between them.
这两者之间一周相差大约20英镑
Well, actually my grant is quite generous and I think the bedsit sound the best option.
事实上，我的补助金很多，我想小套房是最好的选择。
Lovely.
很好
I'll put you down for that and we'll see what availability is like.
我帮你记下，看看有没有可以住的
Now can I ask some other personal details which we like to have on record?
现在我能问你一些需要记录的个人问题吗
Yes, of course.
当然
I wonder if you could let us know what your interests are.
你能否告诉我们你的兴趣是什么
This might help us get a closer match for placing you in a particular hall.
这能帮我们为你找到最适合的公寓
Ummm.
Umm
Well, I love the theatre.
嗯，我喜欢戏剧
Right.
好的
And I enjoy sports, particularly badminton.
我喜欢运动，特别是羽毛球
That's worth knowing.
了解到这点很好
Now, what we finish with on the form is really a list from you of what your priorities are in choosing a hall and we'll do our best to take these into account.
现在我们根据你在宿舍选择时的一列优先考虑事项而完成这个表，我们会尽量把这些考虑在内。
Well, the first thing is I'd prefer a hall where there are other mature students, if possible.
首先我更喜欢宿舍里有其他的成年学生，如果可以的话。
Yes, we do have halls which tend to cater for slightly older students.
是的。我们通常有招待年纪稍长一点的学生的宿舍。
Ummm and I'd prefer to be out of town.
我还是倾向于城外的
That's actually very good for you because we tend have more vacancies in out-of-town halls.
其实这对你来说很好，因为我们在城外有更多的宿舍空缺。
Lucky!
真走运
Yes.
是的
Anything else?
还有什么要求吗
Well, I would like somewhere with a shared area, a TV room for example, or something like that.
我希望有一个共享区域，例如电视机房之类的
It's a good way to socialise.
这是社交的好方法
It certainly is.
当然
That's it.
就是这些
Now, we just need a contact telephone number for you.
现在，我们需要知道你的联系电话。
Sure, I'll just find it.
当然，我找找
It's double 6-7549.
Great, so we'll be in contact with you as soon as possible...
好的，我们会尽快联系你

77.title: C9-Test 2-Section 2
transcript: Hello, I'm delighted to welcome you to our Wildlife Club, and very pleased that you're interested in the countryside and the plants and creatures of this area.
你好，欢迎大家来到我们的野生动物俱乐部，我很高兴你们对乡村和该地区的动植物感兴趣。
I think you'll be surprised at the variety we have here, even though we're not far from London.
我想你们会对这里的多样性感到震惊，即使我们离伦敦不远。
I'll start by telling you about some of the parks and open spaces nearby.
我先来向你们介绍一些附近的公园和露天场所
One very pleasant place is Halland Common.
有一个宜人的地方就是Halland Common
This has been public land for hundreds of years, and what you'll find interesting is that the River Ouse, which flows into the sea eighty kilometers away, had its source in the common.
这里在几百年前就是公共用地，你会发现有趣的是流向80公里外大海的乌斯河，源头在Common。
There's an information board about the plants and animal you can see here, and by the way, the common is accessible 24 hours a day.
这里有一个信息栏写着你能看到的动植物，顺便说一下，Common在一天24小时都可以进入。
Then there's Holt Island, which is noted for its great range of trees.
然后是霍尔特岛，因大面积的树林而闻名。
In the past willows were grown here commercially for basket-making, and this ancient craft has recently been reintroduced.
过去在这里种植杨柳是为了商业上的篮子制作，这些古老的手艺最近再次被引入。
The island is only open to the public from Friday to Sunday, because it's quite small, and if there were people around every day, much of the wildlife would keep away.
这个岛只在周五至周日向公众开放，因为这里很小，如果每天都有人来，那么许多野生动物都会逃走。
From there it's just a short walk across the bridge to Longfield Country Park.
从这里只需走一点经过一座桥就能到达朗菲尔德国家公园。
Longfield has a modern replica of a farm from over two thousand years ago.
朗菲尔德复制出了两千多年前的农场面貌。
Children's activities are often arranged there, like bread-making and face-painting.
儿童活动经常在这里举办，如面包制作和脸部彩绘。
The park is only open during daylight hours, so bear that in mind if you decide to go there.
公园只在白天开放，所以如果你决定来这里，请记住这点
Longfield Park has a programme of activities throughout the year, and to give you a sample, this is what's happening in the next few days.
朗菲尔德公园全年都有活动，举个例子，这是在接下来的几天举行的活动。
On Monday you can learn about herbs, and how they've been used over the centuries.
你可以在周一学习草本植物，几个世纪以来，人们是如何使用它们的。
You'll start with a tour of our herb garden, practise the technique of using them as colour dyes for cloth, and listen to an illustrated talk about their use in cooking and medicine.
你们会先从参观草本植物园开始，练习将它们用作衣服染料的手法，参照图片聆听它们在烹饪和药材上的用途。
Then on Wednesday you can join local experts to discover the variety of insects and birds that appear in the evening.
然后，你可以在周三和当地专家一起研究在晚上出现的各种昆虫和鸟类。
We keep to a small number of people in the group, so if you want to go you'll need to phone the park ranger a few days ahead.
我们在这组上要限制人数， 所以如果你想参加，你需要提前几天给公园管理员打电话。
There's a small charge, which you should pay when you turn up.
这会收取少许费用，你来的时候要交费。
I'm sure you're all keen to help with the practical task of looking after the park, so on Saturday you can join a working party.
我敢肯定你们都希望帮助照看公园，所以你们可以在周六加入工作组。
You'll have a choice of all sorts of activities, from planting hedges to picking up litter, so you'll be able to change from one to another when you feel like it.
你们可以选择各种各样的活动，从种树篱到捡垃圾，所以你们可以根据喜好而改变。
The rangers will be hard at work all day, but do come and join in, even for just a short while.
公园管理员整天都得努力工作，但是来加入吧，即使是加入一会儿。
One thing, though, is to make sure you're wearing something that you don't mind getting dirty or torn.
尽管有一件事是你们得穿不介意弄脏、弄破的衣服。
And finally I'd like to tell you about our new wildlife area, Hinchingbrooke Park, which will be opened to the public next month.
最后，我想向你们介绍新的野生动物区。Hinchingbrooke公园将于下个月向公众开放。
This slide doesn't really indicate how big it is, but anyway, you can see the two gates into the park, and the main paths.
这个幻灯片不能表现出它有多大，但是你可以看到公园的两个大门和主路。
As you can see, there's a lake in the north west of the park, with a bird hide to the west of it, at the end of a path.
正如你看到的，公园的西北部有一座湖，在路的尽头，湖的西边儿有一个观鸟点。
So it'll be a nice quiet place for watching the birds on the lake.
所以这是观看湖中小鸟的安静之所。
Fairly close to where refreshments are available, there's a dog-walking area in the southern part of the park, leading off from the path.
小吃摊旁还有一个遛狗的区域在公园的南部，从小路上延伸开来。
And if you just want to sit and relax, you can go to the flower garden; that's the circular area on the map surrounded by paths.
如果你想坐下来休息，你可以去花园。它在地图上是一个圆形的区域，由小路环绕。
And finally, there's a wooded area in the western section of the park, between two paths.
最后，在公园的西面，两条小路的中间是林地。
Okay, that's enough from me, so let's go on to...
好了，我已经说的够多了，让我们接着……

78.title: C9-Test 2-Section 3
transcript: Hi Jun.
你好 Jun
As you know, I've asked you here today to discuss the future of our Self-Access Centre.
我今天找你来是想讨论自习室的未来，
We have to decide what we want to do about this very important resource for our English language students.
我们得决定如何处理这个对我们英语学生非常重要的资源
So, can you tell me what the students think about this?
所以，你能告诉我学生们都是怎么想的吗？
Well, from the student's point of view, we would like to keep it.
从学生的角度看，我们需要保留它。

The majority of students say that they enjoy using it because it provides a variation on the classroom routine and they see it as a pretty major component of their course, but we would like to see some improvements to the equipment, particularly the computers; there aren't enough for one each at the moment and we always have to share.
大多数学生称自己很喜欢它，因为它为常规的课程带来了变化，他们认为这是课程的重要部分，但是我们希望设备能有所改进，特别是电脑。有时不能达到每个人使用一台，我们总是要和别人一起用。

Well yes, the teachers agree that it is a very valuable resource but one thing we have noticed is that a lot of the students are using it to check their personal emails.We don't want to stop you students using it,but we think the computers should be used as a learning resource, not for emails.
是的，老师们认为这是非常有价值的资源，但是我注意到一件事，许多学生用它查看个人邮件。我们不想阻止学生使用它，但是我们认为电脑应该当作一种学习资源，而不是查看电邮。
Some of us also think that we could benefit a lot more by relocating the Self-Access Centre to the main University library building.
我们中的一些人认为将自习室搬到大学图书馆会使人受益更多
How do you think the students would feel about that, Jun?
琼，你觉得学生们对此怎么看？
Well, the library is big enough to incorporate the Self-Access Centre, but it wouldn't be like a class activity anymore.
图书馆有足够的空间容纳自习室，但是这样就不再像课堂活动了。
Our main worry would be not being able to go to a teacher for advice.
我们主要的担心是这样就无法找老师寻求意见了
I'm sure there would be plenty of things to do but we really need teachers to help us choose the best activities.
我肯定那里会有很多事情要做，但是我们真的需要老师帮我们选择最佳的活动。
Well, there would still be a teacher present and he or she would guide the activities of the students, we wouldn't just leave them to get on with it.
那里仍然会有一位老师在场，他或她将指导学生的活动，我们不会置之不理。
Yes, but I think the students would be much happier keeping the existing set-up; they really like going to the Self-Access Centre with their teacher and staying together as a group to do activities.
是的，但是我认为学生们更希望保持现状，他们很喜欢和老师一起去自习室，一起做活动。
If we could just improve the resources and facilities, I think it would be fine.
如果我们可以只是改善资源和设施，我觉得就很好。
Is the cost going to be a problem?
经费是问题吗
It's not so much the expense that I'm worried about, and we've certainly got room to do it, but it's the problem of timetabling a teacher to be in there outside class hours.
我担心的不是费用问题，而且我们也有地方，但是问题是在课外时间给老师排课。
If we're going to spend a lot of money on equipment and resources, we really need to make sure that everything is looked after properly.
如果我们要在设备和资源上花很多钱，我们真的要确保一切事情都能得到妥善处理。
Anyway, let's make some notes to see just what needs doing to improve the Centre.
总之，让我们记下笔记，看看改善中心需要做什么事。
Now, what about the computers?
现在，电脑怎么样？
I think it might be a good idea to install some new models.
我想装一些新型号是一个好主意
They would take up a lot less room and so that would increase the work space for text books and so on.
它们占用的空间少，这样可以增加参考书等资源的运行空间。
That would be great.
太好了
It is a bit cramped in there at times.
有时里面的地方是很小
What about other resources?
其他资源怎么样
Do you have a list of things that the students would like to see improved?
你有学生们希望改进的单子目录吗

Yes, one of the comments that students frequently make is that they find it difficult to find materials that are appropriate for their level, especially reading resources, so I think we need to label them more clearly.
是的。学生们经常说难以找到适合自己水平的材料，特别是读书资源，所以我想我们需要把它们标记得更清楚一些。
Well that's easy enough, we can get that organized every quickly.
这很简单，我们可以很快弄好。
In fact I think we should review all of the study resources as some of them are looking a bit out-of-date.
事实上，我觉得我们应该检查一下所有的学习资源，因为有些资源已经有点过时了。
Definitely.
当然
The CD section especially needs to be more current.
特别是光盘区需要更新
I think we should get some of the ones that go with our latest course books and also make multiple copies.
我认为我们应该引进一些与最新课本配套的资源，并且多留一些副本。
Good, now I was also thinking about some different materials that we haven't got in there at all.
很好。我还想到了一些我们这里没有的各种资源。
What do you think of the idea of introducing some workbooks?
你觉得引进一些练习册怎么样
If we break them up into separate pages and laminate them, they'd be a great resource.
如果我们将它们拆分成单页，它们将是很好的资源。
The students could study the main course book in class and then do follow-up practice in the Self-Access Centre.
学生们可以在课堂上学习主要的教材知识，然后在自习室做后续的练习，
That sounds good.
听起来不错
Okay, now finally we need to think about how the room is used.
好的，最后我们要想想怎么使用空间。
I'll have to talk to the teachers and make sure we can all reach some agreement on a timetable to supervise the centre after class.
我得跟老师们谈谈，确保在课后监督中心的时间上达成一致。
But we also need to think about security, too.
但是我们也要考虑安全问题
Especially if we're going to invest in some new equipment.
特别是当我们对一些新设备投资时
What about putting in an alarm?
装警报器怎么样
Good idea.
好主意
The other thing I'd like to do is talk to our technicians and see whether we could somehow limit the access to email.
我要做的另一件事是找到技术人员，看看能否减少电邮的使用，
I really don't want to see that resource misused.
我不想看到资源被滥用
What about if we agree to only use it before and after class?
如果我们同意只在课前或课后用怎么样
Yes, that would be fine.
好的，没问题。
OK, anyway... that's great for now.
总之目前还不错，
We'll discuss it further when we've managed to...
我们将进一步讨论，当……

79.title: C9-Test 2-Section 4
transcript: Good morning everyone.
大家早上好
Now whether you're going to University to study business or some other subject, many of you will eventually end up working for a company of some kind.
大现在，无论你们是在大学中学习商业还是其他专业，最后你们中的许多人都会去一些公司上班。
Now, when you first start working somewhere you will realise that the organisation you've joined has certain characteristics.
当你刚开始在某家公司工作时，你会发现你加入的这家公司有某些特征。
And we often refer to these social characteristics as the culture of the organisation- this includes its unwritten ideas, beliefs, values and things like that.
我们经常把这些社会特征称为公司文化。它们包括一些不成文的想法、理念、价值等等。
One well known writer has classified company cultures by identifying four major types.
一位知名作家将公司文化分为四个主要类型
The first type is called the Power Culture, and it's usually found in small organisations.
第一种是权力文化，经常出现在小公司里。
It's the type of culture that needs a central source of power to be effective, and because control is in the hands of just one or two people there aren't many rules or procedures.
这种文化需要权力的中央来源是有效的，因为控制权只在一、两个人的手中，所以没有很多规则或程序。
Another characteristic is that communication usually takes the form of conversations rather than, say, formal meetings or written memos.
另一种特征通常是对话形式的交流，而不是正规的开会或写备忘录。
Now one of the benefits of this culture is that the organisation has the ability to act quickly, so it responds well to threat, or danger on the one hand, and opportunity on the other.
这种文化的优点是公司有迅速作出反应的能力，所以它可以及时应对威胁、危险或机遇。

But on the negative side, this type of organisation doesn't always act effectively, because it depends too much on one or two people at the top, and when these people make poor decisions there's no-one else who can influence them.
但是消极的一面是这类公司不能总是有效的做出行动，因为它太依赖于顶层的一、两个人，当这些人做出错误的选择时，没有人能左右他们。
And the kind of person who does well in this type of business culture is one who is happy to take risks, and for whom job security is a low priority.
在这种公司文化里做得好的人是那种乐意冒险，对工作保障考虑较靠后的人。
The next type is known as Role Culture--that's R-O-L-E, not R-O-double L, by the way, and this type is usually found in large companies, which have lots of different levels in them.
下一种是著名的角色文化，顺便说一下，是ROLE而不是ROLL，这种类型经常出现在大公司，里面有许多不同的层次。
These organisations usually have separate departments that specialise in things like finance, or sales, or maintenance, or whatever.
这些公司通常有独立的部门，专攻于不同的事情，如金融、销售、维修等等。

Each one is co-ordinated at the top by a small group of senior managers and typically everyone's job is controlled by sets of rules and procedures-for example, there are specific job descriptions, rules for discipline, and so on.
每个部门由一小组的高级经理来管理协调，通常每个人的工作受一系列规则和程序的约束，例如有一些特定的工作说明，纪律规则等等。
What are the benefits of this kind of culture?
这种文化的优势是什么呢
Well firstly, because it's found in large organisations, its fixed costs, or overheads as they're known, are low in relation to it's output, or what it produces.
首先因为它存在于大型公司，所以固定成本或日常开销相比它的输出或生产要低。
In other words it can achieve economies of scale.
换句话说，它可以实现规模经济。
And secondly, it is particularly successful in business markets where technical expertise is important.
第二，它能在那些专业技术重要的商业市场上取得成功。
On the other hand, this culture is often very slow to recognise the need for change, and even slower to react.
另一方面，这种文化经常认识不到变革的需要，甚至更加难以作出反应。
What kind of person does this type of culture suit?
什么样的人适合这种文化呢
Well it suits employees who value security, and who don't particularly want to have responsibility.
它适合看重保障、不是很想承担责任的员工。
Moving on now to Task
现在转向任务文化
Cultures-this type is found in organisations that are project-oriented.
这种类型存在于以项目为导向的公司里
You usually find it where the market for the company's product is extremely competitive, or where the products themselves have a short life-span.
你会发现它存在于公司产品极有竞争力的市场上，或寿命很短的产品上。
Usually top management delegates the projects, the people and other resources.
通常，高层管理人员会委派项目、人员和其他资源。
And once these have been allocated, little day-to-day control is exercised from the top, because this would seem like "breaking the rules"
一旦分配好这些，上层会进行小的日常控制，因为这好像是在打破规则。
Now one of the major benefits of this culture is that it's flexible.
这种文化的主要优势之一是它的灵活性
But it does have some major disadvantages too.
但是它也有一些重要的不足
For instance, it can't produce economies of scale or great depth of expertise.
例如，它不能产生规模经济或精深的专业知识。
People who like working in groups or teams prefer this type of culture.
喜欢团队工作的人喜欢这种文化。
And finally, the fourth category is called the Person Culture...
最后，第四种类型是个人文化……

84.title: C9-Test 4-Section 1
transcript: Can I help you?
需要帮忙吗
Yes, I've just moved to this area with my wife and children and I'd like to know where we can all register with a doctor at a Health Center.
是的，我刚和我的妻子、孩子搬到这里，我想知道我们在哪里可以与健康中心的医生登记。
Okay.
好的
Well. There's doctor Green at the Harvey Clinic.
在哈维诊所有格林医生
We always will recommend her for babies, because she is very good with them and she runs a special clinic.
我们总为婴儿推荐她，因为她和他们相处的很好，而且她经营一家特殊的诊所。
Oh... actually my youngest child is five, so that wouldn't be any good for us.
事实上，我最小的孩子5岁了，所以这对我们没有帮助，
Right.
好的
Is there anywhere else I could try?
还有别的地方我可以试试吗
Yes, the Eshcol Health Practice is the next one on my list.
是的，我的单子上的下一个是Eshcol健康诊所。
How do you spell that?
怎么拼写
E-s-h-c-o-l, And it's doctor Fuller, who has space on his list.
E-S-H-C-O-L。富勒医生，他还有名额。
The clinic only opened a year ago, so the facilities are all very modern.
诊所是一年前刚开的，所以设施很现代，
That sounds good.
听起来不错
And it's particularly good if you are busy during the day, because they also do the appointments in the evening.
如果你白天很忙也没有关系，因为他们在晚上也可以预约。
They closed on Saturday, though.
但是，他们在周六关门。
The only other place on the list is the Health Center on Shore Lane.
单子上还剩下一个地方是在Shore Lane的健康中心
You can register with doctor Gormley, that's G-o-r-m-l-e-y.
你可以挂戈姆利医生的号，G-O-R-M-L-E-Y。
He is new there, but the center has very good reputation.
他是新来的，但是中心的名声很好。"
Oh, yes, I think I know the road.
是的，我想我知道路线。
That would be the best one.
这应该是最好的
Thanks.
谢谢
Could you tell me, will all their services be free?
你能告诉我，他们的服务都是免费的吗？
Erm...there are usually some small charges that doctors make.
通常医生会有一些小额收费
Let me see what it says about the Shore Lane Center.
让我看看Shore Lane中心是怎么说的。
If you need to be vaccinated before any trips abroad, you won't have to pay for this.
如果你在去国外旅行之前需要接种疫苗的话，你不需要为此付钱。
Ah, what else?
还有什么呢？
The sports injury treatment service operates on the paying basis, as does the nutritional therapy service.
运动损伤医疗服务需要付费，营养治疗服务也是一样。
Some health centers do offer alternative therapies like homeopathy as part of their pay-to-use service.
一些健康中心提供替代疗法，例如将顺势疗法作为付钱服务的一部分。
Shore Lane are hoping to do this soon.
Shore Lane很快也希望这么做
I think they may start with acupuncture.
我认为他们会从针灸开始

And finally, if you need to prove you're healthy or haven't had any serious injuries before a new employer will accept you, you can get a free fitness check-up there, but you'd most likely have to pay for insurance medicals though.
最后，如果你需要在新雇主录用你之前证明你的健康状况或无严重疾病史，你可以在那里接受免费的健康检查，但是你可能要付医疗险。
Okay, thanks.
好的谢谢
You might be also interested to know the Center is running a pilot scheme talks with patients.
你也许对这个感兴趣，中心正在运营一项与病人谈话的试验计划。
I've got the list here. Actually, they look very interesting.
我这里有一个单子，事实上，它们看起来很有趣。
What sort of things?
什么样的事情
Well, the first one is about giving up smoking.
第一个是关于戒烟的。
It's next week, the 25th of February, at 7 pm, and that's in Room 4.
在下周，2月25日晚上7点，4号房间。
It says, the talk will stress the health benefits, particularly for people with asthma or heart disease.
会谈主要讲的是针对哮喘和心脏病人的健康福利"
That sounds very interesting.
听起来很有趣
There's also a talk for families with children. It's on Healthy Eating, and takes place at the first of March, at 5 o'clock.
还有关于有孩子的家庭的讲话。它在3月1日的5点，“健康饮食”举行。
Will that be at the Health Center?
这是在健康中心里吗
Erm, actually it's the primary school on Shore Lane.
事实上是在Shore Lane的一家小学里
I imagine they're inviting parents of pupils there-and it says here "all welcome".
我想他们会邀请小学生的家长，这上面写的是“全部欢迎”。
Mmm, I might go to that if I have time.
如果我有时间，我会去。
There's a couple of other talks-one giving advice about how to avoid injuries while doing exercise.
这里有许多其他的谈话，一个是关于如何避免在运动中受伤。
It's on the 9th of March.
这是在3月9日
It's a late afternoon talk at 4.30, and it will be in Room 6.
下午晚点开讲，在4点半，6号房间
It also says the talk is suitable for all ages.
上面还说这个讲话适合所有年龄层
And finally, there is a talk called "Stress Management", which is...
最后一个讲话是压力管理……

85.title: C9-Test 4-Section 2
transcript: Hello?
你好
Hi, it's Laura Carlton here.
-你好，我是劳拉·卡瑞顿，
We've just arrived at the holiday flat, but I can't get the hot water and heating to work.
我刚到达假日公寓，但是我不能打开热水和暖气。
Oh right!
好的
That's easy.
这很简单
Don't worry.
别担心
In the upstairs cupboard you'll find the water heater.
在楼上的柜橱里，你会找到热水器。
You'll see three main controls on the left at the bottom of the heater.
你会看到加热器底部的左面有3个主要的控制装置
The first one-the round one on the far left-is the most important one for the heating and hot water.
第一个，也就是最左边圆形的那个是加热和热水器最重要的控制装置。
It's the main control switch.
这是总控制开关
Make sure it's in the " on " position.
确定它位于“开”的位置
The switch itself doesn't light up, but the little square below will be black if the switch is "off".
开关不会变亮，但是如果开关位于“关”的位置，底下的小方块是黑色的。
That's probably what's happened-it's got switched off by mistake.
这可能就是原因，它可能不小心关掉了。
The middle one of these three controls-you'll see it's slightly larger than the first one-controls the radiators.
中间的那个，你可以看到它比第一个大一点，控制着暖气。
If you feel cold while you're there, and need the radiators on, this needs to be turned to maximum.
如果你在那里觉得冷，需要开暖气，你要把它开到最大。
The last of the three controls-the one on the right-is usually on about a number 4 setting, which for the water in the taps is usually quite hot enough.
三个控制装置的最后一个，位于右边的那个，通常开到4号档位，这时从水龙头出来的水通常足够热了。
